Every `portfolio_holdings` INSERT path now dual-writes the matching `holding_accounts(holding_id, account_id, user_id, qty=0, cost_basis=0, is_primary=true)` pairing in the same transaction, and a tracked SQL migration repairs any orphans created since the 2026-05-01 loose-script backfill.

**User-visible symptom resolved:** investment accounts with holdings whose INSERT path skipped the pairing rendered the Portfolio page as `N holdings across 1 accounts` but every Qty / Avg Cost / Mkt Value / Unrealized G/L cell showed `--`, and "All Holdings" reported `No holdings found. Showing only positions with quantity > 0`. Root cause: every aggregator (issue #25) INNER-JOINs through `holding_accounts` on `(holding_id, account_id, user_id)`; without the pairing, every transaction for the holding silently dropped and live `SUM(transactions.quantity)` evaluated to 0.

- **8 INSERT call sites fixed** to dual-write the pairing in the same transaction with `ON CONFLICT (holding_id, account_id) DO NOTHING` for idempotency and orphan-DELETE on hard pairing failure. The canonical pattern lives at [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts:4190-4201](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) (PR #116, issue #95).
  - REST `POST /api/portfolio` ([src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts)) — Add Holding dialog.
  - REST `POST /api/portfolio/crypto` ([src/app/api/portfolio/crypto/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/crypto/route.ts)) — pairing skipped when `accountId` is null (crypto holdings can be unattached).
  - Backup-restore `POST /api/data/import` ([src/app/api/data/import/route.ts](src/app/api/data/import/route.ts)) — bulk insert via `RETURNING { id, accountId }` then bulk pairing INSERT using freshly-`RETURNING`-ed ids + already-remapped `accountId`s (never raw FK ids from the source backup; preserves the cross-tenant FK guard from `strip()`).
  - csv-parser ([src/lib/csv-parser.ts](src/lib/csv-parser.ts)) — per-row `RETURNING` + pairing.
  - Connector pipeline `portfolio-holding-resolver` ([src/lib/external-import/portfolio-holding-resolver.ts](src/lib/external-import/portfolio-holding-resolver.ts)) — auto-create branch.
  - Connector pipeline `zip-orchestrator` ([src/lib/external-import/zip-orchestrator.ts](src/lib/external-import/zip-orchestrator.ts)) — bulk insert via `RETURNING { id, accountId }` then bulk pairing INSERT.
  - `getOrCreateCashHolding` ([src/lib/investment-account.ts](src/lib/investment-account.ts)) — highest-traffic auto-create site (every first transaction in a newly-flagged investment account).
  - MCP HTTP `record_trade` cash-sleeve auto-create ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), the `else` branch immediately after line 3464). Mirrors the canonical pattern from `add_portfolio_holding`.
- **Tracked migration** [scripts/migrations/20260509_holding-accounts-backfill-orphans.sql](scripts/migrations/20260509_holding-accounts-backfill-orphans.sql) — idempotent `INSERT … ON CONFLICT … DO NOTHING` repairs any orphans created between the 2026-05-01 loose-script backfill and the deploy that ships this fix. Applied automatically by `deploy.sh` once per env via `schema_migrations` bookkeeping; deploy ordering is `git pull → npm install → backup → migrations → build → restart` so existing orphans are repaired before the new code starts serving traffic.
- **CLAUDE.md** — the "Every `portfolio_holdings` INSERT path must dual-write a `holding_accounts` row" gotcha rewritten as a positive invariant ("dual-write is enforced at all 9 sites; new INSERT paths must follow the same pattern"), enumerating the 9 enforced sites + a pointer to the canonical pattern.

Out of scope (preserved per CLAUDE.md): the 4 portfolio aggregators continue to read live `SUM(transactions.quantity)` from `transactions`, NOT the cached `holding_accounts.qty` / `cost_basis` (issue #99 trap — there is no invalidation path). Stdio MCP create paths still refuse cleanly post Stream D Phase 4 (no DEK on the stdio transport); they cannot create the bug class. Tool counts unchanged: 90 HTTP / 86 stdio. The non-cash leg of MCP HTTP `record_trade` is auto-created via `createTransferPair` → `buildHoldingResolver` (the resolver branch is already covered by the `portfolio-holding-resolver` fix in this cohort).

### Dashboard balance fallback now branches on `is_investment` (#204, 2026-05-09)

`/api/dashboard` per-account balance previously fell back to `SUM(transactions.amount)` whenever `holdingsByAccount.get(b.accountId)` returned `undefined`. For an investment account whose holdings aggregator dropped a position (orphan `holding_accounts` row, FX outage, freshly-imported snapshot before the holding has any transactions), the dashboard silently rendered the cash-leg sum (just the buy/sell/dividend cash legs) as the account balance — a meaningless number that looked like a real balance. Mirrors the canonical pattern shipped under #151 for `/api/goals`.

- **[src/app/api/dashboard/route.ts](src/app/api/dashboard/route.ts)** — replaced the `holdings ? holdings.value : cashFlowBasis` ternary with explicit branching on `b.isInvestment`. Investment accounts now return `holdings?.value ?? 0` (visible `0` when the aggregator emits nothing — diagnosable, not silently misleading); cash accounts return `cashFlowBasis` unchanged. Defensive `Boolean(b.isInvestment)` coercion in case a future refactor of `getAccountBalances` returns partial rows. `cashFlowBasis`, `holdingsValue`, `holdingsCostBasis`, and `convertedBalance` response fields preserved (no consumer regression on Accounts list, Account detail, Net Worth, or Income vs Expenses panels).
- **Behavior change is user-visible** for any investment account where the aggregator currently returns empty: pre-fix shows a non-zero cash-leg number, post-fix shows `$0.00`. `$0` is the correct answer for "we couldn't compute holdings" and matches the goals page. `getAccountBalances` already returns `isInvestment` on every row ([src/lib/queries.ts:615](src/lib/queries.ts)) — no plumbing change required.
- Independent of the upstream portfolio-aggregator orphan-holdings investigation (#205); the dashboard fallback rule is wrong on its own merits and remains correct after that fix lands.

### MCP `bulk_record_transactions` — fail loud on unknown category names (#203, 2026-05-09)

`bulk_record_transactions` (MCP HTTP) previously coerced an unknown `category` name to `category_id = NULL` and reported the row as `success: true`, silently dropping the user's intent. The buggy branch lived at [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts:2765-2777](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) where a `fuzzyFind` miss returned `null` instead of pushing a per-row failure. Sibling tools (`record_transaction`, `update_transaction`, `execute_bulk_update`) all fail loudly on unknown categories — this was the symmetric gap to issue #61's `unapplied[]` contract on the bulk-update side.

The fix mirrors `update_transaction`'s strict-resolve pattern: per-row `success: false` with message `Category "<input>" not found. Available: <list>` (or a `did you mean "<close-match>" (id=N)?` hint when `resolveCategoryStrict` returns `low_confidence`). The row is NOT inserted; top-level `failed` is bumped automatically via the existing `results.filter(r => !r.success)` post-loop summary. `resolvedAccount` is included on the failure response to match the surrounding per-row failure shape (lines 2736, 2746, 2789).

Preserved:
- Rows with **no** `category` field at all still flow through `autoCategory` (the truthy `if (t.category)` check is unchanged).
- Rows with valid category names still import; `resolvedCategory` is populated.
- `dryRun: true` inherits the new failure behavior automatically (the fix sits before the dryRun branch).
- Stdio `bulk_record_transactions` is unaffected — already refused under Stream D Phase 4 (no DEK on stdio) and only accepts numeric `category_id` anyway.

User-reported during the 2026-05-09 review: a `bulk_record_transactions` call with `category: "Gifts"` (which the user did not have) returned `{"imported": 1, "failed": 0}` and inserted a row with `category_id = NULL`. With this change the same call returns `{"imported": 0, "failed": 1, "results": [{"success": false, "message": "Category \"Gifts\" not found. Available: ..."}]}` and no row is inserted.

### Demo seed — let `PF_ALLOW_DEMO_SEED=1` bypass the multi-user guard (2026-05-09)

The Finlynq prod VPS provisions both the demo user (`00000000-0000-0000-0000-00000000demo`) and the operator's real account onto the same `pf` Postgres DB — the demo doesn't have its own database. The B9 hygiene PR ([#172](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/pull/172)) added a count-based safety guard in `assertDemoDatabase` that refuses to run when the DB has >1 user row, intended to catch wrong-DB-connection mistakes. On this prod topology that guard trips every time and prevented the seed from running today after PR #199 shipped (the seed's actual wipe step is `WHERE user_id = $1` with the demo UUID at every table, so multi-user has always been safe — the count guard was just a backstop). Now the existing `PF_ALLOW_DEMO_SEED=1` opt-in (which already bypassed guard #1, the URL substring check) also bypasses guard #3. Operator on a multi-tenant prod DB sets the env var on `finlynq-demo-reset.service` once; defaults still refuse for fresh self-hosters.

### Demo seed — fix missing `holding_accounts` dual-write + expand portfolio fixture (2026-05-09)

Fixes [scripts/seed-demo.ts](scripts/seed-demo.ts) skipping the `holding_accounts` dual-write that every portfolio aggregator JOINs through (`(holding_id, account_id, user_id)` grain — see CLAUDE.md "Load-bearing gotchas" §"Every `portfolio_holdings` INSERT path must dual-write a `holding_accounts` row"). Without that join row, the demo account's `get_portfolio_analysis`, `analyze_holding`, and `get_portfolio_performance` MCP tools all returned "zero holdings tracked" — which was what Anthropic Connectors Directory reviewers (and the user) hit when querying the demo via Claude.ai. Also expands the demo holdings from 3 to 7 (VTI, VOO, VXUS, VCN.TO, VAB.TO, AAPL, BTC) plus a VOO rebalance sell so the portfolio donut/breakdown looks like a realistic small portfolio (~CA$17K cost basis after the partial sell) and the realized-gain code path is actually exercised by reviewers.

- `holding_accounts` INSERT happens inside the existing `for (const h of holdingsSeed)` loop with `qty=0, cost_basis=0, is_primary=false`. The cached `qty` / `cost_basis` columns aren't read by aggregators (CLAUDE.md flags them as a trap); only the join-row existence matters.
- No change to the wipe step — `holding_accounts.holding_id` already has `ON DELETE CASCADE` on `portfolio_holdings.id`, so `DELETE FROM portfolio_holdings WHERE user_id=$1` cleans the join automatically.
- All transaction INSERTs now stamp `source='sample_data'` (issue #28 audit trio); previously inherited the `'manual'` default and looked indistinguishable from user-typed rows in audit queries.
- Brokerage deposit bumped from $12K to $25K to cover the larger fixture with cushion remaining.

### Branding — replace Vercel default favicon.ico with Finlynq mark (2026-05-08)

Replaces `pf-app/src/app/favicon.ico` (the Next.js scaffolding default — black circle + white triangle) with the Finlynq orange chart mark, multi-size 16/32/48/64/128/256, PNG-encoded ICO entries, 15318 bytes. Modern browsers fetched `/favicon.svg` and rendered correctly, but Google's `s2/favicons` scraper, social-card embeds, and other crawlers that prefer `.ico` over `.svg` were displaying the Vercel triangle as Finlynq's brand mark for finlynq.com.

- Adds [scripts/make-favicon.mjs](scripts/make-favicon.mjs) as the reproducible source of truth — regenerates the .ico from `public/favicon.svg` using the existing `sharp` dependency.
- No layout / metadata changes; no PNG variants; no apple-touch-icon. Strictly the .ico swap + the regen script.
- Note: Google's CDN caches the old icon aggressively (days to weeks) and there is no public refresh API.

### MCP — serverInfo logo + metadata (2026-05-08)

Adds `title`, `websiteUrl`, `description`, and `icons[]` to the `Implementation` object passed to both `McpServer` constructors so compatible MCP clients render the Finlynq mark instead of the default placeholder. Composes with the existing `withAutoAnnotations` wrap shipped in #193; server version stays at `3.0.0` — purely additive metadata, no behavior change.

- HTTP route ([src/app/api/mcp/route.ts](src/app/api/mcp/route.ts)) and stdio entrypoint ([mcp-server/index.ts](mcp-server/index.ts)) both populated with the same fields.
- Icon `src` reuses the existing `https://finlynq.com/favicon.svg` (mimeType `image/svg+xml`, sizes `["any"]`) — no new public asset.
- Motivating context: Anthropic Connectors Directory submission ([HANDOVER_2026-05-08.md](../HANDOVER_2026-05-08.md)) — directory listings render server `icons[0].src` in the "Connect" UI.

### Documentation — formal note on `style-src 'unsafe-inline'` constraint (#9, 2026-05-07)

Captures the technical constraint behind why `style-src 'unsafe-inline'` can't be removed from the CSP today, and the migration path for closing it. Code-only change to [src/middleware.ts](src/middleware.ts) — a 28-line comment block above the CSP directive.

**Constraint**: every React `style={{ ... }}` prop renders as a `style="..."` HTML attribute. HTML attributes can't carry CSP nonces (only `<style>` and `<script>` tags can). Removing `'unsafe-inline'` would break every component using the style prop, plus framework-injected inline styles from Recharts, framer-motion, and Next.js's not-found page. Adding a nonce alongside `'unsafe-inline'` doesn't help — browsers disable `'unsafe-inline'` the moment any nonce or hash appears in the directive.

**Migration path** (out of scope of this PR):
1. Audit every `style={{ ... }}` in `src/` (11 files at last count). Most can move to Tailwind atomic classes; the rest can use CSS custom properties via className + `--var`.
2. Framework inline styles (Recharts SVG positioning, framer-motion animations, Next.js error pages) need hashing with `'sha256-...'` or framework-level workarounds.
3. Verify the migration with the existing CSP test suite + a runtime CSP-violation observer in dev.

**Threat addressed by closing this**: an XSS that escapes script-src (currently impossible post-B10) could exfiltrate via inline `background-image: url(attacker)` or `@import`. Real but narrow vs the script-src vectors B10 already closed.

### Test hygiene — partial triage of pre-existing failures (Open #3, batch 1) (2026-05-07)

The handover noted ~90 pre-existing test failures that pre-dated the security batch (post-Stream-D-Phase-4 cleanup never finished). This first triage batch fixes 11 of them by patching mocks that drifted from current route signatures, and documents the remaining 79 by root cause for future PRs.

- **fx-service mock missing `getDisplayCurrency`** ([tests/api/budgets.test.ts](tests/api/budgets.test.ts), [tests/api/dashboard.test.ts](tests/api/dashboard.test.ts), [tests/api/fx.test.ts](tests/api/fx.test.ts), [tests/api/reports.test.ts](tests/api/reports.test.ts)) — every route gained a `await getDisplayCurrency(userId, ?currency)` call to honor the user's display-currency preference, but the four tests that mock `@/lib/fx-service` never added the function to their mock factory. Fix: add `getDisplayCurrency: vi.fn(async (_userId, override) => override ?? "CAD")` to each mock object.
- **`getRateMap` second-arg mismatch** ([tests/api/budgets.test.ts:99](tests/api/budgets.test.ts), [tests/api/dashboard.test.ts:65](tests/api/dashboard.test.ts)) — Stream D added a `userId` second argument to `getRateMap` for per-user rate caching. Two tests asserted only the currency arg (`toHaveBeenCalledWith("EUR")`); fixed to include the auth-mock's userId (`"default"`).
- **schema mock missing `portfolioHoldings`/`holdingAccounts`** ([tests/api/reports.test.ts](tests/api/reports.test.ts)) — the reports route now calls `getHoldingsValueByAccount` for unrealized P&L, which references `schema.portfolioHoldings.id`. Added stubs for `portfolioHoldings`, `holdingAccounts`, and the additional columns (quantity, portfolioHoldingId, userId, type, linkId, tradeLinkId, enteredAmount, enteredCurrency on transactions; userId on categories/accounts/portfolio_holdings).

**Result**: 90 → 79 failing tests (11 recovered). `tests/security-tenant-isolation.test.ts`, `tests/oauth-scopes.test.ts`, `tests/envelope-pepper-version.test.ts`, `tests/api/middleware-csrf.test.ts`, `tests/login-lockout-enumeration.test.ts`, all middleware/request-origins tests still passing.

**Remaining 79 failures grouped by root cause** (each is its own follow-up PR; this triage is the foundation):

1. **Stream D Phase 4 plaintext-name fallout** (~30 failures) — tests expect routes to return `name: "Groceries"` but they now return `name: null` because the column was dropped and tests don't seed `name_ct` ciphertext. Per-feature fixture update + DEK seeding. Files: `tests/api/categories.test.ts`, `tests/api/goals.test.ts`, `tests/api/loans.test.ts`, `tests/api/subscriptions.test.ts`.
2. **Drizzle proxy-mock `rows.map is not a function`** (~20 failures) — the `mockDbChain` Proxy returns the chain object on every property access; awaited `db.select()` chains expect to terminate in an array. Needs the proxy to be thenable / mock-resolveable. Files: `tests/api/reports.test.ts`, `tests/api/snapshots.test.ts`, `tests/api/recap.test.ts`, `tests/api/scenarios.test.ts`, `tests/api/recurring.test.ts`.
3. **404 vs 200 on routes that gained `requireEncryption()`** (~15 failures) — handlers now refuse with 423 if no DEK, and tests don't supply one. Mock should set `dek: <Buffer>` on the auth context. Files: `tests/api/data.test.ts`, `tests/api/import-execute.test.ts`, `tests/api/portfolio.test.ts`, `tests/api/insights.test.ts`, `tests/api/onboarding.test.ts`, `tests/api/tax.test.ts`, `tests/api/monte-carlo.test.ts`, `tests/api/fire.test.ts`.
4. **`rawGoals.map is not a function`** ([tests/api/goals.test.ts](tests/api/goals.test.ts)) — overlaps with #1 and #2. Goals route now JOINs through `goal_accounts` (issue #130, 2026-05-04); the proxy mock doesn't reflect the new shape.
5. **MFA / require-auth schema drift** ([tests/auth/mfa.test.ts](tests/auth/mfa.test.ts), [tests/auth/require-auth.test.ts](tests/auth/require-auth.test.ts)) — the B7 MFA pending-JWT + `revoked_jtis` work added behavior these tests didn't anticipate.
6. **chat-engine `userId` parameter** ([tests/api/chat.test.ts](tests/api/chat.test.ts)) — the B3 cross-tenant fix added a required `userId` param; test doesn't pass one.
7. **rules POST `match_payee` reference** ([tests/api/rules.test.ts](tests/api/rules.test.ts)) — the well-known pre-existing `match_payee` column bug (per CLAUDE.md "Several MCP rule-management tools reference `match_payee` (a non-existent column)").
8. **transactions schema-drift** ([tests/api/transactions.test.ts](tests/api/transactions.test.ts)) — pre-mock-only references; tests still seed the dropped Stream-D plaintext columns.

Each group is independent; an agent could pick any single bucket and ship a targeted PR. Recommended order: #3 (DEK on auth context) → #1 (Stream D fixtures) → #2 (Drizzle proxy mock) — biggest blocks of failures and least risk of cross-contamination.

### Security — Session 4 batch E: PF_PEPPER rotation support (Open #2) (2026-05-07)
- **Pepper rotation is now non-destructive.** Until this PR, rotating `PF_PEPPER` invalidated every encrypted DEK envelope: the new scrypt input didn't match the wrap, so unwrap failed with a bad-tag error and active users would silently render encrypted columns as null. The handover called this out as a "load-bearing, must be tested against a copy of prod DB first" follow-up.
- **Schema** ([scripts/migrations/20260507_pepper_version.sql](scripts/migrations/20260507_pepper_version.sql)) — `users.pepper_version SMALLINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1`. Names which env var holds the pepper used when the row's DEK envelope was last wrapped: version 1 → `PF_PEPPER` (legacy default), version N → `PF_PEPPER_V<N>` (rotated). Partial index on `pepper_version` to make the rotation script's "find stragglers" SELECT cheap.
- **Envelope code** ([src/lib/crypto/envelope.ts](src/lib/crypto/envelope.ts)) — `deriveKEK` now accepts an optional `pepperVersion` param (defaults to 1 for back-compat). New `getPepperForVersion(n)` reads `PF_PEPPER` for n=1, `PF_PEPPER_V<n>` for n>1, throws on unsupported versions. The dev fallback (empty buffer + warning) is preserved per-version. `HIGHEST_SUPPORTED_PEPPER_VERSION = 2` is the new ceiling — bump it before introducing v3 or the decoder refuses any row at v3.
- **Login route** ([src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts](src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts)) — reads `user.pepperVersion`, passes it through to `deriveKEK` so unrotated rows still unwrap with the OLD pepper. **Lazy rewrap** — when `PF_PEPPER_TARGET_VERSION` env var is set to N>1, login successfully unwraps with the row's current version, then re-wraps with KEK derived from the target pepper, persists, and bumps `pepper_version` in a single follow-up UPDATE. Failure of the rewrap is logged but doesn't block the login (next attempt retries). Result: pepper rotation now happens incrementally as users log in, no force-logout needed.
- **Schema definition** ([src/db/schema-pg.ts](src/db/schema-pg.ts)) — `users.pepperVersion` field added so Drizzle's `select()` on `users` returns it.
- **Admin tool** [scripts/rewrap-peppers.ts](scripts/rewrap-peppers.ts) — operator playbook + dry-run reporter. Reports the current `pepper_version` distribution and lists stragglers (users still at < target). Per the inline doc: lazy rewrap on login does the actual work; this script's role is informational + identifying users who haven't logged in recently. The actual `--revoke-sessions` mode is documented as out of scope for this PR (per-user JWT revocation needs additional plumbing); operator falls back to global `DEPLOY_GENERATION` bump or per-user wipe-account if a force-rotation is genuinely needed.

**Operator playbook** (in [scripts/rewrap-peppers.ts](scripts/rewrap-peppers.ts)):
1. Pre-flight backup. `pg_dump` immediately before any rotation step.
2. Stage `PF_PEPPER_V2=<new>` in the systemd EnvironmentFile alongside the current `PF_PEPPER=<old>`. Restart so both peppers are loaded.
3. Set `PF_PEPPER_TARGET_VERSION=2`. Restart.
4. Watch login error rates over the next N days. Lazy rewrap fires on every successful login.
5. Run `npx tsx scripts/rewrap-peppers.ts --target=2 --stale-days=30` to identify users who haven't logged in. Decide per-user whether to force action or accept that long-dormant accounts stay at the old pepper.
6. Once distribution shows zero rows at the old version, drop `PF_PEPPER` from systemd, rename `PF_PEPPER_V2` → `PF_PEPPER`, bump `pepper_version`'s default in code, and you're back to a single-pepper configuration ready for the next rotation.

**Tests** ([tests/envelope-pepper-version.test.ts](tests/envelope-pepper-version.test.ts)) — 8 cases covering version 1 vs version 2 KEK derivation, wrap/unwrap round-trip per version, the cross-version unwrap failure (auth-tag check), the unsupported-version guard, and a full v1→v2 rotation simulation that mirrors the lazy-rewrap login path.

**This PR ships the plumbing but does NOT trigger any rotation.** No operator action is required. The `pepper_version` column adds with default=1 so every existing row stays on PF_PEPPER. To start a rotation, the operator follows the playbook above (test on a prod-DB copy first per the original handover's load-bearing warning).

Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean. Tests: 8/8 pass.

### Security — Session 4 batch D: OAuth scope plumbing (Open #1) (2026-05-07)
- **OAuth tokens now carry a `scope` claim**. `oauth_authorization_codes` and `oauth_access_tokens` gain a `scope TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'mcp:read mcp:write'` column ([scripts/migrations/20260507_oauth_scopes.sql](scripts/migrations/20260507_oauth_scopes.sql)). Recognized tokens at this PR: `mcp:read` (read-only MCP tools) and `mcp:write` (mutating MCP tools — record/update/delete/approve/etc.).
- **Authorize endpoint** ([src/app/api/oauth/authorize/route.ts](src/app/api/oauth/authorize/route.ts)) accepts a `scope` parameter from the consent POST body, validates it via `normalizeRequestedScope`, and persists it on the auth code. Empty / missing scope → DEFAULT_SCOPE (`mcp:read mcp:write`) for back-compat with pre-PR clients. Unknown scope tokens → RFC 6749 §3.3 `invalid_scope` 400.
- **Token endpoint** ([src/app/api/oauth/token/route.ts](src/app/api/oauth/token/route.ts)) carries scope from auth code → access token, returns it in the token response (`scope` field) per RFC 6749 §5.1, and preserves it across refresh per RFC 6749 §6 (refresh CANNOT escalate scope — refresh requests don't even accept a scope parameter).
- **MCP route** ([src/app/api/mcp/route.ts](src/app/api/mcp/route.ts)) wraps `server.tool()` to drop out-of-scope registrations BEFORE `registerPgTools` runs. Out-of-scope tools never appear in `tools/list` and can't be called — silent filtering rather than "exists but forbidden" so the MCP surface doesn't leak the broader catalog. API-key / session-cookie auth defaults to DEFAULT_SCOPE (no behavior change).
- **Consent screen** ([src/app/oauth/authorize/page.tsx](src/app/oauth/authorize/page.tsx)) reads `scope` from the URL and renders only the permission rows the client actually requested. Read+write clients still see the amber "read AND write" warning; read-only clients now see a green "read-only access" note instead. Pre-scope clients (no `?scope=` query param) render identically to before.
- **New library** [src/lib/oauth-scopes.ts](src/lib/oauth-scopes.ts) — `mcpToolIsReadOnly()` predicate (name-prefix classification: `get_*`, `list_*`, `find_*`, `search_*`, `analyze_*`, `preview_*`, `test_*`, `trace_*`, `detect_*`, `convert_*`, `suggest_*`, `describe_*`, `read_*`, plus `finlynq_help` exact-match), `normalizeRequestedScope` (validation + canonical ordering), `parseScope` (DB string → set), `isToolAllowedForScope` (per-token tool decision; write implies read by convention).
- **Tests:** [tests/oauth-scopes.test.ts](tests/oauth-scopes.test.ts) — 69 cases. 17 known read-only tool names asserted read-only; 39 known write tool names asserted NOT read-only; novel-prefix names default to write (defense-in-depth); `normalizeRequestedScope` covers DEFAULT_SCOPE fallthrough, canonical ordering (read first, write second), whitespace tolerance, and `InvalidScopeError` on unknown tokens; `isToolAllowedForScope` covers read-only, write-only (write implies read), full-scope, and the empty-scope-blocks-everything malformed-token guard.

**Operator action**: deploy applies the migration automatically. **Existing clients keep working** because rows without a stored `scope` fall back to `DEFAULT_SCOPE` in `consumeAuthCode` and `validateOauthToken`. Migrating clients to narrower scopes is a separate rollout — they need to start sending `scope=mcp:read` (or whatever) on the authorize redirect.

**Out of scope of this PR**: a per-client default-scope override (so the operator could force a client to read-only at registration time); a UI-side scope-picker so the user can downgrade `mcp:write` to `mcp:read` at consent time; persisting the issued scope back to the consent UI as a "previous grant" reminder. Each is small; design call before next PR.

Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean. Tests: 69/69 pass.

### Security — Session 4 batch C: backup encryption-at-rest (Open #5) (2026-05-07)
- **`pg_dump` output now encrypted with gpg --symmetric AES-256** when the deploy host has a passphrase file at `$BACKUP_ENCRYPTION_KEY_FILE` (default `/etc/finlynq/backup-key`). Backup filename is `${SERVICE_NAME}_<ts>.sql.gpg` and `chmod 0400` on creation. Plaintext-fallback if the key file is missing or `gpg` isn't installed (with a `WARNING:` log line) so self-hosters who haven't set the key up don't get failed deploys. Closes Open #5 from [SECURITY_HANDOVER_2026-05-07.md](../SECURITY_HANDOVER_2026-05-07.md).
- **New recovery script** [scripts/restore-backup.sh](scripts/restore-backup.sh) — accepts `.sql.gpg` (decrypts), `.sql` (passes through), or `.sql.gz` (gunzips). Default action prints decrypted SQL to stdout for inspection; `--restore <DATABASE_URL>` pipes into `psql` after a `[y/N]` confirmation. Same `BACKUP_ENCRYPTION_KEY_FILE` env override as `deploy.sh`. Setup playbook (one-time per host: `openssl rand -base64 64 > /etc/finlynq/backup-key && chmod 0400`) is documented inline at the top of the script — including the explicit warning that the passphrase MUST also be stored OFF-BOX (1Password / Bitwarden / safe), since a host loss + lost key file means the backups are unrecoverable.
- **Retention prune** ([deploy.sh](deploy.sh)) extended to match `*.sql.gpg` alongside `*.sql`, `*.sql.gz`, and the older `*.sql.enc` filename — same `BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS` (default 14d).
- **Threat-model addressed**: a read-only break-in (e.g. log-aggregator-as-different-user reading `/opt/finlynq-backups` after a perms drift) cannot decrypt without the 0400 root-owned passphrase file. **NOT addressed**: full root compromise — that trivially reads both, and short of moving the key off-box (KMS / HSM) we can't help. Documented inline.
- **Operator action required** (on dev + prod): `sudo install -d -m 0700 -o root -g root /etc/finlynq && sudo bash -c 'openssl rand -base64 64 > /etc/finlynq/backup-key' && sudo chmod 0400 /etc/finlynq/backup-key`. After this, the next deploy writes encrypted backups. Until this is done, deploys keep writing plaintext (with a `WARNING:` log line) — no breakage either way.

Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean (no source code touched). Shell `bash -n` syntax check on both `deploy.sh` and `scripts/restore-backup.sh` passes.

### Security — Session 4 batch B: infra hygiene (GH Actions SHA-pin + next re-pin + deploy.sh chown opener + CHANGELOG merge driver) (2026-05-07)
- **First-party GitHub Actions SHA-pinned** across `.github/workflows/{ci,deploy-dev,deploy-prod,docker,release}.yml` with version comments. `actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4.3.1`, `actions/setup-node@49933ea5288caeca8642d1e84afbd3f7d6820020 # v4.4.0`, `actions/github-script@f28e40c7f34bde8b3046d885e986cb6290c5673b # v7.1.0`, `docker/login-action@c94ce9fb468520275223c153574b00df6fe4bcc9 # v3.7.0`, `docker/metadata-action@c299e40c65443455700f0fdfc63efafe5b349051 # v5.10.0`, `docker/build-push-action@ca052bb54ab0790a636c9b5f226502c73d547a25 # v5.4.0`. Closes session-4 cleanup item #4 + the rest of original M-13. Policy decision documented inline: project pins both first-party AND third-party actions to commit SHAs, consistent with how `appleboy/ssh-action` was pinned in B1. Tradeoff is explicit — SHA-pinning means a future patch (e.g. `v4.3.2` of `actions/checkout`) won't auto-pull; periodic SHA refresh (e.g. quarterly via dependabot or manual) is the intended hygiene cadence.
- **`next` re-pinned to exact** `16.2.5` (was `^16.2.5`) in [package.json](package.json). The B1 bump used a caret to clear the audit finding fast, but with the lockfile already at `16.2.5` an exact pin gets us the same install behavior with one fewer surprise vector. Closes session-4 Open #4.
- **Defensive `deploy.sh` chown opener** ([deploy.sh](deploy.sh)). Before `rm -rf .next` runs, deploy.sh now `sudo chown -R "$REPO_OWNER:$REPO_OWNER" .next` so a half-failed previous deploy that left root-owned files inside `.next/standalone/.next/static/` recovers automatically instead of needing manual SSH chown. Pitfall #1 in [memory/finlynq-deploy-pitfalls.md](../memory/finlynq-deploy-pitfalls.md) has now bitten three times — session 3 prod promotion was the most recent. Conditional on `[ -d .next ] && [ -n "$REPO_OWNER" ]` so a fresh box without `.next/` and without an owner-stat result is a clean no-op. `2>/dev/null || true` so missing entries don't fail the deploy. Closes session-4 #10.
- **CHANGELOG.md merge driver** ([.gitattributes](.gitattributes)). `CHANGELOG.md merge=union` set so two PRs adding entries at the top of `## [Unreleased]` concatenate automatically instead of conflicting. The B1–B10 batch hit a CHANGELOG conflict on every one of the 11 merges; this saves the next multi-PR batch ~30 min of manual rebasing. Reviewers re-order if needed — `union` is git-builtin, no per-developer config required. Also added `*.sh text eol=lf` so Windows checkouts can't silently rewrite `deploy.sh` to CRLF (kills the executable bit and breaks Linux deploy). Closes session-4 Open #6.

Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean. No new tests required (workflow + config changes); CI itself exercises the new SHAs.

### Security — Session 4 quick wins: CSRF login bypass + CSP dedup + lockout-enum probe (2026-05-07)
- **CSRF gate now bypasses pre-auth state-changing routes** ([src/middleware.ts](src/middleware.ts)). New `CSRF_BYPASS_PATHS` allowlist covers `/api/auth/login`, `/api/auth/register`, `/api/auth/password-reset/request`, `/api/auth/password-reset/confirm`, `/api/auth/verify-email`. These authenticate via password / reset token / email-verification token rather than the session cookie — so a stale `pf_session` cookie left over from a previous session no longer 403s a fresh login attempt. The operator hit this in session 2 of the security batch (documented in [SECURITY_HANDOVER_2026-05-07.md](../SECURITY_HANDOVER_2026-05-07.md) session 4 pickup item #1). Login CSRF is mitigated by the freshly-issued cookie's `SameSite=Lax` (an attacker cross-site POST can't read it back) plus the credential requirement itself; the URL-token routes use the unguessable token as the CSRF nonce.
- **CSP `object-src 'none'` deduped** — the directive was emitted twice in the response Content-Security-Policy header (cosmetic; browsers de-dupe). Removed the duplicate.
- **Lockout-window enumeration probe**: `checkRateLimit` ([src/lib/rate-limit.ts](src/lib/rate-limit.ts)) is purely string-keyed with no DB lookup or user-existence branch, so the per-identifier rate-limit window is provably identical for known and unknown identifiers. The login route's bucket key (`login:id:h:${identifier.toLowerCase()}`) is the same shape for a real user and a brand-new fake identifier, and `Retry-After` is computed from `(resetAt - now)` of the bucket — no enumeration oracle. New regression test locks the parity invariant in.
- **Doc/code mismatches in [SECURITY_HANDOVER_2026-05-07.md](../SECURITY_HANDOVER_2026-05-07.md) corrected** — M-15 prose (demo-seed guard) now matches the OR-then-row-count layering in [scripts/seed-demo.ts](scripts/seed-demo.ts); M-17 prose (prod SMTP) now reflects the lazy-on-first-`sendEmail()` throw in [src/lib/email.ts](src/lib/email.ts).

**Tests added:**
- [tests/api/middleware-csrf.test.ts](tests/api/middleware-csrf.test.ts) — covers the 5 CSRF bypass paths, asserts `/api/auth/logout` is still gated when cross-origin, confirms `object-src 'none'` appears exactly once in the CSP, smokes Bearer / API-key / `pf_` URL-token bypass paths.
- [tests/login-lockout-enumeration.test.ts](tests/login-lockout-enumeration.test.ts) — known/unknown identifier window-parity, case-normalized bucket keying, and a static guard that `checkRateLimit` stays pure-in-memory (any future async I/O regression fails this assertion).

**Out of scope of this PR / still open from the handover pickup list:** SHA-pinning first-party GitHub Actions + re-pinning `next` (decision call), bigger CSP work (`style-src 'unsafe-inline'`), defensive `deploy.sh chown` opener, OAuth scope plumbing (Open #1), `PF_PEPPER` re-wrap migration (Open #2), ~90 pre-existing test failures triage (Open #3), backup encryption-at-rest (Open #5), CHANGELOG merge driver (Open #6). Each is documented in the handover with the reason for deferral.

Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean (the pre-existing untracked `src/app/{mcp-guide,privacy,vs}/` parallel-route conflicts are unchanged from prior sessions; flagged in the handover).

### Security — Batch B1: Docker, deps, infra hygiene (2026-05-07)
- **`.dockerignore` added** so `COPY . .` in [Dockerfile](Dockerfile) no longer bakes local `.env*`, `.git`, `node_modules`, `.next`, tests, agent state (`.claude/`), or coverage output into the published GHCR image. Closes finding **C-1** in [SECURITY_REVIEW_2026-05-06.md](../SECURITY_REVIEW_2026-05-06.md). Self-hosters who built images locally before this PR should rotate `PF_JWT_SECRET` / `PF_PEPPER` / `PF_STAGING_KEY` and any DB password that landed in a published layer. Dockerfile gains `# TODO(security): pin to digest` markers on each `node:22-alpine` reference — pinning a digest is a separate cleanup and isn't blocking.
- **Dependency CVEs cleared** — `drizzle-orm` bumped to `^0.45.2` (CVSS-7.5 SQL-injection GHSA-gpj5-g38j-94v9, finding **C-2**); `nodemailer` to `^8.0.7` (SMTP CRLF command injection GHSA-vvjj-xcjg-gr5g, finding **H-14**); `next` to `^16.2.5` to clear high-severity advisories on rewrites / null-origin / DoS paths (related to **H-15**); transitive `picomatch` / `path-to-regexp` ReDoS pulled forward via `npm audit fix` (no `--force`). `npm audit --omit=dev` now reports zero high/critical findings.
- **`PF_PEPPER` + `PF_STAGING_KEY` documented in [.env.example](.env.example)** with the ≥32-char production-required note and `openssl rand -hex 32` generation hint, plus a heads-up that `PF_STAGING_KEY` must also be set on the separate `finlynq-demo-reset.service` systemd unit (it doesn't inherit). Closes finding **H-17** — self-hosters were silently falling back to dev defaults because the example env file omitted them.
- **`DEPLOY_GENERATION` is mandatory in production** ([src/lib/auth/jwt.ts](src/lib/auth/jwt.ts) `currentDeployGeneration()`). Previously the resolver fell back to `"0"` if unset, which silently defeated the force-logout-on-deploy invariant: a missed env stamp meant freshly-minted JWTs accepted forever. Now mirrors the `PF_JWT_SECRET` throw pattern — the helper throws on first call in `NODE_ENV=production` if `process.env.DEPLOY_GENERATION` is unset, with a message pointing at the systemd drop-in. Dev still falls back to `"0"`. Closes finding **M-20**.
- **`deploy.sh` migration runner uses psql parameter binding** instead of bash string interpolation for the `schema_migrations` `SELECT 1` and `INSERT` queries. The upstream `[A-Za-z0-9_-]` filename regex stays in place — the binding is defense-in-depth so a future loosening of that regex can't escalate into deploy-superuser SQL injection. Closes finding **H-16**.
- **CI workflow hardening:**
  - `.github/workflows/release.yml` — changelog interpolation routed through `env: NOTES: ${{ ... }}` and read as `process.env.NOTES` inside `actions/github-script`, instead of being template-pasted into a JS string. A backtick or `${...}` in the changelog can no longer be evaluated as workflow code under the `contents: write` token. Closes finding **M-11**.
  - `.github/workflows/{ci,deploy-prod,deploy-dev}.yml` — `continue-on-error: true` removed from the `npx tsc --noEmit` step. CLAUDE.md notes the codebase passes typecheck today; if a future regression slips in, CI will now fail loud rather than promote a broken build to prod. Closes finding **M-12**.
  - `.github/workflows/{deploy-prod,deploy-dev}.yml` — `appleboy/ssh-action` pinned to commit SHA `7eaf76671a0d7eec5d98ee897acda4f968735a17` (v1.2.0) with an inline comment recording the version. A tag rebase upstream can no longer silently swap the action that ships to prod. Closes finding **M-13**.
- **Out of scope (other batches):** OAuth/DCR fixes (**C-4**, B2), chat-engine and import-hash cross-tenant scoping (**C-3**, **C-5**, **H-9**, B3), ownership-verifier helper across nine routes (**H-1**, B4), CSP/CSRF middleware (**C-8**, **H-2**, B5), auth-flow timing + MFA-pending JWT + JWT revocation (**C-6**, **C-7**, **H-3..H-7**, B6/B7), MCP/SQL polish (**H-13**, **M-1..M-4**, **M-21**, B8). See [SECURITY_FIXES_PLAN.md](../SECURITY_FIXES_PLAN.md). Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean.

### Security — Batch B4: verifyOwnership helper + IDOR fixes on 9 routes (2026-05-07)
- **Cross-tenant FK ownership-verifier helper + IDOR fixes on 9 routes (B4 / H-1, 2026-05-07).** Pattern-wide IDOR per `SECURITY_REVIEW_2026-05-06.md` finding H-1: nine REST routes accepted client-supplied FK ids (`accountId`, `categoryId`, `portfolioHoldingId`, `assignCategoryId`) without verifying that the FK belonged to the authenticated user. Postgres FK constraints are satisfied by global serial PKs, so writes against another user's id succeeded silently — a snapshot for user B's account would land under user A's `user_id`, a budget against user B's category would skew A's "spent vs budget" view, a rule with `assignCategoryId` pointing at user B's category would re-categorize user A's transactions to a category they don't own, etc. **New helper** [src/lib/verify-ownership.ts](src/lib/verify-ownership.ts) — single source of truth modeled on the existing inline `verifyAccountOwnership` in `goals/route.ts` and `assertOwnership` in `holding-accounts/route.ts`. Accepts `{accountIds?, categoryIds?, holdingIds?, goalIds?, loanIds?, subscriptionIds?, ruleIds?}`, runs a `SELECT id FROM <table> WHERE user_id = $1 AND id = ANY($2)` per non-empty list, throws a typed `OwnershipError` (carrying `kind` + `missingIds`) on the first table where any supplied id doesn't belong to the user. Skips null / undefined / non-positive ids cleanly so existing zod-validated "0 sentinel" UI bugs surface their own error rather than this one. Re-uses ids in a Set so callers passing duplicates get a single SELECT. **Routes wired:** `POST /api/transactions` + `PUT /api/transactions` (verify `accountId`, `categoryId`, `portfolioHoldingId` when present) — checked BEFORE the holding-name resolver auto-creates anything; `POST /api/snapshots` (verify `accountId`); `POST /api/budgets` (verify `categoryId`); `POST /api/loans` + `PUT /api/loans` (verify optional `accountId`); `POST /api/subscriptions` + `PUT /api/subscriptions` (verify both `categoryId` and `accountId` — PUT defensively reads the passthrough body); `POST /api/rules` + `PUT /api/rules` (verify `assignCategoryId`); `POST /api/transactions/bulk` `update_account` and `update_category` cases (verify the new FK in the body BEFORE the bulk UPDATE re-points all selected rows); `insertOpeningBalanceAdjustment` in [src/lib/external-import/reconciliation.ts](src/lib/external-import/reconciliation.ts) wired at the helper level + the route in [src/app/api/import/connectors/wealthposition/reconcile/route.ts](src/app/api/import/connectors/wealthposition/reconcile/route.ts) catches the typed error. **`createTransaction` / `updateTransaction` in `src/lib/queries.ts` left alone** — verification stays at the route boundary so the helper signature stays clean and every entry point is grep-discoverable; the only inbound caller of those helpers from a request body is the `/api/transactions` route which already calls `verifyOwnership` directly. **404, not 403** — every cross-tenant rejection returns HTTP 404 ("Not found") so the response shape matches a deleted-or-never-existed id. A 403 with "not yours" would let an authenticated probe enumerate which ids exist in another tenant. **Regression coverage:** [tests/api/authz-ownership.test.ts](tests/api/authz-ownership.test.ts) seeds two users (A + B) with their own accounts/categories/holdings, exercises the helper directly (passes / cross-tenant account / cross-tenant category / cross-tenant holding / null-ids / mixed-batch / dedup), and smoke-tests four representative routes (`/api/snapshots`, `/api/budgets`, `/api/rules`, `/api/transactions/bulk update_account` + `update_category`) for the 404. The existing per-route unit tests (snapshots/budgets/loans/subscriptions/rules/transactions) bypass the new helper via `vi.mock("@/lib/verify-ownership")` — they pre-date this work and don't seed accounts; the dedicated authz-ownership.test.ts is where the cross-tenant invariant is tested. No tool surface change. No schema migration. No new env vars. Files: [src/lib/verify-ownership.ts](src/lib/verify-ownership.ts) (NEW), [src/app/api/transactions/route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/route.ts), [src/app/api/snapshots/route.ts](src/app/api/snapshots/route.ts), [src/app/api/budgets/route.ts](src/app/api/budgets/route.ts), [src/app/api/loans/route.ts](src/app/api/loans/route.ts), [src/app/api/subscriptions/route.ts](src/app/api/subscriptions/route.ts), [src/app/api/rules/route.ts](src/app/api/rules/route.ts), [src/app/api/transactions/bulk/route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/bulk/route.ts), [src/lib/external-import/reconciliation.ts](src/lib/external-import/reconciliation.ts), [src/app/api/import/connectors/wealthposition/reconcile/route.ts](src/app/api/import/connectors/wealthposition/reconcile/route.ts), [tests/api/authz-ownership.test.ts](tests/api/authz-ownership.test.ts) (NEW). Build: `tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` clean. Tests: 12 new pass; full suite shows no net regression vs `dev` (90 pre-existing failures unchanged, +12 new passes).
### Security
- **Cross-tenant data scoping — chat engine, import-hash dedup, backup-restore (B3, 2026-05-07).** Three findings from `SECURITY_REVIEW_2026-05-06.md` (C-3, C-5, H-9) closing two cross-tenant data-leak primitives and one cross-tenant FK-write primitive. **(C-3) chat engine zero `user_id` scoping** ([src/lib/chat-engine.ts](src/lib/chat-engine.ts)): every `db.select(...).from(schema.X)` call (accounts / categories / budgets / transactions / recurring_transactions / goals) now ANDs `eq(schema.X.userId, userId)`. The `processMessage()` signature gains a required `userId: string` parameter next to the existing `dek`; `findCategoryName` / `findAccountName` ditto. The chat route handler at [src/app/api/chat/route.ts](src/app/api/chat/route.ts) threads `auth.context.userId` through. Pre-fix, any dev-mode user invoking the built-in chat aggregated net worth, balances, spending, budgets, and forecast SUMs across the entire DB — a single user's "what's my net worth" returned a global figure. **(H-9) `checkDuplicates` / `checkFitIdDuplicates` cross-tenant probe** ([src/lib/import-hash.ts](src/lib/import-hash.ts)): both functions gain a required `userId: string` parameter; the IN-clause now ANDs `eq(transactions.userId, userId)`. All five callsites threaded ([src/lib/import-pipeline.ts](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts), [src/lib/reconcile.ts](src/lib/reconcile.ts), [src/app/api/import/staging/upload/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staging/upload/route.ts), [src/lib/csv-parser.ts](src/lib/csv-parser.ts)). Pre-fix, an authenticated user could probe whether any 32-char content hash or bank-issued fitId existed anywhere in the DB by uploading a one-row CSV and reading the dedup report. `import_hash` is **NOT** recomputed by the fix — it remains plaintext-payee-derived per CLAUDE.md "import_hash always over plaintext payee" (load-bearing for cross-source dedup). **(C-5) backup-restore FK fallback** ([src/app/api/data/import/route.ts](src/app/api/data/import/route.ts) lines 309-345 / 333-352): the `?? (accountId as number)` and `?? (categoryId as number)` fallbacks in the transactions and splits remap blocks are dropped. An unmapped `accountId` / `categoryId` now throws with a clear error mirroring the canonical `strip()` helper above. Pre-fix, restoring a corrupted backup whose `accounts` or `categories` section was missing or stripped silently inserted the source DB's raw integer FK against the importing user's transactions — giving the importer cross-tenant FK references to whichever user happened to own the matching serial PK. Splits gain the same treatment plus an additional null-parent skip (orphan splits without a mapped parent transaction are silently dropped, the only soft path). **Regression test:** new [tests/security-tenant-isolation.test.ts](tests/security-tenant-isolation.test.ts) seeds two users in a Drizzle-mock DB and asserts (a) chat-engine `processMessage` for user A returns no rows / chartData when only user B has accounts and transactions; (b) `checkDuplicates` and `checkFitIdDuplicates` for user A return empty sets even when user B's row matches the probed hash / fitId, while user B's own probe still hits; (c) the inline transaction/split FK-remap predicate throws on unmapped FKs and passes through nulls cleanly. Verified by intentionally reverting each fix in isolation and confirming the regression test fails, then restoring. **No tool surface change** (MCP unchanged at 90 HTTP / 86 stdio). Files touched: 4 source + 1 chat route + 1 callsite ladder for import-hash (5 files) + 1 new test. Cross-refs: `SECURITY_FIXES_PLAN.md` Batch B3, `SECURITY_REVIEW_2026-05-06.md` findings C-3, C-5, H-9. Build: `npm run build` clean.

### Added
- **MCP staging tools: list / get / update / link-pair / approve / reject (#156, 2026-05-06).** Seven new HTTP MCP tools so Claude can categorize an inbound statement before anything hits `transactions`. The staging tables (`staged_imports` + `staged_transactions`) become the natural surface for the "AI assistant manages your finances" workflow: an email/upload arrives → Claude lists what's pending → edits per-row metadata (category, payee, transfer pairing, holding, quantity) → approves what looks right. Tools registered in [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) at the bottom of `registerPgTools`. **Read tools:** `list_staged_imports` (statement-level metadata + a synthetic `reconciliation` block per the same shape the review page renders — statement balance vs current balance vs projected post-approval balance, NULL when un-bound), `get_staged_import` (full detail with decrypted display fields), `list_staged_transactions` (flat row list, filters by import / dedup-status / row-status / tx-type — useful for "show me every uncategorized pending row across all my pending statements"). **Write tools:** `update_staged_transaction` (per-row edits — same shape and validations as PATCH `/api/import/staged/[id]/rows/[rowId]`), `link_staged_transfer_pair` (sugar over update — sets `tx_type='R'` + cross-points `peer_staged_id` after validating same-import + opposite-amounts + different-account-names), `approve_staged_rows` (materialize into `transactions`), `reject_staged_import` (hard-delete + cascade). **Confirmation-token preview/execute pattern** on the destructive ones via [src/lib/mcp/confirmation-token.ts](src/lib/mcp/confirmation-token.ts): `approve_staged_rows` and `reject_staged_import` are single-tool, two-call — omit `confirmation_token` and you get a summary + signed token (5-min TTL); pass the token + same payload and the tool commits. `approve_staged_rows` ALSO accepts an optional caller-supplied `idempotencyKey` (UUID) — same 72h `mcp_idempotency_keys` pattern as `bulk_record_transactions`, scoped on `(user_id, key, tool_name='approve_staged_rows')`. Stored response body is metadata-only (`{ok, imported, errors, stagedImportId}`) so no plaintext redaction is required (CLAUDE.md "response_json MUST be redacted of plaintext payee / account name before persisting" — already metadata-only here, flagged in code). The preview branch deliberately does NOT consume the idempotency key — only the commit branch does — so a preview never blocks a future real submit. **Per-row encryption-tier branch** on every read (CLAUDE.md "Staged-transactions reads MUST branch on `encryption_tier` per row"): `'service'` rows decrypt with `decryptStaged()` (PF_STAGING_KEY, sv1: envelope); `'user'` rows decrypt with `tryDecryptField(dek, ...)` (user DEK, v1: envelope). Mixed tiers within the same staged_imports batch are expected mid-upgrade and handled correctly. `update_staged_transaction` re-encrypts edited fields under the row's EXISTING tier — never flips mid-edit. `import_hash` is NEVER recomputed by edits (load-bearing for cross-source dedup). The user-tier write path returns a clean error when the DEK is unavailable rather than half-applying. **Approve-time materialization** mirrors the REST `/api/import/staged/[id]/approve` route's classification: peer-paired transfers (both rows checked) mint a server-generated `link_id` (UUID v4 inside the handler) and INSERT both legs in one transaction with inverted amounts, `type='R'`, encrypted payee/note/tags under DEK, audit `source='import'`; target-account-paired transfers delegate to `createTransferPair()` which owns FX, transfer-category resolution, and the four-check link-id rule; everything else flows through `executeImport` with a per-row `portfolioHoldingId` hint that skips the holding-name resolver. Investment-account rows whose holding is unset get the account's Cash sleeve via `defaultHoldingForInvestmentAccount`. After commit, `invalidateUser(userId)` is called on the per-user MCP tx cache (CLAUDE.md "Every MCP tx-mutating write must call `invalidateUser(userId)`"). Staged rows are deleted on materialization; the parent `staged_imports` row is dropped only when nothing remains. **Cross-tenant isolation.** Every tool filters by `user_id` and returns "Not found" (the MCP equivalent of 404) when the id doesn't belong to the caller. `list_staged_transactions` verifies ownership before filtering when `stagedImportId` is supplied so cross-tenant attackers can't probe whether the id exists for someone else (vs. silently returning `[]`). `link_staged_transfer_pair` validates same-import + opposite-amounts + different-account-names. `update_staged_transaction` validates ownership of every FK pointer (`peerStagedId` → same staged_import + user; `targetAccountId` → user; `portfolioHoldingId` → user) and the mutual exclusion of `peerStagedId` vs `targetAccountId`. **Stdio MCP refuses all seven tools cleanly** with a single shared error message ("This tool requires the HTTP MCP transport because staging operations need to read or write encrypted data under your DEK. Connect via the HTTP MCP at /mcp instead, or use the web UI at /import/pending."). Read tools refuse too — even though service-tier rows would technically decrypt without a DEK, user-tier rows would silently surface raw `v1:` ciphertext to Claude. The refusal handler intentionally does NOT echo `PF_USER_ID` so stdio errors never leak the configured user id. Registered in [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts) so the tools appear in `tools/list` on stdio with a clear "use HTTP" pointer rather than vanishing. **MCP tool surface: 83 → 90 HTTP, 79 → 86 stdio** (+7 each). CLAUDE.md preamble + [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md) updated with the new count and per-tool descriptions. **Out of scope:** a "park this for later" tool that lets Claude push transactions INTO staging (separate ticket); a staging-aware `apply_rules_to_uncategorized` (the rule tools have a pre-existing `match_payee` bug — fix that first elsewhere); a bulk variant of `update_staged_transaction` (per-row updates over MCP are fine for the volumes we expect — single statement is ~50 rows max). PDF / connector ingest paths are unchanged. Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean.

- **Transfer / quantity / note / tags editors on the staging review page (#155, 2026-05-06).** Brings `/import/pending` to full-transaction parity. Each row in the review dialog now has an expand toggle that opens an inline editor exposing every editable field the live transactions table supports: **Type** (Expense / Income / Transfer), **Payee**, **Category**, **Note**, **Tags**, and — for investment-account rows — **Holding** + **Quantity**. Cross-currency rows can override **Entered amount** + **Entered currency**. Edits autosave on blur via a new `PATCH /api/import/staged/[id]/rows/[rowId]` endpoint. The dialog stays open across edits so the user can categorize a whole statement before approving. **Transfer pairing UI:** transfer rows (`tx_type='R'`) get a two-option pairing block — pick a **sibling staged row** (writes `peer_staged_id`) OR pick a **target Finlynq account** (writes `target_account_id`). The two are mutually exclusive, enforced both client-side (selecting one disables the other input and clears its value) and server-side (PATCH 400s when both are set). Peer dropdown only lists same-import siblings whose amount is the additive inverse of the editing row; target-account dropdown excludes the row's own source account. **Approve-time materialization extended:** [src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/approve/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/%5Bid%5D/approve/route.ts) classifies selected rows into three buckets before calling the pipeline. **Bucket A — peer-paired transfers:** mint a server-side `link_id` (UUID v4 inside the handler — NEVER from client) and INSERT both legs in one statement with inverted amounts, `type='R'`, `categoryId=<user's Transfer category>`, encrypted `payee`/`note`/`tags` under DEK, audit `source='import'`. Half-pairs (only one of two peer-linked rows checked in the user's selection) error out without materializing — committing one leg orphans it. **Bucket B — target-account-paired transfers:** call `createTransferPair()` (owns FX, transfer-category resolution, the four-check link-id rule). The staged row's amount sign decides which side is "from" / "to": negative = leaving the staged row's account, positive = arriving at the staged row's account. **Bucket C — everything else:** flows through `executeImport` as before. Investment-account rows pre-fill their `portfolio_holding_id` via a new `RawTransaction.portfolioHoldingId` hint that skips the holding-name resolver — no risk of auto-creating a phantom Cash sleeve when the user already picked one. Investment-account rows without a holding pick fall back to the per-account Cash sleeve via `defaultHoldingForInvestmentAccount` (mirrors the upload-pipeline's permissive behavior). **Encryption tier preserved on edits:** PATCH re-encrypts edited text fields under the **row's existing tier**: service-tier rows stay sv1: under `PF_STAGING_KEY`, user-tier rows stay v1: under the user's DEK. Never flips tiers mid-edit (login-time upgrade job is the only path that promotes service → user). **`import_hash` is NEVER recomputed** by the PATCH, even when payee was edited — load-bearing per CLAUDE.md ("`import_hash` always over plaintext payee", computed from bank-supplied plaintext at ingest, dedup-stable across the row's lifetime). Bank-side dedup runs against what the bank sent us, not what the user retitled it to. **Cross-tenant isolation:** every PATCH validates that the staged_import belongs to `userId`, the row belongs to `(userId, stagedImportId)`, the proposed `peer_staged_id` belongs to the same `(userId, stagedImportId)`, the proposed `target_account_id` belongs to `userId`, and the proposed `portfolio_holding_id` belongs to `userId`. All cross-tenant attacks return 404 (same shape as the rest of the staging API). **Per-row PATCH endpoint** added at [src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/rows/[rowId]/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/%5Bid%5D/rows/%5BrowId%5D/route.ts) — Zod-validated body, `requireEncryption()`. **MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio** — staging tools are tracked separately under #156. **Out of scope:** stdio MCP staging tools (#156); a `bulk_update_staged_transactions`; auto-detect transfer pairs at upload time (heuristic-heavy: same date, equal-and-opposite amounts, different accounts — defer; user can manually pair in the UI); backfilling existing email-staged rows with `tx_type='E'` (the schema migration's default already does this); fully-encrypted staging-row tags (still plaintext at staging today). Files touched: NEW [src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/rows/[rowId]/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/%5Bid%5D/rows/%5BrowId%5D/route.ts), NEW [src/components/staging/staged-row-editor.tsx](src/components/staging/staged-row-editor.tsx), [src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/%5Bid%5D/route.ts) (GET surfaces all editable fields), [src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/approve/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/%5Bid%5D/approve/route.ts) (transfer-pair branches + holding-id hint), [src/lib/import-pipeline.ts](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts) (`RawTransaction.portfolioHoldingId` hint), [src/app/(app)/import/pending/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/import/pending/page.tsx) (per-row expand + editor mount + accounts/holdings catalog fetch). Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean.

- **Statement-balance reconciliation callout on `/import/pending` (#154, 2026-05-06).** When a staged import carries a non-null `statement_balance` AND `bound_account_id`, the review dialog now renders a three-column "Statement says / Finlynq has now / After approval" callout above the row table. Match indicator is green when "After approval" lands within 0.01 of "Statement says", amber otherwise. Statement-balance-set-but-no-account renders a one-line "No account linked — statement balance not reconcilable." hint; CSV uploads without a balance render no callout at all (no clutter on the no-data path). **Display-only, never blocks** — the Approve button is unaffected by the match state. Statements lag, pending transactions clear later, the user is the judge. **Live-recompute** as checkboxes toggle: `currentBalance + Σ(amount of selected rows where dedup_status != 'existing')`. EXISTING-classified rows are filtered out of the sum even when selected, since they're already in the live `transactions` table and would double-count. The server's `projectedBalance` is the "approve-everything-eligible" baseline; the client owns the per-toggle live recompute. **Investment-account aware:** "Finlynq has now" routes through `getHoldingsValueByAccount(userId, dek)` for `is_investment=true` accounts (CLAUDE.md "Account balance for accounts with holdings = `holdings.value`, NOT `b.balance + holdings.value`") — same helper the dashboard already uses, no third aggregator added. Pure-cash accounts continue to use `SUM(transactions.amount)`. Statement balance is rendered in `staged_imports.statement_currency`; "Finlynq has now" is FX-converted via `getRate(account_currency → statement_currency, today, userId)` when they differ (same-currency hops at rate=1; FX-fetch failure falls back to displaying in account currency unconverted — accepted tradeoff for a display-only surface). **Manual-override input** for CSV/XLSX uploads was already plumbed under #153 (the upload card's "Statement balance" field) — no change to the upload route. OFX/QFX statements auto-prefill from `<LEDGERBAL>` at upload time. **MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio.** Files touched: [src/components/staging/reconciliation-callout.tsx](src/components/staging/reconciliation-callout.tsx) (new), [src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/route.ts) (GET handler returns a `reconciliation: { currentBalance, projectedBalance, pendingDelta, boundAccountCurrency }` block + surfaces `dedup_status`/`row_status` per row), [src/app/(app)/import/pending/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/import/pending/page.tsx) (imports + renders the callout above the row table inside the review dialog). **Out of scope:** CSV-footer balance auto-detect (heterogeneous formats — manual input handles it), multi-account reconciliation (bound to one account by design), per-row "this row brings the balance from X to Y" annotation (UI clutter, defer), share-count reconciliation against `holding_accounts.qty` (separate problem surface). Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean.

- **Route CSV/OFX/QFX uploads through staging; retire direct-commit (#153, 2026-05-06).** Replaces the old `/api/import/reconcile/{preview,commit}` pair with a unified single review surface — every upload now lands in `staged_imports` + `staged_transactions` and is reviewed at `/import/pending`, the same surface email-imported batches use. **New endpoint:** `POST /api/import/staging/upload` — multipart form (`file`, optional `accountId` / `templateId` / `tolerance`, optional CSV `statementBalance`) — parses, classifies NEW/EXISTING/PROBABLE_DUPLICATE against existing transactions, and persists everything in one transaction. Returns `{stagedImportId, redirectTo: "/import/pending?id=..."}`. CSV needs-mapping returns `422 type:"csv-needs-mapping"` so the same column-mapping dialog the reconcile UI used before still works. **Encryption tier:** uploads have a session DEK, so rows land at `encryption_tier='user'` (v1: under user DEK) — bypasses the service→user login-time upgrade job that email-import staging relies on. `import_hash` is computed from plaintext payee at ingest and never recomputed (load-bearing per CLAUDE.md). Read paths in `/api/import/staged/[id]` + `/approve` already branch on `encryption_tier` per row. **Approve-time format tag:** `/api/import/staged/[id]/approve` now selects `staged.source` + `staged.fileFormat` and tags `transactions.tags` with `source:csv|ofx|qfx|email|excel` accordingly — uploads no longer get `source:email` mis-stamped. Existing tags from staging are preserved (idempotent merge). **Schema fields populated** on `staged_imports`: `source='upload'`, `file_format` (`'csv'|'ofx'|'qfx'`), `original_filename`, `bound_account_id`, `statement_balance` + `statement_balance_date` + `statement_currency` (OFX `<LEDGERBAL>` / user-typed for CSV), `statement_period_start` + `statement_period_end` (derived from row dates). `staged_transactions.dedup_status` is persisted at ingest so the review page renders without re-classifying. **UI changes.** `/import/reconcile` is now an upload entry point only: the in-page edit-and-commit table is gone. Upload card adds an optional "Statement balance" input for CSVs. `/import/pending` reads `?id=…` from the URL and auto-opens the detail dialog (wrapped the page in a `<Suspense>` boundary for `useSearchParams`). Pending list now shows upload icon + filename for upload-source rows and the existing mail icon + subject for email rows. Pending list endpoint surfaces `originalFilename` + `fileFormat`. **Deletes.** Route files `src/app/api/import/reconcile/{preview,commit}/route.ts`, the old test `tests/api/import-reconcile-preview.test.ts`, and `commitReconcile` / `MAX_RECONCILE_ROWS` / `ApprovedReconcileRow` / `CommitResult` from `src/lib/reconcile.ts`. The classifier `classifyForReconcile` is kept for future review surfaces. Account ownership + investment-account holding constraints continue to be enforced inside `executeImport()` (which the approve endpoint calls). **Email-import staging path is unchanged** — the webhook still calls `stageEmailImport()` and rows still land at `encryption_tier='service'`; only the upload path is new. **Out of scope:** XLSX uploads still route through the existing `/import` flow (sheet selection + column mapping); folding them into staging needs a column-mapping dialog on the review page. Statement-balance reconciliation UI (#154), transfer/quantity/note/tags editors on the review page (#155), and MCP staging tools (#156) are separate tickets. MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio. Files touched: `src/app/api/import/staging/upload/route.ts` (new), `src/app/api/import/staged/route.ts`, `src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/approve/route.ts`, `src/app/(app)/import/reconcile/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/import/pending/page.tsx`, `src/components/reconcile/upload-card.tsx`, `src/lib/reconcile.ts`, `src/lib/external-import/parsers/csv-pipeline.ts`, `src/lib/external-import/duplicate-detect-pool.ts`. Build: `tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` clean.

- **Unified-ingest columns on staging tables (#152, 2026-05-06).** Pure additive schema migration that prepares `staged_imports` + `staged_transactions` to become the single landing zone for every import source — CSV/OFX/XLSX uploads, email attachments, future Plaid sync, and MCP "park for later" requests. **No behavior changes:** existing email-import flow continues to work unchanged because every new column either has a sensible default (`tx_type='E'`, `dedup_status='new'`, `row_status='pending'`) or is nullable. **`staged_imports` gains 8 columns** for per-statement metadata: `statement_balance` / `statement_balance_date` / `statement_currency` (OFX `LEDGERBAL.*` or CSV/XLSX footer), `statement_period_start` / `statement_period_end` (transactions window), `bound_account_id` (FK to `accounts(id)`, populated when the user picks an account on upload or OFX `ACCTID` matches), `file_format` (`'ofx' | 'qfx' | 'csv' | 'xlsx' | 'pdf' | 'plaid' | 'mcp'`), and `original_filename` (display only). **`staged_transactions` gains 11 columns** for full-transaction parity: `tx_type` (CHECK in `('E','I','R')`, default `'E'`), `quantity` (investment trades), `portfolio_holding_id` (FK with `ON DELETE SET NULL` — required at approve time for investment accounts per CLAUDE.md), `entered_amount` / `entered_currency` (cross-currency rows per #129), `tags` (matches live `transactions.tags`), `fit_id` (OFX `FITID` carried through staging so dedup at approve time uses the same key the import pipeline uses), `peer_staged_id` (self-FK, `ON DELETE SET NULL DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED` so both legs of an in-staging transfer pair can be inserted in the same transaction), `target_account_id` (FK; mutually exclusive with `peer_staged_id` — used when only one leg is in staging and the user picks the destination), `dedup_status` (CHECK in `('new','existing','probable_duplicate')`, default `'new'` — persisted so the review UI doesn't re-run dedup on every page load), and `row_status` (CHECK in `('pending','approved','rejected')`, default `'pending'` — lets the upcoming MCP "approve a subset" path mark intent before the actual delete). **Two new indexes:** `(staged_import_id, dedup_status)` for the review UI's group-by-status pages, `(user_id, row_status)` for the upcoming MCP `list_staged_transactions`. **Idempotent migration** uses `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` and the `pg_constraint` guard pattern from `20260506_staging_encryption_tier.sql` for CHECK / FK constraints (Postgres has no native `ADD CONSTRAINT IF NOT EXISTS`). New columns are NOT encrypted (structural / numeric / FK); plaintext encryption for `tags` / `original_filename` deferred per the issue spec. Drizzle `stagedImports` / `stagedTransactions` definitions in `src/db/schema-pg.ts` mirror the new columns. **Out of scope:** any code that reads the new columns (review UI, approve endpoint, MCP staging tools) lands in #153–#156. **Migration sort order:** `20260506_staging_unified_columns.sql` sorts after `20260506_staging_encryption_tier.sql` (`u` > `e`) so the encryption_tier column is in place before this migration's `(user_id, row_status)` index touches the same row-set. **MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio.** Files: [scripts/migrations/20260506_staging_unified_columns.sql](scripts/migrations/20260506_staging_unified_columns.sql), [src/db/schema-pg.ts](src/db/schema-pg.ts). Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean.

### Fixed
- **Goals page balance honors investment-account market value (#151, 2026-05-06).** `GET /api/goals` `currentAmount` previously ran a flat `SUM(transactions.amount)` across every linked account, regardless of whether the account was a cash account or an investment account. For investment accounts that produced "net cash contributed" (deposits − withdrawals + uninvested dividend cash) — under the issue #96 trade-pair convention, a stock buy posts a cash leg with `amount=-cost` and a paired stock leg with `amount=0`, so trades net to ≈0 and the holdings market value never enters the rollup. Concrete repro: a six-figure goal linked to a single investment account showed `currentAmount` ~13% of target while the accounts page showed the same account at ~55%; the two numbers should agree (within rounding) when goal currency = account currency. Same class of mistake CLAUDE.md already called out for the accounts page ("Account balance for accounts with holdings = `holdings.value`, NOT `b.balance + holdings.value`"). The goals page didn't get that branch. **Fix:** `/api/goals` now branches on `accounts.is_investment` per linked account. Investment accounts contribute `getHoldingsValueByAccount(userId, dek).get(accountId).value` (the same aggregator the dashboard / accounts page uses — full per-currency cost-basis bucketing per #129, cash-leg substitution per #96, paired-cash-leg sell-branch skip per #128, all preserved). Cash accounts continue to contribute `SUM(transactions.amount)` unchanged. **Per-account FX normalization:** each contribution is converted from the account's currency into the goal's currency via `getLatestFxRate(...)` before summing, so multi-currency goals (e.g. a CAD goal linked to a USD account) now report a meaningful `progress = currentAmount / targetAmount` instead of a 1:1 mis-add. **Batching:** the legacy code ran one SUM query per goal inside `Promise.all`. The new code resolves the union of linked account ids across all goals, fetches account metadata (currency, is_investment) in one round trip, pulls cash-flow basis in one batched query grouped by accountId, calls the holdings aggregator once (shared across goals), and shares an FX cache across goals. **Stdio MCP `get_goals` is unaffected** — it's a separate read path (already returns ciphertext-only rows post Phase 4) and is flagged out-of-scope by the issue. **Cached `holding_accounts.qty`/`cost_basis` columns untouched** per CLAUDE.md "the cache is reserved for future use; today it is a trap" — the aggregator reads live `SUM(transactions.quantity)` × current price like the other aggregators. Acceptance: a goal linked to a single investment account now reports a `currentAmount` that matches the accounts-page balance for the same account when goal currency = account currency; cash-account-only goals report identical numbers to before (no regression on the common path). MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio. Files: [src/app/api/goals/route.ts](src/app/api/goals/route.ts), [CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md). Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean.

- **MCP cross-currency cost basis mislabeling — per-currency bucketing in 3 portfolio aggregators (#129, 2026-05-04).** For a `portfolio_holdings` row whose `currency` differs from its parent `accounts.currency` (e.g. a USD ETF held inside a CAD brokerage account), MCP HTTP `get_portfolio_analysis` and `analyze_holding` returned the account-currency cost basis (`SUM(transactions.amount)`) but tagged it with the holding's currency. The downstream `*Reporting` field then re-FXed the already-account-currency value, producing inflated nonsense. Concrete repro: a single buy `entered_amount=2,819.64 USD`, `account_amount=3,839.38 CAD`, `quantity=10` returned `avgCostPerShare=$383.94 USD` (wrong, that's the CAD figure mislabeled) instead of the correct `$281.96 USD`. The REST `/api/portfolio/overview` path was already correct via `entered_amount` + `entered_currency` per-currency bucketing; this issue ports that pattern to the other read-side aggregators. **MCP HTTP `accumulate()` rewritten:** `aggregateHoldings()` SQL now SELECTs `t.entered_amount`, `t.entered_currency`, `a.currency AS account_currency`, `ph.currency AS holding_currency`, and the cash leg's `entered_amount`/`entered_currency` (issue #96 stays correct). A pre-pass populates an FX cache (`entered_currency → holding_currency`) so `accumulate()` stays synchronous. Each row's buy / sell / dividend amount is FX-converted from `entered_currency` into the holding currency before summation. `get_portfolio_analysis` already tagged `tagAmount(buyAmt, ph.currency, "account")` — that tag is now correct because `buyAmt` is finally in holding currency. `lifetimeCostBasisReporting = buyAmt × fx(holding→reporting)` is a single hop instead of double. **MCP HTTP `analyze_holding` two fixes in one tool:** (a) `holdingCurrency` is sourced from `ph.currency` (the JOINed `portfolio_holdings.currency`), not `txns[0]?.currency` (which was `a.currency` from the JOIN); (b) the per-row buy/sell/dividend loop applies the same FX normalization. Without both, the tool would have looked self-consistent (label and value both wrong but matching) but inconsistent with `get_portfolio_analysis` for the same holding. **`src/lib/holdings-value.ts` per-currency bucketing:** `fkAggRows` now GROUPs BY `(holding_id, entered_currency)`. The post-query loop sums each bucket's `buyAmountInEntered × fx(entered → holding)` into `totalBuyAmountInHoldingCcy`, computes `avgCost` in holding currency, then multiplies by `qty` and FX-converts the resulting holding-currency cost basis back into account currency for the `AccountHoldingsValue` contract. The legacy "approximation that transaction amounts == account currency" comment is removed — it was the bug for cross-currency holdings. **CAD/CAD holdings unaffected** (FX hop is 1.0). Issue #96 `trade_link_id` cash-leg substitution composes with the new normalization (cash leg's `entered_amount` + `entered_currency` are used). Issue #128 paired-cash-leg sell-branch skip preserved. **MCP stdio aggregators were already refusing on Stream D Phase 4** (`streamDRefuseRead`), so the bug only ever manifested on HTTP and `holdings-value.ts`. **MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio.** Files touched: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [src/lib/holdings-value.ts](src/lib/holdings-value.ts), [docs/architecture/encryption.md](docs/architecture/encryption.md). Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean.

- **One-off data fix: tx 29100 reassigned from cash holding 497 to TFSA cash holding 425 (#81, applied prod + dev 2026-05-04).** Transaction 29100 (a +$10,000 contribution) was booked by the bank-import pipeline against the wrong cash-sleeve holding (id 497) in a TFSA account. It belongs on the TFSA CAD cash holding (id 425) which covers account 614. Single-row UPDATE on `transactions` mutates `portfolio_holding_id` from 497 to 425 and bumps `updated_at`; `source` is intentionally **untouched** to honor the audit-trio invariant (`transactions.source` is INSERT-only). Statement is double-scoped (`user_id = ? AND portfolio_holding_id = 497`) so it is idempotent and impossible to widen. Both envs verified pre-state (tx on holding 497 / account 614, both holdings same user, `holding_accounts(425, 614, owner)` covering row present per the issue #95 backfill). UPDATE returned 1 row in each env, post-state confirmed `portfolio_holding_id=425` and zero remaining tx-29100 rows on holding 497. `source='manual'` preserved in both envs. `pf-dev.service` and `pf.service` restarted afterwards so the MCP per-user tx cache drops the affected user (raw psql bypasses `invalidateUser(userId)`). No code or schema change. Docs: [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md) — applied-dates added to the existing playbook entry, staging command line removed (deprecated 2026-05-03), personal account names sanitized for the public open-source repo. Out-of-scope: holding 497's $72,791 phantom `dividendsReceived` figure was tracked under #84 (now shipped); auditing other potentially mis-routed bank-import rows on 497 is a separate sweep.

### Added
- **MCP `get_portfolio_analysis` gains `account_id` / `account` filter (#123, 2026-05-04).** Two new optional parameters scope the response to a single account, mirroring the pattern shipped in `search_transactions`. `account_id: number` is the FK fast-path (cheap ownership check, no fuzzy resolution); `account: string` runs through the existing `resolveAccountStrict` waterfall and rejects low-confidence matches (length-≥3 token overlap gate, same as `record_transaction` et al).
