Text Contains, From/To dates, and numeric Value/Upper bound inputs inside the per-column filter dropdown silently swallowed every keystroke; selection-based filters (source/category/account/accountType checkboxes) were unaffected because they react to `change`, not `keydown`. **Root cause.** The filter editor renders inside a shadcn v4 `<DropdownMenuContent>` (base-ui `Menu.Root`). base-ui attaches keydown listeners on the menu surface for type-ahead (printable chars) and back-navigation (Backspace). The `<Input>` lets the keydown bubble up, so the menu eats the keystroke before the input's own native handling runs. **Fix.** Add `onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key !== "Escape" && e.key !== "Tab") e.stopPropagation(); }}` to all five `<Input>` callsites in `ColFilterEditor` ([src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx)) — date From, date To, text Contains, numeric Value, numeric Upper bound. Escape and Tab still bubble so dropdown-close + focus traversal remain intact; printable chars, arrow keys, Backspace, and space stop propagating so the input handles them natively. `e.stopPropagation()` only — never `e.preventDefault()`, which would block typing. Inline comment at the first site documents the base-ui interaction so a future refactor doesn't strip it. **Out of scope.** Swapping `DropdownMenu` for `Popover` (cleaner long-term — Popover has no keyboard navigation semantics — filed as a follow-up if needed). UX-only change; no DB / encryption / MCP surface impact.
- **MCP investment-account `autoCategory` short-circuits before generic history (2026-05-01, closes [#89](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/89)).** `record_transaction` / `bulk_record_transactions` over MCP HTTP could silently label a brokerage cash leg with a generic expense category (user-reported "Car Maintenance and other", id 475) when no rule, keyword, or fallback matched. The expense-skip guards on the rule + history loops weren't catching every leak path — a category miscatTyped as something other than `'E'`, a row whose `category_id` lookup missed in `catTypeById` (decryption miss), or a non-expense category irrelevant to the payee could all elect themselves. **Fix.** The investment branch of `autoCategory` in [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) now runs an investment-only ladder and short-circuits with `null` BEFORE the generic history pool is consulted: rules pass (expense-skipped) → keyword pass (`pickInvestmentCategoryByPayee`) → fallback (`fallbackInvestmentCategory`) → `null`. The non-investment branch is unchanged: rules → history → null. **Why null beats a wrong guess.** `null` is the established uncategorized convention everywhere — chat-engine, weekly-recap, spotlight all render it as "Uncategorized". A wrong expense category for a brokerage cash leg distorts every spending dashboard the user owns; uncategorized is honest and easy to fix. **Tool descriptions updated** for `record_transaction` and `bulk_record_transactions` (HTTP) to reflect the corrected behaviour: "Investment accounts use a keyword pass (dividend/interest/forex/disbursement) plus a 'Transfers'/'Investment Activity' fallback, and default to uncategorized when none match — never a generic expense category." **Stdio surface unchanged.** The stdio variants of `record_transaction` and `bulk_record_transactions` already refuse investment-account writes (no `portfolioHolding` plumbing on stdio per CLAUDE.md "Stdio MCP `record_transaction`/`bulk_record_transactions` refuse writes to investment accounts"), so `autoCategory` never runs there. No mirror needed. **REST + import-pipeline unchanged.** They use a separate `applyRules` + `suggestCategory` flow in [src/lib/auto-categorize.ts](src/lib/auto-categorize.ts), not the MCP-local helper. **Stream-D Phase 3 / pathfinder DEK-mismatch resilience preserved.** The `investmentHints` materialisation still uses the `decryptField(dek, name_ct) ?? plaintext` ladder so DEK-mismatch users keep working. **Match-field stays `'payee'`** — the known-bad `match_payee` column reference (separate pre-existing bug per CLAUDE.md "Auto-categorize rule schema") is not introduced. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no MCP tool surface change (still 82 HTTP / 78 stdio). Files: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts).
- **Settings sub-pages: shared layout content slot scrolls horizontally for wide tables (2026-05-01, closes [#88](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/88)).** After PR #67 split the settings monolith into 8 sub-pages under a shared `SettingsLayout` with a 224px (`w-56`) left nav, the content slot at [src/app/(app)/settings/layout.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/settings/layout.tsx) line 144 had `min-w-0` (lets the flex item shrink below intrinsic content width) but no `overflow-x-auto`, so wide tables (holding-accounts pairings, dropdown-order rows, categorization rules) clipped on narrow viewports with no way to scroll. **Primary fix.** Added `overflow-x-auto` alongside the existing `min-w-0` (do not replace — the flex-shrink behavior is load-bearing). Pages whose content fits show no visible change; pages whose content overflows now scroll inside the slot, not the page. **Belt-and-suspenders on `/settings/holding-accounts`.** The per-holding `<Table>` (5 columns: Account / Qty / Cost basis / Primary / Actions) is now wrapped in an inner `<div className="overflow-x-auto">` so the table-level wrapper consumes overflow first — no double scrollbars even though the layout slot also has `overflow-x-auto`. **No-op on the other sub-pages.** `/settings/data` already wraps its CSV-preview `<table>` in `overflow-x-auto`. `/settings/categorization`, `/settings/dropdown-order`, and `/settings/investments` don't render `<table>` elements at all — their card-based row lists already use `min-w-0` + `truncate` and handle their own overflow. The mobile pill row (below `md` breakpoint) is on a separate sibling and unaffected. No schema, no encryption invariants, no audit-trio plumbing, no MCP tool surface change (count unchanged at 82 HTTP / 78 stdio). Code-only change. Files: [src/app/(app)/settings/layout.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/settings/layout.tsx), [src/app/(app)/settings/holding-accounts/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/settings/holding-accounts/page.tsx).
- **Combobox filter regression: `itemToStringLabel` returned literal `"[object Object]"`, emptying every dropdown on any keystroke (2026-05-01, closes [#87](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/87)).** PR #68 wired base-ui's built-in `Combobox` filter and fixed the original "list never filters" bug, but introduced a regression: typing any text in the high-level `<Combobox>` emptied the list even when matching items existed. Root cause was a one-line type mismatch in [src/components/ui/combobox.tsx](src/components/ui/combobox.tsx) — `itemToStringLabel={(v) => items.find((it) => it.value === v)?.label ?? String(v ?? "")}` assumed `v` was the raw string id, but base-ui passes the full item object (`{ value, label }`) from the `items` prop. The lookup always returned `undefined`, the `String(v ?? "")` fallback yielded the literal `"[object Object]"` for every entry, and base-ui's substring filter compared the user's input against `"[object Object]"` per row — no matches → empty list. **Fix.** `itemToStringLabel` now reads `item.label` directly when the argument is an object (the actual base-ui contract). The plain-string fallback is preserved as a defensive branch in case a future direct `<ComboboxRoot>` callsite passes a `string[]` `items` prop. **Affects every dropdown migrated to the high-level `<Combobox>`** — account picker, category picker, payee picker, holding picker, currency picker, transfer / split dialog, budgets, goals, loans, subscriptions, FX overrides, reconcile preview/upload (16 callsites). Single-callback fix in one file; no callsite changes. PR #68's filter wiring was correct and is preserved. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no MCP tool count change. Files: [src/components/ui/combobox.tsx](src/components/ui/combobox.tsx).
- **MCP HTTP aggregator keys by `holding_id`; `symbols` filter gains token-overlap match + `warnings` (2026-05-01, closes [#86](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/86)).** The MCP HTTP `accumulate()` aggregator keyed its output Map by holding *display name*, silently merging holdings sharing a name across accounts (e.g. VUN.TO in IBKR TFSA + IBKR RRSP + Mimi TFSA → one inflated row of qty=166 instead of three distinct rows). Re-keys to `holding_id` so two TFSA + RRSP holdings come through as separate rows with their own `holdingId`, `account`, quantity, and cost basis — bringing MCP HTTP into alignment with the other three aggregators (REST `/api/portfolio/overview`, `src/lib/holdings-value.ts`, MCP stdio) that already group by FK id per CLAUDE.md "Portfolio aggregator". **`accumulate()` + `aggregateHoldings()` re-keyed.** [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) — `Map<string, Agg>` → `Map<number, Agg>`. The `accumulate(out, holdingId, name, r, dividendsCategoryId)` signature now takes the holding id as the lookup key; display name remains a row field. Same buy/sell/dividend branching preserved verbatim (qty>0 = buy regardless of amt sign per CLAUDE.md; dividends matched by `category_id` per #84). Defensive skip for any (post-Phase-6) row with a null `portfolio_holding_id`. **`get_portfolio_analysis` (HTTP) `phMap` re-keyed by `ph.id`.** SELECT picks up `ph.id`; lookup is `phMap.get(Number(m.holding_id))` instead of `phMap.get(String(m.name))`. Result rows surface the right `account` (sourced from the holding's own `accounts JOIN portfolio_holdings.account_id` lookup, not from a name-collision fallout that picked the wrong row). **`symbols` filter matches name + symbol via substring + token-overlap.** Splits the filter on whitespace + parens + brackets and accepts any shared non-empty token, so `"VCN.TO (TFSA)"` resolves to a `"VCN.TO"`-named holding. Falls back to substring on the common case. Unmatched filter entries surface as `warnings: ["BNO: no matching holding found"]` alongside `holdings` so the caller can correct typos instead of silently getting empty results. **`get_portfolio_performance` (HTTP) returns `holdingId` per row.** Callers can disambiguate same-name holdings without a follow-up call. **`analyze_holding` (HTTP + stdio) gains optional `holdingId` parameter.** When provided, short-circuits the fuzzy substring filter and scopes strictly to that FK id. When `symbol` substring-matches multiple distinct holding ids (e.g. "VUN" matching VUN.TO across two accounts), the response returns an `ambiguous` array of `{holdingId, name, symbol, account}` candidates and requires the caller to pick one. `symbol` is now optional when `holdingId` is supplied; one of the two is required. **`get_investment_insights` rebalancing + patterns modes sum across same-name holdings.** Aggregator now returns one row per id, but rebalancing targets are user-supplied by name — explicit dedup ensures cross-account positions roll up to a single allocation entry instead of a last-write-wins regression. **`update_portfolio_holding` docstring updated.** The claim "renames cascade because get_portfolio_analysis groups by FK" is now accurate post-fix; wording updated to reference issue #86 and the per-holdingId grouping. **CLAUDE.md "Portfolio aggregator" gotcha updated.** The "MCP HTTP `accumulate()` is the only aggregator that has historically diverged from this rule" note now reads as a resolved historical note pointing at this PR. **Tool surface unchanged at 82 HTTP / 78 stdio** — schema additions are optional fields on existing tools. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron. Files: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts), [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md).

### Changed
- **MCP: extend low-confidence fuzzy-match rejection + `account_id` to `record_transfer` / `record_trade` (2026-05-01, closes [#85](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/85)).** Follow-up to #29 (which fixed the same silent-routing failure mode for `record_transaction` and `bulk_record_transactions`). The remaining money-moving entry points still resolved account names through the loose `fuzzyFind` helper, so a sloppy `fromAccount` / `toAccount` / `account` value could silently route a transfer pair or trade to a vaguely-similar account — the same bug that misrouted 99 IBKR transactions to "Appartment" before #29. **`record_transfer` (HTTP + stdio)** now accepts `from_account_id?: number` and `to_account_id?: number`; when set, fuzzy matching is skipped entirely and the FK is validated against `accounts.user_id = ?`. The `fromAccount` / `toAccount` (name) params are now optional — at least one of (id, name) per side is required. The name path runs through the existing `resolveAccountStrict` helper which only accepts substring/reverse-substring hits when the input and candidate share a whitespace-separated token of length ≥3; otherwise the call fails with `Source account "<name>" did not match strongly — closest is "<name>" (id=N) but no shared whitespace token. Re-call with from_account_id=N if that's right, or pick another from: <list>`. Symmetric on the destination side. **`record_trade` (HTTP + stdio)** already had `account_id` from #29's bulk-record rollout; this issue replaces the loose `fuzzyFind` fallback at [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) and [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts) with `resolveAccountStrict` + the same low-confidence error format. **Response shape additions.** `record_transfer` now returns `resolvedFromAccount: { id, name }` + `resolvedToAccount: { id, name }` so callers can verify routing immediately; `record_trade` returns `resolvedAccount: { id, name }` to match. **Behaviorally identical for confidently-matching names** — exact / alias / startsWith / token-gated-substring tiers preserved. **Audit-trio invariants preserved** (issue #28). `txSource: "mcp_http"` / `"mcp_stdio"` continues to flow through `createTransferPair` / `createTransferPairViaSql`; per-user tx cache invalidation for both legs (called inside the helpers) is preserved. **Stdio carve-out unchanged** — investment-account writes still refused on stdio `record_transaction` (no `portfolioHolding` plumbing), but `record_transfer` and `record_trade` always handled in-kind via the explicit `holding` / `symbol` parameters and continue to. **No schema migration; no env vars; no cron.** Tool surface count unchanged at 82 HTTP / 78 stdio (schema additions are optional fields on existing tools). Files: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts), [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md).

### Fixed
- **Portfolio `dividendsReceived`: classify by Dividends category id, not the qty/amount-sign heuristic (2026-05-01, closes [#84](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/84)).** All four portfolio aggregators previously classified dividend payouts with `quantity == 0 AND amount > 0`, which silently dropped (a) dividend reinvestments (qty>0, amt<0 — re-classified as buys with no dividend credit) and (b) withholding tax / negative-correction entries (qty=0, amt<0 — fell through every branch and disappeared from `dividendsReceived`). Repro from the source review: TFSA-CAD holding 425 reported $661.27 dividends but the SUM over Dividends-category transactions was ~$726.06 — $64.79 unexplained. **Fix.** Aggregators now match `transactions.category_id` against the user's "Dividends" (or "Dividend") category id, mirroring the user's mental model and aligning with [auto-categorize.ts:195](src/lib/auto-categorize.ts) which already routes "dividend"-payee transactions into that category. **New helper [src/lib/dividends-category.ts](src/lib/dividends-category.ts).** Single-source `resolveDividendsCategoryId(db, userId, dek)` returns the category id (or null when the user has no Dividends category — aggregators short-circuit to 0 dividends). Stream-D-aware: when a DEK is supplied, matches both legacy plaintext `categories.name` AND the encrypted `name_lookup` HMAC; without a DEK (e.g. stdio MCP), only plaintext matches. Same fallback ladder (`Dividends` → `Dividend`) as `pickInvestmentCategoryByPayee`. **REST `/api/portfolio/overview`** ([src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts)) — the `dividendsInEntered` SQL CASE now matches `category_id = $dividendsCategoryId` when set, and short-circuits to `0::float8` when null. The `holding_accounts` JOIN grain and per-currency bucket aggregation are unchanged. **MCP HTTP `accumulate()` / `aggregateHoldings()`** ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts)) — `aggregateHoldings` now plumbs `t.category_id` through the SELECT and accepts a `dividendsCategoryId` option; `accumulate()` matches by category id rather than `qty=0+amt>0`. The buy/sell branches still come first (per CLAUDE.md "qty>0 is a buy regardless of amount sign"), then the dividend branch independently adds to the dividend bucket — a dividend reinvestment is now both a "buy" (shares acquired) AND a "dividend" (income received), which is correct. `get_portfolio_analysis` and `get_portfolio_performance` resolve the category id once at the top and pass it into `aggregateHoldings`. The other two `aggregateHoldings` callers (`get_investment_insights` mode='rebalancing' and mode='benchmark') filter `t.amount < 0` (`buysOnly`), don't read the dividends field, and don't need the option — left unchanged. **MCP HTTP `analyze_holding`** — selects `t.category_id`, replaces the in-memory `qty=0+amt>0` branch with a category-id match, and pushes onto the `dividends` array independently of the buy/sell branches. **MCP stdio `analyze_holding`** ([mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts)) — left unchanged. The stdio variant doesn't compute dividends today (no `divAmt` accumulator), so the heuristic-fix is a no-op there. The issue acceptance criteria explicitly allowed leaving stdio as-is. Stdio `get_portfolio_analysis` likewise doesn't expose dividends — separate gap, not regressed. **Load-bearing invariants honored.** Read-only change — no writes, no `invalidateUser(userId)` plumbing. Audit trio (`updated_at` / `source`) untouched. The CLAUDE.md "Portfolio aggregator" gotcha is now updated to note the category-id-based dividend classifier supersedes the qty-sign heuristic. The four-aggregator alignment rule still applies — REST + MCP HTTP `accumulate()` + MCP HTTP `analyze_holding` use the new classifier consistently. Stream-D dual-index pattern (plaintext `name` + encrypted `name_lookup`) preserved on the category lookup. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no MCP tool count change (still 82 HTTP / 78 stdio). Files: [src/lib/dividends-category.ts](src/lib/dividends-category.ts), [src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts), [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts).

### Added
- **Data fix recipe: reassign tx 29100 from holding 497 to holding 425 (2026-05-01, closes [#81](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/81)).** Docs-only addition to [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md) — a per-env psql playbook entry for the one-off `transactions` row mutation that moves the +$10,000 EFT RRSP contribution off the wrong Cash sleeve (holding 497 in Mimi TFSA) onto the correct TFSA-CAD holding 425 in IBKR TFSA. No code change, no schema change. **Recipe shape.** Pre-state verification block (confirms the tx is owned by the right user, currently on 497, both holdings exist, AND that holding 425 has a `holding_accounts` row covering the tx's `account_id` for this user). The single-row `UPDATE` sets `portfolio_holding_id = 425` and bumps `updated_at`; `source` is **deliberately preserved** per the CLAUDE.md audit-trio invariant (`transactions.source` is INSERT-only). Predicates `id = 29100 AND user_id = ? AND portfolio_holding_id = 497` make the statement idempotent and impossible to widen by accident. Post-state spot-check confirms `portfolio_holding_id` flipped and `source` is unchanged. Per-env rollout block covers dev → staging → prod. **Gate.** Per CLAUDE.md, every portfolio aggregator JOINs through `holding_accounts` on `(holding, account)`; running the UPDATE without holding 425's covering pair in place silently drops the leg from the aggregator. The pre-state SELECT catches that and the recipe instructs the operator to STOP if it returns 0 rows (gate on issue [#95](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/95) `holding_accounts` integrity). **Ops impact.** Raw psql bypasses the MCP per-user tx cache (no `invalidateUser(userId)` call), so the recipe also instructs operators to restart the env's `finlynq-*` systemd service after the prod commit so Claude reads fresh attribution on next request. No env vars, no new dependencies, no MCP tool surface change (still 82 HTTP / 78 stdio). The actual SQL execution is a human-run step per [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md) — this PR only ships the recipe.

- **MCP stdio `search_transactions`: add `portfolio_holding_id` filter for HTTP parity (2026-05-01, closes [#80](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/80)).** Closes a long-standing HTTP/stdio drift on `search_transactions`. The HTTP variant has accepted `account_id` + `portfolio_holding_id` together as independent filters that AND together (e.g. "all VCN.TO dividends in IBKR TFSA for Q1 2026"), but the stdio variant was missing `portfolio_holding_id` entirely. **Stdio now exposes `portfolio_holding_id?: number`** in the Zod schema and ANDs `t.portfolio_holding_id = ?` into the WHERE clause alongside the existing `account_id` filter ([mcp-server/tools-v2.ts](mcp-server/tools-v2.ts)). Parameter name is snake-case to mirror the HTTP schema exactly — no XOR guard, no payee-required guard, all filter combinations remain valid alone or combined. **Tool description updated symmetrically on stdio** to surface the new parameter and the "combine with account_id to narrow to one holding in one account" pattern so the model picks the FK fast-path over a `payee` substring search. **Regression-guard comments added on both transports** ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) + [mcp-server/tools-v2.ts](mcp-server/tools-v2.ts)) noting that `account_id` and `portfolio_holding_id` are independent filters and future refactors must not reintroduce an XOR or "must supply payee" guard — cheap insurance against the parity rule existing only in tribal knowledge. **No schema/DDL/env-var/cron impact.** Filter is purely an FK predicate against the existing `transactions.portfolio_holding_id` column; encryption fallback rules unaffected. Stdio reads stay plaintext per the documented "Stdio MCP writes are plaintext" gotcha. `invalidateUserTxCache(userId)` not applicable — this is a read tool. **MCP tool surface count unchanged** at 82 HTTP / 78 stdio — only a parameter added to an existing tool. Code-only change.
- **MCP `search_transactions`: `quantity` field on response (2026-05-01, closes [#79](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/79)).** `transactions.quantity` is now surfaced on every row returned by `search_transactions` (HTTP + stdio) so AI assistants can read share-count state directly without inferring it from aggregator output. Pre-req for the orphaned-quantity-residue diagnostics in #99 — `quantity` is a first-class field on investment-account workflows (share counts move on quantity, not on `amount`). **HTTP variant** ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts)) adds `t.quantity` to the SELECT list alongside `t.portfolio_holding_id`; the existing `...rest` row spread carries it through decryption and tagging unchanged. Tool description updated to document the field shape (`nullable; positive for buys, negative for sells; null for cash-proxy and non-investment transactions`). **Stdio variant** ([mcp-server/tools-v2.ts](mcp-server/tools-v2.ts)) adds `t.quantity` to the SELECT list and extends the `TransactionRow` type with `quantity: number | null`; tool description updated symmetrically. No DDL — `transactions.quantity` already exists. Audit-trio (`createdAt` / `updatedAt` / `source`) remains HTTP-only on this tool, consistent with prior behavior — out of scope here. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no MCP tool count change.

### Changed
- **MCP: fix `record_transfer` `toAccount` description, surface `record_trade` in `finlynq_help` (2026-05-01, closes [#82](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/82)).** Documentation/discovery-only fix to the MCP tool surface — no behavioral change. The runtime already supported same-account in-kind transfers via `createTransferPair` / `createTransferPairViaSql` (the guard at [src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts) checks `!wantsHolding` so same-account passes through fine when `holding` + `destHolding` are set), and the four-check transfer-pair rule (link_id + sole sibling + both type='R' + different accounts, relaxed for in-kind same-account rebalances) is unchanged. Only the parameter description and the `finlynq_help(topic="tools")` discovery list misled agents. **`record_transfer.toAccount` description (HTTP + stdio)** no longer says "must differ from fromAccount". Updated wording: same-as-fromAccount is allowed for intra-account in-kind rebalances (cash sleeve ↔ symbol holding, or different-currency cash sleeves) when `holding` and `destHolding` are also set; same-account cash-only transfers are still rejected. **`record_transfer` tool description (HTTP + stdio)** now cross-references `record_trade` as the preferred tool for brokerage stock/ETF/crypto buys and sells. Wording is intentionally soft ("prefer `record_trade` for buys/sells") so non-trade in-kind moves (forex sleeve, gold-between-brokerages, mergers/share-class conversions, ACATS) continue to use `record_transfer` directly. **`finlynq_help(topic="tools")` discovery list** now exposes both `record_transfer` and `record_trade` under a new `trade_tools` bucket alongside `portfolio_tools`. Previously neither tool appeared in the topic="tools" output, so agents calling the discovery endpoint had no way to find them. The `tip` field is updated symmetrically to point agents at `record_trade` for trades. Mirrored across HTTP (`mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`) and stdio (`mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts`) per the CLAUDE.md "MCP HTTP+stdio mirror" invariant. The architecture doc [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md) brokerage-statement recipe already led with `record_trade` (added 2026-04-30 with `record_trade`'s introduction) — no edits needed there. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron. **MCP tool surface count unchanged at 82 HTTP / 78 stdio** — this is a description-only edit. Files: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts).
- **Portfolio per-account breakdown always renders + zero-qty market-value bug fixed (2026-05-01, follow-up to [#25](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/25)).** Two follow-ups on the canonical-holding rollup. (1) The per-account breakdown table inside an expanded "All Holdings" row was gated on `memberHoldings.length > 1` so single-account holdings only showed the inline `Edit` + `View transactions` links. Gate flipped to `memberHoldings.length > 0` so every expanded row renders the same shape; the now-redundant single-account inline `Edit` button is dropped because clicking the account-name cell in the breakdown table opens the same editor (`Pencil` icon import dropped — was its only consumer). Header gets a `bg-muted/40` tint and the wrapping border bumps from `border-border/50` to full `border-border` for dark-theme legibility. (2) Zero-quantity holdings no longer fake a market value from the unit price. `/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts` had a branch `else if (price !== null && !quantity)` that set `marketValue = price`, leaking the per-share quote (e.g. $61.74 for VJPA.L with qty=0) into both the position's market-value column AND the portfolio-wide `totalValueDisplay` + asset-type breakdowns. For a $600k/share stock this could have inflated portfolio totals catastrophically. Branch deleted; zero-qty holdings now show market value = `null` and only contribute realized G/L + dividends to total return. Files: [src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/portfolio/page.tsx), [src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts). Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars.
- **Portfolio: canonical-holding rollup table + per-account drill-down (2026-05-01, closes [#25](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/25)).** Restructures the Portfolio page so the "All Holdings" table aggregates each canonical position across every account that holds it (e.g. AAPL in TFSA + RRSP shows once with `totalQty=8`, qty-weighted avg cost, summed market value/realized G/L). Expanding a row reveals the existing per-holding stats AND a new per-account breakdown table (account name, qty, avg cost, market value, unrealized G/L, realized G/L) with "View transactions" drill-down. The standalone "By Holding" panel is folded into this restructured table per the issue's resolution comment; the "Holdings by Account" panel is unchanged. **Aggregator JOINs through `holding_accounts`.** `/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts` (FK SQL CASE), `src/lib/holdings-value.ts`, MCP HTTP `accumulate()` + `analyze_holding`, and MCP stdio `get_portfolio_analysis` + `get_portfolio_performance` + `analyze_holding` — every aggregator site now `INNER JOIN holding_accounts ha ON ha.holding_id = t.portfolio_holding_id AND ha.account_id = t.account_id AND ha.user_id = ?`. Today the join table has one `is_primary=true` row per holding so behavior is unchanged; once a canonical position spans multiple `holding_accounts` rows, the join attributes correctly per-(holding, account). CLAUDE.md "Portfolio aggregator" `qty>0 = buy regardless of amount sign` rule preserved at every site. **Per-decision: full crypto symbol preserved.** The `byHolding` canonical-key derivation stops collapsing crypto on `-`, so `BTC-ETH` is a distinct holding from `BTC` (per issue #25 resolution comment 2026-05-01). **`HoldingEditDialog` Name field is disabled on canonical rows** with a hint ("Name is auto-managed for this holding type. Edit the symbol or currency to rename"). Symbol / currency / isCrypto / note remain editable. The PUT handler at `/api/portfolio` rejects name edits on canonical rows with HTTP 400 as the API-layer guard. **New per-user canonical-name backfill helper** at [src/lib/crypto/stream-d-canonicalize-portfolio.ts](src/lib/crypto/stream-d-canonicalize-portfolio.ts) — sibling of `stream-d-phase3-null.ts`. Wired into both the password-login and MFA-verify paths next to `enqueuePhase3NullIfReady`. On first login per user, decrypts each `portfolio_holdings` row's name + symbol, classifies (tickered / cash-sleeve / currency-code / user-defined), and dual-writes the canonical name + `name_ct` + `name_lookup` for tickered/cash/currency rows. User-defined rows keep their free-text name. DEK-mismatch users (e.g. pathfinder per CLAUDE.md "Known open issue") bail silently at the sample-decrypt precondition — same pattern as `nullPlaintextIfReady`. **Schema-prep migration `scripts/migrate-portfolio-canonical-names.sql`.** Adds `users.portfolio_names_canonicalized_at text` (NULL = needs canonicalization, non-NULL = done). Idempotent. **Apply per-env BEFORE deploying matching code** — the helper reads/writes the flag column on first login. Per-env playbook in [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md). CLAUDE.md "deploy.sh does NOT run schema migrations" rule applies. **Files:** [src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/portfolio/page.tsx), [src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts), [src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts), [src/lib/holdings-value.ts](src/lib/holdings-value.ts), [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts), [src/lib/crypto/stream-d-canonicalize-portfolio.ts](src/lib/crypto/stream-d-canonicalize-portfolio.ts), [src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts](src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts), [src/app/api/auth/mfa/verify/route.ts](src/app/api/auth/mfa/verify/route.ts), [scripts/migrate-portfolio-canonical-names.sql](scripts/migrate-portfolio-canonical-names.sql), [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md), [docs/architecture/encryption.md](docs/architecture/encryption.md). MCP tool surface count unchanged (82 HTTP / 78 stdio); only their underlying SQL changed.
- **Transfer dialog: unified holding pickers driven by account types (2026-05-01, closes [#56](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/56)).** Collapses the old two-block approach (explicit "In-kind (transfer shares of a holding)" checkbox + separate "Investment account leg" block) into a single picker section whose visibility and fields are derived entirely from the From/To account types. No checkbox; no pre-selected defaults; quantity starts blank. Behavior matrix: inv+inv → full in-kind UI (source holding + quantity + dest holding + dest quantity); inv+non-inv → From-holding picker only (required); non-inv+inv → To-holding picker only (required); non+non → no holding pickers. Cash sleeves remain selectable in all pickers. The reset effect was refactored to two independent refs (`prevFromIsInvestmentRef` / `prevToIsInvestmentRef`) so each side's holding state clears independently on `true→false` transitions. The From/To account pickers allow same-account selection when both sides are investment (in-kind rebalance within one brokerage). Submit-handler derives `isInKind` from account types (`fromAcct.isInvestment && toAcct.isInvestment`) rather than a form field. No backend changes — `createTransferPair`, the REST handler, and both MCP transports are untouched. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no tool count change. File: [src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx).
- **MCP `bulk_update`: strict schema, name-resolved category/holding, unappliedChanges surface (2026-04-30, closes [#61](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/61)).** The `preview_bulk_update` / `execute_bulk_update` Zod schema previously stripped unknown keys silently — calls like `changes: { category: "Credit Interest" }` returned `success: true, updated: N` while writing nothing. Real-world fallout: 13 IBKR Joint transactions stuck mis-categorized because the silent no-op masked every retry. **Fix.** Both transports' `bulkChangesSchema` is now `.strict()` so unknown keys hard-fail at validation time. **HTTP-accepted `changes` keys (now 11):** added `category` (name → `category_id` via `resolveCategoryStrict`), `quantity` (nullable; `null` clears), `portfolioHoldingId` (FK with ownership pre-check), `portfolioHolding` (name/ticker → FK via the existing `resolvePortfolioHoldingByName`). Disagreement between `category`/`category_id` or `portfolioHolding`/`portfolioHoldingId` errors out the call — silent "I named X but you bound Y" is worse than rejecting. **Stdio surface narrower by design.** Stdio adds only `category` (name → id). `quantity` / `portfolioHoldingId` / `portfolioHolding` stay HTTP-only — mirrors the stdio `record_transaction` carve-out that already refuses investment-account writes (no holding plumbing, no DEK). A stdio caller passing `quantity` now gets a clean strict-mode 400 instead of a silent no-op. **`resolveBulkChanges` helper (new, both transports).** Resolves names → ids ONCE upstream of `previewBulk` and `commitBulkUpdate`. Returns `{ resolved, unapplied[], error? }`. `error` (id-vs-name disagreement) hard-fails the call; `unapplied[]` (e.g. ambiguous category, holding not found) flows through to `unappliedChanges` on the response so callers see WHY any sample row's before/after looks identical. **`previewBulk` / `commitBulkUpdate` refactored to take `ResolvedChanges`.** Sample `after` rows now reflect the post-resolution shape, so identical before/after pairs always come paired with an `unappliedChanges` entry. The confirmation token still signs the user-supplied `changes` (not the resolved form) so preview→execute round-trips with the same payload still match; execute re-runs resolution. **`execute_bulk_update` aborts on empty resolved set.** Previously, a request with only an unresolvable `category` would commit zero rows and report success. Now: if every requested change failed resolution, the call errors with `Resolution failures: …` and writes nothing. **Audit-trio invariant preserved (issue #28).** Every new UPDATE branch in `commitBulkUpdate` (`quantity`, `portfolio_holding_id`) appends `updated_at = NOW()`; `source` is INSERT-only and is never touched by `bulk_update`. `invalidateUserTxCache(userId)` continues to fire after every successful commit. **Tool descriptions + `finlynq_help` updated** on both transports to enumerate accepted keys per transport. **Sanity-test:** the 13 IBKR Joint transactions named in the source review can now be re-categorized with `bulk_update changes: { category: "Credit Interest" }` and the change persists. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no tool count change. Files: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts), [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md).
- **Drop institution-specific source tags; switch to format-based tagging (2026-04-30, closes [#62](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/62)).** The `source:<x>` tag written into `transactions.tags` by every import path is now a **file format** (`csv` / `excel` / `pdf` / `ofx` / `qfx` / `ibkr-xml` / `email`) rather than the connector or institution name. Institution name already lives on the account; per-row tags now describe shape only, which scales as more connectors land. Coordinated with the upcoming unified OFX/QFX/IBKR-XML import work in issue [#64](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/64) — the same vocabulary is the contract both PRs consume. **Canonical vocabulary** in [src/lib/tx-source.ts](src/lib/tx-source.ts) (`FORMAT_TAGS` + `FormatTag` + `isFormatTag` + `sourceTagFor`), mirrored in [packages/import-connectors/src/types.ts](packages/import-connectors/src/types.ts) so the connectors package stays npm-publishable. Distinct from the audit-column `transactions.source` enum (issue #28) — the two coexist in the same file for grep-discovery but their values are independent. **Connector transforms.** `transformTransactions()` ([packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/transform.ts](packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/transform.ts)) renamed the `sourceConnectorId?: string` option to a required `formatTag: FormatTag` and dropped the `?? "wealthposition"` default — runtime guard throws on unknown values. WealthPosition (`packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/index.ts` + `src/lib/external-import/orchestrator.ts`) now passes `formatTag: "csv"`. IBKR (`packages/import-connectors/src/ibkr/orchestrator.ts`) plumbs `csv` vs `ibkr-xml` based on which parser ran. The hard-coded `"source:ibkr"` strings baked into `ExternalTransaction.tags` arrays in `packages/import-connectors/src/ibkr/transform.ts` (4 sites) are dropped — the downstream `transformTransactions()` adds the format tag in one place. Descriptive tags (`ibkr:fx-conversion`, `ibkr:fx-translation`, `ibkr:asset:*`) are kept — they carry semantic info, not provenance. **Direct upload paths now stamp the format tag.** [`/api/import/preview/route.ts`](src/app/api/import/preview/route.ts) tags CSV (3 entry points: explicit template, canonical headers, auto-matched template) and PDF rows. [`/api/import/csv-map/route.ts`](src/app/api/import/csv-map/route.ts) tags column-mapping CSV imports. [`/api/import/excel-map/route.ts`](src/app/api/import/excel-map/route.ts) tags Excel imports. [`/api/import/reconcile/preview/route.ts`](src/app/api/import/reconcile/preview/route.ts) tags reconcile-mode rows with `csv` / `ofx` / `qfx` based on the file extension (qfx now distinguished from ofx in the response payload's new `format` field). The OFX preview client ([src/app/(app)/import/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/import/page.tsx)) picks up the format from the preview response and stamps it on rows before `/api/import/execute`. Email-staged rows are tagged at [`/api/import/staged/[id]/approve/route.ts`](src/app/api/import/staged/%5Bid%5D/approve/route.ts) — the staging tables don't carry `tags`, so the tag is applied at materialize time. **Transfer-pair `source` opt tightened.** [`src/lib/transfer.ts`](src/lib/transfer.ts) — `CreateTransferOpts.source` retyped from `string` to `FormatTag`; `applySourceTag` runtime-guards via `isFormatTag()` so JS callers passing the old `"wealthposition"` / `"ibkr"` strings throw rather than silently writing bogus tags. JSDoc updated to document the format-vs-institution distinction. **Backfill script rewritten.** [`scripts/backfill-source-tag.sql`](scripts/backfill-source-tag.sql) is now a parameterized rewrite-in-place script (`:from_tag` → `:to_tag`) instead of the original "tag everything with `source:<connector>`" script. Defaults: `source:wealthposition` → `source:csv` (WP exports as CSV), `source:ibkr` → `source:csv` (most rows in the wild were CSV-imported per user reports; operators who know specific batches came from XML pass `source:ibkr-xml` explicitly). Idempotent. Boundary-anchored on commas so we don't accidentally rewrite a substring of another tag. Preview SELECT before each UPDATE. Skips encrypted (`v1:%`) rows — those need a re-import or a Node-side rewrite under the user's DEK. **Tests updated.** All 14 `transformTransactions(...)` callsites in `wealthposition/transform.test.ts` now pass `{ formatTag: "csv" }`; the two assertions that compared against `source:wealthposition` now expect `source:csv`. `ibkr/transform.test.ts` no longer expects `source:ibkr` in the intermediate `ExternalTransaction.tags` (added downstream); the orchestrator-level test now expects `source:ibkr-xml` (XML fixture) instead. **Out of scope.** Encrypted-tag rewrite via Node (DEK-aware backfill). Generalizing other reserved tag prefixes. Audit-column `transactions.source` enum stays exactly as-is (its 7-value CHECK constraint is independent of the format tag). **Docs.** New "transactions.tags" section in [docs/architecture/database.md](docs/architecture/database.md) documenting the vocabulary, reserved prefixes, idempotency rules, and the encrypted-row caveat. Invariant 15 in [docs/import-connectors.md](docs/import-connectors.md) §7 rewritten to cover the format-tag rollout and to enumerate the new entry points that stamp the tag. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no tool count change.

### Fixed
- **Reconcile CSV upload accepts non-canonical headers (2026-04-30, closes [#63](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/63)).** `/api/import/reconcile/preview` rejected every CSV with **"No transactions found in file"** unless the file used the exact canonical headers (`Date` / `Amount` / `Account` / `Payee`), blocking the entire reconcile feature at upload. The reconcile route only implemented the explicit-template + canonical-header steps from the regular `/api/import/preview` parser; it was missing the auto-template-match (step 3) and the column-mapping fallback (step 4). Any TD-style export with `Transaction Date` / `Description` / `Debit` / `Credit` collapsed to zero rows and 400'd. **Single source of truth.** New module [src/lib/external-import/parsers/csv-pipeline.ts](src/lib/external-import/parsers/csv-pipeline.ts) exports `parseCsvWithFallback` — the full four-step chain (explicit templateId → canonical headers → auto-matched saved template ≥80% header overlap → `needs-mapping` payload). Both `/api/import/preview` and `/api/import/reconcile/preview` now consume it; the reconcile route is no longer a strictly-narrower fork that can silently drift. The regular preview's prior "canonical parsed but `previewImport` filtered all rows → fall through to step 3" corner case is dropped — the new pipeline only falls through when the parser itself produced zero rows, which covers every real-world failure mode without complicating the shared API. **Reconcile `csv-needs-mapping` response (422).** When auto-detect can't pick a mapping, reconcile now returns `{ type: "csv-needs-mapping", headers, sampleRows, suggestedMapping, fileName, error }` matching the regular preview shape, so a future reconcile UI can show the same column-mapping dialog. Until that dialog ships, the `error` field stays human-readable so the existing UI still tells the user what to do (import via `/import` first to save a template, then re-upload to reconcile). **Richer "0 rows" diagnostic.** `buildEmptyCsvError` reports the format (`csv`), byte count, and the first non-empty line of the file, plus a hint about semicolon/tab separators (the parser is comma-only — European bank exports hit this). Replaces the prior bare "No transactions found in file" so future parser edge cases are actionable from the response alone. **Pure refactor — encryption boundary unchanged.** The pipeline is read-only (no DB writes, no DEK touch). `reconcile.ts` commit boundary still encrypts `payee`/`note`/`tags` at line 625-639, stamps `source: 'import'` + `created_at` / `updated_at`, and calls `invalidateUserTxCache(userId)` on success. `requireAuth()` (regular preview) vs `requireEncryption()` (reconcile preview) split is preserved per the CLAUDE.md "Read vs write auth guards" gotcha. The investment-account holding constraint (`requireHoldingForInvestmentAccount` at line 501-515) is untouched. **Tests.** New [tests/csv-pipeline.test.ts](tests/csv-pipeline.test.ts) — 14 cases covering all four pipeline steps (canonical, BOM, quoted commas, default-account fill, auto-template-match, cross-tenant isolation, explicit templateId, needs-mapping with and without auto-detect success, template-not-found) plus three for `buildEmptyCsvError`. Existing `tests/csv-parser.test.ts` and `tests/csv-parser-multitenancy.test.ts` still green (27 passing). Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no tool count change.
- **MCP `update_transaction`: silent category-update failure on Stream-D Phase-3 users (2026-04-30, closes [#60](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/60)).** The HTTP transport's category resolver was selecting only `categories.name` — for users past the per-user Stream-D Phase 3 cutover (plaintext `name` is NULL, only `name_ct` carries the value), `fuzzyFind` saw `name === null` and the reverse-includes branch (`lo.includes("")` is always true) silently matched the FIRST category in the list. The success message reported `"updated (1 field(s))"` because `updated_at` advanced on whatever else changed, so the dropped category write was invisible. Repro: tx 37052, called `update_transaction(category: "Credit Interest")`, got "updated (1 field(s))", but a follow-up `search_transactions` showed the category unchanged. **Fix.** Both transports now use a new strict `resolveCategoryStrict` helper that mirrors `resolveAccountStrict` (token-overlap-gated substring tier, length-≥3 whitespace tokens) so a sloppy "Cr" can't silently route a write to "Credit Interest". HTTP path now `SELECT id, name, name_ct FROM categories` and runs `decryptNameish(rawCats, dek)` before resolving — same pattern as `record_transaction` at line ~1905. Stdio mirrors the strict resolver but skips the decrypt step (stdio writes are plaintext). Low-confidence misses return `Category "X" did not match strongly — did you mean "Y" (id=N)?` so the agent has an explicit recovery path. **Response shape.** Both transports now return `fieldsUpdated: string[]` listing the column names actually written (`["category_id", "payee"]`) instead of an opaque `(N field(s))` count, so the AI assistant can verify the exact write. When `category` was in the input AND the resolver succeeded, the response also includes `resolvedCategory: { id, name }` — matching the per-row shape `bulk_record_transactions` already returns at [register-tools-pg.ts:1966](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) and [register-core-tools.ts:703](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts). **Load-bearing invariants preserved.** Every UPDATE branch keeps `, updated_at = NOW()` (audit trio); `source` is never touched. `invalidateUserTxCache(userId)` still fires on success. HTTP write path still gated by `requireEncryption()` (423 if no DEK). Stdio still scopes by `PF_USER_ID`. Encryption write helpers for `payee` / `note` / `tags` (`dek ? encryptField(dek, …) : value`) are unchanged. **Out of scope.** `bulk_update`'s schema expansion + same-root-cause fix lives in issue #61. Other resolver sites that already pull `name_ct` (e.g. `record_transaction` line 1905) were not affected. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no tool count change. Files: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts), [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md).

### Added
- **Cross-source import duplicate detector (2026-04-30, closes [#65](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/65)).** Adds a fuzzy duplicate-match heuristic to `/import/preview` and MCP `preview_import` so the same real-world transfer booked from two perspectives (bank statement + brokerage statement, FX-spread + settlement-vs-posting drift) shows up as a probable duplicate before commit. Strict `import_hash` dedup is unchanged — the new pass runs only against rows that survived exact-match dedup. Warning surface only: probable duplicates default to "commit anyway" so the user keeps full control. **New pure helper [src/lib/external-import/duplicate-detect.ts](src/lib/external-import/duplicate-detect.ts).** Scoring rubric: same account + same direction (hard), amount within ±7% OR ±$50 floor (+0.4), date within ±7 days (+0.3), payee Jaccard ≥ 0.5 (+0.1), same holding FK or canonical symbol (+0.1), transfer-pair sibling on the new row's account (+0.1). Threshold 0.6. Each existing transaction is consumed at most once across all input rows; closest date wins on ties. Pct tuned to 7% to flag the Session 2 IBKR scenarios — $22 / $33 / $307 spreads on $6,315 / $12,059 / $5,000 deposits — while keeping recurring same-amount different-month transactions clean. 16 unit tests in [tests/duplicate-detect.test.ts](tests/duplicate-detect.test.ts) cover hard requirements (same account, same direction, importHash skip), FX-spread bands, the absolute-floor branch, soft hints (payee similarity, transfer-pair sibling boost, no-DEK fallback), each-candidate-consumed-once, and reconcile-style tight options. **Pool builder [src/lib/external-import/duplicate-detect-pool.ts](src/lib/external-import/duplicate-detect-pool.ts).** One round trip pulls every candidate transaction in the union of touched accounts in a `[min(date) - tolerance .. max(date) + tolerance]` window, plus a sibling-leg query for transfer-pair `linkId`s and a holding-symbol lookup for the cross-symbol soft hint. Decrypts `payee_ct` with the standard `?? plaintext` fallback (Stream-D parity) — `tryDecryptField` returns `null` on auth-tag failure so the detector silently skips the payee-similarity hint for unreadable rows but still matches on amount/date/holding. **Wired into [src/lib/import-pipeline.ts](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts).** `previewImport` now returns a `probableDuplicates: DuplicateMatch[]` array alongside `valid` / `duplicates` / `errors`. The pass runs on the post-exact-dedup `nonDuplicates` set so we never double-flag rows already caught by strict pipeline. Heuristic failure never blocks the import — it's caught and returned as `[]`. Connector orchestrators (`runWealthPositionPreview` / `runZipPreview` / `runIbkrPreview`) inherit the field automatically because they re-export `PreviewResult` verbatim. **Refactored [src/lib/reconcile.ts](src/lib/reconcile.ts) onto the shared helper.** The pre-existing per-account, exact-amount, ±3-days probable-duplicate matcher (~90 lines) now delegates to `detectProbableDuplicates(...)` with `{ amountTolerancePct: 0, amountToleranceFloor: 0.005, dateToleranceDays: 3, scoreThreshold: 0.5 }` so reconcile semantics stay exactly the same (exact-cent matching, ±3 days, closest-date wins) while the heuristic body lives in one place. Four-way classification (NEW / EXISTING / PROBABLE_DUPLICATE) and pre-consumption of exact-match-id'd candidates preserved. **MCP `preview_import` (HTTP + stdio).** Both transports now include `probableDuplicates` in the response payload. HTTP decrypts payee with the in-scope `dek` and runs raw SQL via the pg-shim; stdio reads plaintext via `sqlite.prepare()` (no DEK in that transport). Tool descriptions updated to mention the heuristic and that flagged rows still commit on `execute_import` unless explicitly skipped. Tool count unchanged (82 HTTP / 78 stdio). **UI surface [src/app/(app)/import/components/import-preview-dialog.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/import/components/import-preview-dialog.tsx).** Probable-duplicate rows render with an orange row tint and an `AlertTriangle` "Probable dup" badge that expands inline to show the matched transaction id, date, amount, source, days off, amount delta (absolute and percent), score, and reason. Per-row checkbox toggles skip/commit; bulk "Skip all" / "Commit all" actions in a header bar. Default action = commit (warning, not block). Counter at the dialog top: `X new, Y duplicates, Z probable duplicates, W errors`. The page client filters skipped rows out client-side before `/api/import/execute`. **Connector tab pass-through.** [src/app/(app)/import/components/connector-tab.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/import/components/connector-tab.tsx) plumbs `probableDuplicates` through to the dialog so WealthPosition / future connector imports see the same warning UI; the per-row skip set is not yet round-tripped through the zip-execute endpoint (deferred — the user still sees the flag). **Out of scope.** No "merge" action (delete the bank-side row + tag an FX-spread fee row) — gets its own follow-up. No persisted `suggested_transfer_match_id` column — recomputed per preview because user thresholds may change. No ML. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no MCP tool count change. **Load-bearing invariants honored.** `tryDecryptField` `?? plaintext` fallback in the pool query. The detector reads, never writes — no `invalidateUser(userId)` calls. `import_hash` skip path so the helper never double-flags exact matches. `link_id` server-only — the detector reads it but the heuristic never mints one (a future "promote to transfer pair" follow-up MUST mint via `createTransferPair`). Pool query intentionally does NOT join through `accounts.archived` — archived accounts can still contain matching transactions. Files: [src/lib/external-import/duplicate-detect.ts](src/lib/external-import/duplicate-detect.ts), [src/lib/external-import/duplicate-detect-pool.ts](src/lib/external-import/duplicate-detect-pool.ts), [src/lib/import-pipeline.ts](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts), [src/lib/reconcile.ts](src/lib/reconcile.ts), [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts), [src/app/(app)/import/components/import-preview-dialog.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/import/components/import-preview-dialog.tsx), [src/app/(app)/import/components/connector-tab.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/import/components/connector-tab.tsx), [src/app/(app)/import/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/import/page.tsx), [tests/duplicate-detect.test.ts](tests/duplicate-detect.test.ts).
- **Unified OFX/QFX/IBKR-XML investment-statement import (2026-04-30, closes [#64](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/64)).** Replaces the broken file-upload path that used to route through the `@finlynq/import-connectors/ibkr` connector with a generic, format-based pipeline in the main importer. OFX investment statements (`<INVSTMTRS>`), QFX investment exports (with `<INTU.BID>` Quicken blocks stripped), and IBKR FlexQuery XML files (`<FlexQueryResponse>` root) all parse through canonical-row emitters that emit the import pipeline's `RawTransaction[]` shape directly. Bank/CC OFX is unchanged — same `parseOfx()` path, same UI dialog, no regression in the 12 existing OFX parser tests. **Extended [src/lib/ofx-parser.ts](src/lib/ofx-parser.ts).** New exports `parseOfxInvestments()` + `hasInvestmentStatement()` recognize `<BUYSTOCK>` / `<BUYMF>` / `<BUYOPT>` / `<BUYOTHER>` / `<BUYDEBT>` / `<SELLSTOCK>` / `<SELLMF>` / `<SELLOPT>` / `<SELLOTHER>` / `<SELLDEBT>`, `<INCOME>`, `<REINVEST>`, `<INVBANKTRAN>`, `<TRANSFER>`, `<SECID>`, `<SECLIST>`, and `<INVBAL>`. SECLIST `<TICKER>` → `<SECNAME>` mapping fills in friendly holding names (`AAPL` → `Apple Inc`); `parseTrade()` deliberately leaves `secName` empty when SECLIST didn't supply one so the file-level post-fill in `parseOfxInvestments()` runs (filling with the ticker prematurely would short-circuit it). The shared `stripHeaders()` helper now strips `<INTU.…>` Quicken blocks upfront so QFX files with `<INTU.BID>` don't pollute sub-block extraction inside STMTRS scans. **New canonical emitters** under [src/lib/external-import/parsers/](src/lib/external-import/parsers/): `ofx.ts` (covers bank/CC AND investment statements in one pass), `qfx.ts` (thin wrapper over `ofx.ts` with `format: "qfx"`), `ibkr-flexquery-xml.ts` (ports the connector's XML parsing + cancellation-triplet netting + forex collapse but emits `RawTransaction[]` instead of `ExternalTransaction[]`), and `detect.ts` (extension-first content-sniff returning a discriminated union). Buy/sell trades emit TWO rows sharing a parser-side `linkId`: a cash leg on the per-account "Cash" sleeve, plus a position leg with `quantity` (signed per WP convention: BUY → +qty, SELL → −qty) and `portfolioHolding` set to the holding NAME (not ticker — see import-connectors.md invariant #2). Commissions and fees emit additional negative cash-sleeve rows tagged `trade-link:<linkId>`. INCOME rows and INVBANKTRAN deposits are single cash-sleeve rows. REINVEST expands into three legs (income credit + cash debit + position credit) sharing one `linkId`. IBKR forex (`assetCategory="CASH"`) collapses to a same-account two-leg currency conversion with synthetic per-currency external account ids. IBKR cancellation triplets (same `actionId`) net to one row or drop entirely if they sum to zero. **Sign convention preserved end-to-end** — `amt > 0 + qty > 0` = position grew. The portfolio aggregator classifies by `qty` direction; the parsers never flip signs. **Wired [/api/import/preview](src/app/api/import/preview/route.ts) to dispatch by format sniff.** `.qfx` always routes to QFX. `.ofx` and `.xml` route through `detectInvestmentFileFormat()` first; IBKR FlexQuery and any OFX containing `<INVSTMTRS>` go to the canonical emitters and return a new `type: "investment-statement"` response with `externalAccounts` for the user to bind. Bank/CC OFX still returns `type: "ofx"` and shows the existing `OfxPreview` dialog unchanged. **New [components/investment-statement-preview.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/import/components/investment-statement-preview.tsx)** — multi-account brokerage files (e.g. an IBKR FlexQuery with USD + CAD sleeves, or one OFX with two `<INVSTMTRS>` blocks) get one dropdown per external account so users bind each to a Finlynq account before import. Single-account files are one dropdown + Import. The dialog rewrites every row's `account` field from the synthetic external id (`ofx:invacct:…` / `ibkr:acct:…`) to the bound Finlynq account name before handing back to `/api/import/execute`. **`.xml` extension added to the file-drop accept list** ([file-drop-zone.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/import/components/file-drop-zone.tsx)) and the unsupported-format error message updated. **Format tags written into `transactions.tags`.** Every row carries `source:ofx` / `source:qfx` / `source:ibkr-xml` per issue #62's vocabulary; descriptive tags like `trade-link:<linkId>` are appended when applicable. **27 new parser tests** in [tests/external-import-parsers.test.ts](tests/external-import-parsers.test.ts) — synthetic OFX SGML, QFX, and IBKR FlexQuery XML fixtures (no real user data; account numbers / MIDs are made up) cover BUYSTOCK + SELLSTOCK pairing (cash leg + position leg + linkId), commission/fees emission, INCOME (DIV) routing, INVBANKTRAN deposits, IBKR cancellation-triplet netting, IBKR forex collapse, source-tag stamping, format detection (SGML / OFX-XML / QFX / IBKR-Flex / unknown), and SECLIST display-name lookup. The connector package's `packages/import-connectors/src/ibkr/` directory is left in place for a future Flex Web Service live-API client (no UI references it anymore — file upload routes around it). **Load-bearing invariants honored.** `importHash` is computed over plaintext payee inside the import pipeline (not the parser) — AES-GCM ciphertext hashes are non-deterministic. `RawTransaction.portfolioHolding` carries the holding NAME so the resolver's dual-index lookup (plaintext lowercase + `name_lookup` HMAC) picks up an existing row rather than auto-creating an unmatchable ticker-named row. Strict-mode investment-account constraint (`requireHoldingForInvestmentAccount` at the import-pipeline boundary) — every emitted row that lands in an investment account carries either a position-leg `portfolioHolding` (the security name) or `Cash` (the per-account Cash sleeve). Stream-D dual-write rules apply downstream when the resolver auto-creates a holding (`buildNameFields` populates `name` + `name_ct` + `name_lookup`). Currency-as-symbol footgun avoided — the parsers never emit ISO 4217 codes as portfolio holdings (the `Cash` sleeve carries the currency on the account, not the holding). **Out of scope (next iterations).** Lot-level cost basis from `<INVPOS>` / `<OpenPositions>` is not modeled — average cost remains the convention. Cross-source duplicate detection between bank and brokerage statements (issue #65) is independent. The `@finlynq/import-connectors/ibkr` package directory was not deleted yet — kept for the future Flex Web Service live-API integration; no UI imports it. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no MCP tool count change. Files: [src/lib/ofx-parser.ts](src/lib/ofx-parser.ts), [src/lib/external-import/parsers/ofx.ts](src/lib/external-import/parsers/ofx.ts), [src/lib/external-import/parsers/qfx.ts](src/lib/external-import/parsers/qfx.ts), [src/lib/external-import/parsers/ibkr-flexquery-xml.ts](src/lib/external-import/parsers/ibkr-flexquery-xml.ts), [src/lib/external-import/parsers/detect.ts](src/lib/external-import/parsers/detect.ts), [src/app/api/import/preview/route.ts](src/app/api/import/preview/route.ts), [src/app/(app)/import/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/import/page.tsx), [src/app/(app)/import/components/investment-statement-preview.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/import/components/investment-statement-preview.tsx), [src/app/(app)/import/components/file-drop-zone.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/import/components/file-drop-zone.tsx), [tests/external-import-parsers.test.ts](tests/external-import-parsers.test.ts), [docs/import-connectors.md](docs/import-connectors.md).
- **Transactions table polish: audit columns, full-width, scroll, sort, per-column filters (2026-04-30, closes [#59](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/59)).** Surfaces the issue #28 audit trio (`created_at`, `updated_at`, `source`) on the `/transactions` table as opt-in columns, expands the table to the full viewport width with horizontal scroll, and adds per-column header sort + per-column filter affordances. Closes REVIEW pending follow-up #6 (issue #28's column-customization deferral). **Schema migration** ([scripts/migrate-tx-audit-indexes.sql](scripts/migrate-tx-audit-indexes.sql)) adds two composite indexes (`transactions_user_updated_at_idx`, `transactions_user_created_at_idx`) so audit-field sorts don't table-scan on 50k+ row data sets. `source` is a 7-value enum — no index needed. Idempotent. Apply BEFORE the code deploy via the [migrations playbook](docs/migrations.md). **Shared column-metadata module** ([src/lib/transactions/columns.ts](src/lib/transactions/columns.ts)). Single authority for the column-id list, default-visibility flags, sortable whitelist (`SORTABLE_COLUMN_IDS`), and the per-column `FILTER_COLUMN_TYPES` map (`date` / `text` / `numeric` / `enum`). Imported by the page client, the GET `/api/transactions` sort whitelist, and the three settings routes — adding a column happens in this file. **Three settings endpoints share the pattern.** Existing `/api/settings/tx-columns` rewired to import from the shared module and now lists `createdAt` / `updatedAt` / `source` (default off). New [src/app/api/settings/tx-sort/route.ts](src/app/api/settings/tx-sort/route.ts) persists `{ columnId, direction }` keyed `tx_table_sort` (zod-bounded; null direction = unsorted). New [src/app/api/settings/tx-filters/route.ts](src/app/api/settings/tx-filters/route.ts) persists a length-bounded JSON blob keyed `tx_table_filters` — discriminated-union schema by filter type, with a per-row `enum on source` sanitizer that drops values outside the `SOURCES` tuple in [src/lib/tx-source.ts](src/lib/tx-source.ts). Both new endpoints follow the tx-columns last-writer-wins / corrupt-blob-falls-back-to-defaults pattern. **Server-side sort + filter pushdown** ([src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts) `getTransactions`, `getTransactionCount`). New `TxSortFilter` type widens the existing filter object with `createdAtFrom`/`To`, `updatedAtFrom`/`To`, `accountIds`/`categoryIds` (multi-id IN), `amountMin`/`Max`/`Eq`, `quantityMin`/`Max`/`Eq`, `sources` (multi-select enum), `sortColumnId`, `sortDirection`. Sort whitelisted at the lib boundary via `txSortExpr(SortableColumnId)` — hard-coded mapping to drizzle column expressions, never user-string-interpolated into SQL. Stable tiebreaker `desc(transactions.id)` so paginated results don't shuffle when sorted by a low-cardinality column like `source`. Default sort stays `date DESC` unchanged. **GET `/api/transactions` parses the new query params** ([src/app/api/transactions/route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/route.ts)). `sort` validated against `isSortableColumnId`; `sources` against `isTransactionSource`. Encrypted-column substring filters (`filter_payee`, `filter_note`, `filter_accountName`, `filter_accountAlias`, `filter_portfolio`, `filter_portfolioTicker`, `filter_tags`) trigger the existing `postDecryptFilter` widening (limit 1000) so SQL doesn't paginate on ciphertext. Read-vs-write auth split preserved — GET still tolerates a missing DEK. **Page UI** ([src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx)). Inline column declarations replaced with imports from the shared module — three new columns (`Created`, `Updated`, `Source`) appear in the column-picker. `source` renders as a Badge via `labelForSource`; `createdAt`/`updatedAt` use the existing `formatDate` (date-portion only — full timestamp lives in the edit-dialog footer). Every sortable header is now a button: clicking cycles `desc → asc → null` with an arrow icon for the active state; persists via `/api/settings/tx-sort` debounced 400ms. Every filterable header gets a small Filter icon dropdown with type-appropriate inputs (date range / substring / numeric op / multi-select enum). Active per-column filters render as chips below the existing top-bar quick filters; "Clear all" wipes the lot (top-bar + per-column + sort). The `<Table>` is wrapped in a `<div className="overflow-x-auto">` so columns scroll horizontally instead of truncating. The page root opts out of the global `max-w-7xl mx-auto` clamp via `-mx-4 sm:-mx-6 lg:-mx-8 px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8 max-w-none` so the table fills the viewport without touching `(app)/layout.tsx`. **Top-bar quick filters kept separate from per-column filters** — URL-driven deep links like `/transactions?categoryId=X` from the rest of the app must keep working, and the top-bar is the more discoverable "give me the common stuff" UX. Per-column popovers are the fine-grain tool. The chip row reflects both. **Drag-reorder behavior, column toggle dropdown, bulk-action bar, edit-dialog footer all unchanged.** **Out of scope.** No `accountDescription` column (no `accounts.description` schema). Drag-reorder UX upgrade to `@dnd-kit` (REVIEW pending follow-up #4). Sticky-columns / density toggle / view presets / sort-by-encrypted-name post-decrypt (would balloon the page-state surface). **Risks honored.** `source` is INSERT-only — read-only on this page (CLAUDE.md "Audit trio" gotcha). Sort whitelist is hard-coded — no user input touches an ORDER BY. Encrypted-column substring filters route through the post-decrypt widening (CLAUDE.md "Read vs write auth guards"). Schema-migration step is index-only and idempotent — applied per env BEFORE code deploy per [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md).

### Changed
- **Settings monolith split into 8-group left-nav layout (2026-04-30, closes [#57](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/57)).** UX rework only — every card calls the same APIs with the same payloads. The 1573-line `/settings/page.tsx` monolith with 13 stacked cards was carved into 8 grouped sub-pages under a shared left-nav shell. The bare `/settings` URL now server-side redirects to `/settings/general`, so every existing link keeps working without churn. **New shared shell** ([src/app/(app)/settings/layout.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/settings/layout.tsx)). Renders a vertical left-nav (~220px) on `md+` with the same idiom as the global app sidebar — amber `bg-primary` left-edge marker, `bg-white/[0.08]` highlight, sticky positioning. On `<md` the nav collapses into a horizontal scrollable pill row above the content. Active state is derived from `usePathname()`; legacy deep links (`/settings/dropdown-order`, `/settings/holding-accounts`) are mapped back to their group entry (`Display & Ordering` / `Investments`) so the right pill highlights without us adding them as nav items. **Eight new group routes.** General (Display Preferences + Active Currencies + FX Overrides + About), Account & Security (API Key + Privacy & Backup), Data (Import + Export + Danger Zone), Categorization (Category Management + Transaction Rules), Investments (Holding ↔ Account map landing), Display & Ordering (Dropdown Ordering landing), Integrations (MCP Server reference), Developer (Dev Mode toggle — last per user decision). Each new file is a self-contained client component with its own state hooks; no shared cross-page state. **Re-parenting existing sub-pages.** `/settings/dropdown-order` and `/settings/holding-accounts` now inherit the new shell automatically. Their previous "Back to Settings" header strips were removed (the persistent left nav covers it). **Cross-page link updates.** [src/app/(app)/mcp-guide/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/mcp-guide/page.tsx) line 384 → `/settings/account` for the API-key reference. [src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx) (4 sites — lines 1442, 1786, 2312, 2379) and [src/app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/dashboard/page.tsx) line 291 → `/settings/general` to land directly on FX overrides / Display Preferences. The global nav at [src/components/nav.tsx](src/components/nav.tsx) keeps `/settings` because its active-state check uses `pathname.startsWith(item.href)` which still highlights correctly across all sub-pages. **No functional regressions.** Each card was lifted as-is; same `useState` shape, same handlers, same fetch payloads. Categorization is the largest sub-page at 585 lines (vs. spec target of ~400) because Category Management + Transaction Rules form one coherent UX group; the rest fit comfortably under the target. Total: monolith 1573 → largest sub-page 585 (63% reduction). **Out of scope.** No card-content changes, no API changes, no settings-search feature, no cross-page state sharing. Pure copy-paste-and-trim refactor per the issue spec. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no tool count change.

### Added
- **Interactive Brokers connector (2026-04-30, closes [#35](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/35)).** Second `@finlynq/import-connectors` provider. Multi-account broker statements via IBKR Flex Query XML (preferred — deterministic attributes) or Activity CSV (fallback — handles header drift via section grouping). Pure transform stays in the package; the Finlynq-side orchestrator + upload UI follow the WP playbook in a follow-up. **New connector at [packages/import-connectors/src/ibkr/](packages/import-connectors/src/ibkr/).** `types.ts` (IbkrStatement / IbkrCashTransaction / IbkrTrade / IbkrOpenPosition / IbkrFxTranslation), `parse-xml.ts` (regex-based reader for IB's self-closing Flex XML — zero deps, format is regular enough to skip a full XML parser), `parse-csv.ts` (multi-section CSV grouped by `Section,Header,Cols…` / `Section,Data,Vals…` rows with mid-section header re-emit support), `transform.ts` (IbkrParsedFile → ExternalAccount + ExternalCategory + ExternalTransaction inventories), `orchestrator.ts` (auto-detects XML vs CSV, then routes through the shared `transformTransactions`), `index.ts` (Connector default export + `parseAndInventory` convenience). Exposed as `@finlynq/import-connectors/ibkr` and re-exported from the package barrel. **Sub-account boundaries.** Each `<FlexStatement>` block becomes one ExternalAccount per (accountId, currency) actually used. Multi-currency holders see distinct USD / CAD / EUR sleeves so the user can map each one to the matching Finlynq account. **Forex pairs collapse to one in-kind currency conversion.** `Trade` rows with `assetCategory="CASH"` (e.g. `EUR.USD` quantity=500 netCash=-550 USD) emit ONE ExternalTransaction with two entries — one per currency sleeve on the SAME sub-account — which `transformTransactions` then routes through the standard 2-account transfer path. Not two separate cash flows. **Cancellation triplets net to one row (or drop entirely).** `netCancellationTriplets` groups CashTransactions by `(accountId, currency, actionId)`. If the group sums to zero (e.g. a withhold + cancel pair), the rows drop. If it sums non-zero (withhold + cancel + re-issue at a different rate), one net row emits at the latest date in the group. Rows without an `actionId` pass through unchanged. **Cash dividends → income; withholding → expense; deposits/withdrawals → 1A+1C.** `categoryForCashType` maps IB's `type` strings to six synthetic categories (`Dividends`, `Withholding Tax`, `Interest Income`, `Broker Fees`, `FX Translation P&L`, `Deposits & Withdrawals`) the user resolves once in the mapping dialog. Stock/fund/option trades emit a 2-leg ExternalTransaction (cash + symbol-keyed holding pseudo-account) so the import pipeline writes `transactions.portfolio_holding_id` correctly. FX translation P&L (`<FxTranslation>` rows) emit as a `FX Translation P&L` revaluation entry. **Sub-account → Finlynq-account inference via held positions.** `inferAccountMapping(parsed, finlynqHoldingsByAccountId)` returns Jaccard-similarity matches at ≥50% threshold. The orchestrator (Finlynq side, follow-up) presents these as defaults the user can override before commit. Below the threshold the row stays unmapped — not silently misrouted. **`transformTransactions` gains a `sourceConnectorId` option** ([packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/transform.ts](packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/transform.ts)) defaulting to `"wealthposition"` so existing WP callers stay byte-identical. IB orchestrator passes `"ibkr"` so every emitted row tags `source:ibkr` (not `source:wealthposition`) — preserves the cross-import dedup story from invariant 15 in [docs/import-connectors.md](docs/import-connectors.md). WP's own connector index now passes its id explicitly too. **Tests** ([packages/import-connectors/src/ibkr/transform.test.ts](packages/import-connectors/src/ibkr/transform.test.ts), [parse-xml.test.ts](packages/import-connectors/src/ibkr/parse-xml.test.ts), [parse-csv.test.ts](packages/import-connectors/src/ibkr/parse-csv.test.ts)). 24 new tests covering each acceptance criterion: forex collapse, cancellation netting, sub-account boundaries, dividend / withholding / deposit shapes, Jaccard inference + threshold, idempotent re-runs, end-to-end orchestrator on real-shape XML. Existing WP suite (31 tests) still green — refactor preserved exact behavior. **What's intentionally NOT in this PR.** No upload UI, no orchestrator under `src/lib/external-import/ibkr-orchestrator.ts`, no routes under `src/app/api/import/connectors/ibkr/`, no Flex Web Service client, no reconciliation. The pure transform half is what issue #35 calls for; the Finlynq-side glue is a separate UI-touching piece of work. Issue [#36](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/36) (Reconciliation Mode UI) consumes this transform. **Docs.** New row in the §9 "First-class connectors" table of [docs/import-connectors.md](docs/import-connectors.md) plus an "IBKR-specific notes" subsection covering the parse → transform → standard-pipeline flow and the new `sourceConnectorId` option. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no tool count change.
- **Transaction audit fields: created_at / updated_at / source (2026-04-30, closes [#28](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/28)).** Adds three audit columns to `transactions`. `created_at` (set on INSERT, never touched again), `updated_at` (set on INSERT and refreshed on every UPDATE), and `source` (writer-surface attribution — `manual` / `import` / `mcp_http` / `mcp_stdio` / `connector` / `sample_data` / `backup_restore`, set on INSERT, never modified). All three are system-time / system-attribution facts distinct from the user-supplied `date`. Lets users (and AI assistants) attribute every row — "where did this come from?" — without having to grep server logs, and gives MCP write tools a way to verify the row landed and self-attributed correctly. **Migration** ([scripts/migrate-tx-audit-fields.sql](scripts/migrate-tx-audit-fields.sql)). `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` for the three columns + a `transactions_source_check` CHECK constraint that mirrors the seven values in [src/lib/tx-source.ts](src/lib/tx-source.ts). Backfill sets timestamps to `NOW()` and source to `'manual'` (the safest default — understates imports / MCP rows but doesn't lie about anything in particular). Pre-migration creation time + true source are unrecoverable; we deliberately do NOT use `entered_at` as a backfill source because semantics differ (entered_at is FX-settlement input for the cron, created_at is system audit). Idempotent. Runs BEFORE the matching code per the standard playbook. **Schema + types.** Three new columns on the `transactions` pgTable in [src/db/schema-pg.ts](src/db/schema-pg.ts), positioned alongside `entered_at` for visual grouping.
