New file [src/lib/tx-source.ts](src/lib/tx-source.ts) exports the `TransactionSource` union, a `SOURCES` const tuple kept in sync with the CHECK constraint, `isTransactionSource` / `coerceSourceForRestore` validators (the restore path falls back to `'backup_restore'` when an old backup has no `source` field), and `labelForSource` for UI rendering. **Application-layer maintenance.** Decision: no Postgres triggers. Matches the existing convention (`grep CREATE TRIGGER scripts/` returns zero matches) and makes attribution explicit at every write site. Every UPDATE site appends `updated_at = NOW()`; every INSERT site stamps an explicit `source` value. Plumbed through ~30 writer sites: [src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts) `createTransaction` / `updateTransaction`, [src/app/api/transactions/bulk/route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/bulk/route.ts), [src/app/api/transactions/audit/route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/audit/route.ts), [src/app/api/transactions/splits/route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/splits/route.ts) (parent's `updated_at` bumps when splits are rewritten — splits are part of the transaction's logical state), [src/app/api/import/backfill/route.ts](src/app/api/import/backfill/route.ts), [src/app/api/onboarding/sample-data/route.ts](src/app/api/onboarding/sample-data/route.ts) (`'sample_data'`), [src/app/api/data/import/route.ts](src/app/api/data/import/route.ts) (preserves original `source` from JSON; falls back to `'backup_restore'` for pre-migration backups), [src/lib/import-pipeline.ts](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts) (new `txSource` param defaults to `'import'`, connector orchestrators pass `'connector'`), [src/lib/external-import/orchestrator.ts](src/lib/external-import/orchestrator.ts), [src/lib/external-import/zip-orchestrator.ts](src/lib/external-import/zip-orchestrator.ts), [src/lib/external-import/reconciliation.ts](src/lib/external-import/reconciliation.ts) (`'connector'`), [src/lib/csv-parser.ts](src/lib/csv-parser.ts) (legacy parser; `'import'`), [src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts) (new `txSource` opt on both `createTransferPair{,ViaSql}` — distinct from the existing `source` opt which is the connector slug for the tag merge — both legs of a pair share the surface attribution; UPDATEs bump `updated_at`), [src/lib/investment-account.ts](src/lib/investment-account.ts) (`backfillInvestmentAccount`), [src/lib/cron/settle-future-fx.ts](src/lib/cron/settle-future-fx.ts) (cron-driven re-rate counts as a real row mutation; `source` preserved), [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) `record_transaction` / `bulk_record_transactions` / `update_transaction` (8 separate UPDATEs all get `, updated_at = NOW()`) / `apply_rules_to_uncategorized` / `commitBulkUpdate` (7 separate UPDATEs) / `record_trade` fee leg (`'mcp_http'`), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts) (stdio counterparts; `'mcp_stdio'`), and [mcp-server/tools-import-templates.ts](mcp-server/tools-import-templates.ts) (`'mcp_stdio'`). **Read-path surfacing.** `getTransactions` projection in [src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts) adds `createdAt` / `updatedAt` / `source` so the REST GET passes them straight through. `search_transactions` in [register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) tags each result row with the trio so AI assistants can sort by freshness or filter by writer surface ("show me everything I entered manually this month"). `record_transaction` (HTTP + stdio) returns the new `createdAt` / `updatedAt` / `source` from `RETURNING` so callers can verify the write landed and self-attributed; `update_transaction` returns the new `updatedAt`. `bulk_record_transactions` keeps per-row results terse — the AI can re-fetch via `search_transactions` if it needs them. **Edit-dialog footer** ([src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx)). New audit-trio footer line on edit ("Created … · Updated … · [Manual]" badge) sourced from the row's three new fields. `Transaction` type extended with the three optional fields. Lightest-touch UI surfacing — column-customization integration is deferred to issue [#20](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/20). **Backup round-trip.** `/api/data/export` ships the new columns automatically (its `select()` has no projection — every column auto-includes). `/api/data/import` extracts `source` per row, runs it through `coerceSourceForRestore`, and falls back to `'backup_restore'` when the JSON predates this migration; `created_at` / `updated_at` round-trip via the per-row INSERT value. Older backups without those fields fall through to the column DEFAULT (NOW()). **Decisions documented in the migration comment.** (1) Splits-touch-parent: yes, splits are part of the parent's logical state. (2) FX cron: yes, any DB-side mutation should be reflected — `source` preservation handles the "this isn't user activity" concern. (3) Investment-account backfill: yes, consistent with the FX-cron decision. (4) Index `(user_id, updated_at DESC)`: deferred until a UI feature actually needs "recently modified" sort. (5) Source granularity: ship the coarse 7-value enum first; a finer breakdown (CSV vs OFX vs PDF vs Email) would balloon the CHECK list — add an optional `source_detail` text column in a follow-up if needed.
- **Portfolio "By Holding" panel + per-account aggregate stats (2026-04-30, closes [#25](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/25)).** Interim Section F slice (issue #25's option (a)). Pools the same canonical position across every account that holds it and adds inline P&L stats to the existing per-account header. The full canonical-name backfill helper (touches `src/lib/crypto/**`) and the SQL aggregator rewrites in the 5 sites listed in CLAUDE.md "Portfolio aggregation" (touches `mcp-server/**`) are deferred to follow-up issues — this PR is the bot-safe UX half. **`/api/portfolio/overview` adds `byHolding`** ([src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts)). Re-aggregates `enrichedHoldings` post-enrichment by canonical key: uppercased symbol for stocks/ETFs, base symbol (split on `-`) for crypto so chain variants like `BTC-ETH` collapse onto `BTC`, metal symbol for XAU/XAG/XPT/XPD, and currency code for cash sleeves so `Cash CAD` and `Cash USD` stay separate rows. User-edited free-text names on non-cash rows fall through to a `custom:` key bucketed by lowercased trimmed name — these still fragment until the canonicalization helper ships, but tickered/cash rows already collapse correctly. Per-row totals fold into displayCurrency via the same `fxRates` map the enriched path uses, so the rollup matches the existing summary totals to within FX rounding. **Avg cost across accounts is qty-weighted** — `Σ(costBasis_acct in display) / Σ(qty_acct)` — to avoid the simple-mean bug when one account holds 10× more shares than another (issue spec §5). **New "By Holding" panel** ([src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/portfolio/page.tsx)). Renders above "Holdings by Account" with columns Holding / Total Qty / Avg Cost / Mkt Value / Unrealized G/L / Realized G/L / Accounts (count badge). All values in display currency. Sorted by market value desc. CSV export reuses `downloadCSV` with the new `exportByHoldingToCSV` shape. No per-row drill-down (issue #25 §"Tasks": "No drill-down"). **Inline aggregate stats on per-account header.** Mkt Value / Unrealized / Realized columns added to the right side of each "Holdings by Account" button row (md+ breakpoint), so the user sees the per-account rollup without expanding. Realized folds into display currency via the same per-holding `marketValueDisplay/marketValue` ratio the row-level toolbar uses. **"View txns" inline link in expanded per-account table.** Each row in the per-account drill-down table now has a per-row "View txns →" link to `/transactions?portfolioHolding=<name>&accountId=<id>` (the existing transactions-page filter contract — no API change needed). **Display currency fix in expanded per-account table.** The hard-coded `formatCurrency(h.marketValueDisplay, "CAD")` at the per-account drill-down table is replaced with `formatCurrency(h.marketValueDisplay, displayCurrency)`. Pre-existing wart — the field has been displayCurrency-denominated since multi-currency support landed; the literal "CAD" was leftover. **Out of scope (deferred).** Canonical-name backfill helper (`src/lib/crypto/stream-d-canonicalize-portfolio.ts`), `users.portfolio_names_canonicalized_at` migration, PUT-handler block on canonical-name edits + HoldingEditDialog name-input hide, and the 5 SQL aggregator rewrites in `register-tools-pg.ts` / `register-core-tools.ts` / `holdings-value.ts` / `overview/route.ts` SQL all stay as separate work. The post-enrichment regroup ships value today and converges with the SQL rewrite cleanly when it lands (the API contract is stable). Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no tool count change.
- **Setup pages: dropdown ordering + holding↔account map (2026-04-30, closes [#26](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/26)).** Two new sub-pages under `/settings`. The first is a UI on top of the dropdown-order infrastructure that issue [#21](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/21) already shipped (the `DropdownOrderProvider` was always a consumer; this gives the user a way to actually edit the saved order). The second is the producer half of the new many-to-many holding ↔ account model — issue [#25](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/25) (Section F) will migrate the 5 portfolio aggregator callsites + 8 investment-account-constraint callsites onto it as a follow-up; Section G ships the schema, CRUD, and UI here so that work isn't blocked on a missing table. **Schema: `holding_accounts` join table** ([src/db/schema-pg.ts](src/db/schema-pg.ts), [scripts/migrate-holding-accounts.sql](scripts/migrate-holding-accounts.sql)). Composite PK `(holding_id, account_id)` + denormalized `user_id` for hot-path filtering + `qty` / `cost_basis` / `is_primary` / `created_at`. ON DELETE CASCADE on both FKs (deleting a holding or account removes its pairings; transactions stay alive via the existing `portfolio_holding_id … ON DELETE SET NULL`). No encrypted columns — only ids + numbers — so no Stream D dual-write and no DEK is required for CRUD. Migration backfills from existing `portfolio_holdings(account_id)` rows + sums `transactions.quantity` and `ABS(amount) WHERE quantity>0` per holding so a fresh env reproduces the aggregator's existing numbers exactly. Idempotent (`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`, `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`). **Legacy `portfolio_holdings.account_id` kept.** Section F (issue #25) is independently migrating the 5 aggregator callsites + 8 investment-account-constraint callsites; coupling the column drop with the schema add would create a deploy-ordering hazard. The legacy column has zero ongoing maintenance cost: every existing write already specifies `accountId`, and the row in `holding_accounts` whose `is_primary=true` mirrors it. Drop is tracked as a follow-up conditioned on Section F shipping. **CRUD endpoint** ([src/app/api/holding-accounts/route.ts](src/app/api/holding-accounts/route.ts)). `GET` lists every pairing for the user with decrypted holding/account names via `decryptNamedRows` (mirrors `/api/portfolio` + `/api/accounts`). `POST` validates ownership of both ids, refuses duplicate `(holding, account)` pairs (409), and when `isPrimary=true` demotes the existing primary in the same flow + updates `portfolio_holdings.account_id` to keep the legacy mirror in sync. `PUT` updates qty / cost_basis / is_primary on an existing pair (composite key in body). `DELETE?holdingId=X&accountId=Y` refuses to remove the last pairing on a holding (409 with hint to delete the holding instead) and refuses to remove a primary while siblings exist (409 with hint to set another pairing as primary first). All endpoints are `requireAuth` only — no encrypted columns means no DEK requirement, per CLAUDE.md "Read vs write auth guards". **Setup page: `/settings/holding-accounts`** ([src/app/(app)/settings/holding-accounts/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/settings/holding-accounts/page.tsx)). Per-holding card pattern matching the existing `/settings` Card-stack visual language. Each holding card has an inner table of `[account, qty, cost basis, primary?, actions]` with inline edit, "Make primary" toggle, and "Add account" dialog (filtered to accounts not already paired and not archived). The Add/Edit-holding dialog on the Portfolio page still owns the first pairing — this page only manages the second-and-beyond cases. **Setup page: `/settings/dropdown-order`** ([src/app/(app)/settings/dropdown-order/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/settings/dropdown-order/page.tsx)). Native HTML5 `draggable` reorder + up/down arrow buttons for keyboard + touch parity. Pinned section is the saved order; Unpinned section lists everything else alphabetically with a click-to-pin shortcut. Items are fetched live from `/api/categories`, `/api/accounts?includeArchived=1`, `/api/portfolio`, and `/api/settings/active-currencies` so newly-created entries appear without breaking the saved order. PUT writes the entire `{ version, lists }` shape so the four kinds stay in sync. No new dependency added — `@dnd-kit/*` is intentionally not installed per the issue spec. **Sub-page nav cards on `/settings`** ([src/app/(app)/settings/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/settings/page.tsx)). Two link cards above the About card. Top-level nav (`nav.tsx`) intentionally untouched — the issue spec asks to keep top-level nav clean. **Migrations playbook** ([docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md)). New `## holding-accounts (2026-04-30)` section with the standard 3-env psql one-liners + an explicit production-deploy ordering block for the `dev → main` promotion. **Applied to prod + staging + dev on 2026-04-30** — 75 rows backfilled per env (matches `COUNT(*) FROM portfolio_holdings WHERE account_id IS NOT NULL`). Migration ran BEFORE the code lands on prod/staging (additive — `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`, no touch on existing tables — so the running pre-`dev` code is unaffected). **Section F follow-up.** No aggregator callsites changed in this issue. The 5 sites listed in CLAUDE.md "Portfolio aggregation" + the 8 investment-account-constraint sites continue to read `portfolio_holdings.account_id`. Section F (issue #25) migrates them as a separate PR.

### Changed
- **Strict-mode investment-account holding constraint on transfers + imports (2026-04-30, closes [#22](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/22)).** Flipped the four currently-permissive callsites from "silently default to Cash" to "fail loud and surface the constraint to the user." The application-layer rule that every transaction in an `is_investment=true` account must reference a `portfolio_holding_id` row is now enforced uniformly — no more accidental cash legs landing on auto-Cash without the user knowing. Existing rows are migrated to Cash via a one-time backfill before the strict code rolls out. **Strict-mode in transfer.ts ([src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts)).** `createTransferPair` and `createTransferPairViaSql` no longer call `defaultHoldingForInvestmentAccount`. Any leg landing on an investment account without a resolved holding now throws `InvestmentHoldingRequiredError` (escapes the helper's `Result` shape on purpose so the call site catches it explicitly). **Cash-leg pin opts.** New `fromHoldingId` / `toHoldingId` opts on `CreateTransferOpts` let callers pin a leg's `portfolio_holding_id` without going through the in-kind name+quantity path — used by the new dialog "Cash (auto)" picker so cash transfers into investment accounts keep working without forcing the user through in-kind mode. Validated against (user, account) ownership before use; a stale or cross-tenant id surfaces as `holding-not-found`. **Strict reject in import pipeline ([src/lib/import-pipeline.ts](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts)).** `executeImport`'s post-resolver pass now appends a per-row error to `importErrors` for each unattributed cash leg in an investment account and excludes the row from the batch insert. The user-typed-`Cash` path still works via the existing resolver. **400 mapping in REST + MCP ([src/app/api/transactions/transfer/route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/transfer/route.ts), [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts)).** `/api/transactions/transfer` POST/PUT catch `InvestmentHoldingRequiredError` and return `{ code: "investment_holding_required", accountId, error }` 400 (mirrors the pattern at `/api/transactions`). MCP HTTP `record_transfer` + `record_trade` and stdio `record_transfer` + `record_trade` map the throw to a friendly tool error rather than crashing the transport. **UI: required holding picker for investment accounts ([src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx)).** Add Transaction dialog renders a violet "Portfolio Holding *" block above the Advanced collapse when the selected account is `isInvestment=true`, defaulting to "Cash (auto)" — the per-account Cash sleeve. The Advanced-collapse copy of the picker hides in this mode. Transfer dialog adds a parallel "Investment account leg" block for cash transfers (`inKind=false`) when either leg's account is investment, with required pickers per investment side. Submit handlers block client-side with friendly errors before the server 400s. Edit-pair flow leaves the existing in-kind / linked-siblings behaviors unchanged; the new cash-leg pin block only renders on create (PUT path doesn't yet accept the new opts). **One-time SQL backfill ([scripts/migrate-investment-cash-backfill.sql](scripts/migrate-investment-cash-backfill.sql), [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md)).** Idempotent psql migration that ensures every `is_investment=true` account has a 'Cash' holding and reassigns every NULL-FK transaction in those accounts to it. Verifies orphan count = 0 inside the transaction; raises rather than commits a partial state. Narrower than `migrate-accounts-is-investment.sql` because Phase 6 already dropped the legacy plaintext `portfolio_holding` column on prod. Run BEFORE pushing the matching code; check `GET /api/admin/investment-orphans` post-migration. **Stdio MCP unchanged for record_transaction/bulk_record_transactions.** Those tools continue to refuse investment-account writes outright (line 671 of `register-core-tools.ts`) — stdio has no `portfolioHolding` parameter, so blocking remains the only safe option. `record_transfer` + `record_trade` accept holding parameters and now map the strict-mode throw to a tool error.

### Added
- **`record_trade` MCP tool + brokerage-statement modeling recipe (2026-04-30, closes [#34](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/34)).** New high-level brokerage-trade primitive on top of `record_transfer`'s same-account in-kind path, plus a canonical "Modeling brokerage statements with MCP" recipe section in [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md). Saves agents from the four-parameter dance (`fromAccount = toAccount`, `holding` + `destHolding`, `quantity` + `destQuantity`, `enteredAmount = cashAmount`, optional `receivedAmount` for FX) on every buy/sell — agents reading the existing tool surface tended to fall back to `record_transaction(quantity=…)`, losing the holding-pair link and atomic two-leg semantics, so trades were silently splitting into two unlinked rows. **Tool — `record_trade(account, side, symbol, quantity, price, currency?, fees?, fxRate?, date?, note?)`** ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts) — both transports). `side='buy'` source = cash sleeve in `currency`, destination = symbol holding (auto-created if missing). `side='sell'` mirrors — symbol holding must exist (pre-flighted with the same lookup as `resolvePortfolioHoldingByName` so the error names candidates), cash sleeve auto-created. Internally calls `createTransferPair` / `createTransferPairViaSql` with `fromAccountId = toAccountId = account.id` so the in-kind same-account path produces both legs atomically with a server-generated UUID `link_id`. **Cash sleeve resolution (per currency).** Same currency as the account → reuses the default `Cash` sleeve via the existing `(symbol IS NULL OR UPPER(symbol)=currency) AND currency=?` lookup; the sort prefers `symbol IS NULL` for determinism. Foreign currency → mints `${currency} Cash` with `symbol = currency` so the portfolio aggregator's `isCurrencyCodeSymbol` branch routes it through the cash path instead of pinging Yahoo for the wrong company (the same gotcha that bit "CAD" → Cadiz Inc). **Cross-currency.** Requires explicit `fxRate` (trade currency → account currency) — the cost-basis amount on both legs locks at `quantity × price × fxRate` in account currency; source quantity stays in cash-sleeve units (e.g. 1500 USD), destination quantity stays in shares (e.g. 10). When `currency` matches the account currency, `fxRate` defaults to 1 and is ignored. **Fees.** Optional `fees` post as a separate negative-amount transaction on the cash sleeve (NOT part of the transfer pair) tagged `source:record_trade,trade-link:<linkId>` so reconciliation can find them later. **Investment-account guard.** Refuses non-investment accounts on both transports — surfaced as an actionable error rather than the FK-constraint violation users would otherwise see. The existing stdio carve-out (`record_transaction` refuses investment accounts because there's no `portfolioHolding` plumbing) does NOT apply to `record_trade` because the tool carries its own holding semantics — stdio writes still stay plaintext per the documented limitation. **Cache invalidation.** HTTP path calls `invalidateUserTxCache(userId)` after the commits per the load-bearing gotcha. Stdio doesn't run the user-tx cache. **Discoverability.** Added to `finlynq_help` `tool_name` docs and the `examples` topic on both transports (3 new examples covering same-currency buy, sell with fees, cross-currency buy). **Recipe doc.** New "Modeling brokerage statements with MCP" section in [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md) covers the three statement shapes that consistently confuse agents: (a) forex trades that look like cross-account transfers but are actually same-account currency conversions between two cash sleeves (use `record_transfer` with `holding`+`destHolding`+`receivedAmount`); (b) wire-out events that look like expenses but are cross-account transfers to a bank (use `record_transfer`, NOT `record_transaction(category="Bank Fees")`); (c) cancellation/re-issue triplets that net to one entry (skip the canceled and re-issued legs entirely, book only the corrected execution). **Tool count: 81 → 82 HTTP, 75 → 76 stdio.** [CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) and [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md) updated. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron.
- **MCP `record_transaction` / `bulk_record_transactions` accept `dryRun: true`; bank-imported transactions auto-tagged `source:<connector>` (2026-04-30, closes [#33](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/33)).** Two related dedup-safety changes. Goal: let callers preview the full validation/resolution pipeline before committing, and let future statement-reconciliation flows identify rows the bank side has already booked. **dryRun ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts)).** New `dryRun: z.boolean().optional()` parameter on both tools, both transports. When true, every check still runs (account ownership / strict fuzzy, FX rate resolution at the trade date, holding binding ownership pre-check, investment-account constraint, category auto-detection) but the `INSERT` is skipped and `invalidateUserTxCache` is NOT called. Response shape mirrors the success path: `dryRun: true`, `wouldBeId: null`, plus `resolvedAccount` / `resolvedCategory` / `resolvedHolding` populated so the caller can verify routing. `bulk_record_transactions` exposes `dryRun` at the top level and propagates it to every per-row result; the response uses `previewed` instead of `imported`. **Source tag — WealthPosition transform ([packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/transform.ts](packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/transform.ts)).** `buildRawTransaction` now appends `source:wealthposition` to every emitted row's tags via the new `withSourceTag` helper. Idempotent — the helper only adds the tag when not already present (case-insensitive scan of the existing tags string). Both flat and split-parent rows pick it up since both go through the same builder. **Source tag — `createTransferPair*` ([src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts)).** `CreateTransferOpts` gains an optional `source?: string`. When set, `applySourceTag(tags, source)` prepends `source:<connector>` to both legs' tags before encryption. Same idempotency rule as the WP transform. `createTransferPairViaSql` inherits the param via `Omit<CreateTransferOpts, "userId" | "dek">`. Existing callers with no `source` parameter are byte-identical. **Optional one-off backfill ([scripts/backfill-source-tag.sql](scripts/backfill-source-tag.sql)).** Parameterized psql script for tagging legacy connector imports retroactively. Caller specifies `:user_id`, `:source` slug, `:account_ids`, `:from_date`, `:to_date`. Skips rows whose `transactions.tags` is encrypted (`v1:%`) — those need a Node-side rewrite under the user's DEK and are out of scope for the SQL path; the script reports a count of skipped rows. Idempotent on re-runs (won't double-tag). **Tests.** Updated [packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/transform.test.ts](packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/transform.test.ts) "serializes tags" case to expect the appended tag and added a fresh case for the no-user-tags path. Tests using `toMatchObject` on flat / split-parent rows continue to pass because they don't assert on `tags`. **No tool count change.** Same 81 HTTP / 75 stdio tools; `dryRun` is a parameter, not a new tool. **Docs.** [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md) §"Tool surface inventory" gains a 2026-04-30 entry; [docs/import-connectors.md](docs/import-connectors.md) §7 gains invariant 15 (connectors auto-tag every emitted row); [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md) gains an "optional source-tag backfill" section. Code-only change; no schema migration required, no env vars, no cron.

### Fixed
- **Combobox filter renders unfiltered list across all 16 dropdowns — fixed at the primitive (2026-04-30, closes [#58](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/58)).** Single-file fix to [src/components/ui/combobox.tsx](src/components/ui/combobox.tsx). The high-level `<Combobox>` wrapper was passing `items` to `ComboboxPrimitive.Root` (which drives base-ui's internal filter and the `<ComboboxEmpty>` state) but then iterating the **original unfiltered `items`** array inside `<ComboboxList>` — typing in the search input flipped `<ComboboxEmpty>` to "No matches" while every `<ComboboxItem>` kept rendering below it, so the dropdown felt broken at every callsite migrated under issue [#21](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/21) (account picker, category picker, holding picker, currency picker, rule field picker, etc.). **Root cause.** Static `items.map(...)` inside `<ComboboxList>` is independent of the typed query — base-ui's Root filters its internal list but the wrapper was re-rendering from the raw prop. Per `@base-ui/react/combobox/list/ComboboxList.d.ts`, `<ComboboxList>` accepts a function-children form `(item, index) => ReactNode` that iterates the filtered list maintained by Root. **Fix.** Switched the wrapper's `<ComboboxList>` to function-children form. `<ComboboxEmpty>` was moved one level up (still inside `<ComboboxContent>`, now a sibling of `<ComboboxList>`) — base-ui's `<Combobox.Empty>` is driven by Root's `empty` state regardless of position, and keeping it outside the `<List>` cleanly separates the two render branches. **Why this covers all 16 dropdowns.** All callsites use the high-level `<Combobox>` component, so the single primitive change inherits everywhere; no callsite changes needed. Per-user pinned ordering still works because `useDropdownOrder` sorts items *before* they reach `<Combobox>` and base-ui's filter preserves input order. **Docs.** [docs/ui-design.md](docs/ui-design.md) Combobox section gains an explicit "Internals — do NOT iterate `items` inside `<ComboboxList>`" warning so the same regression can't sneak back in. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no tool count change.
- **Transfer edit dialog: replace IDs/linkId footer with audit-trio footer (2026-04-30, closes [#55](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/55)).** Cosmetic UI swap. Drops the developer-style `Editing transfer — IDs #N (debit) ↔ #M (credit) · linkId …` footer at the bottom of the transfer edit dialog and replaces it with the same `Created … · Updated … [Source]` audit-trio footer the regular transaction edit dialog already shows. Both legs of every transfer pair already carry the trio (issue [#28](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/28) / PR #50), so no schema, API, or state-shape change was needed. **Lookup-at-render** mirrors the regular-tx pattern in [src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx) — both legs are pulled from the in-scope `txns` array via `transferEdit.fromTxId` / `toTxId`, so no new state plumbing. **Defensive when legs disagree:** `created` = earliest of the two `createdAt` timestamps, `updated` = latest of the two `updatedAt` timestamps, `source` from the debit leg (legs share `source` per CLAUDE.md "Audit trio", with the credit leg as a fallback). If neither leg is in `txns` yet (race / stale state) the footer renders nothing — same null-return shape as the regular-tx footer. **`linkId` still flows through the PUT/DELETE wire path** (POST/DELETE handlers in the same file are unchanged); only the user-facing footer changed. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no tool count change.
- **MCP `record_transaction` / `bulk_record_transactions` no longer auto-categorize investment-account writes onto expense categories (2026-04-30, closes [#32](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/32)).** When `category` was omitted on a write to an `is_investment=true` account, the HTTP MCP `autoCategory` helper fell through rules → historical-frequency tally and could land brokerage rows (forex, dividends, interest, cash disbursements) on user-defined expense categories like "Groceries", corrupting spending reports and forcing manual review of every imported batch. **New pure routing helpers** in [src/lib/auto-categorize.ts](src/lib/auto-categorize.ts): `pickInvestmentCategoryByPayee(payee, categories)` keyword-routes a payee to the user's matching income/transfer category by name, and `fallbackInvestmentCategory(categories)` returns the user's `Transfers` (or `Investment Activity`) id. Both are pure — no DB access — so the MCP caller decrypts category names once and passes the list in. **`autoCategory` mode flag.** [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) — `autoCategory(db, userId, payee, dek)` gains a trailing `isInvestmentAccount: boolean = false` parameter (default false keeps non-investment writes byte-identical). In investment mode it pre-loads `(id, name, type)` for every category, drops expense (`type='E'`) candidates from both the rule and history candidate pools, runs the keyword pass between rules and history, and falls back to `Transfers` / `Investment Activity` after a missed history match instead of returning null. **Keywords (case-insensitive substring on payee).** `dividend` → `Dividends`/`Dividend`; `interest` → `Credit Interest`/`Interest Income`/`Interest`; `forex` / `\bfx\b` (word-boundary so "Affixed" doesn't match) / `currency` → `Currency Revaluation`/`Forex`/`Transfers`; `disbursement` / `withdrawal` → `Transfers`/`Withdrawals`. First name found in the user's existing category list wins. **Caller plumbing.** `record_transaction` resolves `is_investment` once via `isInvestmentAccountFn(userId, accountId)` and reuses the boolean for both the autoCategory call and the downstream holding-FK constraint check (replaces a duplicate query). `bulk_record_transactions` uses a Set lookup against the pre-fetched `investmentAccountIds` per row — no extra round-trip. **Stdio MCP unchanged.** Stdio `record_transaction`/`bulk_record_transactions` already refuse investment-account writes outright (no `portfolioHolding` parameter on stdio), so investment mode never fires there. **Tests.** [tests/auto-categorize.test.ts](tests/auto-categorize.test.ts) covers each keyword branch, the `\bfx\b` word-boundary (keeps "Affixed" null), case-insensitive payee + category matching, missing-fallback paths returning null, and the `Transfers` > `Investment Activity` preference order. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no tool count change.
- **MCP transaction writes now surface a `warnings` field when a `portfolioHoldingId` is bound on a cash-moving row but `quantity` is missing (2026-04-30, closes [#31](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/31)).** `record_transaction`, `bulk_record_transactions`, and `update_transaction` (HTTP MCP) accepted rows that bound to a `portfolio_holding_id` but omitted `quantity`. The transaction was recorded successfully but the holding's unit count was unchanged — silently producing a stale portfolio view for investment accounts using the currency-as-holding cash-sleeve pattern. **New helper** [`deriveTxWriteWarnings`](src/lib/queries.ts) — pure, no DB access. Single check today: `portfolioHoldingId != null && amount != 0 && quantity == null` → `"quantity not set — holding unit count was not updated"`. Designed as the chokepoint for future advisory checks on transaction writes. **HTTP MCP** ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts)). `record_transaction` adds `warnings` to the top-level success response. `bulk_record_transactions` adds `warnings` to per-row results, attached only when non-empty so the common shape is unchanged for callers that don't read it. `update_transaction` warns only when the user *explicitly bound a holding on this update* without also passing `quantity` — touching unrelated fields (e.g. date) on a previously-bound row doesn't fire, so the agent isn't nagged about every cosmetic edit. **Stdio MCP unchanged.** Stdio `record_transaction`/`bulk_record_transactions` refuse investment-account writes outright and don't expose `portfolioHoldingId`/`quantity` parameters at all, so the warning condition can't trigger there. The helper docstring calls this out. **Backwards-compatible.** `warnings` is an additional optional field on the response JSON; existing callers that don't read it are unaffected. No schema change, no env vars, no cron. No tool count change.
- **`getOrCreateCashHolding` reuses currency-coded cash sleeves before inserting a fresh `Cash` row (2026-04-30, closes [#30](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/30)).** `record_transfer` against an investment account whose cash sleeve was a currency-coded holding (`symbol IS NULL`, `currency = account.currency`, named after the account+currency rather than the literal string `Cash`) was failing: [src/lib/investment-account.ts](src/lib/investment-account.ts) `getOrCreateCashHolding` looked up holdings by `lower(trim(name)) = 'cash'` only, so currency-coded sleeves missed and the function fell through to the insert path. The insert could then fail on stale soft-deleted rows hitting the partial unique index `portfolio_holdings_user_account_lookup_uniq` or on NOT-NULL encrypted columns when the DEK was unavailable, rolling the whole transfer back with a generic error and no actionable hint. **Lookup broadened.** The pre-insert SELECT now matches any holding on `(user_id, account_id)` where `symbol IS NULL AND currency = accounts.currency`. When multiple match, prefers the literally-named `Cash` row (case-insensitive `lower(trim(name)) = 'cash'`) and tie-breaks on lowest `id` for determinism. The account-currency lookup was hoisted above the holding lookup so the broader filter has it available. **Insert path unchanged.** The 23505 (unique_violation) concurrent-writer recovery and the post-insert re-SELECT both use the same broad criteria as the pre-insert lookup, so a race where another writer creates a currency-coded sleeve still returns that sleeve's id without spawning a duplicate. **Effect.** `record_transfer` and any other caller routed through `defaultHoldingForInvestmentAccount` now reuses the existing currency-coded cash sleeve instead of creating a duplicate `Cash` row beside it; holdings count stays flat on repeated cash-only transfers. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron.
- **MCP `bulk_record_transactions`/`record_transaction` no longer silently route weak-substring fuzzy matches to the wrong account (2026-04-30, closes [#29](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/29)).** The fuzzy `account` resolver used by `record_transaction` and `bulk_record_transactions` cascaded through exact → alias → startsWith → includes → reverse-includes; the last two tiers silently picked a vaguely-similar account when the agent passed a name that didn't strongly match anything. There was also no way to filter `search_transactions` by account, which broke dedup workflows against blank-payee bank-imported transfers (the existing filters all key on text the agent doesn't have for those rows). **Schema additions (HTTP + stdio).** `search_transactions` gains `account_id?: number` (FK fast-path mirroring the existing `portfolio_holding_id` filter — raise `limit` accordingly, a year of activity in one account easily exceeds 50). `record_transaction` gains `account_id?: number`; `bulk_record_transactions` gains both a top-level `account_id?: number` (applied to every row that omits its own) and a per-row `account_id?: number` (wins over both name and the top-level fallback). When `account_id` is set the resolver skips fuzzy matching entirely; the `account` name parameter is now optional but at least one of the two must be present. **New `resolveAccountStrict` helper** ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) + [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts)) — same exact/alias/startsWith waterfall as `fuzzyFind`, but substring/reverse-substring hits are only accepted when input and candidate share a whitespace-separated token of length ≥3. Otherwise the row fails with `Account "X" did not match strongly — closest is "Y" (id=N) but no shared whitespace token. Re-call with account_id=N if that's right, or pick another from: …`. Reads still use plain `fuzzyFind` — wrong filters are recoverable, wrong writes aren't. **`resolvedAccount: { id, name }` in every per-row write response** so the agent can verify routing immediately. On `record_transaction` it's at the top level; on `bulk_record_transactions` it's per result entry, including per-row failures (holding/cat/FX errors) once the account has resolved. **Stdio carve-out unchanged.** Stdio MCP still refuses writes to investment accounts (no `portfolioHolding` plumbing on stdio); the new `account_id` resolution runs first and the constraint check fires after, same as before. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron. No tool count change.

### Changed
- **Transfer dialog: filter holdings by `accountId`; hide portfolio block for cash↔cash (2026-04-30, closes [#24](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/24)).** Section E item #10 of the dialog hardening plan. Pure client-side change in [src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx). **Filter switch.** The Transfer dialog's source/destination holding dropdowns now match `holdings.filter((h) => h.accountId === acct.id)` instead of the previous `accountName` string match — `accountName` collisions across accounts could surface holdings from another account, and `getPortfolioHoldings` already projects `accountId` ([src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts)). **Visibility guard.** Computed `eitherIsInvestment = fromAcct?.isInvestment || toAcct?.isInvestment` and short-circuit the violet in-kind / portfolio IIFE with `return null` when false, so cash↔cash transfers no longer render the in-kind toggle, holding dropdowns, or quantity inputs. Cross-currency `receivedAmount` panel is unaffected (separate IIFE). Same-account in-kind rebalance still renders because the account is investment ⇒ `eitherIsInvestment === true`. **Reset on transition.** New effect keyed on `[fromAccountId, toAccountId, accounts]` clears `inKind / holdingName / destHoldingName / quantity / destQuantity` plus `destHoldingTouched` / `destQuantityTouched` only on the actual `true → false` transition, so a user who briefly swaps to a cash account and back doesn't lose in-flight values until they actually commit to a cash-only pair. **Why this is safe at the write layer.** Backend `createTransferPair` already auto-defaults a per-account Cash holding for pure-cash legs landing on an investment account ([src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts), `defaultHoldingForInvestmentAccount`), so hiding the UI block does not regress the application-layer "every tx in an investment account references a portfolio_holdings row" constraint documented in CLAUDE.md. **Type extensions.** Local `Account` type gained `isInvestment?: boolean` (already on the `/api/accounts` wire shape via `getAccounts`'s `select()`-all). `Holding.accountId` was already in the type (added in #23). **Out of scope.** Section C mandatory enforcement, Section B/D dialog dedup, and the parallel Add Transaction filter cleanup. Code-only; no schema migration, no API change, no env vars, no cron.

### Fixed
- **Add Transaction holding dropdown empty for Phase-3 users; now decrypts + filters by account (2026-04-29, closes [#23](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/23)).** Two fixes in one issue. **Root cause.** `GET /api/portfolio` ([src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts)) returned `getPortfolioHoldings()` rows verbatim — no `decryptNamedRows()` pass. Every other portfolio read path (`/api/portfolio/overview`, `/api/accounts`) decrypts after the query. For users past Stream D Phase 3 cutover (`users.plaintext_nulled_at` set), `portfolio_holdings.name`/`symbol` are NULL on disk, so the response carried `{name: null, symbol: null}` rows. The Add Transaction "Portfolio Holding" Combobox then rendered items with empty values, which `@base-ui/react` collapses — picker looked completely empty even though the same holdings showed up correctly on `/portfolio` (which decrypts via `/api/portfolio/overview`). **API fix.** Wrapped the result in `decryptNamedRows(rows, auth.context.dek, { nameCt: "name", symbolCt: "symbol", accountNameCt: "accountName" })`, mirroring [/api/portfolio/overview](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts) and [/api/accounts](src/app/api/accounts/route.ts). Read-path soft-fallback honoured: a user without a cached DEK falls through to plaintext (legacy / Pathfinder-style users), unchanged. **Dialog fix.** [src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx) — `Holding` type extended with `accountId: number | null` (the API already returned it through the JOIN, the type just didn't claim it). The Add Transaction "Portfolio Holding" Combobox now restricts options to holdings whose `accountId` matches the currently-selected account, mirroring the existing Transfer-mode filtering pattern. The fallback "unknown holding" line still queries the full `holdings` array so existing-but-out-of-account selections survive on edit (otherwise opening an old row whose holding moved would render the value as a blank pill). The Account-Combobox `onValueChange` now clears `form.portfolioHoldingId` when the previously-selected holding is not in the new account, preventing the silent 23503 FK error on submit (the per-account Cash holding for the old account is invalid for the new one). **Test.** [tests/api/portfolio.test.ts](tests/api/portfolio.test.ts) gains a Phase-3 case: rows with NULL plaintext + populated `nameCt`/`symbolCt`/`accountNameCt`. The mocked auth context now carries a real 32-byte DEK; the test uses `encryptField` to mint ciphertext that the route round-trips through `decryptNamedRows`, then asserts the response shows the decrypted values. The original test would not have caught this — it only mocked `getPortfolioHoldings` and asserted equality of plaintext rows. **Forward-compat note.** When the M2M migration (Section F/G #15) lands, `accountId` becomes `accounts: number[]`; the dialog filter changes from `String(h.accountId) === form.accountId` to `h.accounts.map(String).includes(form.accountId)` — flagged inline so the migration PR catches it. **Out of scope.** Mandatory enforcement (#6), type-ahead overhaul (#4/#5), Transfer-dialog filtering (#9) — handled in their own issues. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron.

### Changed
- **Type-ahead Combobox + per-user dropdown ordering (2026-04-29, closes [#21](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/21)).** Adds a shared `<Combobox>` primitive on top of `@base-ui/react/combobox` so user-data-backed dropdowns (categories, accounts, portfolio holdings, currencies) get type-ahead filtering for free, and lays the data plumbing for a forthcoming Section G "setup pages" UI to let users pin frequently-used items to the top of every list. **New primitive** [src/components/ui/combobox.tsx](src/components/ui/combobox.tsx) wraps base-ui's combobox with the same Tailwind tokens as `<Select>` so the visual design is unchanged; both a high-level `<Combobox items={…} />` (drop-in for `<Select>` migrations) and the lower-level pieces (`ComboboxRoot`, `ComboboxTrigger`, `ComboboxItem`, …) are exported. Filtering uses base-ui's built-in collator against the `label` field of each item; the CLAUDE.md "Select Components" rule is honoured (the wrapper coerces `null` to `""` before invoking the caller's `onValueChange`). **Per-user ordering API** — new `GET`/`PUT /api/settings/dropdown-order` ([route.ts](src/app/api/settings/dropdown-order/route.ts)) stores a JSON map keyed `dropdown_order` in the existing `settings` table. Shape: `{ version: 1, lists: { category, account, holding, currency } }`. Identifiers are opaque (numeric IDs for accounts/holdings/categories, ISO codes for currencies) — no display names enter the row, so the value is unencrypted and `requireAuth` is sufficient. Zod-validated; max 5000 entries per list. No schema migration required (rides the existing `settings` table). **Helpers** — [src/lib/dropdown-order.ts](src/lib/dropdown-order.ts) provides the pure `sortByUserOrder` function (pinned-first by saved index, unpinned in fallback comparator order) plus `parseDropdownOrder` for safe JSON ingest. [src/components/dropdown-order-provider.tsx](src/components/dropdown-order-provider.tsx) fetches the saved order once on mount and exposes a `useDropdownOrder(kind)` hook that returns a memoized sorter; mounted inside `(app)/layout.tsx` next to `<CurrencyProvider>`. **Phase-4 migrations** — replaced `<Select>` with `<Combobox>` at the user-data-backed call sites: budgets (3 categories), goals (account), loans (account), subscriptions (category + account + currency), accounts (currency on add + edit), settings (rule `assignCategoryId`), fx-overrides (currency), transactions split-dialog (category + account), and transactions/page.tsx (add-transaction account/category, advanced holding picker, split-row category, transfer source/destination accounts, filter account/category, bulk-edit category/account). Short fixed enums (transaction type I/E/T/R, frequency, account group/type, date presets) stay on `<Select>`. The transfer dialog's source/destination *holding* pickers retain their custom-sentinel logic on `<Select>` for now and remain a Phase-4 follow-up. **Privacy note** — no category HMAC `name_lookup` is shipped yet; category ordering keys to numeric IDs for now. The issue explicitly OKs this fallback; a future Section G UI may switch to HMACs without an API change because the Zod schema accepts both `string` and `number` entries. **Ops impact** — code-only; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron. First load returns an empty `lists: {}`, so every dropdown falls through to alphabetical fallback — exactly today's behaviour. Users opt in by visiting the (forthcoming) Section G setup page.
- **Restore Category column + expand /transactions column customization (2026-04-29, closes [#20](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/20)).** Two related fixes to the `/transactions` table — (a) the Category badge has been silently empty for every Stream-D Phase-3 user since cutover, and the Reports tabs collapsed every category into one "Uncategorized" bucket for the same reason; (b) the column-prefs UX only had a single `localStorage`-backed Portfolio toggle. **Backend — restore decrypted display names through the JOIN.** [`getTransactions()`](src/lib/queries.ts) now selects `categories.name_ct`, `accounts.name_ct`, `accounts.alias_ct`, `portfolio_holdings.symbol`, and `portfolio_holdings.symbol_ct`. `/api/transactions` GET ([route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/route.ts)) and `/api/transactions/linked` ([linked/route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/linked/route.ts)) decrypt those fields with `decryptName()` (auth-tag failure ladder) and strip the `*_ct` columns before serializing. Without this, every Phase-3-NULL'd row returned `categoryName: null` and the Category cell only showed `<Link2 />` / split badges. **Backend — Reports group on stable id.** [/api/reports](src/app/api/reports/route.ts) (income-statement + tax-summary), [/api/reports/trends](src/app/api/reports/trends/route.ts), and [/api/reports/yoy](src/app/api/reports/yoy/route.ts) now `GROUP BY categories.id` (previously the now-NULL `categories.name`) and resolve `name_ct` per group after the SQL aggregation. Behavior change worth flagging: rows with duplicate category names that previously collided now report separately. Balance-sheet's account name decrypts via the same ladder. **New endpoint `/api/settings/tx-columns`** ([route.ts](src/app/api/settings/tx-columns/route.ts)) — GET/PUT round-trips a JSON blob through the existing `settings(key, user_id)` table under `key='tx_table_columns'`. Zod-validated; defaults merged on read so adding a new column id later doesn't force a migration. No schema change required. **Frontend** ([transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx)) — replaced the `TxColPrefs = { portfolio: boolean }` localStorage hook with a `ColumnPref[]` (visibility + order) backed by the new endpoint. The Columns dropdown lists every toggleable column + a "Reset to default" action. Native HTML5 `draggable` on `<TableHead>` reorders columns; order persists via the same hook (400ms debounce). Legacy `localStorage["pf-tx-cols-v1"]` Portfolio toggle migrates on first load and is then cleared. The page renders columns from a single `columnPrefs.filter(visible).map(...)` pass instead of the previous hard-coded `TableHead` / `TableCell` blocks. **Out of scope.** `accountDescription` toggle dropped — the column doesn't exist on `accounts` and a schema add is out of bounds for a UI/docs-scoped issue. Drag UX is mouse-only; the checkbox toggles in the dropdown remain a keyboard-accessible alternative for visibility (order remains drag-only).
- **`transactions.portfolio_holding` retired end-to-end (Phase 5 + 6, 2026-04-29, [#18](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/pull/18)).** The legacy encrypted text column on `transactions` is gone; the integer FK `transactions.portfolio_holding_id → portfolio_holdings.id` (introduced 2026-04-26 in [#7](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/pull/7)) is now the sole source of truth, and the holding name is sourced via JOIN to `portfolio_holdings`. **Gate fix (root cause).** The `withoutFk` count on prod was permanently stuck at 4 — all 4 rows belonged to the demo user. [scripts/seed-demo.ts](scripts/seed-demo.ts) inserted investment transactions BEFORE the matching `portfolio_holdings` rows, so the seed never set the FK. The lazy-on-login backfill caught them when the demo user logged in, but the nightly reseed wiped the data and re-inserted 4 broken rows, so `withoutFk ≥ 4` between reset and next demo login. Fix: insert holdings first, capture their ids, bind `portfolio_holding_id` directly. After the seed-script fix + a manual reseed, gate dropped to 0. **Phase 5 — code cutover ([a80dcbb](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/a80dcbb)):** migration [scripts/migrate-tx-portfolio-holding-phase5-null.sql](scripts/migrate-tx-portfolio-holding-phase5-null.sql) NULLed the legacy column on every backfilled row (1200 rows on prod). Dropped `"portfolioHolding"` from `TX_ENCRYPTED_FIELDS`. Deleted orphan-fallback decrypt loops in `/api/portfolio/overview`, `holdings-value.ts`, and `mcp-server` `aggregateHoldings()` — the portfolio aggregator no longer needs the DEK. POST/PUT `/api/transactions` `delete data.portfolioHolding` before persisting; GET resolves `portfolioHolding` (name) via the Stream D `name_lookup` HMAC and filters SQL-side. **Phase 6 — DROP COLUMN ([d28258e](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/d28258e)):** migration [scripts/migrate-tx-portfolio-holding-phase6-drop-column.sql](scripts/migrate-tx-portfolio-holding-phase6-drop-column.sql) with a defensive pre-check that refuses to run if any non-NULL plaintext remains. `getTransactions` JOINs `portfolio_holdings` and surfaces `{ portfolioHoldingId, portfolioHoldingName, portfolioHoldingNameCt }`; the `/api/transactions` GET handler decrypts `name_ct` (with plaintext name fallback) and presents it to the UI as `portfolioHolding` so the client contract is unchanged. Schema-pg.ts removes the column from the Drizzle definition. Import pipeline stops encrypting the column; the in-memory `portfolioHolding` field stays on the insertable shape (resolver uses it) and is stripped before `db.insert` via destructuring. **Retired (deleted):** `src/lib/crypto/portfolio-holding-fk-backfill.ts`, `src/app/api/admin/portfolio-holding-fk-progress/route.ts`. Removed the `enqueuePortfolioHoldingFkBackfill` call sites in `auth/login` + `auth/mfa/verify`. **Follow-up fix (post-merge).** Caught after the merge: `analyze_holding` and `get_investment_insights` in both HTTP MCP ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts)) and stdio MCP ([mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts)) still SELECTed and grouped by the dropped column. They were not on the original Phase 5 hit-list (only the three "orphan-fallback" sites were called out). Switched all of them to FK + JOIN aggregation. Without this fix, every MCP `analyze_holding` / `get_investment_insights` invocation on prod would have raised `column "portfolio_holding" does not exist`. **Order of operations.** Phase 5 was migration-first then code (NULLing is backwards-compatible — old code reads NULL gracefully). Phase 6 was code-first then migration (DROP COLUMN is backwards-incompatible — old code SELECTs the column). [PHASE5_PORTFOLIO_HOLDING_FK.md](../PHASE5_PORTFOLIO_HOLDING_FK.md) drove the work; marked complete.
- **Stream D Phase 3: per-user lazy plaintext NULL on login (2026-04-29, closes [#7](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/7)).** Replaces the eager all-or-nothing [`scripts/migrate-stream-d-phase3-null.sql`](scripts/migrate-stream-d-phase3-null.sql) (applied to prod 2026-04-24, blocked on staging + dev because demo's lazy backfill never finished and the strict pre-check rolled back the whole transaction) with a per-user lazy variant that runs on each successful login. Three gates before NULLing a user's plaintext name columns: (1) `users.plaintext_nulled_at IS NULL`, (2) the user's own Stream D backfill is complete (no plaintext-but-no-ct rows across the six Stream D tables), (3) a sample row's `name_ct` actually decrypts with the cached DEK. Stragglers and DEK-mismatch users keep their dual-write fallback indefinitely; healthy users get Phase 3'd one-by-one. Doubles as the diagnostic for the unresolved pathfinder DEK-mismatch — the `[phase3-null] user=… table=… sample decrypt failed` warn names the user, the table, and the underlying error, replacing the planned `PF_CRYPTO_DEBUG=1` fingerprint deploy. New helper [src/lib/crypto/stream-d-phase3-null.ts](src/lib/crypto/stream-d-phase3-null.ts) exposes `nullPlaintextIfReady(userId, dek)` (atomic single-transaction NULL across the six tables, sets `users.plaintext_nulled_at`) and `enqueuePhase3NullIfReady` (fire-and-forget login wrapper that never blocks login). Wired one line each into [src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts](src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts) + [src/app/api/auth/mfa/verify/route.ts](src/app/api/auth/mfa/verify/route.ts) right after the existing `enqueueStreamDBackfill` call. Schema migration [scripts/migrate-stream-d-phase3-per-user.sql](scripts/migrate-stream-d-phase3-per-user.sql) drops NOT NULL on the six plaintext name columns (idempotent — prod's eager migration already did the same) and adds `users.plaintext_nulled_at text`. **Apply per env BEFORE deploying the matching code** ([docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md) playbook). Applied to dev on 2026-04-29; required on staging + prod before this code ships. Drizzle schema intentionally unchanged — helper writes via raw SQL bypassing Drizzle's typing so the six plaintext name columns can stay `.notNull()` and downstream consumers don't have to handle `string | null` cascades. Admin status `GET /api/admin/phase3-null-progress` reports `{ usersTotal, usersDone, usersPending, pendingDetail[] }` with per-user blocking-row counts. Eager migration retired with a SUPERSEDED comment header — kept as the record of prod's 2026-04-24 eager run.
- **`deploy.sh` no longer runs `npm run db:push` (2026-04-29, closes [#5](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/5)).** The step looked like automated schema sync but silently no-op'd on every deploy: `DATABASE_URL` from the systemd unit doesn't survive the `sudo -u $REPO_OWNER` hop in `run_as`, so Drizzle-Kit fell back to its hardcoded `postgres://localhost:5432/pf` default and either failed authentication silently or connected to the wrong DB. Symptom surfaced shipping the investment-account constraint — Deploy Dev reported success, but `accounts.is_investment` wasn't actually added to the dev DB and `/api/dashboard` 500'd until the SQL migration was run by hand. Removed the step in favor of the existing per-env psql playbook in [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md), which is what we already use in practice. Deploy is now strictly "pull + install + build + restart"; the database is the human's responsibility before pushing the matching code. CLAUDE.md gotcha + DEPLOY.md updated to match.
- **Transactions: descriptive account labels in filter + table; optional Portfolio column (2026-04-29, [#10](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/10)).** The Account filter dropdown previously rendered `account.name` verbatim — terse/numeric names like `609` (a credit-card account named after the last 4 digits) read as a stray number with no context. New helper [src/lib/account-label.ts](src/lib/account-label.ts) (`formatAccountLabel({ name, alias, type })`) prepends the account `type` when the display string is ≤ 4 chars or all-digits, so `609` of type `Credit Card` now renders as `Credit Card · 609`. Alias takes precedence over name when set; descriptive names (≥ 5 chars, non-numeric) render unchanged. The same helper is used in the table row's Account cell so the dropdown and the rows stay aligned. `getTransactions()` in [src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts) now surfaces `accountAlias` + `accountType` from the existing `accounts` join — same plaintext-fallback story as `accounts.name` already on this query; Stream-D-correct decryption of `alias_ct` for table-row labelling is a separate follow-up. Also added a "Columns" affordance in the filter bar — a `DropdownMenu` with a "Portfolio" checkbox, persisted to `localStorage` key `pf-tx-cols-v1` and **off by default** so users without investment activity don't see a column of dashes. When toggled on, a Portfolio column renders between Payee and Note showing `t.portfolioHolding || "-"`. `getTransactions()` already selected `portfolioHolding` so no API change. Out of scope (called out in the issue): JOIN-against-`portfolio_holdings`-with-Stream-D-decryption migration, gated on Stream D Phase 5.
- **Edit Transfer dialog now opens for any transfer-shaped pair (2026-04-29, [#8](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/8)).** Previously only clean R↔R pairs (both legs in a `category_type='R'` category) hit the unified Edit Transfer dialog — anything else fell back to the legacy single-leg edit with a "Linked transaction" pill, even when the row was clearly half of a transfer. Dropped the `category_type==='R'` requirement on both sides: client `startEdit()` in [src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx) now opens the unified dialog whenever the row has a `link_id`, exactly one sibling, and a different counterparty account; server `loadTransferPair` in [src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts) accepts the same shape (kept the 2-row + different-accounts structural checks). `updateTransferPair` already leaves `category_id` untouched on save, so the user's renamed category (e.g. `Non-Cash - Transfers`) survives the edit. Stdio MCP path (`loadTransferPairViaSql`) intentionally NOT relaxed — this issue is UI/REST-scoped only. The legacy fallback (3+ leg WP liquidations and same-account conversions) gained a small sky-tinted advisory line above the linked-siblings pills: "This transaction is part of a multi-leg group; legs are edited individually." — so the user understands why category/payee/business-expense fields are showing on a transfer-shaped row.
- **Portfolio allocation charts now value-weighted (2026-04-29, [#6](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/6)).** The two donut charts on `/portfolio` ("By Asset Type" and "By Account") previously sized slices by **holding count** — a $1k ETF position counted the same as a $200k one, so the chart didn't reflect actual exposure. Both charts now key on **market value in display currency**: builders in [src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/portfolio/page.tsx) swap `v.count` → `v.value`, baseline from `summary.totalHoldings` → `summary.totalValueDisplay`, account sort flipped from count to value (legend orders by largest position first). Legend amounts render through `formatCurrency(d.value, displayCurrency)` so they show e.g. `$48,210 (35%)` rather than `21 (35%)`. New `hideZeroAllocations` state (default `true`) drops rows whose value is `<= 0.005` — half-cent boundary keeps anything that visibly rounds to 0% silenced while staying float-safe — fully-sold positions still in the DB no longer add 0% slices. A "Hiding $0" / "Showing all" toggle in each card header is bound to one shared state so clicking either flips both charts. API was already populating `byType[t].value` + `byAccount[a].value` in [src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts) — pure client-side change. `ExposurePieTooltip` unchanged; the rounded percentage it shows now reflects value-weighting.
- **Default theme is now dark for new users (2026-04-28, [#4](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/4)).** Single-line flip in [src/app/layout.tsx](src/app/layout.tsx): `defaultTheme="system"` → `defaultTheme="dark"`. New visitors and fresh signups land in dark mode on first paint instead of mirroring `prefers-color-scheme`, aligning the app with the brand baseline (premium fintech dark palette in [docs/ui-design.md](docs/ui-design.md)) and the marketing-site / product screenshots. Existing users keep whatever they explicitly chose — `next-themes` persists toggles in `localStorage`, so this only affects users with no stored preference. `enableSystem` stayed on so a future 3-state toggle can be added without another provider change. The light/dark `theme-toggle.tsx` is unchanged. No env vars, no schema migration, plain code-only change.

### Fixed
- **Friendly 400 for missing category on transaction create/update (2026-04-28, commits [`72f3a8d`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/72f3a8d) + [`eda999a`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/eda999a)).** `POST /api/transactions` accepted `categoryId: z.number()`, which let the UI's `0` "no selection" sentinel through to the INSERT and surfaced as a 500 on the FK violation. Tightened the Zod schema (`accountId` + `categoryId` require positive ints with friendly messages "Please pick a category" / "Please pick an account"); same for `PUT`. Added a 23503 FK-violation catch in both handlers as defense-in-depth — covers stale UI forms referencing a deleted category / account / holding, returning `code: "fk_violation"` instead of leaking the SQL error. UI form ([src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx)) now refuses the submit with an inline `submitError` ("Pick an account" / "Pick a category" / "Enter an amount") matching the existing transfer-handler validation pattern, so the user sees the issue before the round-trip.

### Added
- **Investment-account constraint — every transaction in a flagged account must reference a portfolio holding (2026-04-28).** New `accounts.is_investment` boolean flag with a per-account opt-in checkbox in the account edit dialog. Once set, every transaction in that account must reference a `portfolio_holdings` row (FK `transactions.portfolio_holding_id`) — trades point at their security; cash legs (deposits, dividends paid as cash, fees, transfers) point at a per-account "Cash" holding using the existing currency-as-holding pattern. Closes the long-standing gap where the portfolio aggregator's orphan-fallback path silently dropped null-FK / null-text rows from cost-basis math, dividend totals, and per-holding performance — even though those rows still affected the cash balance.

  - **Schema** — `ALTER TABLE accounts ADD COLUMN is_investment boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false` ([scripts/migrate-accounts-is-investment.sql](scripts/migrate-accounts-is-investment.sql)). Idempotent. No DB CHECK constraint — enforcement is application-layer (mirrors the four-check transfer-pair pattern in [src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts) since PostgreSQL can't express "NOT NULL only when accounts.is_investment is true" without a trigger). The migration also runs a heuristic backfill (any account with ≥1 `portfolio_holdings` row pre-migration is flagged), ensures a per-account `name='Cash', symbol=NULL, currency=accounts.currency` holding for each flagged account, and points all unattributed null-FK / null-text rows at it. Rows with the legacy plaintext `portfolio_holding` text column populated stay untouched — Phase-4 lazy resolver routes them to their actual holding on next login.

  - **Centralized helper** [src/lib/investment-account.ts](src/lib/investment-account.ts) — `requireHoldingForInvestmentAccount` (strict, throws `InvestmentHoldingRequiredError` for REST + MCP HTTP write tools), `defaultHoldingForInvestmentAccount` (permissive — returns the per-account Cash holding id for import + transfer-pair construction), `getOrCreateCashHolding` (find-or-create using the same plaintext+lookup dual-index pattern as the existing portfolio-holding-resolver, tolerates `dek=null` for stdio MCP), and `backfillInvestmentAccount` (the migration's logic scoped to a single account, called from `PUT /api/accounts` on a false→true toggle).

  - **Call-site coverage** — `createTransaction` / `updateTransaction` ([src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts)), `createTransferPair` + `createTransferPairViaSql` ([src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts), pure-cash legs default to Cash), `executeImport` ([src/lib/import-pipeline.ts](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts), post-resolver Cash-default pass per investment account), MCP HTTP `record_transaction` / `bulk_record_transactions` / `update_transaction` ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts)) take the strict path. The orphan-aggregator path stays in place — staging/dev users still rely on it until Phase 5 cutover.

  - **Stdio MCP** `record_transaction` / `bulk_record_transactions` refuse writes to investment accounts with a pointer to the HTTP MCP at `/mcp` — stdio MCP doesn't expose `portfolioHolding` parameters, so it can't satisfy the constraint. Documented as a self-hosted limitation alongside the existing "stdio writes are plaintext" gotcha. Stdio `record_transfer` is unaffected because it routes through `createTransferPairViaSql` which got the Cash-default treatment.

  - **Account toggle side-effect** — `PUT /api/accounts` with `isInvestment: true` runs the same backfill the SQL migration does (Cash holding ensure + null-FK reassignment) atomically with the flag flip. Backfill failure surfaces a 500 with an actionable error so the user can retry. `true → false` just flips the flag; existing data stays attributed.

  - **Admin queue** `GET /api/admin/investment-orphans` ([src/app/api/admin/investment-orphans/route.ts](src/app/api/admin/investment-orphans/route.ts)) reports rows in investment accounts that still have NULL FK after Phase-4 lazy backfill — typically legacy WP imports where the connector failed to populate even the plaintext text column. Surfaces a count + capped 200-row sample, no payee plaintext through this endpoint.

  - **UI** "Investment account" checkbox added to the account edit dialog at [src/app/(app)/accounts/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/accounts/page.tsx); `AccountBalance` + `getAccountBalances` plumb `isInvestment` through.

  - **Migration playbook** in [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md) under `accounts-is-investment`. **Run the SQL BEFORE deploying the matching code** so the application-layer constraint check has the column to read.

- **MCP holding-id ergonomics — bind transactions to positions by name OR ticker (2026-04-28, commits [`ca0a117`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/ca0a117) + [`f429c6f`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/f429c6f)).** Two follow-ups closing the gap reported in the [2026-04-28 Huron Sale conversation](https://claude.ai/share/a066166f-c5ee-4abe-a01c-25868afafb1c) — Claude recorded a stock sale via the MCP, the `quantity` field made it through (the [`7325448`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/7325448) fix from earlier the same day), but `transactions.portfolio_holding_id` was left NULL so the position never moved. The Edit Transaction dialog rendered `Portfolio Holding: None` for a row with `quantity=-48`. Root cause was upstream of the write tools: `get_portfolio_analysis` and `analyze_holding` never returned the holding's integer id, so an agent had no way to populate the existing `portfolioHoldingId` parameter except by calling `add_portfolio_holding` (which would create a duplicate). The aggregator's `accumulate()` already plumbed `holding_id` through ("first non-null id wins"); the response mappers were dropping it on the floor. Same shape on the stdio twins.

  - **[`ca0a117`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/ca0a117) — Phase 1 (id surfacing) + Phase 2 (name-string write ergonomics).** `get_portfolio_analysis` now emits `id: <number>` per holding; `analyze_holding` emits `holdingId: <number>` at the top level. Both transports. `analyze_holding`'s HTTP find is tightened to prefer rows whose decrypted `portfolio_holding === holdingName` so payee-only fuzzy matches don't surface another holding's id when the analyzed string was a non-investment cash payee like `"Huron Sale"`. The three HTTP write tools (`record_transaction` / `bulk_record_transactions` / `update_transaction`) accept a new `portfolioHolding: string` parameter alongside the integer `portfolioHoldingId`. New helper `resolvePortfolioHoldingByName` in [register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) — single-shot SELECT, exact case-insensitive match scoped to the resolved account, dual-cohort handling (plaintext `name` + `name_lookup` HMAC for Phase-3 NULL'd rows). Lookup-only — preserves the existing policy that MCP write tools never auto-create holdings (only the import pipeline does). Errors with a candidate list on miss; errors when both `portfolioHolding` and `portfolioHoldingId` are passed and resolve to different ids ("I named X but you bound Y" is worse than rejecting). Stdio write tools unchanged — they still don't bind `portfolio_holding` per the existing carve-out.

  - **[`f429c6f`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/f429c6f) — Phase 2.5: ticker resolution.** Follow-up after verifying Phase 2 on dev exposed an inconsistency: `update_transaction(portfolioHolding: "HURN")` errored "not found" while `"Huron Consulting Group Inc."` worked, even though `get_portfolio_analysis(symbols: ["HURN"])` already matched the ticker via the `symbol` column. Resolver SQL extended to also check `LOWER(symbol)` plaintext + `symbol_lookup` HMAC alongside name/name_lookup — the same `nameLookup(dek, trimmed)` HMAC value matches both columns since the HMAC is over trimmed-lowercase input regardless of source. Candidate-list error reformatted as `"Name (TICKER)"` so the agent sees both identifiers. `analyze_holding(symbol)` HTTP path now JOINs `ph.symbol_ct`, decrypts per-row, and the in-memory filter checks symbol via **exact** case-insensitive equality (substring on a 3-4 char ticker would match "GE" inside "ORANGE" — name + payee retain substring matching for the long-string ergonomics). Stdio twin same shape — `LEFT JOIN portfolio_holdings ph` + `OR LOWER(ph.symbol) = LOWER(?)` in the WHERE. The tool's description always claimed "fuzzy match on name OR symbol"; this commit makes it honest.

  - **End-to-end verified on dev** ([Deploy Dev runs `25076508331` + `25077631853`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/actions)): (a) `get_portfolio_analysis(symbols: ["Huron"])` → response includes `id: 452`. (b) `update_transaction({id: 30204, portfolioHolding: "Huron Consulting Group Inc."})` → resolves to id 452, succeeds. (c) `update_transaction({id: 30204, portfolioHolding: "DoesNotExist"})` → errors with `Candidates (name (ticker)): Huron Consulting Group Inc. (HURN), Fidelity - USD (USD), Fidelity - CAD (CAD).`. (d) `update_transaction({id: 30204, portfolioHoldingId: 999999, portfolioHolding: "Huron Consulting Group Inc."})` → errors `portfolioHolding "..." resolves to id #452, but portfolioHoldingId=999999 disagrees.`. (e) After Phase 2.5: `update_transaction({id: 30204, portfolioHolding: "HURN"})` → succeeds (was: error). (f) `analyze_holding(symbol: "HURN")` → finds Huron, returns `holdingId: 452`, full transaction history (was: "No transactions found").

  - **No schema migrations.** No tool count change. Documented in [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md) "Binding transactions to portfolio holdings".

- **First-class Transfer flow + in-kind / portfolio support (2026-04-28, branch [`feat/transfer-flow`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/tree/feat/transfer-flow) merged via [`5d945a6`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/5d945a6); five commits cf411ba → a81d9a7 → c57fe1b → 2c320fc → 269b381 → c533781).** End-to-end primitive for "move money/shares between two of my accounts." Replaces the prior "two manual transactions, no link" pattern and the "edit one leg, click linked-sibling pointer to the other" UX. Handles same-currency cash transfers, cross-currency cash transfers (with editable "Amount received" override to lock the bank's actual landed amount), in-kind / share-based transfers between brokerage accounts, same-account in-kind rebalances (move shares between two holdings inside one brokerage), and asymmetric in-kind events for stock splits / mergers / share-class conversions.

  - **UI** — new "Transfer" tab in the Add/Edit dialog ([src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx)). On edit of a row that satisfies the four-check rule (link_id non-null + 1 sibling + both type='R' + different accounts), the dialog auto-opens in unified Transfer mode with BOTH legs visible in one window — replacing the legacy "Linked transaction" pointer panel for clean transfer pairs. Non-symmetric multi-leg imports (WP liquidations, etc.) still use the existing linked-siblings panel for navigation. The "In-kind" toggle exposes Source holding + Quantity + Destination holding + Destination quantity. Holdings dropdowns show " · X shares" for every option (including 0 for empty positions). The destination defaults to "Same as source" with a hint about whether it'll bind to an existing dest holding or auto-create — overridable to bind to a different label OR to a brand-new name. Destination quantity defaults to source quantity but accepts a different value with an amber "Asymmetric" hint when they differ.

  - **REST endpoint** `POST/PUT/DELETE /api/transactions/transfer` ([src/app/api/transactions/transfer/route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/transfer/route.ts)) — atomic dual-INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE inside a single `db.transaction()`. Server-generated UUID `link_id` (never accepted from client). Auto-creates a Transfer category (`type='R'`) on first use, mirroring the WP ZIP orchestrator pattern. Tri-state PUT for the in-kind side: omit = leave alone, null = clear, value = (re)bind. Same for `destHoldingName` + `destQuantity`. 409 `fx-currency-needs-override` carries a `side: 'source' | 'destination'` discriminator so the UI can highlight the offending leg.

  - **MCP** — three new tools `record_transfer` / `update_transfer` / `delete_transfer` on both transports (HTTP **78 → 81**, stdio **72 → 75**). HTTP variant gates on session DEK + dual-writes encrypted payee/note/tags via `encryptTxWrite`; stdio variant runs dek-less per existing convention but still uses an atomic single-pg-client transaction (sidesteps the pg-compat `transaction()` helper's footgun where inner `prepare()` calls acquire fresh pool clients). All three reuse the shared helpers in [src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts).

  - **Shared helper** [src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts) — `createTransferPair` / `loadTransferPair` / `updateTransferPair` / `deleteTransferPair` (Drizzle, used by REST + HTTP MCP) plus `*ViaSql` variants (raw `pg.Pool` for stdio MCP). Single source of truth for both transports — same validation, same FX resolution, same atomic write semantics. Source holding lookup is strict (refuses if missing — you can't send shares you don't have); destination resolves via the existing `buildHoldingResolver` ([src/lib/external-import/portfolio-holding-resolver.ts](src/lib/external-import/portfolio-holding-resolver.ts)) which auto-creates with Stream D dual-write. A no-op guard rejects if source AND destination resolve to the SAME holding row (would silently write two cancelling rows).

  - **`/api/transactions/linked` extended** ([src/app/api/transactions/linked/route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/linked/route.ts)) to return `categoryType` + `accountCurrency` + `entered_*` fields + a JOIN on `portfolio_holdings` so the client can run the four-check rule and pre-fill the unified edit view without a second round-trip.
