- **Shared `<HoldingEditForm>` mounted on /portfolio + /settings/investments (2026-05-01, closes [#100](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/100)).** The inline `HoldingEditDialog` from `src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx` is extracted into a single shared component; both `/portfolio` and `/settings/investments` now mount the SAME form, which structurally prevents the two surfaces from drifting apart on field set or submit-payload shape. The previously-empty Investments settings page now hosts a full holding-CRUD list, and both surfaces gain a visible **Add holding** button (the existing `POST /api/portfolio` endpoint had no UI entry point). **New shared component:** [src/components/holdings/holding-edit-form.tsx](src/components/holdings/holding-edit-form.tsx) — `<HoldingEditForm>` with `{ holdingId?, initialHolding?, defaultAccountId?, onSave, onCancel }` props. Owns its own fetch calls to `POST` / `PUT` / `DELETE /api/portfolio`; surfaces only react to the `onSave` callback (refetch their own list). Same field set in both modes; canonical-row Name-disabled UX preserved from PR #77. **New shared schema module:** [src/lib/schemas/holding.ts](src/lib/schemas/holding.ts) — single source of truth for `holdingCreateSchema` + `holdingUpdateSchema` + `isCanonicalHolding()` predicate. Imported by both the form component AND `src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts`. The previous inline `postSchema` / `putSchema` / `isCanonicalHolding` in the API route are deleted; the route now imports from the shared module. **`/portfolio` page:** the inline ~280-line `HoldingEditDialog` is removed and replaced with a Dialog wrapping `<HoldingEditForm>`. New **Add holding** button in the header. Existing pencil-icon entry points keep working unchanged — they now feed `editingHolding` into the same Dialog. **`/settings/investments` page:** the previous 45-line landing page is replaced with a real holding-CRUD page (account-grouped table + Add/Edit dialog). The Holding ↔ Account Map sub-page remains linked at the bottom. **`getPortfolioHoldings` returns `isCrypto`** so `/api/portfolio` payload can drive the form's "Crypto asset" checkbox without an extra round-trip. **Drift test:** [tests/components/holding-edit-form.test.tsx](tests/components/holding-edit-form.test.tsx) asserts the field set rendered in create + edit modes, the canonical-row Name-disabled UX (symbol "VCN.TO"), Cancel callback wiring, and that submit fires `POST /api/portfolio`. **Stream D / encryption:** writes go through `/api/portfolio` so the dual-write of `name` + `name_ct` + `name_lookup` (and `symbol` + `symbol_ct` + `symbol_lookup`) happens server-side via `buildNameFields()`. The form does NOT introduce any direct DB writes. **No schema migration. No MCP tool surface change** (still 83 HTTP / 79 stdio). No backfill required. **Ops impact:** code-only change. Plain `git merge dev` is sufficient — no env vars, no migrations, no cron, no CSP changes. Files: [src/components/holdings/holding-edit-form.tsx](src/components/holdings/holding-edit-form.tsx), [src/lib/schemas/holding.ts](src/lib/schemas/holding.ts), [src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/portfolio/page.tsx), [src/app/(app)/settings/investments/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/settings/investments/page.tsx), [src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts), [src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts), [tests/components/holding-edit-form.test.tsx](tests/components/holding-edit-form.test.tsx).
- **MCP `trace_holding_quantity` diagnostic + `update_portfolio_holding` refuses account moves (2026-05-01, closes [#99](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/99)).** Two related fixes for the holding-quantity-residue investigation. The aggregator-correctness gotcha (`holding_accounts.qty` is a stale cache that no aggregator reads) was already documented when issue #25 / #86 landed; this issue closes the loop on the *update path* that was creating the stale rows in the first place, and adds a diagnostic tool so the next "the brokerage statement says 79 but Finlynq says 86" investigation doesn't need ad-hoc SQL. **New read tool `trace_holding_quantity` (HTTP + stdio).** Given `symbol` or `holdingId`, returns per-transaction quantity contributions for a single holding with a running sum, plus a `perAccount` rollup. JOINs through `holding_accounts` (issue #25) so the rows match exactly what the four portfolio aggregators see. Rows whose `(holding_id, account_id)` pair is missing from `holding_accounts` are OMITTED from `legs` but counted in `unjoinedTransactionCount` — surfacing the invisibility-to-aggregators gap that prior tools couldn't see. Same `holdingId`-disambiguation pattern as `analyze_holding`: when only `symbol` is given and it spans multiple distinct holdings, the response surfaces an `ambiguous` candidate list with `{holdingId, name, symbol, account}` for each candidate, and the caller must re-call with `holdingId`. Stdio reads plaintext (no DEK in that transport, per the existing carve-out). Read-only; no `invalidateUserTxCache`. **`update_portfolio_holding` (HTTP + stdio) refuses the `account` parameter.** Both transports now return a clean error pointing the caller to `record_transfer` (in-kind) for share moves, or `update_transaction` to re-attribute individual rows. Prior behavior updated only `portfolio_holdings.account_id` — leaving (a) a stale `(holding, old_account)` row in `holding_accounts` (issue #25's JOIN grain) and (b) every prior `transactions.account_id` still pointing to the OLD account. The audit-correct path is `record_transfer` for actual share movements; rewriting historical transaction account_ids would destroy the audit trail of where the user actually held the position. Renames + symbol/currency/isCrypto/note edits remain unchanged. **Tool count: 82 → 83 HTTP, 78 → 79 stdio.** `mcp-guide`, `api-docs` page, `finlynq_help` listings (HTTP + stdio), and [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md) all updated. **One-off stale-data cleanup** (recommended after deploy, NOT included in deploy automation): `DELETE FROM holding_accounts ha WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM transactions t WHERE t.user_id=ha.user_id AND t.portfolio_holding_id=ha.holding_id AND t.account_id=ha.account_id);` — safe (cascade only drops cached aggregates, no transactions touched). Operator-applied per env. **Out of scope.** Phase 1 of issue #99's plan (live-data investigation against prod data) is still a manual operator step — `trace_holding_quantity` is the new tool that makes it tractable. The bulk-rewrite-history alternative was rejected (would destroy audit trails). The cached `holding_accounts.qty` / `cost_basis` columns remain unwritten by any post-INSERT path; the gotcha already documented in CLAUDE.md stands. **Ops impact.** No schema migration. The `account` parameter on `update_portfolio_holding` is technically a breaking API change, but the operation it disabled was producing silently-wrong state — the new error makes the failure mode explicit. AI agents that previously called `update_portfolio_holding({account: ...})` will get a clean error pointing them to `record_transfer`. Files: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts), [src/app/(app)/mcp-guide/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/mcp-guide/page.tsx), [src/app/(app)/api-docs/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/api-docs/page.tsx), [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md).

### Changed
- **MCP `bulk_update`: structured `unappliedChanges` + `sampleAfter.category` re-hydration (2026-05-01, closes [#93](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/93)).** `preview_bulk_update` / `execute_bulk_update` (HTTP + stdio) now surface every requested change that failed to resolve as `{ field: string, requestedValue: unknown, reason: string }` (was `{ key, reason }`). `field` replaces `key`; `requestedValue` carries the value the caller passed (e.g. `"Credit Interesst"` when category lookup failed) so callers don't have to regex the `reason` string to recover what they sent. Identical shape on HTTP ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts)) and stdio ([mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts)); identical on both `preview_*` and `execute_*`. Tool descriptions and the matching `finlynq_help` snippets updated symmetrically. **`sampleAfter.category` re-hydration.** When `changes.category` (name) resolves successfully, the preview's `sampleAfter.category` (the joined display-name string) now reflects the resolved category instead of the row's old category. Previously `sampleAfter.category_id` flipped to the new id but the name didn't change, so a successful update looked like a no-op. The resolver writes the resolved name onto a new preview-only `ResolvedChanges.category_name` field and `applyChangesToRow` reads it back when building `sampleAfter`. `category_name` is NOT a DB column and `commitBulkUpdate` doesn't write it — purely preview metadata. The "all changes failed to resolve" abort guard in `execute_bulk_update` filters `category_name` out of the resolved-key count, so the guard still fires correctly when only an unresolvable name was passed. **Caller-visible breaking change.** Any external client that parsed `unappliedChanges[].key` will need to read `.field`. **No DB schema change. No new write paths.** Confirmation-token derivation (still signs the user-supplied `changes`, not the resolved form), audit-trio (`updated_at` / `source`), and `invalidateUserTxCache` invariants are unchanged. **Stdio surface narrower by design (unchanged).** Stdio still rejects `portfolioHolding` / `portfolioHoldingId` / `quantity` at strict-mode validation; the new shape only adds `requestedValue` to the keys that branch on stdio (just `category`). Tool surface unchanged at 82 HTTP / 78 stdio. Files: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts), [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md).

### Added
- **MCP `bulk_record_transactions`: optional `idempotencyKey` for retry-safe batch inserts (2026-05-01, closes [#98](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/98)).** `bulk_record_transactions` (HTTP + stdio) now accepts an optional top-level `idempotencyKey: UUID v4`. First call with `(user, key)` inserts the rows AND stashes the response JSON in a new `mcp_idempotency_keys` table; any retry within 72h returns the stored response verbatim with `replayed: true` appended — no INSERTs into `transactions`, no `invalidateUserTxCache(userId)`, no FX prefetch. Makes large batch imports (e.g. 50-99 row IBKR statements) safe to retry on network timeouts without creating duplicates. **New schema table** ([scripts/migrate-mcp-idempotency.sql](scripts/migrate-mcp-idempotency.sql)): `mcp_idempotency_keys (id SERIAL, user_id TEXT, key UUID, tool_name TEXT, response_json JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW())` + `UNIQUE (user_id, key)` + `(created_at)` btree index. The unique constraint spans the **pair**, not the key alone — Alice's UUID K and Bob's UUID K are independent batches, never collide. Per-env step in [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md). Drizzle binding added at [src/db/schema-pg.ts](src/db/schema-pg.ts) (`mcpIdempotencyKeys`). **Lookup** runs BEFORE any account/category/holdings prefetch in both transports so a hit returns immediately, filtering `(user_id, key, tool_name='bulk_record_transactions', created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '72 hours')`. Stdio uses the pg-compat shim with `?::uuid` casting. Lookup failures are caught and logged — they fall through to the live write path rather than 500'ing. **Storage** runs after the response is built; uses `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (user_id, key) DO NOTHING` to close the concurrent-retry race. Skipped on `dryRun: true` (preview must never block a future real submit) and on `ok === 0` (entire batch failed — caller should retry, not replay). **Stream D / encryption contract preserved.** `response_json` is **redacted** before persisting: per-row `message` becomes `"row #${index}: redacted on replay"` (originally contained the plaintext payee), `resolvedAccount.name` / `resolvedCategory.name` become `"[redacted]"`. Ids (`transactionId`, `resolvedAccount.id`, `resolvedCategory.id`, `resolvedHolding.id`, `tradeLinkId`, `dryRun`, `wouldBeId`, `warnings`) are preserved — those are the load-bearing identifiers for the caller. Callers needing plaintext on replay must `search_transactions` with the returned ids. Same redaction policy on both transports so the at-rest blob contract doesn't fork (stdio writes are plaintext per the load-bearing rule, but the response_json blob still gets the same redaction). **Audit trio (issue [#28](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/28)) preserved.** Replay returns the original result and does NOT touch `transactions` at all — `source='mcp_http'` (or `'mcp_stdio'`) and the original `created_at` / `updated_at` are preserved by construction. Replay is not a "refresh". **Daily cleanup cron** at [src/lib/cron/sweep-mcp-idempotency.ts](src/lib/cron/sweep-mcp-idempotency.ts), wired in [instrumentation.ts](instrumentation.ts) alongside the existing settle-future-fx + email-cleanup + mcp-upload-cleanup timers. Deletes rows older than 72h via `DELETE WHERE created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '72 hours'`. The replay lookup also filters on freshness so the cron is purely a table-growth bound. **Concurrent-retry residual.** Two parallel calls with the same key + miss could both INSERT transaction rows on the first racing pair before either gets to write the idempotency row; the second's `mcp_idempotency_keys` INSERT is silently dropped. That residual is no worse than calling without an idempotency key — strictly better on every subsequent retry. The simpler `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` is preferred over wrapping the entire bulk batch in a `SERIALIZABLE` transaction (the per-row INSERTs already happen outside any transaction in the current writer; widening that scope is a larger refactor than the residual risk warrants). **Tool surface unchanged at 82 HTTP / 78 stdio** — one HTTP + one stdio write tool gained an optional parameter; no new tools. Tool descriptions updated symmetrically on both transports. **Out of scope.** Idempotency for `record_transaction`, `update_transaction`, `delete_transaction`, `record_trade`, `record_transfer` — lower volume / lower retry risk; revisit when callers actually exercise the new parameter on `bulk_record_transactions`. Content-hash-based dedup — the key is intentionally caller-supplied, never derived from the payload. **Ops impact.** Schema migration `migrate-mcp-idempotency.sql` MUST run per env BEFORE the matching code deploy (per CLAUDE.md "deploy.sh does NOT run schema migrations"). Idempotent (`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` / `CREATE [UNIQUE] INDEX IF NOT EXISTS`). Files: [scripts/migrate-mcp-idempotency.sql](scripts/migrate-mcp-idempotency.sql), [src/db/schema-pg.ts](src/db/schema-pg.ts), [src/lib/cron/sweep-mcp-idempotency.ts](src/lib/cron/sweep-mcp-idempotency.ts), [instrumentation.ts](instrumentation.ts), [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts), [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md), [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md).
- **MCP `bulk_record_transactions`: post-insert duplicate hints (2026-05-01, closes [#90](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/90)).** After a successful (non-`dryRun`) `bulk_record_transactions` batch the response now carries a top-level `possibleDuplicates: PossibleDup[]` field flagging newly-inserted rows that look suspiciously like an existing row in the same account — same direction, amount within 5%, dates within 7 days. **Hints only — never blocks the insert.** Always present on success; empty array when nothing matches; never `null`. Skipped on `dryRun: true` (nothing inserted, nothing to scan against). **Match criteria, per inserted row:** same `account_id`, same direction (sign of amount; zero-amount rows skipped because direction is undefined for RSU vests / in-kind transfers), `|new.amount - existing.amount| / max(|new.amount|, |existing.amount|) <= 0.05`, `|new.date - existing.date| <= 7 days`, `existing.id != newId`. **Score** = `1 - (ratio/0.05) * 0.5 - (deltaDays/7) * 0.5` (range [0, 1], higher = closer match). The hint engine doesn't dedupe its own output — same `import_hash` pairs still surface so callers can inspect both legs. **HTTP path** ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts)) collects every committed row via `RETURNING id`, then runs ONE indexed pool query bounded by `[globalMinDate-7d, globalMaxDate+7d]` across every account that received a row, then filters per-row in JS through the shared scoring helper. Existing-row payees are decrypted via `tryDecryptField(dek, payee_raw, "transactions.payee") ?? rawPayee` per CLAUDE.md "tryDecryptField MUST return null on auth-tag failure" — auth-tag-failed entries decode to empty string rather than the v1: ciphertext, preserving the `?? plaintext` fallback at every call site. **Stdio path** ([mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts)) follows the same shape but reads payees as plaintext (stdio writes are plaintext per the existing stdio carve-out). Stdio's prepared INSERT statement gains `RETURNING id` and reads `runRes.lastInsertRowid` — the pg-compat shim's `run()` already plumbs `RETURNING id` rows to `lastInsertRowid` so the change is a one-line addition. **Shared helper** at [src/lib/mcp/duplicate-hints.ts](src/lib/mcp/duplicate-hints.ts) — `scanForPossibleDuplicates(committed, candidates)` returns `PossibleDup[]`; `dateBoundsForScan(committed)` computes the union date window. Pure functions, no DB / encryption / I/O dependencies. Both transports build their own candidate pool (encrypted vs plaintext payee handling differs) and feed the shared scoring helper — duplication is explicit and labelled with a "MIRROR" comment so future drift is obvious. **Performance.** Worst case: a 100-row batch into one account → one `SELECT ... WHERE user_id = ? AND account_id = ANY(...) AND date BETWEEN ?-7d AND ?+7d AND id <> ALL(<newIds>)`, indexed on `(user_id, account_id, date)`, fine. Across many accounts in one batch the `ANY($accountIds)` form keeps it to one query. **Scan must never fail the response** — wrapped in try/catch; on error it logs `[bulk_record_transactions] duplicate-hint scan failed:` and returns an empty `possibleDuplicates: []` so the caller still sees `imported` / `failed` / `results`. **`invalidateUserTxCache(userId)` ordering preserved** — invalidation still fires before the scan so a scan exception can't leave the cache stale. **Audit trio (`source` / `created_at` / `updated_at`) untouched** — purely additive read on the existing rows; no UPDATE, no `source` mutation. **Tool surface unchanged at 82 HTTP / 78 stdio** — purely additive on the response shape; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no MCP tool count change. Tool descriptions updated symmetrically on both transports. Files: [src/lib/mcp/duplicate-hints.ts](src/lib/mcp/duplicate-hints.ts), [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts), [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md).

### Fixed
- **Reconcile page: template selector + ColumnMappingDialog for non-canonical CSVs (2026-05-01, closes [#97](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/97)).** The dedicated `/import/reconcile` page returned a generic "no transactions detected" red banner for any CSV whose headers didn't match the canonical `Date,Account,Amount,Payee` shape (IBKR Activity statements, brokerage exports with custom headers, anything `parseCsvWithFallback` couldn't auto-match at >=80% header overlap). The API was already returning the structured 422 `csv-needs-mapping` payload (`headers` / `sampleRows` / `suggestedMapping`) but the page just threw `json.error` as a generic Error → red banner. **Fix is UI-only**, in two parts. **(1) Template selector.** `ReconcileUploadCard` ([src/components/reconcile/upload-card.tsx](src/components/reconcile/upload-card.tsx)) now exposes a "CSV template (optional — for non-standard formats like IBKR)" `<Combobox>` populated from `GET /api/import/templates`, with an "— Auto-detect —" empty option (`onValueChange` coerced via `?? ""` per CLAUDE.md "Select Components" rule). The selected `templateId` is appended to the FormData POST whenever non-empty; auto-detect on canonical-header CSVs is unchanged. **(2) Column-mapping dialog on 422.** `ReconcilePage` ([src/app/(app)/import/reconcile/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/import/reconcile/page.tsx)) now intercepts a 422 with body shape `{ type: "csv-needs-mapping", headers, sampleRows, suggestedMapping, fileName }` and opens the same `ColumnMappingDialog` the `/import` page uses (imported from `@/app/(app)/import/components/column-mapping-dialog`). After the user confirms a mapping, the page POSTs `/api/import/templates` to persist it, then re-fires the reconcile preview with the saved `templateId` so `parseCsvWithFallback` (template path) → `classifyForReconcile` runs end-to-end and the preview table shows NEW / EXISTING / PROBABLE_DUPLICATE classification. The page deliberately does NOT route through `/api/import/csv-map` (which only returns `previewImport` shape — no reconcile classification), so the post-mapping flow goes straight back through `/api/import/reconcile/preview` with the new `templateId`. Cancelling the mapping dialog drops the stashed `pendingFile` so a fresh upload doesn't inherit it. **No API changes.** The reconcile preview route ([src/app/api/import/reconcile/preview/route.ts](src/app/api/import/reconcile/preview/route.ts)) already read `templateId` from FormData and the 422 body already carried the dialog payload; only the client UI needed to catch up (the route's outdated comment "Until that dialog is wired in" is now obsolete but left in place to avoid scope creep on this issue). **Existing reconcile invariants preserved.** The post-mapping commit path runs through `/api/import/reconcile/commit` which still: stamps `transactions.source = 'import'` per the audit-trio invariant (issue #28); defaults a Cash holding for any row landing on an `accounts.is_investment = true` account per the investment-account holding-FK invariant (8 callsites); honours `import_hash` SHA-256 dedup over plaintext payee (decrypts before hashing) and includes archived accounts in dedup per [docs/import-connectors.md](docs/import-connectors.md) §7. **Out of scope.** PDF / Excel reconcile flows (intentionally unsupported by design — existing error directs users to `/import`); OFX/QFX mapping (no column mapping needed — direct field mapping); template *creation* outside the dialog (the import page owns `SaveTemplateDialog` separately; the mapping-dialog auto-save covers the reconcile case); IBKR-specific multi-section CSV quirks of `parseCsvWithFallback` itself (handled in PR #72). **Tool surface unchanged at 82 HTTP / 78 stdio.** No schema migration, no env vars, no cron. Files: [src/components/reconcile/upload-card.tsx](src/components/reconcile/upload-card.tsx), [src/app/(app)/import/reconcile/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/import/reconcile/page.tsx).
- **Multi-currency trade pair: cash-leg cost basis substitution + new `tradeGroupKey` / `tradeLinkId` MCP HTTP params (2026-05-01, closes [#96](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/96)).** For multi-currency stock trades booked as a *pair* of separate transactions (cash-out leg + stock-in leg, each with its own `enteredAmount` / `enteredCurrency`), the four cost-basis aggregators now pull the **cash leg's** `enteredAmount` (the broker's actual settlement at IBKR's FX rate) instead of the stock leg's amount (Finlynq's live FX rate that under-counts the broker's spread). Concrete user-visible bug: BNO bought at USD 375.90 / CAD 514.12 and sold at USD 482.998 / CAD 661.27 used to show realized gain ≈ $2 because the cost basis used was the stock leg's CAD 509.76 (server-FX'd), not the cash leg's CAD 514.12; expected ~CAD $147. **New schema column [scripts/migrate-trade-link-id.sql](scripts/migrate-trade-link-id.sql)**: `transactions.trade_link_id text` (nullable) + partial index `(user_id, trade_link_id) WHERE trade_link_id IS NOT NULL`. Distinct from `link_id` which the four-check transfer-pair rule reserves for `record_transfer` siblings (CLAUDE.md). Server-generated UUID only — never accepted as a client-supplied id (mirrors the `link_id = randomUUID()` pattern in [src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts)). Per-env step in [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md). **Schema:** `transactions.tradeLinkId` added in [src/db/schema-pg.ts](src/db/schema-pg.ts). **All four aggregators** updated with a LEFT JOIN to the cash-leg sibling on `(same user, same trade_link_id, qty=0 or NULL, different id)`. When matched on a buy row (qty>0), they substitute `cash.amount` (or for the per-currency-bucket REST path, `cash.entered_amount` + `cash.entered_currency`) for the stock leg's value. Legacy / unpaired rows fall back to the stock leg's own amount unchanged. Files: [src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts), [src/lib/holdings-value.ts](src/lib/holdings-value.ts), [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) (HTTP `aggregateHoldings` + `analyze_holding`), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts) (stdio `get_portfolio_performance` + `analyze_holding` + `get_investment_insights` rebalancing). **MCP HTTP `bulk_record_transactions`** accepts a new optional per-row `tradeGroupKey` (any string label, e.g. "BNO-buy-2025") — rows in the same batch sharing a key get the same server-minted UUID stamped into `trade_link_id`. Pre-pass validation requires exactly two rows per group with one stock leg (qty>0) and one cash leg (qty=0 or omitted); group-validation errors fail just the affected rows, not the whole batch. Per-row response surfaces the minted `tradeLinkId`. **MCP HTTP `record_transaction`** accepts a new optional `tradeLinkId` for binding a *second* leg to a previously-recorded first leg. Server validates UUID shape, that the UUID exists for this user, and that at most one existing row references it (a trade has exactly two legs). New trades typically use `bulk_record_transactions` with `tradeGroupKey`; the single-row path is for incremental binding. **Stdio MCP read-only:** stdio refuses investment-account writes (CLAUDE.md), so it can't *create* trade pairs but it MUST read them correctly. The stdio aggregators all apply the same cash-leg JOIN. **Tool surface unchanged at 82 HTTP / 78 stdio** — two HTTP write tools gained optional parameters; no new tools. **Out of scope.** B5 (`record_transfer` intra-account same-account support — the long-term clean fix is to make the trade one atomic operation with a `link_id` populated; tracked separately). Backfill of historical multi-currency trades — fix is forward-looking; old data continues to use stock-leg amount until a user re-records the trade with a `tradeGroupKey`. UI changes (the dialog flow doesn't currently expose paired cash-leg recording). **Ops impact.** Schema migration `migrate-trade-link-id.sql` MUST run per env BEFORE the matching code deploy (per CLAUDE.md "deploy.sh does NOT run schema migrations"). Idempotent (`ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` / `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS`). Docs: [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md) "Cost basis rules" section.
- **MCP `add_portfolio_holding` now dual-writes `holding_accounts` pairing (2026-05-01, closes [#95](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/95)).** `add_portfolio_holding` (MCP HTTP and stdio) now inserts the matching `holding_accounts(holding_id, account_id, user_id, qty=0, cost_basis=0, is_primary=true)` row in the same flow as the `portfolio_holdings` INSERT. Without that pairing, every portfolio aggregator (issue #25 — JOINs through `holding_accounts` on `(holding_id, account_id, user_id)`) silently filtered the holding out of `get_portfolio_analysis`, `get_portfolio_performance`, and `analyze_holding`. The user-visible symptom was a holding that showed up in the web UI but not in any MCP analysis tool. **HTTP** ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts)): the existing handler uses the file's narrow `DbLike = { execute }` type which has no Drizzle `.transaction()` method, so the fix uses a try/catch around the `holding_accounts` INSERT that DELETEs the orphan `portfolio_holdings` row on failure — same atomicity guarantee as a transaction (the user never gets stuck with an aggregator-invisible holding) without widening the type. **stdio** ([mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts)): same try/catch + DELETE pattern for symmetry with the HTTP path. `ON CONFLICT (holding_id, account_id) DO NOTHING` keeps both paths idempotent on retries. `is_primary=true` on the new row honors the legacy `portfolio_holdings.account_id` mirror invariant (every fresh holding starts single-account). `qty=0`/`cost_basis=0` match the same defaults the original `holding-accounts (2026-04-30)` backfill used; aggregators read live qty/cost from `transactions` per CLAUDE.md "Portfolio aggregator". **Two SQL playbook entries** added to [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md): `holding-accounts-backfill-orphans` (inserts pairings for the holdings already created without one — at minimum holdings 539 / 540 / 541 in dev per the source review) and `holding-accounts-repair-divergence` (aligns `holding_accounts.account_id` with `portfolio_holdings.account_id` for primary-flagged rows where they diverge — fixes holding 428 / VUN.TO whose pairing pointed at account 600 while every transaction lives on 614). Both scripts are operator-applied per env per the standing convention (`deploy.sh` does not run schema migrations) and idempotent (`ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`). The repair-divergence script ships a step-1b collision pre-check that returns rows when a holding has BOTH a divergent primary row AND a separate non-primary row at the target `ph.account_id` — operator must DELETE the orphan or merge `qty`/`cost_basis` manually before re-running. **No `invalidateUser`** — `holding_accounts` isn't in the per-user tx cache; only `transactions` writes invalidate. **No `transactions` audit-trio plumbing** — `add_portfolio_holding` doesn't write the transactions table; the `created_at`/`updated_at`/`source` invariant is irrelevant here. **No DEK changes** — `holding_accounts` has no encrypted columns. **Tool surface unchanged at 82 HTTP / 78 stdio.** No schema migration ships in this PR — the playbook scripts are data repair, not schema change. **Out of scope.** The wider INSERT-site audit listed in the issue (REST `POST /api/portfolio`, REST crypto add, backup-restore, csv-parser, the connector pipeline, `getOrCreateCashHolding`, transfer auto-create, both `record_trade` cash-sleeve auto-create branches) — same bug class, separate code paths, separate follow-up. The repair script catches existing orphans, but each of those paths still creates new orphans until they're each migrated to dual-write. Files: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts), [scripts/migrate-holding-accounts-backfill-orphans.sql](scripts/migrate-holding-accounts-backfill-orphans.sql), [scripts/migrate-holding-accounts-repair-divergence.sql](scripts/migrate-holding-accounts-repair-divergence.sql), [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md).
- **Transfer dialog: same-account allowed for in-kind rebalance + quantity input on single-investment-side path (2026-05-01, closes [#94](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/94)).** Two transfer-dialog UX bugs surfaced in the 2026-05-01 review (Section 11). **Bug 1 — Same-account submit guard.** `handleTransferSubmit` in [src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx) unconditionally rejected `fromAccountId === toAccountId` even when both legs were investment accounts. The inline hint at the violet block already invited the rebalance and the server's `createTransferPair` already supports it (`wantsHolding=true` bypass at [src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts)). The dialog now relaxes the guard when `bothInv` is true (in-kind rebalance between two holdings in the same brokerage). Same-account when either side is non-investment still rejects with "From and To accounts must differ for a cash transfer". **Bug 2 — Quantity missing on single-investment-side path.** When exactly one side was an investment account (`inv+non` / `non+inv`), the violet block rendered only a holding `<Combobox>` — no quantity `<Input>`. The block now pairs each holding picker with a `Quantity (shares)` input in a `grid grid-cols-2` layout. The submit path validates `quantity > 0` whenever a holding pin is set on a non-in-kind transfer and threads it through to the POST body. Edit mode for the single-side path stays deferred (the block returns null when `transferEdit` is set, per issue #22). **Server-side persistence.** `createTransferPair` (Drizzle) and `createTransferPairViaSql` (raw SQL) now apply `opts.quantity` to the cash-pin leg(s) when `fromHoldingId` / `toHoldingId` is set without `holdingName`. Source leg gets `-|q|`, dest gets `+|q|`. The validation contract widened: `quantity` without `holdingName` is now legal when at least one holding pin is supplied (previously rejected as `invalid-holding-spec`). The in-kind path (`holdingName + quantity`) is unchanged. Server-side same-account guard is unchanged — `wantsHolding=true` (in-kind) still bypasses; cash-pin path still rejects same-account. **Out of scope.** Dest-quantity override on the single-side path (only meaningful for `inv+inv` splits / conversions), edit-mode for transfer holding fields on the single-side path (issue #22 deferral), server-side relaxation of the four-check transfer-pair rule. **Tool surface unchanged at 82 HTTP / 78 stdio.** No schema migration. The REST schema at [src/app/api/transactions/transfer/route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/transfer/route.ts) already accepted `quantity` alongside `fromHoldingId` / `toHoldingId` — it now actually persists. Files: [src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx), [src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts).
- **`record_transfer` / `record_trade`: fail fast when destination cash holding is missing (2026-05-01, closes [#92](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/92)).** `record_transfer` (and `record_trade`, which routes through `createTransferPair`) no longer auto-creates the destination holding row when one is missing. The Drizzle path (HTTP MCP + REST) used `buildHoldingResolver` to silently insert a `Cash` sleeve on miss; the raw-SQL path (stdio MCP) used `findOrCreateHoldingViaSql` for the same. On accounts that already had a partial-cash row, that hit the `portfolio_holdings` partial unique index and threw a raw `23505` duplicate-key DB exception with no actionable guidance — surfacing as a 500 to the AI assistant. **Fix.** Both paths converted to strict find-only on the destination side: the Drizzle path now reuses `findHoldingIdByAccountAndName` (already used at [transfer.ts:524](src/lib/transfer.ts) for the source side); the raw-SQL path reuses `findHoldingIdViaSql` (already used at [transfer.ts:1722](src/lib/transfer.ts) for the source side). On miss both return a new `Result.code = "no-cash-holding"` whose message names the missing holding, the destination account, and the exact `add_portfolio_holding(account="…", name="…", currency="…")` call to make. The `TransferPairResult` discriminated union grows the new code; `record_transfer` / `record_trade` (HTTP + stdio) already surface `result.message` on failure so the four MCP entry points pick up the new error without any tool-side branching. **Dead code deleted.** `findOrCreateHoldingViaSql` (the auto-create raw-SQL helper) had no remaining caller and is removed. `buildHoldingResolver`'s auto-create branch stays — still used by the import pipeline (`src/lib/import-pipeline.ts:508`, REST `POST /api/transactions` cash-side fallbacks at lines 456 / 511, and the `updateTransferPair` re-resolve branch when accounts are swapped on an in-kind pair) which has its own resolver-report UX. **Out of scope (deliberately).** `getOrCreateCashHolding` (`is_investment` toggle backfill), the `fromHoldingId` / `toHoldingId` FK-pin entry points (validated via `holdingBelongsToAccount`), and the import-pipeline auto-create — different paths, different UX. The strict-mode `InvestmentHoldingRequiredError` guard at [transfer.ts:602-607](src/lib/transfer.ts) still catches the case where neither a `holdingName` nor a `fromHoldingId` / `toHoldingId` was supplied. **MCP tool descriptions updated** in [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) and [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts) so the AI assistant knows to call `add_portfolio_holding` first when a cash sleeve doesn't exist. **Tool surface unchanged at 82 HTTP / 78 stdio.** Schema unchanged. Behavior change is failure-path only — the success-path duplicate-key exception goes away. No migration, no env vars, no cron. Files: [src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts), [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts).
- **Transactions per-column filter inputs accept keystrokes again (2026-05-01, closes [#91](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/91)).** Regression from PR #69. Text Contains, From/To dates, and numeric Value/Upper bound inputs inside the per-column filter dropdown silently swallowed every keystroke; selection-based filters (source/category/account/accountType checkboxes) were unaffected because they react to `change`, not `keydown`. **Root cause.** The filter editor renders inside a shadcn v4 `<DropdownMenuContent>` (base-ui `Menu.Root`). base-ui attaches keydown listeners on the menu surface for type-ahead (printable chars) and back-navigation (Backspace). The `<Input>` lets the keydown bubble up, so the menu eats the keystroke before the input's own native handling runs. **Fix.** Add `onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key !== "Escape" && e.key !== "Tab") e.stopPropagation(); }}` to all five `<Input>` callsites in `ColFilterEditor` ([src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx)) — date From, date To, text Contains, numeric Value, numeric Upper bound. Escape and Tab still bubble so dropdown-close + focus traversal remain intact; printable chars, arrow keys, Backspace, and space stop propagating so the input handles them natively. `e.stopPropagation()` only — never `e.preventDefault()`, which would block typing. Inline comment at the first site documents the base-ui interaction so a future refactor doesn't strip it. **Out of scope.** Swapping `DropdownMenu` for `Popover` (cleaner long-term — Popover has no keyboard navigation semantics — filed as a follow-up if needed). UX-only change; no DB / encryption / MCP surface impact.
- **MCP investment-account `autoCategory` short-circuits before generic history (2026-05-01, closes [#89](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/89)).** `record_transaction` / `bulk_record_transactions` over MCP HTTP could silently label a brokerage cash leg with a generic expense category (user-reported "Car Maintenance and other", id 475) when no rule, keyword, or fallback matched. The expense-skip guards on the rule + history loops weren't catching every leak path — a category miscatTyped as something other than `'E'`, a row whose `category_id` lookup missed in `catTypeById` (decryption miss), or a non-expense category irrelevant to the payee could all elect themselves. **Fix.** The investment branch of `autoCategory` in [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) now runs an investment-only ladder and short-circuits with `null` BEFORE the generic history pool is consulted: rules pass (expense-skipped) → keyword pass (`pickInvestmentCategoryByPayee`) → fallback (`fallbackInvestmentCategory`) → `null`. The non-investment branch is unchanged: rules → history → null. **Why null beats a wrong guess.** `null` is the established uncategorized convention everywhere — chat-engine, weekly-recap, spotlight all render it as "Uncategorized". A wrong expense category for a brokerage cash leg distorts every spending dashboard the user owns; uncategorized is honest and easy to fix. **Tool descriptions updated** for `record_transaction` and `bulk_record_transactions` (HTTP) to reflect the corrected behaviour: "Investment accounts use a keyword pass (dividend/interest/forex/disbursement) plus a 'Transfers'/'Investment Activity' fallback, and default to uncategorized when none match — never a generic expense category." **Stdio surface unchanged.** The stdio variants of `record_transaction` and `bulk_record_transactions` already refuse investment-account writes (no `portfolioHolding` plumbing on stdio per CLAUDE.md "Stdio MCP `record_transaction`/`bulk_record_transactions` refuse writes to investment accounts"), so `autoCategory` never runs there. No mirror needed. **REST + import-pipeline unchanged.** They use a separate `applyRules` + `suggestCategory` flow in [src/lib/auto-categorize.ts](src/lib/auto-categorize.ts), not the MCP-local helper. **Stream-D Phase 3 / pathfinder DEK-mismatch resilience preserved.** The `investmentHints` materialisation still uses the `decryptField(dek, name_ct) ?? plaintext` ladder so DEK-mismatch users keep working. **Match-field stays `'payee'`** — the known-bad `match_payee` column reference (separate pre-existing bug per CLAUDE.md "Auto-categorize rule schema") is not introduced. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no MCP tool surface change (still 82 HTTP / 78 stdio). Files: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts).
- **Settings sub-pages: shared layout content slot scrolls horizontally for wide tables (2026-05-01, closes [#88](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/88)).** After PR #67 split the settings monolith into 8 sub-pages under a shared `SettingsLayout` with a 224px (`w-56`) left nav, the content slot at [src/app/(app)/settings/layout.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/settings/layout.tsx) line 144 had `min-w-0` (lets the flex item shrink below intrinsic content width) but no `overflow-x-auto`, so wide tables (holding-accounts pairings, dropdown-order rows, categorization rules) clipped on narrow viewports with no way to scroll. **Primary fix.** Added `overflow-x-auto` alongside the existing `min-w-0` (do not replace — the flex-shrink behavior is load-bearing). Pages whose content fits show no visible change; pages whose content overflows now scroll inside the slot, not the page. **Belt-and-suspenders on `/settings/holding-accounts`.** The per-holding `<Table>` (5 columns: Account / Qty / Cost basis / Primary / Actions) is now wrapped in an inner `<div className="overflow-x-auto">` so the table-level wrapper consumes overflow first — no double scrollbars even though the layout slot also has `overflow-x-auto`. **No-op on the other sub-pages.** `/settings/data` already wraps its CSV-preview `<table>` in `overflow-x-auto`. `/settings/categorization`, `/settings/dropdown-order`, and `/settings/investments` don't render `<table>` elements at all — their card-based row lists already use `min-w-0` + `truncate` and handle their own overflow. The mobile pill row (below `md` breakpoint) is on a separate sibling and unaffected. No schema, no encryption invariants, no audit-trio plumbing, no MCP tool surface change (count unchanged at 82 HTTP / 78 stdio). Code-only change. Files: [src/app/(app)/settings/layout.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/settings/layout.tsx), [src/app/(app)/settings/holding-accounts/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/settings/holding-accounts/page.tsx).
- **Combobox filter regression: `itemToStringLabel` returned literal `"[object Object]"`, emptying every dropdown on any keystroke (2026-05-01, closes [#87](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/87)).** PR #68 wired base-ui's built-in `Combobox` filter and fixed the original "list never filters" bug, but introduced a regression: typing any text in the high-level `<Combobox>` emptied the list even when matching items existed. Root cause was a one-line type mismatch in [src/components/ui/combobox.tsx](src/components/ui/combobox.tsx) — `itemToStringLabel={(v) => items.find((it) => it.value === v)?.label ?? String(v ?? "")}` assumed `v` was the raw string id, but base-ui passes the full item object (`{ value, label }`) from the `items` prop. The lookup always returned `undefined`, the `String(v ?? "")` fallback yielded the literal `"[object Object]"` for every entry, and base-ui's substring filter compared the user's input against `"[object Object]"` per row — no matches → empty list. **Fix.** `itemToStringLabel` now reads `item.label` directly when the argument is an object (the actual base-ui contract). The plain-string fallback is preserved as a defensive branch in case a future direct `<ComboboxRoot>` callsite passes a `string[]` `items` prop. **Affects every dropdown migrated to the high-level `<Combobox>`** — account picker, category picker, payee picker, holding picker, currency picker, transfer / split dialog, budgets, goals, loans, subscriptions, FX overrides, reconcile preview/upload (16 callsites). Single-callback fix in one file; no callsite changes. PR #68's filter wiring was correct and is preserved. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no MCP tool count change. Files: [src/components/ui/combobox.tsx](src/components/ui/combobox.tsx).
- **MCP HTTP aggregator keys by `holding_id`; `symbols` filter gains token-overlap match + `warnings` (2026-05-01, closes [#86](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/86)).** The MCP HTTP `accumulate()` aggregator keyed its output Map by holding *display name*, silently merging holdings sharing a name across accounts (e.g. VUN.TO in IBKR TFSA + IBKR RRSP + Mimi TFSA → one inflated row of qty=166 instead of three distinct rows). Re-keys to `holding_id` so two TFSA + RRSP holdings come through as separate rows with their own `holdingId`, `account`, quantity, and cost basis — bringing MCP HTTP into alignment with the other three aggregators (REST `/api/portfolio/overview`, `src/lib/holdings-value.ts`, MCP stdio) that already group by FK id per CLAUDE.md "Portfolio aggregator". **`accumulate()` + `aggregateHoldings()` re-keyed.** [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) — `Map<string, Agg>` → `Map<number, Agg>`. The `accumulate(out, holdingId, name, r, dividendsCategoryId)` signature now takes the holding id as the lookup key; display name remains a row field. Same buy/sell/dividend branching preserved verbatim (qty>0 = buy regardless of amt sign per CLAUDE.md; dividends matched by `category_id` per #84). Defensive skip for any (post-Phase-6) row with a null `portfolio_holding_id`. **`get_portfolio_analysis` (HTTP) `phMap` re-keyed by `ph.id`.** SELECT picks up `ph.id`; lookup is `phMap.get(Number(m.holding_id))` instead of `phMap.get(String(m.name))`. Result rows surface the right `account` (sourced from the holding's own `accounts JOIN portfolio_holdings.account_id` lookup, not from a name-collision fallout that picked the wrong row). **`symbols` filter matches name + symbol via substring + token-overlap.** Splits the filter on whitespace + parens + brackets and accepts any shared non-empty token, so `"VCN.TO (TFSA)"` resolves to a `"VCN.TO"`-named holding. Falls back to substring on the common case. Unmatched filter entries surface as `warnings: ["BNO: no matching holding found"]` alongside `holdings` so the caller can correct typos instead of silently getting empty results. **`get_portfolio_performance` (HTTP) returns `holdingId` per row.** Callers can disambiguate same-name holdings without a follow-up call. **`analyze_holding` (HTTP + stdio) gains optional `holdingId` parameter.** When provided, short-circuits the fuzzy substring filter and scopes strictly to that FK id. When `symbol` substring-matches multiple distinct holding ids (e.g. "VUN" matching VUN.TO across two accounts), the response returns an `ambiguous` array of `{holdingId, name, symbol, account}` candidates and requires the caller to pick one. `symbol` is now optional when `holdingId` is supplied; one of the two is required. **`get_investment_insights` rebalancing + patterns modes sum across same-name holdings.** Aggregator now returns one row per id, but rebalancing targets are user-supplied by name — explicit dedup ensures cross-account positions roll up to a single allocation entry instead of a last-write-wins regression. **`update_portfolio_holding` docstring updated.** The claim "renames cascade because get_portfolio_analysis groups by FK" is now accurate post-fix; wording updated to reference issue #86 and the per-holdingId grouping. **CLAUDE.md "Portfolio aggregator" gotcha updated.** The "MCP HTTP `accumulate()` is the only aggregator that has historically diverged from this rule" note now reads as a resolved historical note pointing at this PR. **Tool surface unchanged at 82 HTTP / 78 stdio** — schema additions are optional fields on existing tools. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron. Files: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts), [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md).

### Changed
- **MCP: extend low-confidence fuzzy-match rejection + `account_id` to `record_transfer` / `record_trade` (2026-05-01, closes [#85](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/85)).** Follow-up to #29 (which fixed the same silent-routing failure mode for `record_transaction` and `bulk_record_transactions`). The remaining money-moving entry points still resolved account names through the loose `fuzzyFind` helper, so a sloppy `fromAccount` / `toAccount` / `account` value could silently route a transfer pair or trade to a vaguely-similar account — the same bug that misrouted 99 IBKR transactions to "Appartment" before #29. **`record_transfer` (HTTP + stdio)** now accepts `from_account_id?: number` and `to_account_id?: number`; when set, fuzzy matching is skipped entirely and the FK is validated against `accounts.user_id = ?`. The `fromAccount` / `toAccount` (name) params are now optional — at least one of (id, name) per side is required. The name path runs through the existing `resolveAccountStrict` helper which only accepts substring/reverse-substring hits when the input and candidate share a whitespace-separated token of length ≥3; otherwise the call fails with `Source account "<name>" did not match strongly — closest is "<name>" (id=N) but no shared whitespace token. Re-call with from_account_id=N if that's right, or pick another from: <list>`. Symmetric on the destination side. **`record_trade` (HTTP + stdio)** already had `account_id` from #29's bulk-record rollout; this issue replaces the loose `fuzzyFind` fallback at [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) and [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts) with `resolveAccountStrict` + the same low-confidence error format. **Response shape additions.** `record_transfer` now returns `resolvedFromAccount: { id, name }` + `resolvedToAccount: { id, name }` so callers can verify routing immediately; `record_trade` returns `resolvedAccount: { id, name }` to match. **Behaviorally identical for confidently-matching names** — exact / alias / startsWith / token-gated-substring tiers preserved. **Audit-trio invariants preserved** (issue #28). `txSource: "mcp_http"` / `"mcp_stdio"` continues to flow through `createTransferPair` / `createTransferPairViaSql`; per-user tx cache invalidation for both legs (called inside the helpers) is preserved. **Stdio carve-out unchanged** — investment-account writes still refused on stdio `record_transaction` (no `portfolioHolding` plumbing), but `record_transfer` and `record_trade` always handled in-kind via the explicit `holding` / `symbol` parameters and continue to. **No schema migration; no env vars; no cron.** Tool surface count unchanged at 82 HTTP / 78 stdio (schema additions are optional fields on existing tools). Files: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts), [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md).

### Fixed
- **Portfolio `dividendsReceived`: classify by Dividends category id, not the qty/amount-sign heuristic (2026-05-01, closes [#84](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/84)).** All four portfolio aggregators previously classified dividend payouts with `quantity == 0 AND amount > 0`, which silently dropped (a) dividend reinvestments (qty>0, amt<0 — re-classified as buys with no dividend credit) and (b) withholding tax / negative-correction entries (qty=0, amt<0 — fell through every branch and disappeared from `dividendsReceived`). Repro from the source review: TFSA-CAD holding 425 reported $661.27 dividends but the SUM over Dividends-category transactions was ~$726.06 — $64.79 unexplained. **Fix.** Aggregators now match `transactions.category_id` against the user's "Dividends" (or "Dividend") category id, mirroring the user's mental model and aligning with [auto-categorize.ts:195](src/lib/auto-categorize.ts) which already routes "dividend"-payee transactions into that category. **New helper [src/lib/dividends-category.ts](src/lib/dividends-category.ts).** Single-source `resolveDividendsCategoryId(db, userId, dek)` returns the category id (or null when the user has no Dividends category — aggregators short-circuit to 0 dividends). Stream-D-aware: when a DEK is supplied, matches both legacy plaintext `categories.name` AND the encrypted `name_lookup` HMAC; without a DEK (e.g. stdio MCP), only plaintext matches. Same fallback ladder (`Dividends` → `Dividend`) as `pickInvestmentCategoryByPayee`. **REST `/api/portfolio/overview`** ([src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts)) — the `dividendsInEntered` SQL CASE now matches `category_id = $dividendsCategoryId` when set, and short-circuits to `0::float8` when null. The `holding_accounts` JOIN grain and per-currency bucket aggregation are unchanged. **MCP HTTP `accumulate()` / `aggregateHoldings()`** ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts)) — `aggregateHoldings` now plumbs `t.category_id` through the SELECT and accepts a `dividendsCategoryId` option; `accumulate()` matches by category id rather than `qty=0+amt>0`. The buy/sell branches still come first (per CLAUDE.md "qty>0 is a buy regardless of amount sign"), then the dividend branch independently adds to the dividend bucket — a dividend reinvestment is now both a "buy" (shares acquired) AND a "dividend" (income received), which is correct. `get_portfolio_analysis` and `get_portfolio_performance` resolve the category id once at the top and pass it into `aggregateHoldings`. The other two `aggregateHoldings` callers (`get_investment_insights` mode='rebalancing' and mode='benchmark') filter `t.amount < 0` (`buysOnly`), don't read the dividends field, and don't need the option — left unchanged. **MCP HTTP `analyze_holding`** — selects `t.category_id`, replaces the in-memory `qty=0+amt>0` branch with a category-id match, and pushes onto the `dividends` array independently of the buy/sell branches. **MCP stdio `analyze_holding`** ([mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts)) — left unchanged. The stdio variant doesn't compute dividends today (no `divAmt` accumulator), so the heuristic-fix is a no-op there. The issue acceptance criteria explicitly allowed leaving stdio as-is. Stdio `get_portfolio_analysis` likewise doesn't expose dividends — separate gap, not regressed. **Load-bearing invariants honored.** Read-only change — no writes, no `invalidateUser(userId)` plumbing. Audit trio (`updated_at` / `source`) untouched. The CLAUDE.md "Portfolio aggregator" gotcha is now updated to note the category-id-based dividend classifier supersedes the qty-sign heuristic. The four-aggregator alignment rule still applies — REST + MCP HTTP `accumulate()` + MCP HTTP `analyze_holding` use the new classifier consistently. Stream-D dual-index pattern (plaintext `name` + encrypted `name_lookup`) preserved on the category lookup. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no MCP tool count change (still 82 HTTP / 78 stdio). Files: [src/lib/dividends-category.ts](src/lib/dividends-category.ts), [src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts), [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts).

### Added
- **Data fix recipe: reassign tx 29100 from holding 497 to holding 425 (2026-05-01, closes [#81](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/81)).** Docs-only addition to [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md) — a per-env psql playbook entry for the one-off `transactions` row mutation that moves the +$10,000 EFT RRSP contribution off the wrong Cash sleeve (holding 497 in Mimi TFSA) onto the correct TFSA-CAD holding 425 in IBKR TFSA. No code change, no schema change. **Recipe shape.** Pre-state verification block (confirms the tx is owned by the right user, currently on 497, both holdings exist, AND that holding 425 has a `holding_accounts` row covering the tx's `account_id` for this user). The single-row `UPDATE` sets `portfolio_holding_id = 425` and bumps `updated_at`; `source` is **deliberately preserved** per the CLAUDE.md audit-trio invariant (`transactions.source` is INSERT-only). Predicates `id = 29100 AND user_id = ? AND portfolio_holding_id = 497` make the statement idempotent and impossible to widen by accident. Post-state spot-check confirms `portfolio_holding_id` flipped and `source` is unchanged. Per-env rollout block covers dev → staging → prod. **Gate.** Per CLAUDE.md, every portfolio aggregator JOINs through `holding_accounts` on `(holding, account)`; running the UPDATE without holding 425's covering pair in place silently drops the leg from the aggregator. The pre-state SELECT catches that and the recipe instructs the operator to STOP if it returns 0 rows (gate on issue [#95](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/95) `holding_accounts` integrity). **Ops impact.** Raw psql bypasses the MCP per-user tx cache (no `invalidateUser(userId)` call), so the recipe also instructs operators to restart the env's `finlynq-*` systemd service after the prod commit so Claude reads fresh attribution on next request. No env vars, no new dependencies, no MCP tool surface change (still 82 HTTP / 78 stdio). The actual SQL execution is a human-run step per [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md) — this PR only ships the recipe.

- **MCP stdio `search_transactions`: add `portfolio_holding_id` filter for HTTP parity (2026-05-01, closes [#80](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/80)).** Closes a long-standing HTTP/stdio drift on `search_transactions`. The HTTP variant has accepted `account_id` + `portfolio_holding_id` together as independent filters that AND together (e.g. "all VCN.TO dividends in IBKR TFSA for Q1 2026"), but the stdio variant was missing `portfolio_holding_id` entirely. **Stdio now exposes `portfolio_holding_id?: number`** in the Zod schema and ANDs `t.portfolio_holding_id = ?` into the WHERE clause alongside the existing `account_id` filter ([mcp-server/tools-v2.ts](mcp-server/tools-v2.ts)). Parameter name is snake-case to mirror the HTTP schema exactly — no XOR guard, no payee-required guard, all filter combinations remain valid alone or combined. **Tool description updated symmetrically on stdio** to surface the new parameter and the "combine with account_id to narrow to one holding in one account" pattern so the model picks the FK fast-path over a `payee` substring search. **Regression-guard comments added on both transports** ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) + [mcp-server/tools-v2.ts](mcp-server/tools-v2.ts)) noting that `account_id` and `portfolio_holding_id` are independent filters and future refactors must not reintroduce an XOR or "must supply payee" guard — cheap insurance against the parity rule existing only in tribal knowledge. **No schema/DDL/env-var/cron impact.** Filter is purely an FK predicate against the existing `transactions.portfolio_holding_id` column; encryption fallback rules unaffected. Stdio reads stay plaintext per the documented "Stdio MCP writes are plaintext" gotcha. `invalidateUserTxCache(userId)` not applicable — this is a read tool. **MCP tool surface count unchanged** at 82 HTTP / 78 stdio — only a parameter added to an existing tool. Code-only change.
- **MCP `search_transactions`: `quantity` field on response (2026-05-01, closes [#79](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/79)).** `transactions.quantity` is now surfaced on every row returned by `search_transactions` (HTTP + stdio) so AI assistants can read share-count state directly without inferring it from aggregator output. Pre-req for the orphaned-quantity-residue diagnostics in #99 — `quantity` is a first-class field on investment-account workflows (share counts move on quantity, not on `amount`). **HTTP variant** ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts)) adds `t.quantity` to the SELECT list alongside `t.portfolio_holding_id`; the existing `...rest` row spread carries it through decryption and tagging unchanged. Tool description updated to document the field shape (`nullable; positive for buys, negative for sells; null for cash-proxy and non-investment transactions`). **Stdio variant** ([mcp-server/tools-v2.ts](mcp-server/tools-v2.ts)) adds `t.quantity` to the SELECT list and extends the `TransactionRow` type with `quantity: number | null`; tool description updated symmetrically. No DDL — `transactions.quantity` already exists. Audit-trio (`createdAt` / `updatedAt` / `source`) remains HTTP-only on this tool, consistent with prior behavior — out of scope here. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no MCP tool count change.

### Changed
- **MCP: fix `record_transfer` `toAccount` description, surface `record_trade` in `finlynq_help` (2026-05-01, closes [#82](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/82)).** Documentation/discovery-only fix to the MCP tool surface — no behavioral change. The runtime already supported same-account in-kind transfers via `createTransferPair` / `createTransferPairViaSql` (the guard at [src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts) checks `!wantsHolding` so same-account passes through fine when `holding` + `destHolding` are set), and the four-check transfer-pair rule (link_id + sole sibling + both type='R' + different accounts, relaxed for in-kind same-account rebalances) is unchanged. Only the parameter description and the `finlynq_help(topic="tools")` discovery list misled agents. **`record_transfer.toAccount` description (HTTP + stdio)** no longer says "must differ from fromAccount". Updated wording: same-as-fromAccount is allowed for intra-account in-kind rebalances (cash sleeve ↔ symbol holding, or different-currency cash sleeves) when `holding` and `destHolding` are also set; same-account cash-only transfers are still rejected. **`record_transfer` tool description (HTTP + stdio)** now cross-references `record_trade` as the preferred tool for brokerage stock/ETF/crypto buys and sells. Wording is intentionally soft ("prefer `record_trade` for buys/sells") so non-trade in-kind moves (forex sleeve, gold-between-brokerages, mergers/share-class conversions, ACATS) continue to use `record_transfer` directly. **`finlynq_help(topic="tools")` discovery list** now exposes both `record_transfer` and `record_trade` under a new `trade_tools` bucket alongside `portfolio_tools`. Previously neither tool appeared in the topic="tools" output, so agents calling the discovery endpoint had no way to find them. The `tip` field is updated symmetrically to point agents at `record_trade` for trades. Mirrored across HTTP (`mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`) and stdio (`mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts`) per the CLAUDE.md "MCP HTTP+stdio mirror" invariant. The architecture doc [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md) brokerage-statement recipe already led with `record_trade` (added 2026-04-30 with `record_trade`'s introduction) — no edits needed there. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron. **MCP tool surface count unchanged at 82 HTTP / 78 stdio** — this is a description-only edit. Files: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts).
- **Portfolio per-account breakdown always renders + zero-qty market-value bug fixed (2026-05-01, follow-up to [#25](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/25)).** Two follow-ups on the canonical-holding rollup. (1) The per-account breakdown table inside an expanded "All Holdings" row was gated on `memberHoldings.length > 1` so single-account holdings only showed the inline `Edit` + `View transactions` links. Gate flipped to `memberHoldings.length > 0` so every expanded row renders the same shape; the now-redundant single-account inline `Edit` button is dropped because clicking the account-name cell in the breakdown table opens the same editor (`Pencil` icon import dropped — was its only consumer). Header gets a `bg-muted/40` tint and the wrapping border bumps from `border-border/50` to full `border-border` for dark-theme legibility. (2) Zero-quantity holdings no longer fake a market value from the unit price. `/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts` had a branch `else if (price !== null && !quantity)` that set `marketValue = price`, leaking the per-share quote (e.g. $61.74 for VJPA.L with qty=0) into both the position's market-value column AND the portfolio-wide `totalValueDisplay` + asset-type breakdowns. For a $600k/share stock this could have inflated portfolio totals catastrophically. Branch deleted; zero-qty holdings now show market value = `null` and only contribute realized G/L + dividends to total return. Files: [src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/portfolio/page.tsx), [src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts). Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars.
- **Portfolio: canonical-holding rollup table + per-account drill-down (2026-05-01, closes [#25](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/25)).** Restructures the Portfolio page so the "All Holdings" table aggregates each canonical position across every account that holds it (e.g. AAPL in TFSA + RRSP shows once with `totalQty=8`, qty-weighted avg cost, summed market value/realized G/L). Expanding a row reveals the existing per-holding stats AND a new per-account breakdown table (account name, qty, avg cost, market value, unrealized G/L, realized G/L) with "View transactions" drill-down. The standalone "By Holding" panel is folded into this restructured table per the issue's resolution comment; the "Holdings by Account" panel is unchanged. **Aggregator JOINs through `holding_accounts`.** `/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts` (FK SQL CASE), `src/lib/holdings-value.ts`, MCP HTTP `accumulate()` + `analyze_holding`, and MCP stdio `get_portfolio_analysis` + `get_portfolio_performance` + `analyze_holding` — every aggregator site now `INNER JOIN holding_accounts ha ON ha.holding_id = t.portfolio_holding_id AND ha.account_id = t.account_id AND ha.user_id = ?`. Today the join table has one `is_primary=true` row per holding so behavior is unchanged; once a canonical position spans multiple `holding_accounts` rows, the join attributes correctly per-(holding, account). CLAUDE.md "Portfolio aggregator" `qty>0 = buy regardless of amount sign` rule preserved at every site. **Per-decision: full crypto symbol preserved.** The `byHolding` canonical-key derivation stops collapsing crypto on `-`, so `BTC-ETH` is a distinct holding from `BTC` (per issue #25 resolution comment 2026-05-01). **`HoldingEditDialog` Name field is disabled on canonical rows** with a hint ("Name is auto-managed for this holding type. Edit the symbol or currency to rename"). Symbol / currency / isCrypto / note remain editable. The PUT handler at `/api/portfolio` rejects name edits on canonical rows with HTTP 400 as the API-layer guard. **New per-user canonical-name backfill helper** at [src/lib/crypto/stream-d-canonicalize-portfolio.ts](src/lib/crypto/stream-d-canonicalize-portfolio.ts) — sibling of `stream-d-phase3-null.ts`. Wired into both the password-login and MFA-verify paths next to `enqueuePhase3NullIfReady`. On first login per user, decrypts each `portfolio_holdings` row's name + symbol, classifies (tickered / cash-sleeve / currency-code / user-defined), and dual-writes the canonical name + `name_ct` + `name_lookup` for tickered/cash/currency rows. User-defined rows keep their free-text name. DEK-mismatch users (e.g. pathfinder per CLAUDE.md "Known open issue") bail silently at the sample-decrypt precondition — same pattern as `nullPlaintextIfReady`. **Schema-prep migration `scripts/migrate-portfolio-canonical-names.sql`.** Adds `users.portfolio_names_canonicalized_at text` (NULL = needs canonicalization, non-NULL = done). Idempotent. **Apply per-env BEFORE deploying matching code** — the helper reads/writes the flag column on first login. Per-env playbook in [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md). CLAUDE.md "deploy.sh does NOT run schema migrations" rule applies. **Files:** [src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/portfolio/page.tsx), [src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts), [src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts), [src/lib/holdings-value.ts](src/lib/holdings-value.ts), [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts), [src/lib/crypto/stream-d-canonicalize-portfolio.ts](src/lib/crypto/stream-d-canonicalize-portfolio.ts), [src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts](src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts), [src/app/api/auth/mfa/verify/route.ts](src/app/api/auth/mfa/verify/route.ts), [scripts/migrate-portfolio-canonical-names.sql](scripts/migrate-portfolio-canonical-names.sql), [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md), [docs/architecture/encryption.md](docs/architecture/encryption.md). MCP tool surface count unchanged (82 HTTP / 78 stdio); only their underlying SQL changed.
- **Transfer dialog: unified holding pickers driven by account types (2026-05-01, closes [#56](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/56)).** Collapses the old two-block approach (explicit "In-kind (transfer shares of a holding)" checkbox + separate "Investment account leg" block) into a single picker section whose visibility and fields are derived entirely from the From/To account types. No checkbox; no pre-selected defaults; quantity starts blank. Behavior matrix: inv+inv → full in-kind UI (source holding + quantity + dest holding + dest quantity); inv+non-inv → From-holding picker only (required); non-inv+inv → To-holding picker only (required); non+non → no holding pickers. Cash sleeves remain selectable in all pickers. The reset effect was refactored to two independent refs (`prevFromIsInvestmentRef` / `prevToIsInvestmentRef`) so each side's holding state clears independently on `true→false` transitions. The From/To account pickers allow same-account selection when both sides are investment (in-kind rebalance within one brokerage). Submit-handler derives `isInKind` from account types (`fromAcct.isInvestment && toAcct.isInvestment`) rather than a form field. No backend changes — `createTransferPair`, the REST handler, and both MCP transports are untouched. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no tool count change. File: [src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx).
- **MCP `bulk_update`: strict schema, name-resolved category/holding, unappliedChanges surface (2026-04-30, closes [#61](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/61)).** The `preview_bulk_update` / `execute_bulk_update` Zod schema previously stripped unknown keys silently — calls like `changes: { category: "Credit Interest" }` returned `success: true, updated: N` while writing nothing. Real-world fallout: 13 IBKR Joint transactions stuck mis-categorized because the silent no-op masked every retry. **Fix.** Both transports' `bulkChangesSchema` is now `.strict()` so unknown keys hard-fail at validation time. **HTTP-accepted `changes` keys (now 11):** added `category` (name → `category_id` via `resolveCategoryStrict`), `quantity` (nullable; `null` clears), `portfolioHoldingId` (FK with ownership pre-check), `portfolioHolding` (name/ticker → FK via the existing `resolvePortfolioHoldingByName`). Disagreement between `category`/`category_id` or `portfolioHolding`/`portfolioHoldingId` errors out the call — silent "I named X but you bound Y" is worse than rejecting. **Stdio surface narrower by design.** Stdio adds only `category` (name → id). `quantity` / `portfolioHoldingId` / `portfolioHolding` stay HTTP-only — mirrors the stdio `record_transaction` carve-out that already refuses investment-account writes (no holding plumbing, no DEK). A stdio caller passing `quantity` now gets a clean strict-mode 400 instead of a silent no-op. **`resolveBulkChanges` helper (new, both transports).** Resolves names → ids ONCE upstream of `previewBulk` and `commitBulkUpdate`. Returns `{ resolved, unapplied[], error? }`. `error` (id-vs-name disagreement) hard-fails the call; `unapplied[]` (e.g. ambiguous category, holding not found) flows through to `unappliedChanges` on the response so callers see WHY any sample row's before/after looks identical. **`previewBulk` / `commitBulkUpdate` refactored to take `ResolvedChanges`.** Sample `after` rows now reflect the post-resolution shape, so identical before/after pairs always come paired with an `unappliedChanges` entry. The confirmation token still signs the user-supplied `changes` (not the resolved form) so preview→execute round-trips with the same payload still match; execute re-runs resolution. **`execute_bulk_update` aborts on empty resolved set.** Previously, a request with only an unresolvable `category` would commit zero rows and report success. Now: if every requested change failed resolution, the call errors with `Resolution failures: …` and writes nothing. **Audit-trio invariant preserved (issue #28).** Every new UPDATE branch in `commitBulkUpdate` (`quantity`, `portfolio_holding_id`) appends `updated_at = NOW()`; `source` is INSERT-only and is never touched by `bulk_update`. `invalidateUserTxCache(userId)` continues to fire after every successful commit. **Tool descriptions + `finlynq_help` updated** on both transports to enumerate accepted keys per transport. **Sanity-test:** the 13 IBKR Joint transactions named in the source review can now be re-categorized with `bulk_update changes: { category: "Credit Interest" }` and the change persists. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron, no tool count change. Files: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts), [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md).
- **Drop institution-specific source tags; switch to format-based tagging (2026-04-30, closes [#62](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/62)).** The `source:<x>` tag written into `transactions.tags` by every import path is now a **file format** (`csv` / `excel` / `pdf` / `ofx` / `qfx` / `ibkr-xml` / `email`) rather than the connector or institution name. Institution name already lives on the account; per-row tags now describe shape only, which scales as more connectors land. Coordinated with the upcoming unified OFX/QFX/IBKR-XML import work in issue [#64](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/64) — the same vocabulary is the contract both PRs consume. **Canonical vocabulary** in [src/lib/tx-source.ts](src/lib/tx-source.ts) (`FORMAT_TAGS` + `FormatTag` + `isFormatTag` + `sourceTagFor`), mirrored in [packages/import-connectors/src/types.ts](packages/import-connectors/src/types.ts) so the connectors package stays npm-publishable. Distinct from the audit-column `transactions.source` enum (issue #28) — the two coexist in the same file for grep-discovery but their values are independent. **Connector transforms.** `transformTransactions()` ([packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/transform.ts](packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/transform.ts)) renamed the `sourceConnectorId?: string` option to a required `formatTag: FormatTag` and dropped the `?? "wealthposition"` default — runtime guard throws on unknown values. WealthPosition (`packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/index.ts` + `src/lib/external-import/orchestrator.ts`) now passes `formatTag: "csv"`. IBKR (`packages/import-connectors/src/ibkr/orchestrator.ts`) plumbs `csv` vs `ibkr-xml` based on which parser ran. The hard-coded `"source:ibkr"` strings baked into `ExternalTransaction.tags` arrays in `packages/import-connectors/src/ibkr/transform.ts` (4 sites) are dropped — the downstream `transformTransactions()` adds the format tag in one place. Descriptive tags (`ibkr:fx-conversion`, `ibkr:fx-translation`, `ibkr:asset:*`) are kept — they carry semantic info, not provenance. **Direct upload paths now stamp the format tag.** [`/api/import/preview/route.ts`](src/app/api/import/preview/route.ts) tags CSV (3 entry points: explicit template, canonical headers, auto-matched template) and PDF rows. [`/api/import/csv-map/route.ts`](src/app/api/import/csv-map/route.ts) tags column-mapping CSV imports. [`/api/import/excel-map/route.ts`](src/app/api/import/excel-map/route.ts) tags Excel imports. [`/api/import/reconcile/preview/route.ts`](src/app/api/import/reconcile/preview/route.ts) tags reconcile-mode rows with `csv` / `ofx` / `qfx` based on the file extension (qfx now distinguished from ofx in the response payload's new `format` field). The OFX preview client ([src/app/(app)/import/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/import/page.tsx)) picks up the format from the preview response and stamps it on rows before `/api/import/execute`. Email-staged rows are tagged at [`/api/import/staged/[id]/approve/route.ts`](src/app/api/import/staged/%5Bid%5D/approve/route.ts) — the staging tables don't carry `tags`, so the tag is applied at materialize time. **Transfer-pair `source` opt tightened.** [`src/lib/transfer.ts`](src/lib/transfer.ts) — `CreateTransferOpts.source` retyped from `string` to `FormatTag`; `applySourceTag` runtime-guards via `isFormatTag()` so JS callers passing the old `"wealthposition"` / `"ibkr"` strings throw rather than silently writing bogus tags. JSDoc updated to document the format-vs-institution distinction. **Backfill script rewritten.** [`scripts/backfill-source-tag.sql`](scripts/backfill-source-tag.sql) is now a parameterized rewrite-in-place script (`:from_tag` → `:to_tag`) instead of the original "tag everything with `source:<connector>`" script.
