Same-account in-kind rebalance still renders because the account is investment ⇒ `eitherIsInvestment === true`. **Reset on transition.** New effect keyed on `[fromAccountId, toAccountId, accounts]` clears `inKind / holdingName / destHoldingName / quantity / destQuantity` plus `destHoldingTouched` / `destQuantityTouched` only on the actual `true → false` transition, so a user who briefly swaps to a cash account and back doesn't lose in-flight values until they actually commit to a cash-only pair. **Why this is safe at the write layer.** Backend `createTransferPair` already auto-defaults a per-account Cash holding for pure-cash legs landing on an investment account ([src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts), `defaultHoldingForInvestmentAccount`), so hiding the UI block does not regress the application-layer "every tx in an investment account references a portfolio_holdings row" constraint documented in CLAUDE.md. **Type extensions.** Local `Account` type gained `isInvestment?: boolean` (already on the `/api/accounts` wire shape via `getAccounts`'s `select()`-all). `Holding.accountId` was already in the type (added in #23). **Out of scope.** Section C mandatory enforcement, Section B/D dialog dedup, and the parallel Add Transaction filter cleanup. Code-only; no schema migration, no API change, no env vars, no cron.

### Fixed
- **Add Transaction holding dropdown empty for Phase-3 users; now decrypts + filters by account (2026-04-29, closes [#23](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/23)).** Two fixes in one issue. **Root cause.** `GET /api/portfolio` ([src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts)) returned `getPortfolioHoldings()` rows verbatim — no `decryptNamedRows()` pass. Every other portfolio read path (`/api/portfolio/overview`, `/api/accounts`) decrypts after the query. For users past Stream D Phase 3 cutover (`users.plaintext_nulled_at` set), `portfolio_holdings.name`/`symbol` are NULL on disk, so the response carried `{name: null, symbol: null}` rows. The Add Transaction "Portfolio Holding" Combobox then rendered items with empty values, which `@base-ui/react` collapses — picker looked completely empty even though the same holdings showed up correctly on `/portfolio` (which decrypts via `/api/portfolio/overview`). **API fix.** Wrapped the result in `decryptNamedRows(rows, auth.context.dek, { nameCt: "name", symbolCt: "symbol", accountNameCt: "accountName" })`, mirroring [/api/portfolio/overview](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts) and [/api/accounts](src/app/api/accounts/route.ts). Read-path soft-fallback honoured: a user without a cached DEK falls through to plaintext (legacy / Pathfinder-style users), unchanged. **Dialog fix.** [src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx) — `Holding` type extended with `accountId: number | null` (the API already returned it through the JOIN, the type just didn't claim it). The Add Transaction "Portfolio Holding" Combobox now restricts options to holdings whose `accountId` matches the currently-selected account, mirroring the existing Transfer-mode filtering pattern. The fallback "unknown holding" line still queries the full `holdings` array so existing-but-out-of-account selections survive on edit (otherwise opening an old row whose holding moved would render the value as a blank pill). The Account-Combobox `onValueChange` now clears `form.portfolioHoldingId` when the previously-selected holding is not in the new account, preventing the silent 23503 FK error on submit (the per-account Cash holding for the old account is invalid for the new one). **Test.** [tests/api/portfolio.test.ts](tests/api/portfolio.test.ts) gains a Phase-3 case: rows with NULL plaintext + populated `nameCt`/`symbolCt`/`accountNameCt`. The mocked auth context now carries a real 32-byte DEK; the test uses `encryptField` to mint ciphertext that the route round-trips through `decryptNamedRows`, then asserts the response shows the decrypted values. The original test would not have caught this — it only mocked `getPortfolioHoldings` and asserted equality of plaintext rows. **Forward-compat note.** When the M2M migration (Section F/G #15) lands, `accountId` becomes `accounts: number[]`; the dialog filter changes from `String(h.accountId) === form.accountId` to `h.accounts.map(String).includes(form.accountId)` — flagged inline so the migration PR catches it. **Out of scope.** Mandatory enforcement (#6), type-ahead overhaul (#4/#5), Transfer-dialog filtering (#9) — handled in their own issues. Code-only change; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron.

### Changed
- **Type-ahead Combobox + per-user dropdown ordering (2026-04-29, closes [#21](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/21)).** Adds a shared `<Combobox>` primitive on top of `@base-ui/react/combobox` so user-data-backed dropdowns (categories, accounts, portfolio holdings, currencies) get type-ahead filtering for free, and lays the data plumbing for a forthcoming Section G "setup pages" UI to let users pin frequently-used items to the top of every list. **New primitive** [src/components/ui/combobox.tsx](src/components/ui/combobox.tsx) wraps base-ui's combobox with the same Tailwind tokens as `<Select>` so the visual design is unchanged; both a high-level `<Combobox items={…} />` (drop-in for `<Select>` migrations) and the lower-level pieces (`ComboboxRoot`, `ComboboxTrigger`, `ComboboxItem`, …) are exported. Filtering uses base-ui's built-in collator against the `label` field of each item; the CLAUDE.md "Select Components" rule is honoured (the wrapper coerces `null` to `""` before invoking the caller's `onValueChange`). **Per-user ordering API** — new `GET`/`PUT /api/settings/dropdown-order` ([route.ts](src/app/api/settings/dropdown-order/route.ts)) stores a JSON map keyed `dropdown_order` in the existing `settings` table. Shape: `{ version: 1, lists: { category, account, holding, currency } }`. Identifiers are opaque (numeric IDs for accounts/holdings/categories, ISO codes for currencies) — no display names enter the row, so the value is unencrypted and `requireAuth` is sufficient. Zod-validated; max 5000 entries per list. No schema migration required (rides the existing `settings` table). **Helpers** — [src/lib/dropdown-order.ts](src/lib/dropdown-order.ts) provides the pure `sortByUserOrder` function (pinned-first by saved index, unpinned in fallback comparator order) plus `parseDropdownOrder` for safe JSON ingest. [src/components/dropdown-order-provider.tsx](src/components/dropdown-order-provider.tsx) fetches the saved order once on mount and exposes a `useDropdownOrder(kind)` hook that returns a memoized sorter; mounted inside `(app)/layout.tsx` next to `<CurrencyProvider>`. **Phase-4 migrations** — replaced `<Select>` with `<Combobox>` at the user-data-backed call sites: budgets (3 categories), goals (account), loans (account), subscriptions (category + account + currency), accounts (currency on add + edit), settings (rule `assignCategoryId`), fx-overrides (currency), transactions split-dialog (category + account), and transactions/page.tsx (add-transaction account/category, advanced holding picker, split-row category, transfer source/destination accounts, filter account/category, bulk-edit category/account). Short fixed enums (transaction type I/E/T/R, frequency, account group/type, date presets) stay on `<Select>`. The transfer dialog's source/destination *holding* pickers retain their custom-sentinel logic on `<Select>` for now and remain a Phase-4 follow-up. **Privacy note** — no category HMAC `name_lookup` is shipped yet; category ordering keys to numeric IDs for now. The issue explicitly OKs this fallback; a future Section G UI may switch to HMACs without an API change because the Zod schema accepts both `string` and `number` entries. **Ops impact** — code-only; no schema migration, no env vars, no cron. First load returns an empty `lists: {}`, so every dropdown falls through to alphabetical fallback — exactly today's behaviour. Users opt in by visiting the (forthcoming) Section G setup page.
- **Restore Category column + expand /transactions column customization (2026-04-29, closes [#20](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/20)).** Two related fixes to the `/transactions` table — (a) the Category badge has been silently empty for every Stream-D Phase-3 user since cutover, and the Reports tabs collapsed every category into one "Uncategorized" bucket for the same reason; (b) the column-prefs UX only had a single `localStorage`-backed Portfolio toggle. **Backend — restore decrypted display names through the JOIN.** [`getTransactions()`](src/lib/queries.ts) now selects `categories.name_ct`, `accounts.name_ct`, `accounts.alias_ct`, `portfolio_holdings.symbol`, and `portfolio_holdings.symbol_ct`. `/api/transactions` GET ([route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/route.ts)) and `/api/transactions/linked` ([linked/route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/linked/route.ts)) decrypt those fields with `decryptName()` (auth-tag failure ladder) and strip the `*_ct` columns before serializing. Without this, every Phase-3-NULL'd row returned `categoryName: null` and the Category cell only showed `<Link2 />` / split badges. **Backend — Reports group on stable id.** [/api/reports](src/app/api/reports/route.ts) (income-statement + tax-summary), [/api/reports/trends](src/app/api/reports/trends/route.ts), and [/api/reports/yoy](src/app/api/reports/yoy/route.ts) now `GROUP BY categories.id` (previously the now-NULL `categories.name`) and resolve `name_ct` per group after the SQL aggregation. Behavior change worth flagging: rows with duplicate category names that previously collided now report separately. Balance-sheet's account name decrypts via the same ladder. **New endpoint `/api/settings/tx-columns`** ([route.ts](src/app/api/settings/tx-columns/route.ts)) — GET/PUT round-trips a JSON blob through the existing `settings(key, user_id)` table under `key='tx_table_columns'`. Zod-validated; defaults merged on read so adding a new column id later doesn't force a migration. No schema change required. **Frontend** ([transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx)) — replaced the `TxColPrefs = { portfolio: boolean }` localStorage hook with a `ColumnPref[]` (visibility + order) backed by the new endpoint. The Columns dropdown lists every toggleable column + a "Reset to default" action. Native HTML5 `draggable` on `<TableHead>` reorders columns; order persists via the same hook (400ms debounce). Legacy `localStorage["pf-tx-cols-v1"]` Portfolio toggle migrates on first load and is then cleared. The page renders columns from a single `columnPrefs.filter(visible).map(...)` pass instead of the previous hard-coded `TableHead` / `TableCell` blocks. **Out of scope.** `accountDescription` toggle dropped — the column doesn't exist on `accounts` and a schema add is out of bounds for a UI/docs-scoped issue. Drag UX is mouse-only; the checkbox toggles in the dropdown remain a keyboard-accessible alternative for visibility (order remains drag-only).
- **`transactions.portfolio_holding` retired end-to-end (Phase 5 + 6, 2026-04-29, [#18](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/pull/18)).** The legacy encrypted text column on `transactions` is gone; the integer FK `transactions.portfolio_holding_id → portfolio_holdings.id` (introduced 2026-04-26 in [#7](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/pull/7)) is now the sole source of truth, and the holding name is sourced via JOIN to `portfolio_holdings`. **Gate fix (root cause).** The `withoutFk` count on prod was permanently stuck at 4 — all 4 rows belonged to the demo user. [scripts/seed-demo.ts](scripts/seed-demo.ts) inserted investment transactions BEFORE the matching `portfolio_holdings` rows, so the seed never set the FK. The lazy-on-login backfill caught them when the demo user logged in, but the nightly reseed wiped the data and re-inserted 4 broken rows, so `withoutFk ≥ 4` between reset and next demo login. Fix: insert holdings first, capture their ids, bind `portfolio_holding_id` directly. After the seed-script fix + a manual reseed, gate dropped to 0. **Phase 5 — code cutover ([a80dcbb](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/a80dcbb)):** migration [scripts/migrate-tx-portfolio-holding-phase5-null.sql](scripts/migrate-tx-portfolio-holding-phase5-null.sql) NULLed the legacy column on every backfilled row (1200 rows on prod). Dropped `"portfolioHolding"` from `TX_ENCRYPTED_FIELDS`. Deleted orphan-fallback decrypt loops in `/api/portfolio/overview`, `holdings-value.ts`, and `mcp-server` `aggregateHoldings()` — the portfolio aggregator no longer needs the DEK. POST/PUT `/api/transactions` `delete data.portfolioHolding` before persisting; GET resolves `portfolioHolding` (name) via the Stream D `name_lookup` HMAC and filters SQL-side. **Phase 6 — DROP COLUMN ([d28258e](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/d28258e)):** migration [scripts/migrate-tx-portfolio-holding-phase6-drop-column.sql](scripts/migrate-tx-portfolio-holding-phase6-drop-column.sql) with a defensive pre-check that refuses to run if any non-NULL plaintext remains. `getTransactions` JOINs `portfolio_holdings` and surfaces `{ portfolioHoldingId, portfolioHoldingName, portfolioHoldingNameCt }`; the `/api/transactions` GET handler decrypts `name_ct` (with plaintext name fallback) and presents it to the UI as `portfolioHolding` so the client contract is unchanged. Schema-pg.ts removes the column from the Drizzle definition. Import pipeline stops encrypting the column; the in-memory `portfolioHolding` field stays on the insertable shape (resolver uses it) and is stripped before `db.insert` via destructuring. **Retired (deleted):** `src/lib/crypto/portfolio-holding-fk-backfill.ts`, `src/app/api/admin/portfolio-holding-fk-progress/route.ts`. Removed the `enqueuePortfolioHoldingFkBackfill` call sites in `auth/login` + `auth/mfa/verify`. **Follow-up fix (post-merge).** Caught after the merge: `analyze_holding` and `get_investment_insights` in both HTTP MCP ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts)) and stdio MCP ([mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts)) still SELECTed and grouped by the dropped column. They were not on the original Phase 5 hit-list (only the three "orphan-fallback" sites were called out). Switched all of them to FK + JOIN aggregation. Without this fix, every MCP `analyze_holding` / `get_investment_insights` invocation on prod would have raised `column "portfolio_holding" does not exist`. **Order of operations.** Phase 5 was migration-first then code (NULLing is backwards-compatible — old code reads NULL gracefully). Phase 6 was code-first then migration (DROP COLUMN is backwards-incompatible — old code SELECTs the column). [PHASE5_PORTFOLIO_HOLDING_FK.md](../PHASE5_PORTFOLIO_HOLDING_FK.md) drove the work; marked complete.
- **Stream D Phase 3: per-user lazy plaintext NULL on login (2026-04-29, closes [#7](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/7)).** Replaces the eager all-or-nothing [`scripts/migrate-stream-d-phase3-null.sql`](scripts/migrate-stream-d-phase3-null.sql) (applied to prod 2026-04-24, blocked on staging + dev because demo's lazy backfill never finished and the strict pre-check rolled back the whole transaction) with a per-user lazy variant that runs on each successful login. Three gates before NULLing a user's plaintext name columns: (1) `users.plaintext_nulled_at IS NULL`, (2) the user's own Stream D backfill is complete (no plaintext-but-no-ct rows across the six Stream D tables), (3) a sample row's `name_ct` actually decrypts with the cached DEK. Stragglers and DEK-mismatch users keep their dual-write fallback indefinitely; healthy users get Phase 3'd one-by-one. Doubles as the diagnostic for the unresolved pathfinder DEK-mismatch — the `[phase3-null] user=… table=… sample decrypt failed` warn names the user, the table, and the underlying error, replacing the planned `PF_CRYPTO_DEBUG=1` fingerprint deploy. New helper [src/lib/crypto/stream-d-phase3-null.ts](src/lib/crypto/stream-d-phase3-null.ts) exposes `nullPlaintextIfReady(userId, dek)` (atomic single-transaction NULL across the six tables, sets `users.plaintext_nulled_at`) and `enqueuePhase3NullIfReady` (fire-and-forget login wrapper that never blocks login). Wired one line each into [src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts](src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts) + [src/app/api/auth/mfa/verify/route.ts](src/app/api/auth/mfa/verify/route.ts) right after the existing `enqueueStreamDBackfill` call. Schema migration [scripts/migrate-stream-d-phase3-per-user.sql](scripts/migrate-stream-d-phase3-per-user.sql) drops NOT NULL on the six plaintext name columns (idempotent — prod's eager migration already did the same) and adds `users.plaintext_nulled_at text`. **Apply per env BEFORE deploying the matching code** ([docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md) playbook). Applied to dev on 2026-04-29; required on staging + prod before this code ships. Drizzle schema intentionally unchanged — helper writes via raw SQL bypassing Drizzle's typing so the six plaintext name columns can stay `.notNull()` and downstream consumers don't have to handle `string | null` cascades. Admin status `GET /api/admin/phase3-null-progress` reports `{ usersTotal, usersDone, usersPending, pendingDetail[] }` with per-user blocking-row counts. Eager migration retired with a SUPERSEDED comment header — kept as the record of prod's 2026-04-24 eager run.
- **`deploy.sh` no longer runs `npm run db:push` (2026-04-29, closes [#5](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/5)).** The step looked like automated schema sync but silently no-op'd on every deploy: `DATABASE_URL` from the systemd unit doesn't survive the `sudo -u $REPO_OWNER` hop in `run_as`, so Drizzle-Kit fell back to its hardcoded `postgres://localhost:5432/pf` default and either failed authentication silently or connected to the wrong DB. Symptom surfaced shipping the investment-account constraint — Deploy Dev reported success, but `accounts.is_investment` wasn't actually added to the dev DB and `/api/dashboard` 500'd until the SQL migration was run by hand. Removed the step in favor of the existing per-env psql playbook in [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md), which is what we already use in practice. Deploy is now strictly "pull + install + build + restart"; the database is the human's responsibility before pushing the matching code. CLAUDE.md gotcha + DEPLOY.md updated to match.
- **Transactions: descriptive account labels in filter + table; optional Portfolio column (2026-04-29, [#10](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/10)).** The Account filter dropdown previously rendered `account.name` verbatim — terse/numeric names like `609` (a credit-card account named after the last 4 digits) read as a stray number with no context. New helper [src/lib/account-label.ts](src/lib/account-label.ts) (`formatAccountLabel({ name, alias, type })`) prepends the account `type` when the display string is ≤ 4 chars or all-digits, so `609` of type `Credit Card` now renders as `Credit Card · 609`. Alias takes precedence over name when set; descriptive names (≥ 5 chars, non-numeric) render unchanged. The same helper is used in the table row's Account cell so the dropdown and the rows stay aligned. `getTransactions()` in [src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts) now surfaces `accountAlias` + `accountType` from the existing `accounts` join — same plaintext-fallback story as `accounts.name` already on this query; Stream-D-correct decryption of `alias_ct` for table-row labelling is a separate follow-up. Also added a "Columns" affordance in the filter bar — a `DropdownMenu` with a "Portfolio" checkbox, persisted to `localStorage` key `pf-tx-cols-v1` and **off by default** so users without investment activity don't see a column of dashes. When toggled on, a Portfolio column renders between Payee and Note showing `t.portfolioHolding || "-"`. `getTransactions()` already selected `portfolioHolding` so no API change. Out of scope (called out in the issue): JOIN-against-`portfolio_holdings`-with-Stream-D-decryption migration, gated on Stream D Phase 5.
- **Edit Transfer dialog now opens for any transfer-shaped pair (2026-04-29, [#8](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/8)).** Previously only clean R↔R pairs (both legs in a `category_type='R'` category) hit the unified Edit Transfer dialog — anything else fell back to the legacy single-leg edit with a "Linked transaction" pill, even when the row was clearly half of a transfer. Dropped the `category_type==='R'` requirement on both sides: client `startEdit()` in [src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx) now opens the unified dialog whenever the row has a `link_id`, exactly one sibling, and a different counterparty account; server `loadTransferPair` in [src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts) accepts the same shape (kept the 2-row + different-accounts structural checks). `updateTransferPair` already leaves `category_id` untouched on save, so the user's renamed category (e.g. `Non-Cash - Transfers`) survives the edit. Stdio MCP path (`loadTransferPairViaSql`) intentionally NOT relaxed — this issue is UI/REST-scoped only. The legacy fallback (3+ leg WP liquidations and same-account conversions) gained a small sky-tinted advisory line above the linked-siblings pills: "This transaction is part of a multi-leg group; legs are edited individually." — so the user understands why category/payee/business-expense fields are showing on a transfer-shaped row.
- **Portfolio allocation charts now value-weighted (2026-04-29, [#6](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/6)).** The two donut charts on `/portfolio` ("By Asset Type" and "By Account") previously sized slices by **holding count** — a $1k ETF position counted the same as a $200k one, so the chart didn't reflect actual exposure. Both charts now key on **market value in display currency**: builders in [src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/portfolio/page.tsx) swap `v.count` → `v.value`, baseline from `summary.totalHoldings` → `summary.totalValueDisplay`, account sort flipped from count to value (legend orders by largest position first). Legend amounts render through `formatCurrency(d.value, displayCurrency)` so they show e.g. `$48,210 (35%)` rather than `21 (35%)`. New `hideZeroAllocations` state (default `true`) drops rows whose value is `<= 0.005` — half-cent boundary keeps anything that visibly rounds to 0% silenced while staying float-safe — fully-sold positions still in the DB no longer add 0% slices. A "Hiding $0" / "Showing all" toggle in each card header is bound to one shared state so clicking either flips both charts. API was already populating `byType[t].value` + `byAccount[a].value` in [src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts) — pure client-side change. `ExposurePieTooltip` unchanged; the rounded percentage it shows now reflects value-weighting.
- **Default theme is now dark for new users (2026-04-28, [#4](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/4)).** Single-line flip in [src/app/layout.tsx](src/app/layout.tsx): `defaultTheme="system"` → `defaultTheme="dark"`. New visitors and fresh signups land in dark mode on first paint instead of mirroring `prefers-color-scheme`, aligning the app with the brand baseline (premium fintech dark palette in [docs/ui-design.md](docs/ui-design.md)) and the marketing-site / product screenshots. Existing users keep whatever they explicitly chose — `next-themes` persists toggles in `localStorage`, so this only affects users with no stored preference. `enableSystem` stayed on so a future 3-state toggle can be added without another provider change. The light/dark `theme-toggle.tsx` is unchanged. No env vars, no schema migration, plain code-only change.

### Fixed
- **Friendly 400 for missing category on transaction create/update (2026-04-28, commits [`72f3a8d`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/72f3a8d) + [`eda999a`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/eda999a)).** `POST /api/transactions` accepted `categoryId: z.number()`, which let the UI's `0` "no selection" sentinel through to the INSERT and surfaced as a 500 on the FK violation. Tightened the Zod schema (`accountId` + `categoryId` require positive ints with friendly messages "Please pick a category" / "Please pick an account"); same for `PUT`. Added a 23503 FK-violation catch in both handlers as defense-in-depth — covers stale UI forms referencing a deleted category / account / holding, returning `code: "fk_violation"` instead of leaking the SQL error. UI form ([src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx)) now refuses the submit with an inline `submitError` ("Pick an account" / "Pick a category" / "Enter an amount") matching the existing transfer-handler validation pattern, so the user sees the issue before the round-trip.

### Added
- **Investment-account constraint — every transaction in a flagged account must reference a portfolio holding (2026-04-28).** New `accounts.is_investment` boolean flag with a per-account opt-in checkbox in the account edit dialog. Once set, every transaction in that account must reference a `portfolio_holdings` row (FK `transactions.portfolio_holding_id`) — trades point at their security; cash legs (deposits, dividends paid as cash, fees, transfers) point at a per-account "Cash" holding using the existing currency-as-holding pattern. Closes the long-standing gap where the portfolio aggregator's orphan-fallback path silently dropped null-FK / null-text rows from cost-basis math, dividend totals, and per-holding performance — even though those rows still affected the cash balance.

  - **Schema** — `ALTER TABLE accounts ADD COLUMN is_investment boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false` ([scripts/migrate-accounts-is-investment.sql](scripts/migrate-accounts-is-investment.sql)). Idempotent. No DB CHECK constraint — enforcement is application-layer (mirrors the four-check transfer-pair pattern in [src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts) since PostgreSQL can't express "NOT NULL only when accounts.is_investment is true" without a trigger). The migration also runs a heuristic backfill (any account with ≥1 `portfolio_holdings` row pre-migration is flagged), ensures a per-account `name='Cash', symbol=NULL, currency=accounts.currency` holding for each flagged account, and points all unattributed null-FK / null-text rows at it. Rows with the legacy plaintext `portfolio_holding` text column populated stay untouched — Phase-4 lazy resolver routes them to their actual holding on next login.

  - **Centralized helper** [src/lib/investment-account.ts](src/lib/investment-account.ts) — `requireHoldingForInvestmentAccount` (strict, throws `InvestmentHoldingRequiredError` for REST + MCP HTTP write tools), `defaultHoldingForInvestmentAccount` (permissive — returns the per-account Cash holding id for import + transfer-pair construction), `getOrCreateCashHolding` (find-or-create using the same plaintext+lookup dual-index pattern as the existing portfolio-holding-resolver, tolerates `dek=null` for stdio MCP), and `backfillInvestmentAccount` (the migration's logic scoped to a single account, called from `PUT /api/accounts` on a false→true toggle).

  - **Call-site coverage** — `createTransaction` / `updateTransaction` ([src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts)), `createTransferPair` + `createTransferPairViaSql` ([src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts), pure-cash legs default to Cash), `executeImport` ([src/lib/import-pipeline.ts](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts), post-resolver Cash-default pass per investment account), MCP HTTP `record_transaction` / `bulk_record_transactions` / `update_transaction` ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts)) take the strict path. The orphan-aggregator path stays in place — staging/dev users still rely on it until Phase 5 cutover.

  - **Stdio MCP** `record_transaction` / `bulk_record_transactions` refuse writes to investment accounts with a pointer to the HTTP MCP at `/mcp` — stdio MCP doesn't expose `portfolioHolding` parameters, so it can't satisfy the constraint. Documented as a self-hosted limitation alongside the existing "stdio writes are plaintext" gotcha. Stdio `record_transfer` is unaffected because it routes through `createTransferPairViaSql` which got the Cash-default treatment.

  - **Account toggle side-effect** — `PUT /api/accounts` with `isInvestment: true` runs the same backfill the SQL migration does (Cash holding ensure + null-FK reassignment) atomically with the flag flip. Backfill failure surfaces a 500 with an actionable error so the user can retry. `true → false` just flips the flag; existing data stays attributed.

  - **Admin queue** `GET /api/admin/investment-orphans` ([src/app/api/admin/investment-orphans/route.ts](src/app/api/admin/investment-orphans/route.ts)) reports rows in investment accounts that still have NULL FK after Phase-4 lazy backfill — typically legacy WP imports where the connector failed to populate even the plaintext text column. Surfaces a count + capped 200-row sample, no payee plaintext through this endpoint.

  - **UI** "Investment account" checkbox added to the account edit dialog at [src/app/(app)/accounts/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/accounts/page.tsx); `AccountBalance` + `getAccountBalances` plumb `isInvestment` through.

  - **Migration playbook** in [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md) under `accounts-is-investment`. **Run the SQL BEFORE deploying the matching code** so the application-layer constraint check has the column to read.

- **MCP holding-id ergonomics — bind transactions to positions by name OR ticker (2026-04-28, commits [`ca0a117`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/ca0a117) + [`f429c6f`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/f429c6f)).** Two follow-ups closing the gap reported in the [2026-04-28 Huron Sale conversation](https://claude.ai/share/a066166f-c5ee-4abe-a01c-25868afafb1c) — Claude recorded a stock sale via the MCP, the `quantity` field made it through (the [`7325448`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/7325448) fix from earlier the same day), but `transactions.portfolio_holding_id` was left NULL so the position never moved. The Edit Transaction dialog rendered `Portfolio Holding: None` for a row with `quantity=-48`. Root cause was upstream of the write tools: `get_portfolio_analysis` and `analyze_holding` never returned the holding's integer id, so an agent had no way to populate the existing `portfolioHoldingId` parameter except by calling `add_portfolio_holding` (which would create a duplicate). The aggregator's `accumulate()` already plumbed `holding_id` through ("first non-null id wins"); the response mappers were dropping it on the floor. Same shape on the stdio twins.

  - **[`ca0a117`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/ca0a117) — Phase 1 (id surfacing) + Phase 2 (name-string write ergonomics).** `get_portfolio_analysis` now emits `id: <number>` per holding; `analyze_holding` emits `holdingId: <number>` at the top level. Both transports. `analyze_holding`'s HTTP find is tightened to prefer rows whose decrypted `portfolio_holding === holdingName` so payee-only fuzzy matches don't surface another holding's id when the analyzed string was a non-investment cash payee like `"Huron Sale"`. The three HTTP write tools (`record_transaction` / `bulk_record_transactions` / `update_transaction`) accept a new `portfolioHolding: string` parameter alongside the integer `portfolioHoldingId`. New helper `resolvePortfolioHoldingByName` in [register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) — single-shot SELECT, exact case-insensitive match scoped to the resolved account, dual-cohort handling (plaintext `name` + `name_lookup` HMAC for Phase-3 NULL'd rows). Lookup-only — preserves the existing policy that MCP write tools never auto-create holdings (only the import pipeline does). Errors with a candidate list on miss; errors when both `portfolioHolding` and `portfolioHoldingId` are passed and resolve to different ids ("I named X but you bound Y" is worse than rejecting). Stdio write tools unchanged — they still don't bind `portfolio_holding` per the existing carve-out.

  - **[`f429c6f`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/f429c6f) — Phase 2.5: ticker resolution.** Follow-up after verifying Phase 2 on dev exposed an inconsistency: `update_transaction(portfolioHolding: "HURN")` errored "not found" while `"Huron Consulting Group Inc."` worked, even though `get_portfolio_analysis(symbols: ["HURN"])` already matched the ticker via the `symbol` column. Resolver SQL extended to also check `LOWER(symbol)` plaintext + `symbol_lookup` HMAC alongside name/name_lookup — the same `nameLookup(dek, trimmed)` HMAC value matches both columns since the HMAC is over trimmed-lowercase input regardless of source. Candidate-list error reformatted as `"Name (TICKER)"` so the agent sees both identifiers. `analyze_holding(symbol)` HTTP path now JOINs `ph.symbol_ct`, decrypts per-row, and the in-memory filter checks symbol via **exact** case-insensitive equality (substring on a 3-4 char ticker would match "GE" inside "ORANGE" — name + payee retain substring matching for the long-string ergonomics). Stdio twin same shape — `LEFT JOIN portfolio_holdings ph` + `OR LOWER(ph.symbol) = LOWER(?)` in the WHERE. The tool's description always claimed "fuzzy match on name OR symbol"; this commit makes it honest.

  - **End-to-end verified on dev** ([Deploy Dev runs `25076508331` + `25077631853`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/actions)): (a) `get_portfolio_analysis(symbols: ["Huron"])` → response includes `id: 452`. (b) `update_transaction({id: 30204, portfolioHolding: "Huron Consulting Group Inc."})` → resolves to id 452, succeeds. (c) `update_transaction({id: 30204, portfolioHolding: "DoesNotExist"})` → errors with `Candidates (name (ticker)): Huron Consulting Group Inc. (HURN), Fidelity - USD (USD), Fidelity - CAD (CAD).`. (d) `update_transaction({id: 30204, portfolioHoldingId: 999999, portfolioHolding: "Huron Consulting Group Inc."})` → errors `portfolioHolding "..." resolves to id #452, but portfolioHoldingId=999999 disagrees.`. (e) After Phase 2.5: `update_transaction({id: 30204, portfolioHolding: "HURN"})` → succeeds (was: error). (f) `analyze_holding(symbol: "HURN")` → finds Huron, returns `holdingId: 452`, full transaction history (was: "No transactions found").

  - **No schema migrations.** No tool count change. Documented in [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md) "Binding transactions to portfolio holdings".

- **First-class Transfer flow + in-kind / portfolio support (2026-04-28, branch [`feat/transfer-flow`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/tree/feat/transfer-flow) merged via [`5d945a6`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/5d945a6); five commits cf411ba → a81d9a7 → c57fe1b → 2c320fc → 269b381 → c533781).** End-to-end primitive for "move money/shares between two of my accounts." Replaces the prior "two manual transactions, no link" pattern and the "edit one leg, click linked-sibling pointer to the other" UX. Handles same-currency cash transfers, cross-currency cash transfers (with editable "Amount received" override to lock the bank's actual landed amount), in-kind / share-based transfers between brokerage accounts, same-account in-kind rebalances (move shares between two holdings inside one brokerage), and asymmetric in-kind events for stock splits / mergers / share-class conversions.

  - **UI** — new "Transfer" tab in the Add/Edit dialog ([src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/transactions/page.tsx)). On edit of a row that satisfies the four-check rule (link_id non-null + 1 sibling + both type='R' + different accounts), the dialog auto-opens in unified Transfer mode with BOTH legs visible in one window — replacing the legacy "Linked transaction" pointer panel for clean transfer pairs. Non-symmetric multi-leg imports (WP liquidations, etc.) still use the existing linked-siblings panel for navigation. The "In-kind" toggle exposes Source holding + Quantity + Destination holding + Destination quantity. Holdings dropdowns show " · X shares" for every option (including 0 for empty positions). The destination defaults to "Same as source" with a hint about whether it'll bind to an existing dest holding or auto-create — overridable to bind to a different label OR to a brand-new name. Destination quantity defaults to source quantity but accepts a different value with an amber "Asymmetric" hint when they differ.

  - **REST endpoint** `POST/PUT/DELETE /api/transactions/transfer` ([src/app/api/transactions/transfer/route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/transfer/route.ts)) — atomic dual-INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE inside a single `db.transaction()`. Server-generated UUID `link_id` (never accepted from client). Auto-creates a Transfer category (`type='R'`) on first use, mirroring the WP ZIP orchestrator pattern. Tri-state PUT for the in-kind side: omit = leave alone, null = clear, value = (re)bind. Same for `destHoldingName` + `destQuantity`. 409 `fx-currency-needs-override` carries a `side: 'source' | 'destination'` discriminator so the UI can highlight the offending leg.

  - **MCP** — three new tools `record_transfer` / `update_transfer` / `delete_transfer` on both transports (HTTP **78 → 81**, stdio **72 → 75**). HTTP variant gates on session DEK + dual-writes encrypted payee/note/tags via `encryptTxWrite`; stdio variant runs dek-less per existing convention but still uses an atomic single-pg-client transaction (sidesteps the pg-compat `transaction()` helper's footgun where inner `prepare()` calls acquire fresh pool clients). All three reuse the shared helpers in [src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts).

  - **Shared helper** [src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts) — `createTransferPair` / `loadTransferPair` / `updateTransferPair` / `deleteTransferPair` (Drizzle, used by REST + HTTP MCP) plus `*ViaSql` variants (raw `pg.Pool` for stdio MCP). Single source of truth for both transports — same validation, same FX resolution, same atomic write semantics. Source holding lookup is strict (refuses if missing — you can't send shares you don't have); destination resolves via the existing `buildHoldingResolver` ([src/lib/external-import/portfolio-holding-resolver.ts](src/lib/external-import/portfolio-holding-resolver.ts)) which auto-creates with Stream D dual-write. A no-op guard rejects if source AND destination resolve to the SAME holding row (would silently write two cancelling rows).

  - **`/api/transactions/linked` extended** ([src/app/api/transactions/linked/route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/linked/route.ts)) to return `categoryType` + `accountCurrency` + `entered_*` fields + a JOIN on `portfolio_holdings` so the client can run the four-check rule and pre-fill the unified edit view without a second round-trip. The holding name resolution uses a fallback ladder: JOINed plaintext → JOINed `nameCt` decrypted with session DEK → legacy text column on the tx (already decrypted).

  - **`getPortfolioHoldings` returns `currentShares`** ([src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts)) — correlated subquery summing `transactions.quantity` per holding via the `portfolio_holding_id` FK, cast to `::float` so the pg driver returns a JS number rather than a numeric string. Powers the share-count display in the Transfer dialog dropdowns.

  - **No schema migrations** — `transactions.link_id`, `transactions.portfolio_holding_id`, `transactions.quantity` columns already existed from prior batches. Encryption invariants kept: `link_id` stays plaintext (excluded from `TX_ENCRYPTED_FIELDS`), payee/note/tags encrypted via `encryptTxWrite`, `requireEncryption()` gate on the new POST/PUT/DELETE endpoint and on MCP HTTP tools.

- **MCP `record_transaction` / `bulk_record_transactions` / `update_transaction` accept `quantity` (2026-04-28, commit [`7325448`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/7325448)).** All three HTTP MCP write tools now expose an optional `quantity: number` parameter so an LLM booking a stock/ETF/crypto row can move the holding's share count, not just the cash side. Closes the gap reported in the [2026-04-27 Fidelity reconciliation conversation](https://claude.ai/share/e2f2415d-0276-4b84-b8a7-af7c4805f8a3) — Claude correctly identified 11 missing HURN rows + 6 dividends, booked them via `bulk_record_transactions`, but the holding's share count never moved because the tool's Zod schema + raw INSERT both omitted the `quantity` column. The `transactions.quantity` column already existed (`schema-pg.ts:76`, `doublePrecision`, nullable), the import pipeline + REST POST/PUT already wrote it, and the portfolio aggregator's `SUM(CASE WHEN quantity > 0 …)` already read it — the MCP write path was the only blind spot. Tool descriptions now encode the canonical sign convention (matches [packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/zip-parser.ts:103](packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/zip-parser.ts) and the SQL aggregator at [/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts:186](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts)):
  - **RSU vest net of tax** → `amount=0, quantity=+net_shares`
  - **ESPP / plain buy** → `amount=negative_cash, quantity=+shares`
  - **Sell** → `amount=positive_proceeds, quantity=-shares`
  - **Dividend / interest / cash-only** → omit `quantity`

  `update_transaction` accepts `.nullable().optional()` quantity so callers can backfill or clear. End-to-end tested on dev against the same HURN row from the share-link conversation: T1 `record_transaction(qty=+1.5)` moved 71.3701 → 72.8701; T2 `bulk_record_transactions` with mixed signs (+0.7, −0.5, dividend-no-qty) moved by +0.2 net; T3 created a row without qty then `update_transaction({id, quantity: 2.0})` moved by +2.0. Cleanup brought HURN back to 71.3701 baseline. HTTP MCP only — stdio MCP `register-core-tools.ts` versions still lack both `quantity` and `portfolioHoldingId` (pre-existing asymmetry, separate follow-up).

- **Precious-metals spot rates via stooq.com (2026-04-28, branch [`feat/precious-metals-fx`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/tree/feat/precious-metals-fx), three commits).** XAU (gold), XAG (silver), XPT (platinum), and XPD (palladium) are now first-class supported currencies — auto-fetched from stooq's free unauthenticated CSV endpoint, registered in `SUPPORTED_CURRENCIES` so symbol-info classifies them as cash positions, and routed through a new metal-aware quoting rule in the portfolio overview so a gold holding in a USD account displays as `US$32,287` (6.9 oz × ~$4,679/oz spot) instead of `XAU 6.90`.

  - **[`e3a0e3a`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/e3a0e3a) — Stooq fetch path parallel to crypto.** Triggered by a user report that a Gold Ounce holding showed ~$2 CAD instead of ~$13,000. Root cause: XAU wasn't in `SUPPORTED_FIAT_CURRENCIES`, Yahoo's `<CCY>USD=X` pattern returns HTTP 404 for `XAUUSD=X` (verified live), and there was no fallback in `FALLBACK_RATE_TO_USD` — so [`getRateToUsdDetailed`](src/lib/fx-service.ts) walked all six lookup tiers and returned `{rate: 1, source: "fallback"}` (1 oz of gold = 1 USD). New `fetchStooqMetalRateToUsd(currency, date)` in [src/lib/fx-service.ts](src/lib/fx-service.ts) maps `XAU → "xauusd"` etc. and parses stooq's CSV at `https://stooq.com/q/l/?s=xauusd&f=sd2t2ohlc&h&e=csv` for current dates and `https://stooq.com/q/d/l/?s=xauusd&d1=...&d2=...&i=d` for historical (5-day window covers weekend/holiday gaps). New `SUPPORTED_METAL_CURRENCIES` constant + `isMetalCurrency()` helper in [src/lib/fx/supported-currencies.ts](src/lib/fx/supported-currencies.ts). `getRateToUsdDetailed` routing extended from binary (crypto/Yahoo) to 3-way (crypto→CoinGecko, metal→stooq, fiat→Yahoo). Hardcoded fallbacks added (XAU≈4700, XAG≈75, XPT≈1000, XPD≈1000) only used when stooq is unreachable AND nothing is cached AND no override exists.

  - **[`ded2304`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/ded2304) — Metal symbols quote in holding currency, not symbol.** Initial symptom after the stooq fetch shipped: gold showed `XAU 6.90` market value, `XAU 0.51` avg cost — numerically correct (cost basis was being converted USD→XAU at the cross-rate) but unreadable. Nobody quotes gold "in XAU per XAU" — they quote it in dollars per ounce. Two-place fix in [/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts) `quoteCurrencyById` map (line 395-407) and the cash-position branch (line 542-554): when symbol is a metal AND symbol ≠ holding currency, treat the symbol as a tradeable unit priced in `h.currency` — `price = (symbol→display) / (h.currency→display)` cross-rate, `quoteCurrency = h.currency`, `marketValue = qty × price (in h.currency)`. Pure fiat-cash positions (`USD` cash in a CAD account, etc.) keep the symbol as quote so users see the actual currency they hold. Same logic mirrored in [src/lib/holdings-value.ts:227-244](src/lib/holdings-value.ts) so per-account dashboard balances stay consistent.

  - **[`2b2edc0`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/2b2edc0) — Two follow-up bugs.** (a) The cross-rate price computation needed both `fxRates.get("XAU")` AND `fxRates.get("USD")`, but `fxRates` only got populated from `h.currency` (USD) and the chosen `quoteCurrencyById` values (also USD here, because of the metal-quote rule), so XAU never made it into the map and the price column showed `"—"`. Fix: pre-add any metal symbol seen on a holding to the `currencies` set before populating `fxRates`. (b) The Edit Holding dialog reset `currencyTouched=false` on open, which let the symbol-info debounce silently rewrite the saved currency on the next lookup — opening a USD-account gold holding would visually flip currency to XAU, and a Save without further edit would corrupt the row. Fix in [src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx:1631](src/app/%28app%29/portfolio/page.tsx): treat any pre-existing `holding.currency` as a manual override on dialog open. Field stays editable.

- **Investment-account balance redesign + unrealized P&L by period (2026-04-27, branch [`feat/account-balance-unrealized-pnl`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/tree/feat/account-balance-unrealized-pnl), four commits).** Triggered by a user-reported discrepancy on a CanadaLife group RRSP: account detail page showed Balance = **$105,872** while the holdings table showed Market value = **$57,409**. The 187 transactions on the account were all "Payroll Deposit HURON ADVISORS" rows (employer contributions auto-invested by the custodian); no buy-leg transactions ever land in Finlynq because CanadaLife's exports don't include them. The old formula at [src/app/api/dashboard/route.ts:49](src/app/api/dashboard/route.ts) was `totalBalance = b.balance + holdings.value`, which double-counted the cash-side of every contribution. Plan + decisions: `plans/can-you-check-the-shiny-key.md`.

  - **[`06345bb`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/06345bb) — Balance redesign (`balance = market value` for accounts with holdings).** [src/app/api/dashboard/route.ts](src/app/api/dashboard/route.ts) now returns `totalBalance = holdings ? holdings.value : b.balance` plus a new `cashFlowBasis` field carrying the transaction sum. The cash sleeve is already inside `holdings.value` via the currency-as-holding pattern (a `CL - CAD` row with 8,672 units × $1), so no double-count. Account detail page ([src/app/(app)/accounts/[id]/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/accounts/%5Bid%5D/page.tsx)) now shows "Market value" as the headline with "Cash flow: $X" below it for any account that has holdings; pure-cash accounts (no holdings) still show a single Balance number unchanged.

  - **[`4df7ba0`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/4df7ba0) — Currency-code symbols treated as cash, not Yahoo tickers.** A regression: with the new balance formula, CanadaLife showed **$1,185,022** instead of $57,409. Root cause: `holdings-value.ts` was passing the literal symbol `"CAD"` (the cash sleeve's symbol) to Yahoo Finance, which returned **Cadiz Inc** (NASDAQ: CAD ≈ $95 USD). 8,672 units × $95 × FX(USD→CAD)=1.37 ≈ $1.13M of phantom value. The same fix was already in `/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts` per the 2026-04-27 currency rework but was never propagated to `holdings-value.ts`. Added [`isCurrencyCodeSymbol()`](src/lib/holdings-value.ts) — excludes 3-4 letter ISO 4217 codes from the Yahoo fetch and routes them through the cash branch (`price=1`, `priceCurrency=symbol`, FX hop to account currency). Verified: CanadaLife now reports $57,409.84.

  - **[`ea6d9fb`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/ea6d9fb) — Per-period unrealized P&L from start-vs-end snapshots.** Previously `valuationGL` was cumulative-since-acquisition with the period only affecting FX baseline — picking MTD vs QTD vs YTD produced the same number, useless for monthly/quarterly/annual reports. Switched to the snapshot-difference model the user asked for: `delta_UGL = UGL_at_periodEnd - UGL_at_periodStart`. Pieces:
    - [src/lib/price-service.ts](src/lib/price-service.ts) — new `fetchQuoteAtDate(symbol, date)` and `fetchMultipleQuotesAtDate(symbols, date)` via Yahoo's `chart` endpoint with `period1`/`period2` spanning the target date ± a small window (handles weekends/holidays).
    - [src/lib/holdings-value.ts](src/lib/holdings-value.ts) — new `HoldingsValueOpts.asOfDate`. Filters transactions to `date <= asOfDate` and routes price/FX through historical endpoints when `asOfDate < today`. Backwards-compat: omitting `asOfDate` preserves the live-quote behavior used by the dashboard.
    - [src/lib/unrealized-pnl.ts](src/lib/unrealized-pnl.ts) — runs `getHoldingsValueByAccount` twice in parallel (one for `periodStart`, one for `periodEnd`). Returns per-account `start` + `end` snapshots plus the headline `valuationGL` / `fxGL` / `totalGL` as the **period delta**. FX G/L = `balanceNative × (FX_end - FX_start)` so a USD savings account translated to CAD shows non-zero `fxGL` over a period where USD/CAD moved, even with no transactions.
    - [/api/reports?type=income-statement](src/app/api/reports/route.ts), the [Reports → Income tab](src/app/(app)/reports/page.tsx) "Unrealized gains / losses (period change)" card, and MCP HTTP [`get_income_statement`](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) all consume the new shape — pick MTD/QTD/YTD/last-month/last-quarter/last-year/last-12 from the existing period selector and the numbers update accordingly.

  - **[`a397c96`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/a397c96) — Price cache as a service layer (mirrors fx_rates pattern).** `fetchQuote` and `fetchMultipleQuotes` now check `price_cache` for a row at `date=today` before any Yahoo call. On miss they fetch live and INSERT the result; on hit they return cached and skip the network entirely. Today's row is reused for the rest of the calendar day; tomorrow rolls over naturally to a new date key. Historical prices (any past date) are immutable — cached forever. New shared helpers `readPriceCache` / `readPriceCacheBulk` / `writePriceCache` so the today / historical / batched paths all share the same idempotent insert. Mirrors the cache ladder in [`getRateToUsdDetailed`](src/lib/fx-service.ts) (override → cached → live + write → nearest → fallback). Result: subsequent dashboard/portfolio loads on the same day are zero-API-call from the price + FX side. Schema unchanged — `price_cache (id, symbol, date, price, currency)` was already in place.

### Fixed
- **MCP `autoCategory` queried a non-existent `transaction_rules.match_payee` column (2026-04-28, commit [`7d70677`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/7d70677)).** Every `record_transaction` / `bulk_record_transactions` call 500'd whenever the user had at least one active rule, with `Failed query: SELECT assign_category_id FROM transaction_rules WHERE user_id = $1 AND is_active = 1 AND (LOWER(match_payee) LIKE $2 OR LOWER($3) LIKE LOWER(match_payee))`. The schema in [src/db/schema-pg.ts:285-300](src/db/schema-pg.ts) is `(match_field, match_type, match_value)` — no `match_payee` column exists, hasn't for a long time. This was the *real* root cause of the "Finlynq server bug on the category-rules lookup when portfolioHoldingId is set" reported in the [2026-04-27 share-link conversation](https://claude.ai/share/e2f2415d-0276-4b84-b8a7-af7c4805f8a3) — it wasn't actually scoped to `portfolioHoldingId` at all, any record/bulk_record call hit it whenever an active rule existed. The share-link Claude's workaround (using a "simpler payee" that didn't match a rule) bypassed the bug by accident. Fix replaces the broken SQL with a bounded in-memory match that mirrors [src/lib/auto-categorize.ts](src/lib/auto-categorize.ts) semantics: pull active payee-rules with a non-null category, then iterate in priority order — `contains` → `payeeLower.includes(valueLower)`, `exact` → equality, `regex` → JS `RegExp`. Rule count per user is small (typically <100), so the in-memory loop is cheaper than four CASE branches in SQL. **Other MCP rule-management tools (`apply_rules_to_uncategorized`, `create_rule`, `list_rules`, `update_rule` in HTTP, plus the stdio versions in [register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts)) all reference `match_payee` similarly and are similarly broken — they were broken before this commit too, no regression, but they need the same parallel migration in a follow-up sweep.**

- **Cross-currency portfolio P&L (Fidelity-CAD bug, 2026-04-27, commit [`21766b9`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/21766b9)).** A holding with `symbol="CAD"` (CAD cash held in a USD Fidelity account) was showing `Avg Cost $0.71` (correct, USD-per-CAD purchase rate), `Mkt Value $4,900 CAD`, and `Unrealized G/L +$1,401.94 (+40.08%)` — the gain was nonsense. Root cause: `marketValue = quantity × price = 4900 CAD` lived in the holding's quote currency, while `totalCostBasis = sum(transactions.amount) = ~$3479 USD` lived in the account's settlement currency. Subtracting them treated `4900 - 3479 = +1421` as a single-currency number. Fix: [/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts) now groups its tx aggregation SQL by `(portfolio_holding_id, entered_currency)` and sums `ABS(entered_amount)` per bucket. A new `quoteCurrencyById` map pre-computes the target currency per holding (cash → symbol code, crypto → "CAD" per crypto-service quirk, stock/ETF → Yahoo's `q.currency`), and each bucket is converted to that currency via FX before merging into `TxAgg`. Cross-rate cache via `getCrossRate(from, to)` keeps repeat lookups free. Same fix applied to the orphan-fallback path. Verified: Fidelity-CAD now shows market value and cost basis in the same currency, so unrealized G/L is meaningful (~+$80 CAD instead of +$1,402 nonsense).

- **MCP + dashboard portfolio aggregator dropped WP-convention buys (2026-04-27, commit [`8046b9b`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/8046b9b)).** Web Portfolio page showed Uniswap with quantity **1.2606** but MCP `analyze_holding` and `get_portfolio_analysis` returned **0** — same DB, same row. Root cause: every aggregator EXCEPT `/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts` keyed buys on `amount < 0`, which silently dropped WealthPosition-imported holding legs (WP records buys as `amount > 0 + quantity > 0` — see CLAUDE.md "Portfolio aggregator" bullet). Three files / six call sites had to be aligned with [route.ts:119-124](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts) which already used the canonical `quantity > 0 = buy` rule:

  - **MCP HTTP** [register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts): `accumulate()` (feeds `get_portfolio_analysis` / `get_portfolio_performance` / `get_investment_insights`); the in-memory loop in `analyze_holding`; the `recentTransactions[].type` display label (was returning `"dividend"` for every WP buy, which was actively misleading even before the count bug was visible).
  - **Dashboard balance-of-holdings** [holdings-value.ts](src/lib/holdings-value.ts): the FK-keyed SQL CASE and the orphan-fallback delta. Affects the "live market value of holdings" rolled into account balances on the dashboard — fixes WP-imported brokerage accounts that were silently undercounting share-bearing holdings.
  - **MCP stdio** [register-core-tools.ts:945](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts): self-hoster `analyze_holding`. Was even worse than HTTP: the `else` branch did `totalShares -= qty`, so a WP buy of `qty=1.26` reported `currentShares: -1.26`.

  **Canonical rule (now applied everywhere):** `quantity > 0` = buy regardless of amount sign · `quantity < 0` = sell regardless of amount sign · `quantity = 0 ∧ amount > 0` = dividend. No DB migration. Verified on prod against the originally-reported Uniswap row: MCP now returns `currentShares: 1.2606`, `avgCostPerShare: 7.93`, `lifetimeCostBasis: 10`, and the recent-tx label is `"buy"`.

- **Cross-tenant FK leak in backup-restore + atomic, strict wipe (2026-04-27).** Two commits ([`5571070`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/5571070) → [`ad87419`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/ad87419)) closing one root cause and one wipe-side hardening, surfaced by the admin's "Wipe Account" 500ing with `Failed query: delete from "accounts" where "accounts"."user_id" = $1`.

  **Root cause — `/api/data/import` strip().** The JSON backup-restore handler at [src/app/api/data/import/route.ts](src/app/api/data/import/route.ts) used a generic `strip(rows, userId)` helper that only deleted `id` and forced `userId`, leaving `accountId` / `categoryId` / `assignCategoryId` columns UNCHANGED from the source backup. The route already builds an `accountIdMap` and `categoryIdMap` (old-id → new-id, populated when accounts and categories are inserted), and `transactions` + `transaction_splits` had explicit per-table remap blocks — but the strip-based inserts for `portfolio_holdings`, `loans`, `goals`, `snapshots`, `recurring_transactions`, `subscriptions`, `budgets`, `budget_templates`, and `transaction_rules` didn't touch the maps. So restoring user A's backup as user B silently wrote rows owned by B but with FK references to A's account/category integer IDs — accepted by Postgres because those IDs still existed in the same DB, producing a cross-tenant data leak that also became a wipe-blocker via FK 23503 the next time A tried to delete their account. Fix: `strip()` now takes optional `{ accountIdMap, categoryIdMap }`, remaps the FK columns in-place, and **throws on an unmapped FK** rather than passing the raw id through (fail-loud — a corrupt or partial backup is now a 500 instead of a silent leak). Every restore call site that touches an FK-bearing table passes the right map; tables with no FKs keep the old single-arg call. Concrete prod blast: a test user `pathfinder` restored the admin's backup, which created 60 portfolio_holdings owned by `pathfinder` but pointing at admin's accounts (IDs 437–461). All 60 rows were cleaned up via SQL as part of the same fix — they had no business existing.

  **Wipe-side hardening — atomicity + strict isolation.** [`wipeUserDataAndRewrap`](src/lib/auth/queries.ts) used to run each `db.delete(...)` as its own auto-commit, so a late failure (like the FK violation above) left the user signed in to a half-wiped account whose DEK was never rotated, and re-running the wipe just looped on the same FK. Wrap the entire delete sequence + the final DEK rewrap in a single `db.transaction(async (tx) => ...)` — atomic. Every delete still filters strictly by `user_id` only — **never by FK reach** — so the wipe can ONLY remove rows owned by the wiping user. If a cross-tenant row exists, the wipe fails with FK 23503 + transaction rollback rather than silently destroying another user's data. (An earlier version of `5571070` did add a defensive `account_id`-keyed sweep across all 8 referrer tables; that was reverted in `ad87419` because the right fix is to prevent cross-tenant rows from being created in the first place — the `strip()` change above.) The `mcp_uploads` file unlink loop runs BEFORE the DB transaction so we'd rather leak a DB row than orphan a plaintext file on disk if the wipe later fails.

- **Decryption resilience for stale-DEK ciphertext (2026-04-27).** Three-commit batch ([`152e4e6`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/152e4e6) → [`b45c9cf`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/b45c9cf) → [`efd7de2`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/efd7de2)) that hardens every read path against AES-GCM auth-tag failures so a single undecryptable row no longer 500s the whole endpoint. Triggered by a prod incident on user `pathfinder` whose `name_ct` ciphertext columns can't be decrypted with the current cached DEK (root cause still under investigation — scheduled agent to dig in).

  **Soft-fallback decryption.** [decryptName](src/lib/crypto/encrypted-columns.ts), [decryptTxRow](src/lib/crypto/encrypted-columns.ts), and [decryptSplitRow](src/lib/crypto/encrypted-columns.ts) now `try/catch` `Decipheriv.final()`. On failure they log a single `[envelope] decryptName failed` warn (so the underlying mismatch stays observable) and return the plaintext column when present, or the raw `v1:...` ciphertext as a UI marker when there's no fallback. Encryption is defense-in-depth — when the dual-write plaintext is still on disk, rendering it beats crashing the dashboard.

  **`tryDecryptField` helper** added in [envelope.ts](src/lib/crypto/envelope.ts). Same as `decryptField` but returns `null` (not the raw ciphertext, not a throw) on auth-tag failure, so callers can use the standard `tryDecryptField(...) ?? plaintextFallback` pattern. The two-commit chain is instructive: v1 of the helper returned the raw ciphertext on failure, which silently broke `?? plaintext` fallbacks at every call site (`"v1:..."` is truthy, so `??` skipped the fallback). Concrete blast radius: pathfinder's import preview parsed 6409 rows and reported "Unknown account: 'Mortage'" on every single one because [import-pipeline.buildLookups](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts) keyed `accountMap` on the unmatched ciphertext. v2 returns null and the lookup hits "Mortage". **Lesson for future fallback helpers**: never return a truthy "marker" value when the caller's fallback chain depends on null/undefined.

  **Threaded through every read path:** [import-pipeline.buildLookups](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts), [/api/forecast](src/app/api/forecast/route.ts), [/api/recurring](src/app/api/recurring/route.ts), [/api/insights](src/app/api/insights/route.ts), [/api/transactions/suggest](src/app/api/transactions/suggest/route.ts), [/api/data/export](src/app/api/data/export/route.ts), [holdings-value.ts](src/lib/holdings-value.ts), [weekly-recap.ts](src/lib/weekly-recap.ts) (5 call sites — restructured to fall back plaintext-then-default rather than skipping plaintext on decrypt failure), [external-import/credentials.ts](src/lib/external-import/credentials.ts) (returns `null` so the connector treats it as "no credentials saved" rather than crashing the flow). [/api/categories](src/app/api/categories/route.ts) GET also got a try/catch wrapper to match the `/api/accounts` pattern (was returning empty 500 bodies that triggered "Unexpected end of JSON input" on the client).

- **WP import dedup respects archived accounts (2026-04-27).** Both `materializeMapping` (API path) and `materializeZipMapping` (ZIP path) in [src/lib/external-import/orchestrator.ts](src/lib/external-import/orchestrator.ts) and [src/lib/external-import/zip-orchestrator.ts](src/lib/external-import/zip-orchestrator.ts) now call `getAccounts(userId, { includeArchived: true })` for the dedup view. The DB's `UNIQUE (user_id, name_lookup) WHERE name_lookup IS NOT NULL` partial index spans archived rows too, so excluding archived accounts from dedup let the orchestrator miss-match and 500 on the first INSERT (e.g. user previously archived "Nissan Pathfinder", re-imports the same WP file → dedup misses → INSERT collides on the archived row's `name_lookup`). New `bindToExisting` helper auto-unarchives an archived account when the import binds to it via either auto-create or explicit `finlynqId`, since transactions landing on a hidden account would silently disappear from balances + pickers. Commit [`152e4e6`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/152e4e6).

### Changed
- **Currency rework follow-up — write paths, MCP tagging, polish (2026-04-27, branches `feat/currency-followups` [`21766b9`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/21766b9) + `feat/mcp-currency-tagging-tail` [`72d33a0`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/72d33a0), merged via [`6ef78fc`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/6ef78fc)).** Closes 11 of 12 items in the currency rework's remaining-items punch list. Only #10 (sub/recurring 3-currency expansion) remains as decision-pending.

  **Import pipeline writes the trilogy.** [src/lib/import-pipeline.ts](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts) prewarms FX rates per `(currency, date)` batch up-front, then per-row calls `safeConvertToAccountCurrency` to derive `(amount, currency, entered_fx_rate)` from `(row.amount, row.currency, account.currency, date)` and writes all 6 trilogy fields. `RawTransaction` interface gained optional `enteredAmount`/`enteredCurrency` for callers that want to bypass the row-currency-as-entered default. WP ZIP and API connector orchestrators inherit automatically via `executeImport`. Hash dedup keys remain on the user-typed amount so re-imports stay stable across FX rate moves.

  **`resolveTxAmountsCore` chokepoint** lifted into [src/lib/currency-conversion.ts](src/lib/currency-conversion.ts). Returns `{ ok: true, ... } | { ok: false, code, message }` instead of NextResponse so callers in any transport can adapt. Wired into:
  - `/api/transactions/splits` POST — each split inherits parent's `entered_*` via locked-rate inversion (`entered = amount / parent.enteredFxRate`).
  - MCP HTTP `record_transaction` / `bulk_record_transactions` / `update_transaction` ([register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts)).
  - MCP stdio counterparts ([register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts)).

  Tool descriptions updated to call out the optional `enteredAmount` + `enteredCurrency` fields.

  **Future-dated FX settlement cron.** [src/lib/cron/settle-future-fx.ts](src/lib/cron/settle-future-fx.ts) sweeps daily for rows where `date <= today AND date > entered_at::date AND entered_currency != currency` (forward-dated rows whose date has now arrived AND have a real conversion to settle). Re-fetches the rate at the actual tx date, skips rows where the new rate is still `'fallback'` (waits for next sweep) or where the change is below 1e-6 (no-op churn protection), and updates `entered_fx_rate + amount`. Wired in [instrumentation.ts](instrumentation.ts) next to the email + MCP-upload cleanup intervals.

  **MCP currency tagging across both transports — completed Phase 5.** Every MCP read tool that returns amounts accepts an optional `reportingCurrency` parameter and surfaces the resolved value as response metadata. New helper [mcp-server/reporting-currency.ts](mcp-server/reporting-currency.ts) (`resolveReportingCurrency(db, userId, paramOrUndefined)`) for HTTP MCP; matching `resolveReportingCurrencyStdio` inline in [register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts) for stdio (queries `settings.display_currency` via pg-compat `prepare/get`). 18 HTTP tools wired: `get_account_balances`, `get_net_worth`, `search_transactions`, `get_budget_summary`, `get_spending_trends`, `get_income_statement`, `get_cash_flow_forecast`, `get_recurring_transactions`, `get_subscription_summary`, `get_financial_health_score`, `get_spending_anomalies`, `get_weekly_recap`, `get_portfolio_analysis`, `get_portfolio_performance`, `get_investment_insights`, `analyze_holding`, `get_loan_amortization`, `get_debt_payoff_plan`. Stdio gets the same set plus `get_spotlight_items`. Per-row reads expose `enteredAmount` / `accountAmount` / `reportingAmount` tagged objects via `tagAmount`; aggregations include a unified total in `reportingCurrency`. Per-pair lookups go through the existing `getRate(ccy, reporting, today, userId)` from `fx-service.ts`. `get_holding_metrics` + `get_rebalancing_suggestions` weren't wired separately — they were consolidated into `get_investment_insights` mode parameter on 2026-04-23.

  **Audit banner refresh after Convert/Keep.** [currency-audit-banner.tsx](src/components/currency-audit-banner.tsx) listens for the `currency-audit-changed` window event; the audit page emits it after a row is resolved so the dashboard banner refreshes without a reload.

  **`*CAD` → `*Display` rename.** Every `marketValueCAD` / `totalValueCAD` / `dayChangeCAD` / `totalCostBasisCAD` / `totalUnrealizedGainCAD` / `totalRealizedGainCAD` / `totalDividendsCAD` / `totalReturnCAD` / `unrealizedGainCAD` / `lifetimeCostBasisCAD` / `etfTotalValueCAD` / `effectiveValueCAD` / `remainingValueCAD` field renamed across [/api/portfolio/overview](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts) and [portfolio/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx). The values were always in display currency; the CAD suffix was a leftover from the CAD-only era and confusing for non-CAD users.

  **CSV export header uses display currency.** `exportHoldingsToCSV` and `exportStocksToCSV` in [portfolio/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx) take a `displayCurrency` arg and interpolate it into the header (`Mkt Value USD`, `Value EUR`, etc.). Was hardcoded `Mkt Value CAD`.

  **Migrations:** [scripts/migrate-fx-rates-legacy-drop.sql](scripts/migrate-fx-rates-legacy-drop.sql) ready to run after the soak window. Idempotent. No other schema changes — all column additions / table creations were applied in the prior batch on 2026-04-27.

- **Settings → Dev Mode is now per-user, not admin-only (2026-04-27, commit [`69fece3`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/69fece3)).** Previously the toggle wrote a single global row keyed on `DEFAULT_USER_ID = "default"` and `PUT /api/settings/dev-mode` ran through `requireAdmin`, so only the admin could enable the 17 dev-only nav items + their backing routes — and flipping it changed behavior for every user on the instance. Two-line change: [src/lib/require-dev-mode.ts](src/lib/require-dev-mode.ts) — `isDevModeEnabled(userId)` now takes a userId and `requireDevMode(request)` authenticates first then gates on the requesting user's row; [src/app/api/settings/dev-mode/route.ts](src/app/api/settings/dev-mode/route.ts) — `PUT` switched from `requireAdmin` to `requireAuth` and the upsert targets the composite `(key, userId)` PK with `auth.context.userId` instead of `DEFAULT_USER_ID`. The 9 dev-only API routes that call `requireDevMode` (chat, fire, fire/monte-carlo, loans, tax, scenarios, subscriptions, recurring, insights) and the nav's `useDevMode()` hook + `DevModeGuard` automatically pick up per-user state because they all read through the same GET endpoint. No schema migration — the `settings` table has always supported per-user rows; the old global row stays inert (its userId doesn't match any real user) and can be cleaned up out-of-band. Each user previously relying on the global flag will see dev mode default to OFF on next page load and can re-enable it themselves from Settings without admin help.

### Added
- **Currency selectors on goals + loans (2026-04-27, commit [`21766b9`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/21766b9)).** `/api/goals` and `/api/loans` POST/PUT schemas now accept `currency: z.string().regex(/^[A-Z]{3,4}$/)`; GET responses include it; edit forms in [goals/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/goals/page.tsx) + [loans/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/loans/page.tsx) expose a `<Select>` populated from `SUPPORTED_FIAT_CURRENCIES`. New `PUT /api/loans` endpoint added — loans previously had POST + DELETE only. Defaults to the user's display currency on form open. Existing rows backfilled from linked account currency in the prior batch's migration ([scripts/migrate-goals-loans-currency.sql](scripts/migrate-goals-loans-currency.sql)).

- **Holding edit dialog `<datalist>` for currency-code symbols (2026-04-27, commit [`21766b9`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/21766b9)).** Symbol `<Input>` in HoldingEditDialog has a `<datalist id="symbol-suggestions">` populated from `SUPPORTED_FIAT_CURRENCIES` so users typing a currency code (USD, EUR, XAU, …) for a cash position see autocomplete suggestions. Functional today either way; just less discoverable without the hint.

- **Portfolio holdings CRUD over MCP (2026-04-27, commit [`d3fb225`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/d3fb225)).** Adds `add_portfolio_holding` / `update_portfolio_holding` / `delete_portfolio_holding` on both HTTP (75 → 78 tools) and stdio (69 → 72 tools, with the inline-registered `get_loans` / `get_goals` / `get_categories` reads counted) transports. Pre-existing read tools were the only access path; the import pipeline auto-created rows but Claude couldn't manually add a position the user wanted to track outside an import.

  **Stream D dual-write on HTTP** ([register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts)). Every write populates `name_ct` / `name_lookup` + `symbol_ct` / `symbol_lookup` when a DEK is available, pre-checks the partial UNIQUE on `(user_id, account_id, name_lookup) WHERE name_lookup IS NOT NULL` for a friendly duplicate error, and catches Postgres `23505` as a backstop. Updates use the parameterized `sql.join` SET-clause builder (no `sql.raw`, per the post-`50a1742` audit fix). Stdio writes plaintext only — no DEK at that layer; the next browser login's Stream D backfill encrypts the row.

  **Identifier semantics.** `add_portfolio_holding` requires `name` + `account` (fuzzy matched against decrypted account name + alias) so uniqueness is scoped per brokerage. `update_portfolio_holding` and `delete_portfolio_holding` accept a single `holding` arg and fuzzy match it against decrypted name OR symbol — same UX as the existing `analyze_holding`. `update_portfolio_holding` also accepts `account` to move a holding between brokerages (transactions stay where they are; only the holding row's `account_id` flips).

  **No data loss on delete.** Transactions referencing a deleted holding survive — the FK `transactions.portfolio_holding_id … ON DELETE SET NULL` ([schema-pg.ts:67-70](src/db/schema-pg.ts:67)) auto-NULLs them; they continue to render via the orphan-fallback aggregation path until reassigned. The response reports `unlinkedTransactions: N` for transparency.

  **REST bundled fix — [PUT /api/portfolio](src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts) was silently breaking renames on Phase 3 prod.** The handler used `requireAuth` (not `requireEncryption`) and called `db.update().set(data)` directly without populating `name_ct` / `name_lookup` / `symbol_ct` / `symbol_lookup`. On Phase 3 prod (where Stream D Phase 3 NULL'd the plaintext columns on existing rows) a rename via the web edit dialog touched only the now-NULL plaintext column, so post-rename reads kept decrypting the old `name_ct` and the rename appeared to "not stick." Fixed by switching to `requireEncryption` + spreading `buildNameFields(dek, { name, symbol })` into the `.set()` payload, and catching 23505 with a 409 response. Same change adds the missing `POST /api/portfolio` (mirrors the new MCP `add_portfolio_holding` semantics — accountId required, duplicate pre-check, 409 on collision) and makes `DELETE` return `unlinkedTransactions` like the MCP tool.

  **Doc surfaces updated** in the same change: new "Portfolio holdings" card on `/mcp-guide` (consolidates the 3 new CRUD tools + the 4 pre-existing portfolio reads, dropped from the "Reads & dashboards" mega-card to avoid duplication), POST/PUT/DELETE rows on `/api-docs` for `/api/portfolio`, the public `.well-known/mcp.json` tool count + headline, and the top of CLAUDE.md.

- **Per-account alias for receipt + import matching (2026-04-24).** Adds a nullable `accounts.alias text` column so a single short shorthand (e.g. last 4 digits of a card — `"1234"` — or a label like `"Visa4242"`) can be set on any account. Two consumers:

  **MCP `fuzzyFind` ([register-tools-pg.ts:74](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [register-core-tools.ts:74](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts))** — adds an exact-on-alias branch between exact-name and startsWith-name. Alias match is exact-only (case-insensitive, trimmed) because loose matching on a 4-character alias would false-match too often. Every fuzzyFind call site inherits the new branch via the helper: `record_transaction`, `bulk_record_transactions`, `update_transaction`, `update_account`, `delete_account`, `add_loan`, `update_loan`, `add_snapshot`, `add_goal`, `add_subscription`, `update_subscription` (HTTP + stdio parity). `add_account` and `update_account` accept a new `alias` param (empty string clears on update). `get_account_balances` returns `alias` on every row so Claude can see the available shorthands when planning a write.

  **CSV import ([src/lib/import-pipeline.ts](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts) `buildLookups`)** — `accountMap` is now keyed by lowercase-trimmed name AND alias (case-insensitive end-to-end). Name wins on collision so users who haven't set an alias see no behavior change. Unknown-account error message now suggests adding the input string as an alias on an existing account.

  **Web UI ([src/app/(app)/accounts/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/accounts/page.tsx))** — both create + edit dialogs gain an "Alias (optional)" input below the name field with help text. Inline duplicate warning fires when the alias matches another account's name or alias for the same user (soft warning, not a save block). Account list rows now show `(alias)` in dim text next to the name when set. REST API ([src/app/api/accounts/route.ts](src/app/api/accounts/route.ts)) `POST` and `PUT` accept `alias` (Zod-validated, max 64 chars; empty string coerces to `null` so the DB doesn't store `""`).

  **Schema migration** at [scripts/migrate-accounts-alias.sql](scripts/migrate-accounts-alias.sql) — `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS alias text` (idempotent). Applied prod/staging/dev on 2026-04-24 before the code push.

  **One alias per account is intentional** for v1. Future multi-alias is a separate additive feature (new `account_aliases(id, user_id, account_id, alias)` table + UI list editor + update both resolvers to union the column with the table). Don't try to overload the single column with comma-separated values or JSON. Commit [`34daa8f`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/34daa8f).

- **Privacy-friendly signup with usernames (2026-04-26).** Account login is now keyed on `users.username` (required at signup, 3–254 chars, `[a-z0-9._@+_-]`, lowercased + case-insensitive unique via partial index on `lower(username)`). The character class deliberately allows `@` and `.` so an email-shaped string is a valid username — privacy-conscious users can register with `cool.dragon@madeup.fake` to avoid leaking a real email if the DB is compromised. Email is now optional; when present it's the password-reset / verification mail destination, otherwise the account is fully zero-knowledge (forgot password = wipe + rewrap, no recovery key — same policy that's existed since the AGPL pivot, but now the consequence is acked explicitly at signup via a dedicated checkbox).

  **Schema migration** at [scripts/migrate-username.sql](scripts/migrate-username.sql) — applied prod/staging/dev on 2026-04-26. Adds `users.username text` (nullable), drops `email`'s NOT NULL + single-column UNIQUE, creates case-insensitive partial unique indexes on `lower(email)` and `lower(username)`, backfills legacy rows with `username = LOWER(email)` (valid because the username regex now permits `@` + `.`). Idempotent.
