Tool descriptions now encode the canonical sign convention (matches [packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/zip-parser.ts:103](packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/zip-parser.ts) and the SQL aggregator at [/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts:186](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts)):
  - **RSU vest net of tax** → `amount=0, quantity=+net_shares`
  - **ESPP / plain buy** → `amount=negative_cash, quantity=+shares`
  - **Sell** → `amount=positive_proceeds, quantity=-shares`
  - **Dividend / interest / cash-only** → omit `quantity`

  `update_transaction` accepts `.nullable().optional()` quantity so callers can backfill or clear. End-to-end tested on dev against the same HURN row from the share-link conversation: T1 `record_transaction(qty=+1.5)` moved 71.3701 → 72.8701; T2 `bulk_record_transactions` with mixed signs (+0.7, −0.5, dividend-no-qty) moved by +0.2 net; T3 created a row without qty then `update_transaction({id, quantity: 2.0})` moved by +2.0. Cleanup brought HURN back to 71.3701 baseline. HTTP MCP only — stdio MCP `register-core-tools.ts` versions still lack both `quantity` and `portfolioHoldingId` (pre-existing asymmetry, separate follow-up).

- **Precious-metals spot rates via stooq.com (2026-04-28, branch [`feat/precious-metals-fx`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/tree/feat/precious-metals-fx), three commits).** XAU (gold), XAG (silver), XPT (platinum), and XPD (palladium) are now first-class supported currencies — auto-fetched from stooq's free unauthenticated CSV endpoint, registered in `SUPPORTED_CURRENCIES` so symbol-info classifies them as cash positions, and routed through a new metal-aware quoting rule in the portfolio overview so a gold holding in a USD account displays as `US$32,287` (6.9 oz × ~$4,679/oz spot) instead of `XAU 6.90`.

  - **[`e3a0e3a`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/e3a0e3a) — Stooq fetch path parallel to crypto.** Triggered by a user report that a Gold Ounce holding showed ~$2 CAD instead of ~$13,000. Root cause: XAU wasn't in `SUPPORTED_FIAT_CURRENCIES`, Yahoo's `<CCY>USD=X` pattern returns HTTP 404 for `XAUUSD=X` (verified live), and there was no fallback in `FALLBACK_RATE_TO_USD` — so [`getRateToUsdDetailed`](src/lib/fx-service.ts) walked all six lookup tiers and returned `{rate: 1, source: "fallback"}` (1 oz of gold = 1 USD). New `fetchStooqMetalRateToUsd(currency, date)` in [src/lib/fx-service.ts](src/lib/fx-service.ts) maps `XAU → "xauusd"` etc. and parses stooq's CSV at `https://stooq.com/q/l/?s=xauusd&f=sd2t2ohlc&h&e=csv` for current dates and `https://stooq.com/q/d/l/?s=xauusd&d1=...&d2=...&i=d` for historical (5-day window covers weekend/holiday gaps). New `SUPPORTED_METAL_CURRENCIES` constant + `isMetalCurrency()` helper in [src/lib/fx/supported-currencies.ts](src/lib/fx/supported-currencies.ts). `getRateToUsdDetailed` routing extended from binary (crypto/Yahoo) to 3-way (crypto→CoinGecko, metal→stooq, fiat→Yahoo). Hardcoded fallbacks added (XAU≈4700, XAG≈75, XPT≈1000, XPD≈1000) only used when stooq is unreachable AND nothing is cached AND no override exists.

  - **[`ded2304`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/ded2304) — Metal symbols quote in holding currency, not symbol.** Initial symptom after the stooq fetch shipped: gold showed `XAU 6.90` market value, `XAU 0.51` avg cost — numerically correct (cost basis was being converted USD→XAU at the cross-rate) but unreadable. Nobody quotes gold "in XAU per XAU" — they quote it in dollars per ounce. Two-place fix in [/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts) `quoteCurrencyById` map (line 395-407) and the cash-position branch (line 542-554): when symbol is a metal AND symbol ≠ holding currency, treat the symbol as a tradeable unit priced in `h.currency` — `price = (symbol→display) / (h.currency→display)` cross-rate, `quoteCurrency = h.currency`, `marketValue = qty × price (in h.currency)`. Pure fiat-cash positions (`USD` cash in a CAD account, etc.) keep the symbol as quote so users see the actual currency they hold. Same logic mirrored in [src/lib/holdings-value.ts:227-244](src/lib/holdings-value.ts) so per-account dashboard balances stay consistent.

  - **[`2b2edc0`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/2b2edc0) — Two follow-up bugs.** (a) The cross-rate price computation needed both `fxRates.get("XAU")` AND `fxRates.get("USD")`, but `fxRates` only got populated from `h.currency` (USD) and the chosen `quoteCurrencyById` values (also USD here, because of the metal-quote rule), so XAU never made it into the map and the price column showed `"—"`. Fix: pre-add any metal symbol seen on a holding to the `currencies` set before populating `fxRates`. (b) The Edit Holding dialog reset `currencyTouched=false` on open, which let the symbol-info debounce silently rewrite the saved currency on the next lookup — opening a USD-account gold holding would visually flip currency to XAU, and a Save without further edit would corrupt the row. Fix in [src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx:1631](src/app/%28app%29/portfolio/page.tsx): treat any pre-existing `holding.currency` as a manual override on dialog open. Field stays editable.

- **Investment-account balance redesign + unrealized P&L by period (2026-04-27, branch [`feat/account-balance-unrealized-pnl`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/tree/feat/account-balance-unrealized-pnl), four commits).** Triggered by a user-reported discrepancy on a CanadaLife group RRSP: account detail page showed Balance = **$105,872** while the holdings table showed Market value = **$57,409**. The 187 transactions on the account were all "Payroll Deposit HURON ADVISORS" rows (employer contributions auto-invested by the custodian); no buy-leg transactions ever land in Finlynq because CanadaLife's exports don't include them. The old formula at [src/app/api/dashboard/route.ts:49](src/app/api/dashboard/route.ts) was `totalBalance = b.balance + holdings.value`, which double-counted the cash-side of every contribution. Plan + decisions: `plans/can-you-check-the-shiny-key.md`.

  - **[`06345bb`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/06345bb) — Balance redesign (`balance = market value` for accounts with holdings).** [src/app/api/dashboard/route.ts](src/app/api/dashboard/route.ts) now returns `totalBalance = holdings ? holdings.value : b.balance` plus a new `cashFlowBasis` field carrying the transaction sum. The cash sleeve is already inside `holdings.value` via the currency-as-holding pattern (a `CL - CAD` row with 8,672 units × $1), so no double-count. Account detail page ([src/app/(app)/accounts/[id]/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/accounts/%5Bid%5D/page.tsx)) now shows "Market value" as the headline with "Cash flow: $X" below it for any account that has holdings; pure-cash accounts (no holdings) still show a single Balance number unchanged.

  - **[`4df7ba0`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/4df7ba0) — Currency-code symbols treated as cash, not Yahoo tickers.** A regression: with the new balance formula, CanadaLife showed **$1,185,022** instead of $57,409. Root cause: `holdings-value.ts` was passing the literal symbol `"CAD"` (the cash sleeve's symbol) to Yahoo Finance, which returned **Cadiz Inc** (NASDAQ: CAD ≈ $95 USD). 8,672 units × $95 × FX(USD→CAD)=1.37 ≈ $1.13M of phantom value. The same fix was already in `/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts` per the 2026-04-27 currency rework but was never propagated to `holdings-value.ts`. Added [`isCurrencyCodeSymbol()`](src/lib/holdings-value.ts) — excludes 3-4 letter ISO 4217 codes from the Yahoo fetch and routes them through the cash branch (`price=1`, `priceCurrency=symbol`, FX hop to account currency). Verified: CanadaLife now reports $57,409.84.

  - **[`ea6d9fb`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/ea6d9fb) — Per-period unrealized P&L from start-vs-end snapshots.** Previously `valuationGL` was cumulative-since-acquisition with the period only affecting FX baseline — picking MTD vs QTD vs YTD produced the same number, useless for monthly/quarterly/annual reports. Switched to the snapshot-difference model the user asked for: `delta_UGL = UGL_at_periodEnd - UGL_at_periodStart`. Pieces:
    - [src/lib/price-service.ts](src/lib/price-service.ts) — new `fetchQuoteAtDate(symbol, date)` and `fetchMultipleQuotesAtDate(symbols, date)` via Yahoo's `chart` endpoint with `period1`/`period2` spanning the target date ± a small window (handles weekends/holidays).
    - [src/lib/holdings-value.ts](src/lib/holdings-value.ts) — new `HoldingsValueOpts.asOfDate`. Filters transactions to `date <= asOfDate` and routes price/FX through historical endpoints when `asOfDate < today`. Backwards-compat: omitting `asOfDate` preserves the live-quote behavior used by the dashboard.
    - [src/lib/unrealized-pnl.ts](src/lib/unrealized-pnl.ts) — runs `getHoldingsValueByAccount` twice in parallel (one for `periodStart`, one for `periodEnd`). Returns per-account `start` + `end` snapshots plus the headline `valuationGL` / `fxGL` / `totalGL` as the **period delta**. FX G/L = `balanceNative × (FX_end - FX_start)` so a USD savings account translated to CAD shows non-zero `fxGL` over a period where USD/CAD moved, even with no transactions.
    - [/api/reports?type=income-statement](src/app/api/reports/route.ts), the [Reports → Income tab](src/app/(app)/reports/page.tsx) "Unrealized gains / losses (period change)" card, and MCP HTTP [`get_income_statement`](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) all consume the new shape — pick MTD/QTD/YTD/last-month/last-quarter/last-year/last-12 from the existing period selector and the numbers update accordingly.

  - **[`a397c96`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/a397c96) — Price cache as a service layer (mirrors fx_rates pattern).** `fetchQuote` and `fetchMultipleQuotes` now check `price_cache` for a row at `date=today` before any Yahoo call. On miss they fetch live and INSERT the result; on hit they return cached and skip the network entirely. Today's row is reused for the rest of the calendar day; tomorrow rolls over naturally to a new date key. Historical prices (any past date) are immutable — cached forever. New shared helpers `readPriceCache` / `readPriceCacheBulk` / `writePriceCache` so the today / historical / batched paths all share the same idempotent insert. Mirrors the cache ladder in [`getRateToUsdDetailed`](src/lib/fx-service.ts) (override → cached → live + write → nearest → fallback). Result: subsequent dashboard/portfolio loads on the same day are zero-API-call from the price + FX side. Schema unchanged — `price_cache (id, symbol, date, price, currency)` was already in place.

### Fixed
- **MCP `autoCategory` queried a non-existent `transaction_rules.match_payee` column (2026-04-28, commit [`7d70677`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/7d70677)).** Every `record_transaction` / `bulk_record_transactions` call 500'd whenever the user had at least one active rule, with `Failed query: SELECT assign_category_id FROM transaction_rules WHERE user_id = $1 AND is_active = 1 AND (LOWER(match_payee) LIKE $2 OR LOWER($3) LIKE LOWER(match_payee))`. The schema in [src/db/schema-pg.ts:285-300](src/db/schema-pg.ts) is `(match_field, match_type, match_value)` — no `match_payee` column exists, hasn't for a long time. This was the *real* root cause of the "Finlynq server bug on the category-rules lookup when portfolioHoldingId is set" reported in the [2026-04-27 share-link conversation](https://claude.ai/share/e2f2415d-0276-4b84-b8a7-af7c4805f8a3) — it wasn't actually scoped to `portfolioHoldingId` at all, any record/bulk_record call hit it whenever an active rule existed. The share-link Claude's workaround (using a "simpler payee" that didn't match a rule) bypassed the bug by accident. Fix replaces the broken SQL with a bounded in-memory match that mirrors [src/lib/auto-categorize.ts](src/lib/auto-categorize.ts) semantics: pull active payee-rules with a non-null category, then iterate in priority order — `contains` → `payeeLower.includes(valueLower)`, `exact` → equality, `regex` → JS `RegExp`. Rule count per user is small (typically <100), so the in-memory loop is cheaper than four CASE branches in SQL. **Other MCP rule-management tools (`apply_rules_to_uncategorized`, `create_rule`, `list_rules`, `update_rule` in HTTP, plus the stdio versions in [register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts)) all reference `match_payee` similarly and are similarly broken — they were broken before this commit too, no regression, but they need the same parallel migration in a follow-up sweep.**

- **Cross-currency portfolio P&L (Fidelity-CAD bug, 2026-04-27, commit [`21766b9`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/21766b9)).** A holding with `symbol="CAD"` (CAD cash held in a USD Fidelity account) was showing `Avg Cost $0.71` (correct, USD-per-CAD purchase rate), `Mkt Value $4,900 CAD`, and `Unrealized G/L +$1,401.94 (+40.08%)` — the gain was nonsense. Root cause: `marketValue = quantity × price = 4900 CAD` lived in the holding's quote currency, while `totalCostBasis = sum(transactions.amount) = ~$3479 USD` lived in the account's settlement currency. Subtracting them treated `4900 - 3479 = +1421` as a single-currency number. Fix: [/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts) now groups its tx aggregation SQL by `(portfolio_holding_id, entered_currency)` and sums `ABS(entered_amount)` per bucket. A new `quoteCurrencyById` map pre-computes the target currency per holding (cash → symbol code, crypto → "CAD" per crypto-service quirk, stock/ETF → Yahoo's `q.currency`), and each bucket is converted to that currency via FX before merging into `TxAgg`. Cross-rate cache via `getCrossRate(from, to)` keeps repeat lookups free. Same fix applied to the orphan-fallback path. Verified: Fidelity-CAD now shows market value and cost basis in the same currency, so unrealized G/L is meaningful (~+$80 CAD instead of +$1,402 nonsense).

- **MCP + dashboard portfolio aggregator dropped WP-convention buys (2026-04-27, commit [`8046b9b`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/8046b9b)).** Web Portfolio page showed Uniswap with quantity **1.2606** but MCP `analyze_holding` and `get_portfolio_analysis` returned **0** — same DB, same row. Root cause: every aggregator EXCEPT `/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts` keyed buys on `amount < 0`, which silently dropped WealthPosition-imported holding legs (WP records buys as `amount > 0 + quantity > 0` — see CLAUDE.md "Portfolio aggregator" bullet). Three files / six call sites had to be aligned with [route.ts:119-124](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts) which already used the canonical `quantity > 0 = buy` rule:

  - **MCP HTTP** [register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts): `accumulate()` (feeds `get_portfolio_analysis` / `get_portfolio_performance` / `get_investment_insights`); the in-memory loop in `analyze_holding`; the `recentTransactions[].type` display label (was returning `"dividend"` for every WP buy, which was actively misleading even before the count bug was visible).
  - **Dashboard balance-of-holdings** [holdings-value.ts](src/lib/holdings-value.ts): the FK-keyed SQL CASE and the orphan-fallback delta. Affects the "live market value of holdings" rolled into account balances on the dashboard — fixes WP-imported brokerage accounts that were silently undercounting share-bearing holdings.
  - **MCP stdio** [register-core-tools.ts:945](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts): self-hoster `analyze_holding`. Was even worse than HTTP: the `else` branch did `totalShares -= qty`, so a WP buy of `qty=1.26` reported `currentShares: -1.26`.

  **Canonical rule (now applied everywhere):** `quantity > 0` = buy regardless of amount sign · `quantity < 0` = sell regardless of amount sign · `quantity = 0 ∧ amount > 0` = dividend. No DB migration. Verified on prod against the originally-reported Uniswap row: MCP now returns `currentShares: 1.2606`, `avgCostPerShare: 7.93`, `lifetimeCostBasis: 10`, and the recent-tx label is `"buy"`.

- **Cross-tenant FK leak in backup-restore + atomic, strict wipe (2026-04-27).** Two commits ([`5571070`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/5571070) → [`ad87419`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/ad87419)) closing one root cause and one wipe-side hardening, surfaced by the admin's "Wipe Account" 500ing with `Failed query: delete from "accounts" where "accounts"."user_id" = $1`.

  **Root cause — `/api/data/import` strip().** The JSON backup-restore handler at [src/app/api/data/import/route.ts](src/app/api/data/import/route.ts) used a generic `strip(rows, userId)` helper that only deleted `id` and forced `userId`, leaving `accountId` / `categoryId` / `assignCategoryId` columns UNCHANGED from the source backup. The route already builds an `accountIdMap` and `categoryIdMap` (old-id → new-id, populated when accounts and categories are inserted), and `transactions` + `transaction_splits` had explicit per-table remap blocks — but the strip-based inserts for `portfolio_holdings`, `loans`, `goals`, `snapshots`, `recurring_transactions`, `subscriptions`, `budgets`, `budget_templates`, and `transaction_rules` didn't touch the maps. So restoring user A's backup as user B silently wrote rows owned by B but with FK references to A's account/category integer IDs — accepted by Postgres because those IDs still existed in the same DB, producing a cross-tenant data leak that also became a wipe-blocker via FK 23503 the next time A tried to delete their account. Fix: `strip()` now takes optional `{ accountIdMap, categoryIdMap }`, remaps the FK columns in-place, and **throws on an unmapped FK** rather than passing the raw id through (fail-loud — a corrupt or partial backup is now a 500 instead of a silent leak). Every restore call site that touches an FK-bearing table passes the right map; tables with no FKs keep the old single-arg call. Concrete prod blast: a test user `pathfinder` restored the admin's backup, which created 60 portfolio_holdings owned by `pathfinder` but pointing at admin's accounts (IDs 437–461). All 60 rows were cleaned up via SQL as part of the same fix — they had no business existing.

  **Wipe-side hardening — atomicity + strict isolation.** [`wipeUserDataAndRewrap`](src/lib/auth/queries.ts) used to run each `db.delete(...)` as its own auto-commit, so a late failure (like the FK violation above) left the user signed in to a half-wiped account whose DEK was never rotated, and re-running the wipe just looped on the same FK. Wrap the entire delete sequence + the final DEK rewrap in a single `db.transaction(async (tx) => ...)` — atomic. Every delete still filters strictly by `user_id` only — **never by FK reach** — so the wipe can ONLY remove rows owned by the wiping user. If a cross-tenant row exists, the wipe fails with FK 23503 + transaction rollback rather than silently destroying another user's data. (An earlier version of `5571070` did add a defensive `account_id`-keyed sweep across all 8 referrer tables; that was reverted in `ad87419` because the right fix is to prevent cross-tenant rows from being created in the first place — the `strip()` change above.) The `mcp_uploads` file unlink loop runs BEFORE the DB transaction so we'd rather leak a DB row than orphan a plaintext file on disk if the wipe later fails.

- **Decryption resilience for stale-DEK ciphertext (2026-04-27).** Three-commit batch ([`152e4e6`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/152e4e6) → [`b45c9cf`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/b45c9cf) → [`efd7de2`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/efd7de2)) that hardens every read path against AES-GCM auth-tag failures so a single undecryptable row no longer 500s the whole endpoint. Triggered by a prod incident on user `pathfinder` whose `name_ct` ciphertext columns can't be decrypted with the current cached DEK (root cause still under investigation — scheduled agent to dig in).

  **Soft-fallback decryption.** [decryptName](src/lib/crypto/encrypted-columns.ts), [decryptTxRow](src/lib/crypto/encrypted-columns.ts), and [decryptSplitRow](src/lib/crypto/encrypted-columns.ts) now `try/catch` `Decipheriv.final()`. On failure they log a single `[envelope] decryptName failed` warn (so the underlying mismatch stays observable) and return the plaintext column when present, or the raw `v1:...` ciphertext as a UI marker when there's no fallback. Encryption is defense-in-depth — when the dual-write plaintext is still on disk, rendering it beats crashing the dashboard.

  **`tryDecryptField` helper** added in [envelope.ts](src/lib/crypto/envelope.ts). Same as `decryptField` but returns `null` (not the raw ciphertext, not a throw) on auth-tag failure, so callers can use the standard `tryDecryptField(...) ?? plaintextFallback` pattern. The two-commit chain is instructive: v1 of the helper returned the raw ciphertext on failure, which silently broke `?? plaintext` fallbacks at every call site (`"v1:..."` is truthy, so `??` skipped the fallback). Concrete blast radius: pathfinder's import preview parsed 6409 rows and reported "Unknown account: 'Mortage'" on every single one because [import-pipeline.buildLookups](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts) keyed `accountMap` on the unmatched ciphertext. v2 returns null and the lookup hits "Mortage". **Lesson for future fallback helpers**: never return a truthy "marker" value when the caller's fallback chain depends on null/undefined.

  **Threaded through every read path:** [import-pipeline.buildLookups](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts), [/api/forecast](src/app/api/forecast/route.ts), [/api/recurring](src/app/api/recurring/route.ts), [/api/insights](src/app/api/insights/route.ts), [/api/transactions/suggest](src/app/api/transactions/suggest/route.ts), [/api/data/export](src/app/api/data/export/route.ts), [holdings-value.ts](src/lib/holdings-value.ts), [weekly-recap.ts](src/lib/weekly-recap.ts) (5 call sites — restructured to fall back plaintext-then-default rather than skipping plaintext on decrypt failure), [external-import/credentials.ts](src/lib/external-import/credentials.ts) (returns `null` so the connector treats it as "no credentials saved" rather than crashing the flow). [/api/categories](src/app/api/categories/route.ts) GET also got a try/catch wrapper to match the `/api/accounts` pattern (was returning empty 500 bodies that triggered "Unexpected end of JSON input" on the client).

- **WP import dedup respects archived accounts (2026-04-27).** Both `materializeMapping` (API path) and `materializeZipMapping` (ZIP path) in [src/lib/external-import/orchestrator.ts](src/lib/external-import/orchestrator.ts) and [src/lib/external-import/zip-orchestrator.ts](src/lib/external-import/zip-orchestrator.ts) now call `getAccounts(userId, { includeArchived: true })` for the dedup view. The DB's `UNIQUE (user_id, name_lookup) WHERE name_lookup IS NOT NULL` partial index spans archived rows too, so excluding archived accounts from dedup let the orchestrator miss-match and 500 on the first INSERT (e.g. user previously archived "Nissan Pathfinder", re-imports the same WP file → dedup misses → INSERT collides on the archived row's `name_lookup`). New `bindToExisting` helper auto-unarchives an archived account when the import binds to it via either auto-create or explicit `finlynqId`, since transactions landing on a hidden account would silently disappear from balances + pickers. Commit [`152e4e6`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/152e4e6).

### Changed
- **Currency rework follow-up — write paths, MCP tagging, polish (2026-04-27, branches `feat/currency-followups` [`21766b9`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/21766b9) + `feat/mcp-currency-tagging-tail` [`72d33a0`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/72d33a0), merged via [`6ef78fc`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/6ef78fc)).** Closes 11 of 12 items in the currency rework's remaining-items punch list. Only #10 (sub/recurring 3-currency expansion) remains as decision-pending.

  **Import pipeline writes the trilogy.** [src/lib/import-pipeline.ts](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts) prewarms FX rates per `(currency, date)` batch up-front, then per-row calls `safeConvertToAccountCurrency` to derive `(amount, currency, entered_fx_rate)` from `(row.amount, row.currency, account.currency, date)` and writes all 6 trilogy fields. `RawTransaction` interface gained optional `enteredAmount`/`enteredCurrency` for callers that want to bypass the row-currency-as-entered default. WP ZIP and API connector orchestrators inherit automatically via `executeImport`. Hash dedup keys remain on the user-typed amount so re-imports stay stable across FX rate moves.

  **`resolveTxAmountsCore` chokepoint** lifted into [src/lib/currency-conversion.ts](src/lib/currency-conversion.ts). Returns `{ ok: true, ... } | { ok: false, code, message }` instead of NextResponse so callers in any transport can adapt. Wired into:
  - `/api/transactions/splits` POST — each split inherits parent's `entered_*` via locked-rate inversion (`entered = amount / parent.enteredFxRate`).
  - MCP HTTP `record_transaction` / `bulk_record_transactions` / `update_transaction` ([register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts)).
  - MCP stdio counterparts ([register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts)).

  Tool descriptions updated to call out the optional `enteredAmount` + `enteredCurrency` fields.

  **Future-dated FX settlement cron.** [src/lib/cron/settle-future-fx.ts](src/lib/cron/settle-future-fx.ts) sweeps daily for rows where `date <= today AND date > entered_at::date AND entered_currency != currency` (forward-dated rows whose date has now arrived AND have a real conversion to settle). Re-fetches the rate at the actual tx date, skips rows where the new rate is still `'fallback'` (waits for next sweep) or where the change is below 1e-6 (no-op churn protection), and updates `entered_fx_rate + amount`. Wired in [instrumentation.ts](instrumentation.ts) next to the email + MCP-upload cleanup intervals.

  **MCP currency tagging across both transports — completed Phase 5.** Every MCP read tool that returns amounts accepts an optional `reportingCurrency` parameter and surfaces the resolved value as response metadata. New helper [mcp-server/reporting-currency.ts](mcp-server/reporting-currency.ts) (`resolveReportingCurrency(db, userId, paramOrUndefined)`) for HTTP MCP; matching `resolveReportingCurrencyStdio` inline in [register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts) for stdio (queries `settings.display_currency` via pg-compat `prepare/get`). 18 HTTP tools wired: `get_account_balances`, `get_net_worth`, `search_transactions`, `get_budget_summary`, `get_spending_trends`, `get_income_statement`, `get_cash_flow_forecast`, `get_recurring_transactions`, `get_subscription_summary`, `get_financial_health_score`, `get_spending_anomalies`, `get_weekly_recap`, `get_portfolio_analysis`, `get_portfolio_performance`, `get_investment_insights`, `analyze_holding`, `get_loan_amortization`, `get_debt_payoff_plan`. Stdio gets the same set plus `get_spotlight_items`. Per-row reads expose `enteredAmount` / `accountAmount` / `reportingAmount` tagged objects via `tagAmount`; aggregations include a unified total in `reportingCurrency`. Per-pair lookups go through the existing `getRate(ccy, reporting, today, userId)` from `fx-service.ts`. `get_holding_metrics` + `get_rebalancing_suggestions` weren't wired separately — they were consolidated into `get_investment_insights` mode parameter on 2026-04-23.

  **Audit banner refresh after Convert/Keep.** [currency-audit-banner.tsx](src/components/currency-audit-banner.tsx) listens for the `currency-audit-changed` window event; the audit page emits it after a row is resolved so the dashboard banner refreshes without a reload.

  **`*CAD` → `*Display` rename.** Every `marketValueCAD` / `totalValueCAD` / `dayChangeCAD` / `totalCostBasisCAD` / `totalUnrealizedGainCAD` / `totalRealizedGainCAD` / `totalDividendsCAD` / `totalReturnCAD` / `unrealizedGainCAD` / `lifetimeCostBasisCAD` / `etfTotalValueCAD` / `effectiveValueCAD` / `remainingValueCAD` field renamed across [/api/portfolio/overview](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts) and [portfolio/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx). The values were always in display currency; the CAD suffix was a leftover from the CAD-only era and confusing for non-CAD users.

  **CSV export header uses display currency.** `exportHoldingsToCSV` and `exportStocksToCSV` in [portfolio/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx) take a `displayCurrency` arg and interpolate it into the header (`Mkt Value USD`, `Value EUR`, etc.). Was hardcoded `Mkt Value CAD`.

  **Migrations:** [scripts/migrate-fx-rates-legacy-drop.sql](scripts/migrate-fx-rates-legacy-drop.sql) ready to run after the soak window. Idempotent. No other schema changes — all column additions / table creations were applied in the prior batch on 2026-04-27.

- **Settings → Dev Mode is now per-user, not admin-only (2026-04-27, commit [`69fece3`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/69fece3)).** Previously the toggle wrote a single global row keyed on `DEFAULT_USER_ID = "default"` and `PUT /api/settings/dev-mode` ran through `requireAdmin`, so only the admin could enable the 17 dev-only nav items + their backing routes — and flipping it changed behavior for every user on the instance. Two-line change: [src/lib/require-dev-mode.ts](src/lib/require-dev-mode.ts) — `isDevModeEnabled(userId)` now takes a userId and `requireDevMode(request)` authenticates first then gates on the requesting user's row; [src/app/api/settings/dev-mode/route.ts](src/app/api/settings/dev-mode/route.ts) — `PUT` switched from `requireAdmin` to `requireAuth` and the upsert targets the composite `(key, userId)` PK with `auth.context.userId` instead of `DEFAULT_USER_ID`. The 9 dev-only API routes that call `requireDevMode` (chat, fire, fire/monte-carlo, loans, tax, scenarios, subscriptions, recurring, insights) and the nav's `useDevMode()` hook + `DevModeGuard` automatically pick up per-user state because they all read through the same GET endpoint. No schema migration — the `settings` table has always supported per-user rows; the old global row stays inert (its userId doesn't match any real user) and can be cleaned up out-of-band. Each user previously relying on the global flag will see dev mode default to OFF on next page load and can re-enable it themselves from Settings without admin help.

### Added
- **Currency selectors on goals + loans (2026-04-27, commit [`21766b9`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/21766b9)).** `/api/goals` and `/api/loans` POST/PUT schemas now accept `currency: z.string().regex(/^[A-Z]{3,4}$/)`; GET responses include it; edit forms in [goals/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/goals/page.tsx) + [loans/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/loans/page.tsx) expose a `<Select>` populated from `SUPPORTED_FIAT_CURRENCIES`. New `PUT /api/loans` endpoint added — loans previously had POST + DELETE only. Defaults to the user's display currency on form open. Existing rows backfilled from linked account currency in the prior batch's migration ([scripts/migrate-goals-loans-currency.sql](scripts/migrate-goals-loans-currency.sql)).

- **Holding edit dialog `<datalist>` for currency-code symbols (2026-04-27, commit [`21766b9`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/21766b9)).** Symbol `<Input>` in HoldingEditDialog has a `<datalist id="symbol-suggestions">` populated from `SUPPORTED_FIAT_CURRENCIES` so users typing a currency code (USD, EUR, XAU, …) for a cash position see autocomplete suggestions. Functional today either way; just less discoverable without the hint.

- **Portfolio holdings CRUD over MCP (2026-04-27, commit [`d3fb225`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/d3fb225)).** Adds `add_portfolio_holding` / `update_portfolio_holding` / `delete_portfolio_holding` on both HTTP (75 → 78 tools) and stdio (69 → 72 tools, with the inline-registered `get_loans` / `get_goals` / `get_categories` reads counted) transports. Pre-existing read tools were the only access path; the import pipeline auto-created rows but Claude couldn't manually add a position the user wanted to track outside an import.

  **Stream D dual-write on HTTP** ([register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts)). Every write populates `name_ct` / `name_lookup` + `symbol_ct` / `symbol_lookup` when a DEK is available, pre-checks the partial UNIQUE on `(user_id, account_id, name_lookup) WHERE name_lookup IS NOT NULL` for a friendly duplicate error, and catches Postgres `23505` as a backstop. Updates use the parameterized `sql.join` SET-clause builder (no `sql.raw`, per the post-`50a1742` audit fix). Stdio writes plaintext only — no DEK at that layer; the next browser login's Stream D backfill encrypts the row.

  **Identifier semantics.** `add_portfolio_holding` requires `name` + `account` (fuzzy matched against decrypted account name + alias) so uniqueness is scoped per brokerage. `update_portfolio_holding` and `delete_portfolio_holding` accept a single `holding` arg and fuzzy match it against decrypted name OR symbol — same UX as the existing `analyze_holding`. `update_portfolio_holding` also accepts `account` to move a holding between brokerages (transactions stay where they are; only the holding row's `account_id` flips).

  **No data loss on delete.** Transactions referencing a deleted holding survive — the FK `transactions.portfolio_holding_id … ON DELETE SET NULL` ([schema-pg.ts:67-70](src/db/schema-pg.ts:67)) auto-NULLs them; they continue to render via the orphan-fallback aggregation path until reassigned. The response reports `unlinkedTransactions: N` for transparency.

  **REST bundled fix — [PUT /api/portfolio](src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts) was silently breaking renames on Phase 3 prod.** The handler used `requireAuth` (not `requireEncryption`) and called `db.update().set(data)` directly without populating `name_ct` / `name_lookup` / `symbol_ct` / `symbol_lookup`. On Phase 3 prod (where Stream D Phase 3 NULL'd the plaintext columns on existing rows) a rename via the web edit dialog touched only the now-NULL plaintext column, so post-rename reads kept decrypting the old `name_ct` and the rename appeared to "not stick." Fixed by switching to `requireEncryption` + spreading `buildNameFields(dek, { name, symbol })` into the `.set()` payload, and catching 23505 with a 409 response. Same change adds the missing `POST /api/portfolio` (mirrors the new MCP `add_portfolio_holding` semantics — accountId required, duplicate pre-check, 409 on collision) and makes `DELETE` return `unlinkedTransactions` like the MCP tool.

  **Doc surfaces updated** in the same change: new "Portfolio holdings" card on `/mcp-guide` (consolidates the 3 new CRUD tools + the 4 pre-existing portfolio reads, dropped from the "Reads & dashboards" mega-card to avoid duplication), POST/PUT/DELETE rows on `/api-docs` for `/api/portfolio`, the public `.well-known/mcp.json` tool count + headline, and the top of CLAUDE.md.

- **Per-account alias for receipt + import matching (2026-04-24).** Adds a nullable `accounts.alias text` column so a single short shorthand (e.g. last 4 digits of a card — `"1234"` — or a label like `"Visa4242"`) can be set on any account. Two consumers:

  **MCP `fuzzyFind` ([register-tools-pg.ts:74](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [register-core-tools.ts:74](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts))** — adds an exact-on-alias branch between exact-name and startsWith-name. Alias match is exact-only (case-insensitive, trimmed) because loose matching on a 4-character alias would false-match too often. Every fuzzyFind call site inherits the new branch via the helper: `record_transaction`, `bulk_record_transactions`, `update_transaction`, `update_account`, `delete_account`, `add_loan`, `update_loan`, `add_snapshot`, `add_goal`, `add_subscription`, `update_subscription` (HTTP + stdio parity). `add_account` and `update_account` accept a new `alias` param (empty string clears on update). `get_account_balances` returns `alias` on every row so Claude can see the available shorthands when planning a write.

  **CSV import ([src/lib/import-pipeline.ts](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts) `buildLookups`)** — `accountMap` is now keyed by lowercase-trimmed name AND alias (case-insensitive end-to-end). Name wins on collision so users who haven't set an alias see no behavior change. Unknown-account error message now suggests adding the input string as an alias on an existing account.

  **Web UI ([src/app/(app)/accounts/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/accounts/page.tsx))** — both create + edit dialogs gain an "Alias (optional)" input below the name field with help text. Inline duplicate warning fires when the alias matches another account's name or alias for the same user (soft warning, not a save block). Account list rows now show `(alias)` in dim text next to the name when set. REST API ([src/app/api/accounts/route.ts](src/app/api/accounts/route.ts)) `POST` and `PUT` accept `alias` (Zod-validated, max 64 chars; empty string coerces to `null` so the DB doesn't store `""`).

  **Schema migration** at [scripts/migrate-accounts-alias.sql](scripts/migrate-accounts-alias.sql) — `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS alias text` (idempotent). Applied prod/staging/dev on 2026-04-24 before the code push.

  **One alias per account is intentional** for v1. Future multi-alias is a separate additive feature (new `account_aliases(id, user_id, account_id, alias)` table + UI list editor + update both resolvers to union the column with the table). Don't try to overload the single column with comma-separated values or JSON. Commit [`34daa8f`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/34daa8f).

- **Privacy-friendly signup with usernames (2026-04-26).** Account login is now keyed on `users.username` (required at signup, 3–254 chars, `[a-z0-9._@+_-]`, lowercased + case-insensitive unique via partial index on `lower(username)`). The character class deliberately allows `@` and `.` so an email-shaped string is a valid username — privacy-conscious users can register with `cool.dragon@madeup.fake` to avoid leaking a real email if the DB is compromised. Email is now optional; when present it's the password-reset / verification mail destination, otherwise the account is fully zero-knowledge (forgot password = wipe + rewrap, no recovery key — same policy that's existed since the AGPL pivot, but now the consequence is acked explicitly at signup via a dedicated checkbox).

  **Schema migration** at [scripts/migrate-username.sql](scripts/migrate-username.sql) — applied prod/staging/dev on 2026-04-26. Adds `users.username text` (nullable), drops `email`'s NOT NULL + single-column UNIQUE, creates case-insensitive partial unique indexes on `lower(email)` and `lower(username)`, backfills legacy rows with `username = LOWER(email)` (valid because the username regex now permits `@` + `.`). Idempotent.

  **Login** accepts a single `identifier` field (username OR email) — legacy clients sending `{email, password}` still work via a Zod union. `getUserByIdentifier` always tries the username column first then falls back to email. Cross-column collision is enforced at signup time via `isIdentifierClaimed` ([src/lib/auth/queries.ts](src/lib/auth/queries.ts)) so a single string can match at most one user — without this, registering username `foo@x.com` after another user has email `foo@x.com` would route future logins of `foo@x.com` to the wrong account.

  **Validator** at [src/lib/auth/username.ts](src/lib/auth/username.ts). Reserved bare-handle list (admin/support/import-/...) blocks new signups from claiming routing-conflicting names; doesn't apply when the username contains `@` because the email-import router never collides with email-shaped strings.

  **JWT shape** simplified — `email` claim removed from `SessionPayload`. `sub` (userId) is the source of truth and identity fields (username, email, displayName) are looked up fresh from the DB on every `/api/auth/session` read. Renaming a username doesn't require token rotation.

  **UI** at [src/app/cloud/page.tsx](src/app/cloud/page.tsx) — single "Username or email" input on login; Username + optional Email + ack-no-recovery checkbox on register; debounced live availability check via new `GET /api/auth/username-check?u=...` (30/min/IP rate limit). Checkbox copy is honest about the full consequence: data loss + no reset path on lost password.

  **Demo account** keeps its email (`demo@finlynq.com`) and now has username `demo`. Login accepts either. Seed script at [scripts/seed-demo.ts](scripts/seed-demo.ts) was updated to set `username: "demo"` on the idempotent upsert and switch its `ON CONFLICT` target from `(email)` (now a partial unique index, not a full constraint) to `(id)`.

  Files changed: 18 (`+703 / -139`). Full file list in commit [`0105946`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/0105946) plus copy fix [`27321fe`](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/27321fe).

- **Transaction linking + portfolio-holding UX batch (2026-04-24).** Targeted follow-ups on the WealthPosition ZIP import surfacing as edge-case gaps.

  **Multi-leg transaction linking.** New nullable `transactions.link_id text` column + partial index on `link_id IS NOT NULL` ([scripts/migrate-transactions-link-id.sql](scripts/migrate-transactions-link-id.sql), applied prod/staging/dev). Every multi-leg `#SPLIT#` group that the WP ZIP transform emits (transfer set, same-account currency conversion, position liquidation, stock buy with cash + position legs) gets one `randomUUID()` `linkId` carried on every emitted row. UI: the edit dialog gains a blue "Linked transaction(s)" section listing siblings by date + account + amount when `link_id` is set (fetches `/api/transactions/linked?linkId=…&excludeId=…` — in-memory exact-match filter, user-scoped, inherits the same soft-DEK policy as `GET /api/transactions`). A small `Link2` icon in the category column of the table flags linked rows. Clicking a sibling jumps to its edit view; if it's on another page, the list filters to its date.

  **Three WP ZIP transform edge cases closed.** The single-walk through `emitGroup`'s holding-children branch used to skip the parent leg whenever the parent itself was a holding (`parentHoldingRef` set). That path was always-emit for cash parents but drop-parent for holding parents, and the consequences were silent:
  - **Aug-11-style same-account currency conversions** (parent `Joint - USD`, child `Joint - CAD`, both on IBKR Joint) emitted only the child with `category: undefined` — user saw one side land on IBKR Joint with a null category. Fix: always emit parent, use `holdingForAccountName(parent.account)` so the dual-natured account tags its own holding name as `portfolio_holding`, and tag both legs with the user's Transfer category.
  - **Jul-31-style liquidations** (sell 40 shares of `Joint - Dev Asia ex Japan - A` into `Joint - USD` cash) emitted only the cash leg — the aggregator never saw `-40 shares` so the position stayed at its pre-sell quantity. Fix: the parent sell leg now emits on its brokerage with `portfolio_holding = parent holding name`, `quantity < 0`, and the Transfer category.
  - **Aug-14-style cross-account transfers** (Joint - CAD → RBC Checking) worked but the two txs weren't linked. Fix comes from the `linkId` threading above — same group-level uuid on both.

  **Dividend source-holding as a tag, not a share move.** A WP row like `Joint - USD, Dividends, CAD, 20, 14.29, Joint - All W - D` encodes **two events in one row**: Joint - USD received $20 CAD / +14.29 USD qty, and that dividend was *generated by* the Joint - All W - D position. The previous transform stuffed `Joint - All W - D` into `tx.portfolio_holding`, so the aggregator credited +14.29 shares to Joint - All W - D (zeroing its cost basis) while Joint - USD's balance was never touched. The right read (confirmed against WP's UI): the balance change lands on the row's Account, and the "Portfolio holding" column is a reporting attribution. Both `emitSingleRowTx` (dual-natured account case) and `emitHoldingSideTx` (Portfolio-only case) now detect `csv.portfolio_holding != account.name` and append `source:<holding-name>` to `tags`, keeping the balance + qty on the account. Clickable tag chips + deep-linkable `?tag=source:X` filter let the user audit "all dividends from this holding" without a report script.

  **Portfolio holding edit + delete.** Each row in the `/portfolio` holdings table gains an **Edit** button in its expanded panel (next to "View transactions"). Dialog lets the user change name / symbol / currency / is-crypto flag / note, with a two-step confirm on delete. New `PUT /api/portfolio` (updates via user-scoped ownership pre-check) and `DELETE /api/portfolio?id=…` (returns 404 when nothing deletes, avoiding silent no-ops). The rename caveat is called out inline — `tx.portfolio_holding` is a plaintext (now encrypted) string not a FK, so renaming here doesn't cascade to existing transactions. Follow-up task spawned to switch to an integer FK so renames become free; tracked separately.

  **Portfolio deep-link to transactions.** Holding row expansion → **"View transactions →"** opens `/transactions?portfolioHolding=<name>&accountId=<id>`. `GET /api/transactions` now accepts `portfolioHolding` and `tag` query params — both are in-memory exact-match filters applied after `decryptTxRows` (SQL `LIKE` doesn't work on AES-GCM ciphertext, and substring match would false-match related tags). When either is set the server over-fetches `limit=1000 offset=0`, filters in memory, then re-applies the client's pagination to the filtered result. Transactions page reads URL params at mount (inside a new `<Suspense>` wrapper for `useSearchParams`), renders pill chips for active post-decrypt filters, and now surfaces a **Quantity** column between Note and Amount so investment legs show their share counts inline.

  **Portfolio-overview DEK-miss defense.** `GET /api/portfolio/overview` wrapped `decryptField` in `try/catch` and skips any row whose decrypted key still starts with `v1:` — post-restart DEK-cache misses previously rendered every unique AES-GCM IV as its own "auto-detected" orphan holding (1,000+ `v1:…` rows for a 6 000-tx user). The skipped count surfaces as `undecryptedTxCount`; the Portfolio page renders an amber "Sign in again to unlock" banner when non-zero so the user knows exactly what to do.

  **Case-insensitive orchestrator dedup + Stream D Phase 3 compat.** Both [zip-orchestrator.ts](src/lib/external-import/zip-orchestrator.ts) and [orchestrator.ts](src/lib/external-import/orchestrator.ts) now maintain two dedup indexes per table — one keyed by `trim().toLowerCase()` plaintext (legacy rows) and one keyed by the `name_lookup` HMAC (Stream D Phase 3 rows whose plaintext is nulled). Before this, case-sensitive plaintext-only lookup missed two real-world collisions: a single CSV containing both `Balance adjustments` and `Balance Adjustments` (same HMAC, unique-index collision on the second INSERT) and a prod user whose `Entertainment` category was Phase-3 encrypted (null plaintext, non-null `name_lookup`) so the orchestrator didn't see it in the Map and tried to auto-create a duplicate. `nameKey()` is also null-safe now — rows with null plaintext don't crash the map build.

  Files modified: [packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/zip-parser.ts](packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/zip-parser.ts) + [.test.ts](packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/zip-parser.test.ts), [packages/import-connectors/src/types.ts](packages/import-connectors/src/types.ts) (RawTransaction.linkId), [src/lib/import-pipeline.ts](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts), [src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts), [src/db/schema-pg.ts](src/db/schema-pg.ts), [src/lib/external-import/{zip-orchestrator,orchestrator}.ts](src/lib/external-import/), [src/app/api/transactions/route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/route.ts), [src/app/api/transactions/linked/route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/linked/route.ts) (new), [src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts) (PUT/DELETE), [src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts), [src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx), [src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx). 34 vitest assertions green.

- **WealthPosition migration connector — pluggable third-party import (2026-04-24).** First external-service connector, shipped as a new workspace package [`@finlynq/import-connectors`](packages/import-connectors/) (private, npm-publishable once a second connector validates the API). Lives at [/import → Connect a Service](src/app/(app)/import/components/connector-tab.tsx). Ships with **two paths**:

  **Primary: ZIP export upload.** User downloads WealthPosition → Profile → Export (a 4-CSV bundle: Accounts.csv / Categories.csv / Portfolio.csv / Transactions.csv) and uploads the ZIP. Server extracts via `jszip` (no native deps), parses via a hand-rolled RFC-4180 CSV reader ([csv.ts](packages/import-connectors/src/wealthposition/csv.ts)), and transforms into Finlynq's `RawTransaction[]` + `transaction_splits[]` + `portfolio_holdings[]`. Routes: [`/api/import/connectors/wealthposition/zip-{probe,preview,execute}`](src/app/api/import/connectors/wealthposition/). Instant (no rate limits) and handles 100% of transaction shapes. Portfolio.csv is the key: it's the authoritative holding → brokerage + ticker symbol mapping, which WP's API never exposes. Verified against a real 6,596-row export → 5,817 flat txs + 111 split parents + 60 portfolio_holdings rows + 0 transform errors.

  **Secondary: API key.** Live [WP API](https://www.wealthposition.com/api/v0.1) access, kept for balance reconciliation against `/account_balances`. Key is AES-GCM-encrypted under the user's DEK and persisted in `settings` under `connector:wealthposition:credentials`. Rate-limited client (1 req/s) with SHA-keyed global bucket on `globalThis` so state survives across HTTP requests in the same process; automatic retry with jittered backoff on `RATE_LIMIT_ERROR` / HTTP 429. Original plan had the API as the primary import path — pivoted to ZIP after discovering the API's `/accounts` flattens every position (Bitcoin, Ethereum, every stock ticker) as a separate top-level account with no way to tie them back to their brokerage.

  **Transform rules (ZIP path).** The CSV uses a `#SPLIT#` sentinel to preserve multi-leg transactions (parent row has `account=<real>` + `categorization=#SPLIT#`, consecutive children have `account=#SPLIT#`). Walker groups parent + consecutive children, classifies each child as category / account / holding, and emits:
    - **Simple 1A + 1C** → 1 Finlynq flat tx (4,204 rows, 63.5%).
    - **Cash + #SPLIT# + N categories** → 1 parent + N rows in `transaction_splits` (paycheck splits).
    - **Cash + #SPLIT# + N holdings** → parent cash leg preserved on the funding account + N position legs routed via `Portfolio.csv` to each holding's own brokerage (so a stock buy funded from RBC lands the cash leg on RBC and the shares on Questrade - RESP, each with `portfolioHolding = holding_name` + `quantity`).
    - **Cash + #SPLIT# + N accounts** → transfer set (parent + N flat txs, all tagged with the Transfer category).
    - **Orphan #SPLIT# rows** → surfaced as transform errors (shown in the preview dialog, not dropped silently).

  **Mapping + reconciliation.** Probe returns Finlynq's live accounts + categories so the mapping dialog can render names (not opaque ids); stale overrides (pointing at accounts deleted outside the import flow) are filtered client-side before opening the dialog and re-validated server-side in `materializeZipMapping`. Reconciliation endpoint fetches WP's `/accounts` once to build a `name→UUID` bridge, so CSV synthetic ids (`csv:acct:<name>`) match WP's real UUIDs from `/account_balances`. After a successful import, reconciliation runs inline when an API key is on file — per-account WP-vs-Finlynq diff table surfaces in the summary card, with an "Add opening balance" affordance for constant-offset mismatches.

  **Files.** New: [packages/import-connectors/](packages/import-connectors/), [src/lib/external-import/{credentials,mapping,orchestrator,zip-orchestrator,reconciliation}.ts](src/lib/external-import/), 8 routes under `src/app/api/import/connectors/wealthposition/`, 3 UI components under `src/app/(app)/import/components/connector-*.tsx`. Modified: [src/app/(app)/import/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/import/page.tsx) (new tab), [src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts) (aggregator now classifies buys by `qty>0` regardless of amount sign — covers both Finlynq-native `amt<0+qty>0` and WP-native `amt>0+qty>0` conventions), [src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx) (`startEdit` now pre-loads existing splits — pre-existing UX gap fixed while in the area), [next.config.ts](next.config.ts) (added `transpilePackages: ["@finlynq/import-connectors"]`). Dependency added: `jszip@^3.10.1`. No schema migration — reuses `settings` + existing `transactions` / `transaction_splits` / `portfolio_holdings` tables. Architecture notes for adding future connectors are in CLAUDE.md's "External import connectors" section; package contract is in [packages/import-connectors/src/types.ts](packages/import-connectors/src/types.ts) + [packages/import-connectors/README.md](packages/import-connectors/README.md).
- **Archive or delete accounts (2026-04-24).** The Accounts page's edit dialog now has an Account actions footer with **Archive / Unarchive** and **Delete**. Archive is a soft hide — the account drops out of balances, net-worth totals, and every account picker (transactions, loans, goals, subscriptions, import, onboarding) while keeping history intact. A "Show archived" toggle on the page header brings archived rows back into view at 60% opacity with an Archived badge so they can still be edited or unarchived; totals always exclude archived regardless of the toggle. Delete is permanent and guarded: the DELETE handler catches Postgres `foreign_key_violation` (SQLSTATE `23503`) and returns 409 with "Archive it instead, or remove the related records first" when any of the 8 referencing tables (transactions, portfolio_holdings, loans, subscriptions, goals, snapshots, transaction_splits, recurring_transactions) still points at the account. New column `accounts.archived boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false`, migration at [scripts/migrate-accounts-archived.sql](scripts/migrate-accounts-archived.sql) (applied to prod; staging + dev pending). `getAccounts()` and `getAccountBalances()` gained an `{ includeArchived }` option that defaults to excluding archived — every current caller gets the right behavior without opt-in. `GET /api/accounts?includeArchived=1` and `GET /api/dashboard?includeArchived=1` are the opt-in paths used by the toggle. `PUT /api/accounts` accepts `archived: boolean`; `DELETE /api/accounts?id=…` is new.
- **Email import via Resend Inbound with staging review queue (2026-04-23, commits [8dbf0db](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/8dbf0db) → [a1e1b51](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/a1e1b51)).** End-to-end inbound email flow replacing the vaporware `@pf.app` addresses. Users forward bank statements to `import-<uuid>@finlynq.com`, and attachments land in a review queue at [/import/pending](src/app/(app)/import/pending/page.tsx) where per-row checkboxes let them approve selectively; approved rows materialize into encrypted `transactions` under the session DEK. Non-import addresses go through a 3-way router: reserved prefixes (`info`/`admin`/`support`/`hello`/`contact`/`sales`/`help`) and named-human matches land in the **admin inbox** at [/admin/inbox](src/app/(app)/admin/inbox/page.tsx) (kept indefinitely, body HTML rendered in a sandboxed iframe); everything else goes to a **trash bin** with a 24-hour TTL and an optional bounce back to the sender (gated on `RESEND_API_KEY` + SPF/DKIM pass — no backscatter). Webhook handler at [/api/import/email-webhook](src/app/api/import/email-webhook/route.ts) accepts Resend JSON (svix-signature-verified via a hand-rolled HMAC-SHA256 verifier at [src/lib/webhooks/svix.ts](src/lib/webhooks/svix.ts) — no new npm dep) with fallbacks: 401 on bad signature, 413 on >10 MB body, 429 on >25 msgs/hour per recipient, 415 on wrong content-type. New schema: `staged_imports`, `staged_transactions`, `incoming_emails` ([scripts/migrate-email-import-staging.sql](scripts/migrate-email-import-staging.sql), applied to prod). The staging design deliberately removes the need for a persistent `email_webhook_dek` envelope — rows sit plaintext for a bounded 14-day window and get encrypted only at user-approve time, shrinking the attack surface. Cleanup cron sweeps both staged imports (14d) and trash (24h) every 30 min. Self-hosted multipart path preserved for backward compat. Full integration plan: [Research/email-import-resend-plan.md](../Research/email-import-resend-plan.md). Ops setup (DNS MX, Resend dashboard, signing secret): [docs/resend-inbound-setup.md](docs/resend-inbound-setup.md). **Current prod state:** code live + DB migrated + placeholder signing secret in env; MX records and real Resend secret still need to be wired per the setup guide.
- **CSV import — column-mapping fallback for non-canonical headers (2026-04-23, commit [df2143c](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/df2143c)).** Bank exports (CIBC, RBC, etc.) use headers like `Transaction Date` / `CAD$` / `Description 1` that don't match the canonical `Date`/`Amount`/`Account`/`Payee` parser, so every row was failing as `Invalid date: ""`. [`/api/import/preview`](src/app/api/import/preview/route.ts) now:
  1. Tries the canonical parser first (existing behavior).
  2. **Honors `templateId`** when the user picks a saved template (was silently ignored — latent bug).
  3. Falls back to a **saved template with ≥80% header overlap** via `findBestTemplate()`.
  4. Returns `type: "csv-needs-mapping"` with `headers`, `sampleRows`, and an auto-detected `suggestedMapping` when nothing else works.
  A new [ColumnMappingDialog](src/app/(app)/import/components/column-mapping-dialog.tsx) opens for case 4: user picks columns + a default account (CIBC-style CSVs have no usable account column), sees a live 3-row sample, confirms, and the client (a) parses via `/api/import/csv-map` and (b) **auto-saves the mapping as a template** so subsequent uploads hit case 3 silently. Also tightened `autoDetectColumnMapping()` in [src/lib/import-templates.ts](src/lib/import-templates.ts): currency-suffix matcher for amount (`CAD$` / `USD$` / `$`), exact-match-first pass before substring fallback, and account binds only on literal `account` / `account name` — so `Account Type` (= "MasterCard") doesn't get bound to a user account name.

### Fixed
- **Deleting a transaction that had splits returned 500 (2026-04-23).** `transaction_splits.transaction_id` was defined as a FK to `transactions.id` without `ON DELETE CASCADE`, so every delete path — web `DELETE /api/transactions`, `POST /api/transactions/bulk {action:"delete"}`, MCP `delete_transaction`, MCP `execute_bulk_delete` — failed with a Postgres FK violation whenever the target had any split rows. `wipe-account` was unaffected because it already deletes splits explicitly. Fix: added `{ onDelete: "cascade" }` on the splits FK in [src/db/schema-pg.ts](src/db/schema-pg.ts) and shipped an idempotent migration at [scripts/migrate-tx-splits-cascade.sql](scripts/migrate-tx-splits-cascade.sql) that drops + recreates the constraint with `CASCADE`. The migration introspects `pg_constraint` by (table, column) rather than hardcoding the constraint name, because prod + staging carry the Postgres-default name `transaction_splits_transaction_id_fkey` while dev carries drizzle's name `transaction_splits_transaction_id_transactions_id_fk`. Applied to prod + staging + dev; verified `confdeltype='c'` in all three. No code deploy or restart required — constraint-only change.
- **Edit Transaction dialog — readable account/category + holdings dropdown (2026-04-23, commit [cd47689](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/cd47689)).** The dialog's Account and Category triggers were rendering raw IDs (`"9"`, `"52"`) instead of names. base-ui's `Select.Value` renders the underlying value by default — our previous usage relied on implicit label lookup that doesn't exist in `@base-ui/react@1.2.0`. Passed a function child `(value) => ...` that looks up the selected account/category and returns its display string (`"Checking"`, `"Food - Groceries"`). Same change applied to the Portfolio Holding field, which was also swapped from a free-text `<Input>` to a `<Select>` populated from `/api/portfolio` — options render as `Name (SYMBOL) — Account`. Legacy free-form `portfolio_holding` strings that don't match a current holding are preserved as a fallback option so existing transactions keep their value. See [src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx).
- **GA scoped to website routes; CSP no longer blocks gtag on app pages (2026-04-23, commit [4d36085](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/4d36085)).** Google Analytics moved out of the root layout into a `<GoogleAnalytics />` component included only on `/`, `/cloud`, `/self-hosted`. Authenticated app routes (`(app)/*`) no longer load gtag and stop logging the `script-src` CSP violation. The middleware CSP is now route-aware: GTM/GA hosts (`https://www.googletagmanager.com`, `https://www.google-analytics.com`, `https://*.analytics.google.com`) are whitelisted in `script-src` / `img-src` / `connect-src` only on website routes. See [src/middleware.ts](src/middleware.ts) + [src/components/google-analytics.tsx](src/components/google-analytics.tsx).
- **Removed dead `/api/billing/status` callers (commit 4d36085).** The endpoint was deleted in the OSS pivot but two callers remained — the dashboard onboarding-wizard gate and the settings Plan & Billing card. Dashboard now reads `onboardingComplete` + identity from `/api/auth/session` (which gained `email` and `displayName` in this commit). The Plan & Billing card, its `billingStatus` / `billingLoading` state, the `handleBillingUpgrade` handler, and the `CreditCard` icon import were stripped from [settings/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/settings/page.tsx).
- **Quick Import on the dashboard no longer 404s (commit 4d36085).** The `_components/quick-import.tsx` widget POSTed to `/api/import/upload`, which never existed. Rewrote it as a navigation aid that opens `/import` on click or drop, matching the inline `QuickImportWidget` pattern already in [dashboard/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx).
- **Recharts `width(-1) and height(-1)` console warning (commit 4d36085).** Added `minWidth={0}` to the `ResponsiveContainer`s in [sparkline.tsx](src/components/sparkline.tsx), [spending-category-chart.tsx](src/app/(app)/dashboard/_components/spending-category-chart.tsx), and [weekly-recap.tsx](src/app/(app)/dashboard/_components/weekly-recap.tsx). Sparkline also pinned to a fixed pixel height instead of `100%` since the wrapper already has `height: 30`.
- **Malformed cubic-bezier paths in the landing FIRE viz (commit 4d36085).** [page.tsx:193–196](src/app/page.tsx#L193) declared four-curve `<path d="…">` strings with two `C`-segment endpoints but only enough numbers for one and a half (`C40,35 80,25 120,18 160,12 200,8` — the trailing `160,12 200,8` is 4 numbers where the second segment needs 6). Switched the second segment to `S` (smooth-cubic continuation), which only needs 4 numbers and preserves the intended shape. Stops the four `<path> attribute d: Unexpected end of attribute` errors on `/`.
- **Admin nav gate restored (2026-04-23, commit [4656656](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/4656656)).** `db9fd75` (SQLite purge) removed `/api/auth/unlock` but `src/components/nav.tsx` was still fetching it — the 404 put `isAdmin` at `false` for every user and the **Admin** link never rendered. Repointed nav at `/api/auth/session`, and taught that endpoint to emit `authMethod`, `isAdmin` (looked up from `users.role` in managed mode), plus `email` and `displayName` for future client consumers. No schema change. See [src/app/api/auth/session/route.ts](src/app/api/auth/session/route.ts).

### Security
- **V2 remediation batch (2026-04-23, commits [0479d88](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/0479d88) + [5948c91](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/5948c91) + [689c199](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/689c199)).** Four of the five streams from [AUDIT_REMEDIATION_PLAN_V2.md](../AUDIT_REMEDIATION_PLAN_V2.md) shipped in one push. Only Stream D (encryption coverage for `accounts.name` et al.) remains — explicitly scoped as multi-session.
  - **Stream A — `xlsx@0.18.5` CVE-2023-30533.** Swapped to `exceljs@^4.4.0`. [src/lib/excel-parser.ts](src/lib/excel-parser.ts) rewritten against the new API; both exports (`parseExcelSheets`, `extractExcelRows`) are now async because `workbook.xlsx.load()` is Promise-based. Added a `cellToString()` helper normalizing exceljs's richer cell shapes (rich text, hyperlink, formula `{result}`, Date, error) to the plain strings the downstream row logic expects. Four callers (`/api/import/preview`, `/api/import/excel-map`, `/api/import/email-webhook`, `src/lib/email-import/parse-attachments`) updated to `await`. `load()` cast through `any` because exceljs's `index.d.ts` declares its own `Buffer extends ArrayBuffer` that doesn't line up with Node's `Buffer<ArrayBufferLike>` under modern `@types/node` — runtime is fine. Package-lock regenerated on the Linux deploy host (same c08b194 pattern) to preserve `@emnapi/runtime` + `@esbuild/linux-x64` optional deps that Windows npm strips.
  - **Stream B — CSP flip Report-Only → enforced.** One-line header-name change in [next.config.ts](next.config.ts). Watch window in 50a1742 was clean. Verified on prod: `curl -I https://finlynq.com/` returns `Content-Security-Policy:` (no `-Report-Only` suffix) on both public (`/`, with GA whitelist) and app (`/dashboard`, locked-down) routes — the route-aware middleware CSP takes precedence on website paths and the `next.config.ts` CSP covers the rest. Rollback is a one-line flip of the header name back.
  - **Stream C — OAuth hygiene.** [src/lib/oauth.ts](src/lib/oauth.ts): `consumeAuthCode` switched from `SELECT → UPDATE SET used=1` to `DELETE ... RETURNING` for atomic claim (concurrent exchanges on the same code can't both succeed; spent codes + their `dek_wrapped` are gone from DB immediately). `refreshAccessToken` now does rotation + reuse detection — rows carry a `revoked_at timestamptz`, refresh flips the live row to revoked atomically (`UPDATE ... WHERE revoked_at IS NULL RETURNING`), and a subsequent presentation of the same (already-revoked) refresh token triggers revocation of every live access token for that user (token-theft containment). `validateOauthToken` filters `revoked_at IS NULL`. Schema migration [scripts/migrate-oauth-revoked-at.sql](scripts/migrate-oauth-revoked-at.sql) adds the column + a partial index `(token) WHERE revoked_at IS NULL` so validation stays fast as revoked rows accumulate. Idempotent (`ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`). Applied to prod + staging + dev before the code deploy.
  - **Stream E — `mcp_uploads`.** Verified on prod 2026-04-23: table was already present with full column set, both expected indexes, and `users(id)` FK. CLAUDE.md's stale bullet got cleared with no code change.
  - **Deploy:** one GHA run ([24851507625](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/actions/runs/24851507625)), typecheck + build + SSH deploy green. No downtime. Schema migration for Stream C applied via `psql` before the code push.
- **Re-audit follow-up: API keys now hashed at rest (2026-04-23, commit [b60a439](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/b60a439)).** `settings.value` for `key='api_key'` stored the raw `pf_*` secret; a DB read compromised every active key. Switched to `sha256:<64 hex>` at rest. [src/lib/api-auth.ts](src/lib/api-auth.ts) hashes on write (`getOrCreateApiKey`, `regenerateApiKey`) and on validation lookup; a raw-value fallback in `validateApiKey` silently migrates legacy rows on their next use, so there was no deploy-vs-migration ordering window. The DEK-envelope path is untouched — the raw key provided per-request is still the unwrap input; only the server's stored copy changed. New response contract on `/api/settings/api-key` GET: `{ apiKey: string | null, hasKey: true, hasDekWrap }` — raw key is returned only on first creation or regenerate, `null` thereafter. Settings + MCP-guide pages updated with a "save this key now" banner on fresh keys and a "regenerate to view" state when only a hash is on file. One-shot sweep at [scripts/migrate-hash-api-keys.ts](scripts/migrate-hash-api-keys.ts) hashed all dormant keys on prod (5 rows).
- **Re-audit follow-up: `update_goal` SQL injection + 128-bit email-import token (2026-04-23, commit [df1de92](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/commit/df1de92)).** Two regressions surfaced during the 2026-04-23 re-audit, both fixed in one commit.
  - `update_goal` in [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) was using `sql.raw(updates.join(", "))` with manual quote escaping on `name` and unescaped `deadline`/`status` interpolation — same class of SQLi as the `update_account` bug fixed in `50a1742`, same file, missed in the V1 sweep. Switched to the parameterized `sql.join` pattern used by `update_account` / `update_subscription`, plus a `rowCount` ownership check. Scope of the bug was self-mutation (user id filter still in WHERE) but the class matters.
  - Email-import tokens regressed from 128 bits to 32 bits when the Resend Phase A code (`385cda5`) rewrote `/api/import/email-config` — `randomUUID().slice(0, 8)` instead of the `randomBytes(16).toString("hex")` H6 fix from the April 11 audit. Restored at [src/app/api/import/email-config/route.ts](src/app/api/import/email-config/route.ts) and widened the [address-router.ts](src/lib/email-import/address-router.ts) regex to `[a-f0-9]{8,64}` so any existing 8-hex addresses keep routing during the transition — the DB lookup on the full address is the actual authorization.
- **Plan for remaining exposures at [AUDIT_REMEDIATION_PLAN_V2.md](../AUDIT_REMEDIATION_PLAN_V2.md)**: `xlsx` CVE, CSP flip to enforced, OAuth hygiene, encryption coverage for `accounts.name` et al., and the `mcp_uploads` schema drift on prod.
- **Full audit remediation (2026-04-22).** Addressed 3 critical, 4 high, 4 medium findings in one commit. Plan: [AUDIT_REMEDIATION_PLAN.md](../AUDIT_REMEDIATION_PLAN.md).
  - **Critical — stdio MCP user isolation.** `mcp-server/index.ts` now requires `PF_USER_ID` at boot (exits otherwise). ~68 SQL queries across `register-core-tools.ts`, `tools-v2.ts`, `tools-import-templates.ts` now filter by `user_id`. Every UPDATE/DELETE has an ownership pre-check. INSERTs bind userId from closure, never from tool arguments. Before the fix, a stdio caller against a multi-user DB could read and destructively write across all tenants.
  - **Critical — SQL injection in MCP `update_account`.** Replaced `sql.raw()` + manual quote escaping with parameterized `sql` fragments.
  - **Critical — CSV import cross-user attach.** All name-based lookups in `src/lib/csv-parser.ts` now filter by userId. Before, one user's import could attach transactions to another user's account if names collided.
  - **High — `encryptionV` bump in `wipeUserDataAndRewrap`.** Multi-tab password resets now correctly invalidate cached DEKs.
  - **High — `PF_JWT_SECRET` fatal in prod.** `src/lib/auth/jwt.ts` throws at module load if missing in production; dev still falls back with a one-time warn.
  - **High — OAuth DCR rate limit.** `/api/oauth/register` capped at 10 registrations/hour/IP.
  - **High — Security headers in `next.config.ts`.** HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy enforced. CSP ships as Report-Only — flip to enforced after a week of clean console.
  - **Medium — Input/request caps.** `/api/chat` message ≤ 2000 chars. `/api/data/import` and `/api/import/execute` reject bodies > 20 MB (413) and imports > 50 000 transactions (422).
  - **Medium — Verified OAuth DEK envelope** end-to-end in `src/lib/oauth.ts` (authorize → token-exchange → refresh all wrap/unwrap correctly; audit's "unknown" was a wrong path).
  - **Deferred:** `xlsx@0.18.5` has CVE-2023-30533 — fix lives only in SheetJS paid/CDN channel, not npm. `pdf-parser.ts:55` pre-existing type error.

### Changed
- **Premium fintech dark redesign.** Landing page ([src/app/page.tsx](src/app/page.tsx)) rebuilt from the claude.ai/design handoff — sticky blur nav, animated hero chart (scrolling ticker + draw-on SVG line), 6-tile feature grid, 3-step flow, MCP query demo, 4-node zero-knowledge privacy diagram, $0 pricing. Styles scoped under `.fl-landing` in [src/app/landing.css](src/app/landing.css). Scroll reveals via `IntersectionObserver`.
- **App-wide design system refreshed to match.** shadcn CSS tokens in [globals.css](src/app/globals.css) remapped from indigo `hue 265` to amber `#f5a623` primary + teal/coral chart semantics; ink palette `#0b0d10`/`#101317`/`#161a1f`/`#1e242b`. Light mode shares the amber accent. `.text-gradient` retuned to amber→warm-orange.
- **Logo & favicon** updated to the new amber-stroked rounded square with ascending bar-chart path (`#f5a623`). Old indigo/violet "F + chain link" mark removed. See [FinlynqLogo.tsx](src/components/FinlynqLogo.tsx) + [public/favicon.svg](public/favicon.svg).
- **Nav** collapsed 12 per-category icon accents (blue, violet, indigo, emerald, pink, sky, cyan, rose, slate, teal, purple, red) down to a single `text-primary` amber accent on active state. Inactive icons muted. `ACTIVE_ACCENT` constant in [nav.tsx](src/components/nav.tsx).
- **Typography.** Added `Instrument Serif` (italic) via `next/font/google` for display accents in `<em>` on the landing; `--font-instrument-serif` available to any component that wants it.

### Added
- **MCP parity expansion — 41 new tools bringing the surface to 86 HTTP / 80 stdio (2026-04-23).** Plan: [Research/mcp-parity-plan.md](../Research/mcp-parity-plan.md).
  - **Loans (6):** `list_loans`, `add_loan`, `update_loan`, `delete_loan`, `get_loan_amortization`, `get_debt_payoff_plan` (avalanche / snowball comparison)
  - **FX rates (5):** `get_fx_rate`, `list_fx_overrides`, `set_fx_override`, `delete_fx_override`, `convert_amount`
  - **Subscriptions (9):** `list_subscriptions`, `add_subscription`, `update_subscription`, `pause_subscription`, `resume_subscription`, `cancel_subscription`, `delete_subscription`, plus `detect_subscriptions` + `bulk_add_subscriptions` (payee-grouping runs against the per-user in-memory tx cache since SQL GROUP BY on encrypted ciphertext is broken)
  - **Rules (5):** `list_rules`, `update_rule`, `delete_rule`, `reorder_rules`, `test_rule` (dry-run against user's transactions before committing)
  - **Transaction splits (5):** `list_splits`, `add_split`, `update_split`, `delete_split`, `replace_splits` (atomic, validates sum equals parent amount)
  - **Bulk edit (6):** `preview_bulk_update`/`execute_bulk_update`, `preview_bulk_delete`/`execute_bulk_delete`, `preview_bulk_categorize`/`execute_bulk_categorize` — all preview/execute pairs gated by a confirmation token so Claude cannot widen scope between steps
  - **Suggest (1):** `suggest_transaction_details` returns top-N category + tag suggestions using rule matches + historical frequency
  - **File import (4) + upload endpoint:** `POST /api/mcp/upload` (CSV/OFX/QFX, 5 MB cap) → `list_pending_uploads` → `preview_import` → `execute_import` / `cancel_import`. Uploads expire after 24 h; a 30-min cleanup sweep runs from [instrumentation.ts](instrumentation.ts)
- **Foundation primitives supporting the MCP expansion:**
  - [src/lib/mcp/confirmation-token.ts](src/lib/mcp/confirmation-token.ts) — HMAC-SHA256 signed, 5-min TTL, canonical-JSON payload hash; distinct rejection reasons (`payload-mismatch`, `expired`, `user-mismatch`, `operation-mismatch`, `bad-signature`, `malformed`) so tool errors are actionable
  - [src/lib/mcp/user-tx-cache.ts](src/lib/mcp/user-tx-cache.ts) — per-user LRU (10 users × 50k rows) of decrypted transactions; `invalidateUser(userId)` wired into 11 API routes + 6 existing MCP write tools
  - Deploy-generation force-logout: JWT `gen` claim rotates on every deploy, 401s carry `{code: "deploy-reauth-required"}`; [deploy.sh](deploy.sh) installs a systemd drop-in stamping `DEPLOY_GENERATION` before restart
- **Schema:** [drizzle-pg/0003_add_mcp_uploads.sql](drizzle-pg/0003_add_mcp_uploads.sql) adds the `mcp_uploads` table tracking upload lifecycle (pending → previewed → executed / cancelled / expired). Apply per environment with `psql -f` before deploying (same pattern as prior migrations).
- **Public demo account** (`demo@finlynq.com` / `finlynq-demo`) seeded on production with 6 months of realistic sample data (253 transactions across 4 accounts, 4 investment buys, 4 budgets, 3 portfolio holdings, 2 goals) so first-time visitors can explore the app without signing up
- `scripts/seed-demo.ts` — idempotent demo seeder; `npm run seed:demo`
- `deploy/finlynq-demo-reset.{service,timer}` — systemd unit + timer that reseeds the demo account nightly at 03:00 UTC; install script at `deploy/install-demo-reset.sh`
- `login_count` and `last_login_at` columns on the users table; incremented on every successful login (and every MFA-completed login) so demo engagement can be measured with one SQL query
- `/mcp` and `/mcp/*` vanity redirects (308) to `/api/mcp` — shorter URL for the "Connect to Claude" paste flow
- `src/lib/holdings-value.ts` — computes live market value of portfolio holdings grouped by account, with FX conversion to the account's native currency
- Dashboard account balances now include market value of holdings (previously cash-only — brokerage accounts looked artificially negative)
- ETF registry expanded from 19 to ~65 symbols in `src/lib/price-service.ts`: SPY, IVV, QQQ, SCHB, SCHD, VUG, VTV, VYM, DIA, ITOT, SPLG, VO, VB, VXF, IJH, IJR, IWM, sector ETFs (XLK/XLF/XLV/XLE/…), VEA/IEFA/EFA/SCHF, VWO/IEMG/EEM/SCHE, VXUS/VT/ACWI/IXUS, bond ETFs (BND/AGG/TLT/SHY/LQD/HYG/MUB/BNDX), VNQ/VNQI/GLD/IAU/SLV, and Canadian ETFs (VFV/XIC/XIU/ZCN/VGRO/VEQT/VBAL/XEQT/XGRO/XBAL/ZSP/ZEA/XEC/XUU/XEF/VDY/XAW). Fixes common ETFs being miscategorized as "stock"
- Donation banner (shown after 30 days, fully dismissible)
- Support page with donation links
- docker-compose.yml for one-command self-hosting

### Changed
- Pivoted to open source under AGPL v3 with commercial exception
- Replaced Stripe subscription billing with donation model (GitHub Sponsors, Ko-fi)
- Removed SQLite support — PostgreSQL only for simpler, cleaner self-hosting
- Added staging/demo environment (demo.finlynq.com)
- Multi-branch CI/CD pipeline (dev → staging → main)
- Docker image published to ghcr.io/finlynq/finlynq on every release
- CSP `img-src` now allows CoinGecko image domains so crypto icons render in the portfolio page

### Removed
- Stripe billing integration
- SQLite / SQLCipher database adapter
- Trial banner and subscription gating
- Redis and S3/AWS backup sections removed from `.env.example` (no longer applicable)

## [0.1.0] - 2026-04-11

### Added
- Initial open-source release
- Transaction management (import CSV, OFX, PDF, Excel; manual entry; bulk edit; splits)
- Budget tracking with envelope budgeting, rollover, and templates
- Investment portfolio (stocks, ETFs, crypto) with XIRR, benchmarking, Monte Carlo
- Goals tracking with progress visualization
- Loan amortization and debt payoff planner
