- FIRE / retirement calculator with Monte Carlo simulation
- Reports: income statement, balance sheet, Sankey cash flow, year-over-year
- Dashboard: net worth, health score, spotlight alerts, weekly recap
- Subscription tracker with auto-detection
- Bill calendar
- AI chat UI with natural language financial queries
- What-if scenario modeling
- MCP server v3 with 27+ tools (read + write, Streamable HTTP + stdio)
- OAuth 2.1 + Dynamic Client Registration for MCP
- PostgreSQL database with Drizzle ORM (22 tables)
- Dark / light / system theme
- Mobile-responsive layout with bottom tab bar
- Self-hosting via Docker or manual Node.js setup
# Changelog

All notable changes to Finlynq are documented here.
Format: [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/)
Versioning: [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/)

## [Unreleased]

### Security
- **OAuth DCR + PKCE + consent-screen hardening (B2, 2026-05-07).** Closes the C-4 / H-10 / H-11 / H-12 chain in [SECURITY_REVIEW_2026-05-06.md](../SECURITY_REVIEW_2026-05-06.md): a freshly-registered DCR client could ship empty `redirect_uris` and the authorize handler had an "allow any if list empty" branch, which (combined with the wrapped DEK on the auth code, the `plain` PKCE downgrade also accepted, and a consent screen that lied about "Read-only access") turned one phished click into a DEK-exfil primitive on attacker.example.com. **Registration:** `registerClient` now validates every entry of `redirect_uris` BEFORE the INSERT — must be HTTPS, or `http://localhost` / `http://127.0.0.1` (with optional port and path) for local dev. Empty arrays, oversized arrays (>5 entries), non-string entries, and any other scheme/host are rejected with a new `ClientRegistrationError` mapped to RFC 7591 §3.2.2 shape (`{error: "invalid_redirect_uri", error_description: ...}`, 400). **Authorize:** the `/api/oauth/authorize` POST handler dropped the legacy "allow any if registered list empty" branch and now requires the presented `redirect_uri` to be an exact-string match against the client's registered list — same check on BOTH `allow` AND `deny` (the deny path used to skip the membership check, which let an attacker craft an authorize URL pointing at attacker.com, wait for the user to click Deny, and exfil the `state` parameter). Off-list URIs return a 400 JSON response and keep the user on the Finlynq origin instead of redirecting. The page-level `handleDeny()` was rewritten to route through the same server-side endpoint so the client-side branch can't bypass the check. **PKCE:** `code_challenge_method=plain` is rejected at three layers — at the route handler (clean 400 with `invalid_request`), at `createAuthCode` (throws if a non-S256 method ever reaches issue time), and at `consumeAuthCode` (refuses to validate non-S256 rows even if one somehow got persisted). New `isValidPkceMethod` helper centralizes the check; `verifyPkceS256` was switched from `===` to `crypto.timingSafeEqual` (length-equal first, then constant-time compare) — pure hygiene since the challenge is technically public on the redirect URL, but the discipline pays off if the helper is ever reused in a more sensitive context. **Auth code TTL:** lowered from 10 minutes to 60 seconds. RFC 6749 §4.1.2 explicitly notes auth codes "should be short lived"; production OAuth deployments typically issue 60–120s. The auth code carries the wrapped DEK so a stolen code is a DEK-exfil primitive — tightening the window from 600s to 60s narrows the exploit window dramatically while still leaving plenty of slack for the network round-trip a legit client makes between the redirect callback and `/api/oauth/token`. **Consent screen:** the misleading "Read-only access — no transactions will be modified" copy was replaced with an amber-bordered warning callout: "[Client] will be able to read AND write your financial data — including creating, editing, and deleting transactions — until you revoke the connection." Permission list updated with an explicit "Create, edit, and delete transactions, rules, and goals" row. The page now fetches the actual registered client metadata from a new `GET /api/oauth/client/[clientId]` endpoint and displays the registered `client_name` plus a "Registered as ..." subtitle when the well-known mapping doesn't cover the client_id — lets the user spot phishing clients whose registered name doesn't match what they expected (e.g. "Cloude" or "Anthropic Helper"). The redirect URI is now displayed in full in a monospace block instead of just hostname-only, so a phishing client can't hide behind a path-based exfil URL like `https://api.example.com/legit-looking-prefix/exfil/...`. **Out of scope (follow-up):** real OAuth scope plumbing that would let a client request a read-only subset of the MCP tool surface — the consent screen no longer overstates the access being granted, but the underlying token still gets the full 90-tool HTTP MCP surface. That's a larger RFC + tool-registry refactor tracked as a separate issue. Files: [src/lib/oauth.ts](src/lib/oauth.ts), [src/app/api/oauth/authorize/route.ts](src/app/api/oauth/authorize/route.ts), [src/app/api/oauth/register/route.ts](src/app/api/oauth/register/route.ts), [src/app/api/oauth/client/[clientId]/route.ts](src/app/api/oauth/client/%5BclientId%5D/route.ts) (NEW), [src/app/oauth/authorize/page.tsx](src/app/oauth/authorize/page.tsx). Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean.
- **Middleware: CSRF Origin/Referer gate, CSP `object-src 'none'`, ALLOWED_ORIGINS startup validator (security review C-8 / H-2 + low; B5, 2026-05-07).** Three middleware-only hardenings in `pf-app/src/middleware.ts`. **(1) CSRF Origin/Referer gate.** State-changing methods (`POST` / `PUT` / `PATCH` / `DELETE`) that authenticate via the `pf_session` cookie now have their `Origin` (or `Referer` when `Origin` is missing) checked against an allowlist before any handler runs. The allowlist is the request's own origin (`request.nextUrl.origin`) plus anything explicitly listed in the `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` env var. Same-origin requests pass; cross-origin POSTs from a phishing page get a `403 { error: "csrf-rejected" }`. Bearer-authenticated requests, `X-API-Key`-header requests, and `?token=pf_*` query-token requests are explicitly skipped — they carry their own bound credential and are not vulnerable to confused-deputy CSRF (mirrors the strategy selection in `selectStrategy()` at `src/lib/auth/require-auth.ts`). Pre-login routes that don't yet carry the cookie also pass through (so `POST /api/auth/login` keeps working). The check runs in middleware so every state-changing route — including `/api/auth/wipe-account`, `/api/auth/logout`, `/api/auth/mfa/setup`, every staging mutation, every transaction write — gets it for free. Replaces the previous "no Origin check + SameSite=Lax cookie" posture (review finding H-2). **(2) CSP `object-src 'none'`.** Free win — every page now refuses to load Flash / `<embed>` / `<object>` content. Belt-and-suspenders against legacy plugin XSS sinks. **(3) `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` startup validator.** Each entry of `process.env.ALLOWED_ORIGINS` is validated at module load against `^https:\\/\\/[^\\s/]+$` / `^http:\\/\\/localhost(:\\d+)?$` / `^http:\\/\\/127\\.0\\.0\\.1(:\\d+)?$`. Wildcards (`*`), `null`, and paths throw a clear error at boot — fail-fast on a misconfigured prod deploy rather than silently accepting an over-broad CORS+cookie posture. **Deferred (CSP nonce migration).** The B5 plan also called for removing `'unsafe-inline'` from `script-src`. While reviewing the rendered HTML on app routes in production, every page server-rendered by Next.js 16 ships inline `<script>(self.__next_f=self.__next_f||[]).push([…])</script>` tags for RSC streaming, plus the `next-themes` FOUC-prevention script in the root layout. Removing `'unsafe-inline'` without first plumbing a per-request nonce through Next.js's RSC runtime + `next-themes` (which does accept a `nonce` prop) + the GA `<Script>` tags would break hydration on every page. The "fallback path" suggested in the plan ("remove `'unsafe-inline'` only from app routes — they should have zero inline scripts") proved wrong on inspection: app routes also ship Next.js's RSC inline scripts. The nonce + `'strict-dynamic'` migration is therefore tracked as a separate follow-up so it can be implemented and verified end-to-end without rolling back this batch. **Residual risk** (carried forward to that follow-up): an HTML-injection sink in a server-rendered template — e.g. an unescaped `displayName` interpolation in an HTML email body that is also rendered to the user's browser — could still execute. Email template HTML-escaping is finding M-19 in this same review and is owned by Batch B6. **Out of scope:** nonce-based CSP (follow-up — drop `'unsafe-inline'` site-wide once `next-themes` + GA + RSC inline scripts have nonces threaded); CSRF tokens (Origin/Referer is the simpler, equally-effective fix for the cookie-confused-deputy class); Permissions-Policy expansion. MCP tool surface unchanged at 90 HTTP / 86 stdio. Files: [pf-app/src/middleware.ts](pf-app/src/middleware.ts). Build: `npm run build` clean.
- **CSP nonce migration — remove `'unsafe-inline'` from `script-src` (B10, finding C-8, 2026-05-07).** Removes the last `'unsafe-inline'` from the `script-src` Content-Security-Policy directive that was carried over from B5 (PR #167) as a deliberate deferral. Middleware now mints a per-request nonce on every HTML response (16 random bytes via `crypto.getRandomValues` → base64) and emits `script-src 'self' 'nonce-<...>' 'strict-dynamic' https: http:` on the response CSP, plus `'unsafe-eval'` in dev only (Next.js HMR / React Refresh need it; production builds do not). The nonce is also propagated on the inbound request headers as `x-nonce` so server components can read it via `headers().get('x-nonce')`. **Auto-propagation to framework scripts:** Next.js extracts the nonce from the CSP header at app-render time (`get-script-nonce-from-header.js`) and applies it to every framework-emitted `<script>` tag including the RSC streaming inline `<script>(self.__next_f...)</script>` pushes, the bootstrap chunks, and the font/CSS preload `<link>` tags — verified end-to-end on `/`, `/cloud`, `/login`, `/dashboard` in both `npm run dev` and `npm run build && npm run start`. **Explicit propagation** for the `next-themes` FOUC-prevention inline script (root layout reads the nonce from `headers()` and passes it to `<ThemeProvider nonce={...}>`); the GA `<Script>` tags rely on the framework auto-extraction since the marketing pages they live on are `"use client"` and can't import an async server component. **`'strict-dynamic'`** propagates trust transitively: a script tagged with a valid nonce can in turn inject other scripts (covers Next.js HMR's runtime-injected chunks and the GA `gtag.js` external loader) without needing host-source allowlists. The legacy `https:` / `http:` fallback is for CSP1 browsers that don't understand `'strict-dynamic'`; CSP3-compliant browsers ignore those when `'strict-dynamic'` is present. **Marketing-route GA hosts removed from `script-src`** — `'strict-dynamic'` makes them redundant; the marketing-only branch in middleware now affects `img-src`/`connect-src` only. **Static CSP in `next.config.ts` left as a defensive fallback** — middleware overrides on every HTML response (matcher excludes only `_next/static` etc., which don't render HTML). **Acceptance:** `script-src` directive contains `'nonce-<22+ char base64>'` + `'strict-dynamic'` and does NOT contain `'unsafe-inline'`; nonce is fresh per-request; `x-nonce` response header matches the CSP nonce; `object-src 'none'` retained; manual browser verification on the four critical pages reports zero CSP violations and full hydration (`document.documentElement.classList` shows `"dark"` from the next-themes script, page is interactive). MCP tool surface unchanged at 90 HTTP / 86 stdio. Files: [src/middleware.ts](src/middleware.ts), [src/app/layout.tsx](src/app/layout.tsx), [src/components/google-analytics.tsx](src/components/google-analytics.tsx), [next.config.ts](next.config.ts) (comment update only), [tests/api/middleware.test.ts](tests/api/middleware.test.ts) (new B10 regression block — 8 tests asserting nonce presence, strict-dynamic, no unsafe-inline, fresh-per-request, header round-trip, ≥128-bit entropy, object-src none). Build: `npm run build` clean. Tests: `tests/api/middleware.test.ts` 14/14 passing.
- **MCP polish — staged-row `inArray`, pg-compat fixes, idempotency mutex, body cap (B8, 2026-05-07).** Wave-2 batch from `SECURITY_REVIEW_2026-05-06.md`: H-13, M-1, M-2, M-3, M-4, M-21, plus the low-finding `sql.raw` IN-list builders. **H-13 / staged-row delete:** the `approve_staged_rows` cleanup pass now uses Drizzle's `inArray(stagedTransactions.id, [...])` for the post-materialization DELETE instead of a hand-rolled `sql.raw`-with-CSV builder. Ids ride as parameterized values; the previous quote-escape pass was fragile in the face of new staged-row id formats. **Low / remaining `sql.raw` IN-list builders** (lines 6190 / 6277 / 6581 / 6777 / 6809 / 6957 / 6991 / 7721 / 7737 in `register-tools-pg.ts`): each swapped to a parameterized `ANY(ARRAY[...]::int[])` predicate built with `sql.join(...)` over `Number()`-coerced ids. Defense-in-depth — `Number()` coercion already gates input upstream, but the fragile pattern is now removed across the file. **M-1 / `bulk_record_transactions` idempotency replay race:** added an in-memory per-(user, idempotency-key) mutex that serializes the lookup → execute → persist window inside the Node process. The fast-path (cache hit on first lookup) is unchanged; the mutex only engages when the first lookup misses. After acquiring the mutex, the handler re-checks the cache before running writes, so a sibling call that already finished is replayed instead of double-inserting. The mutex map is stored on `globalThis` for HMR resilience and self-trims when fn() resolves. **M-3 / `pg-compat.transaction()` rewrite:** the helper now accepts `transaction<T>(fn: (tx: PgCompatQuerier) => Promise<T>)` and passes the BEGIN-ed `PoolClient`-backed querier to `fn`. The previous signature took a no-arg fn whose body called the OUTER `db.prepare(...)` (each acquiring a fresh pool client) — meaning ROLLBACK on the BEGIN-ed client couldn't undo writes that ran on sibling clients. Approach: signature change rather than wrapping the outer db in a proxy; current tree has zero callers (verified via repo-wide grep, plus a load-bearing comment in `register-core-tools.ts:956` confirming the historical no-op), so the breaking signature change has no production blast radius. ROLLBACK errors are now swallowed so the original exception always surfaces. **M-4 / `?` → `$N` translator literal-aware:** `convertPlaceholders` is now a small state machine that skips `?` characters inside single-quoted string literals (with `''` escape), double-quoted identifiers (with `""` escape), line comments (`-- … \n`), block comments (`/* … */`), and dollar-quoted strings (`$$ … $$` and tagged `$tag$ … $tag$`). The previous one-line `replace(/\?/g, ...)` pass would silently corrupt any literal SQL string that legitimately contained a `?`. No current caller embeds a `?` inside a literal; defense-in-depth. **M-2 / confirmation-token jti single-use:** `signConfirmationToken` now mints a `jti: crypto.randomUUID()` claim per token. `verifyConfirmationToken` rejects the second use of the same `jti` with `reason: "replay"`. The store is a bounded LRU (10k entries, sized to the operation rate; falls back to evict-oldest then per-token TTL prune) on `globalThis` for HMR resilience. The jti is consumed only AFTER all other verify checks pass — a wrong-user / wrong-payload attempt does NOT burn the token, so a legitimate retry isn't punished. Old tokens (no jti) verify without single-use protection — graceful degradation across deploys. Mismatch reasons (`user-mismatch`, `operation-mismatch`, `payload-mismatch`) are checked BEFORE the jti so existing tests over the same shared `token` continue to pass. **M-21 / body cap on `/api/mcp` POST:** Content-Length pre-check rejects bodies > 1 MB with HTTP 413 BEFORE running auth. Missing Content-Length returns 411; non-numeric returns 400. Mirrors the pattern in `/api/import/staging/upload/route.ts:91-97`. The MCP transport otherwise has no body-size guard. **Regression tests added.** `tests/mcp/pg-compat.test.ts` (9 cases) covers the literal-aware translator across single-quoted strings, `''` escape, double-quoted identifiers, line comments, block comments, dollar-quoted (untagged + tagged), and renumbering across mixed positions. `tests/mcp/idempotency-mutex.test.ts` (3 cases) covers same-key serialization, distinct-key non-serialization, and slot release on fn throw — mirrors the production helper's shape. `tests/mcp/confirmation-token.test.ts` extended with three M-2 cases — second-use rejection, mismatch-doesn't-burn-jti, distinct-jti-per-sign. `tests/api/mcp-body-cap.test.ts` (4 cases) covers oversize 413, missing Content-Length 411, non-numeric 400, and at-cap pass-through. Files touched: `mcp-server/pg-compat.ts`, `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `src/lib/mcp/confirmation-token.ts`, `src/app/api/mcp/route.ts`, plus the four test files. **MCP tool surface unchanged at 90 HTTP / 86 stdio.** Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean.
- **Hygiene PR — H-8 + 8 medium findings (B9, 2026-05-07).** Closes the following findings from [SECURITY_REVIEW_2026-05-06.md](../SECURITY_REVIEW_2026-05-06.md):
  - **H-8** — `validateApiKey` ([src/lib/api-auth.ts](src/lib/api-auth.ts)) compares stored / candidate API-key hashes via `crypto.timingSafeEqual` and exhausts the row scan so wall-clock does not depend on row order. The legacy plaintext-fallback branch is removed (dead — the original migration script wrote pre-domain-separation hashes that no longer match the current `authLookupHash` format).
  - **M-5** — `notifications/route.ts` POST handler awaits the four Drizzle `.update()` / `.insert()` chains it was previously firing-and-forgetting.
  - **M-8** — Backup export ([data/export/route.ts](src/app/api/data/export/route.ts)) writes `null` and bumps a top-level `decryptFailures` counter when an envelope's auth-tag check fails, instead of falling back to raw `v1:` ciphertext (CLAUDE.md "tryDecryptField MUST return null on failure" invariant). Stale `portfolioHolding` field dropped from `TX_FIELDS`.
  - **M-10** — Landing-page copy updated from "PBKDF2" to "scrypt" — DEK-wrapping KEK uses memory-hard scrypt per [envelope.ts](src/lib/crypto/envelope.ts). PBKDF2 still mentioned for the optional backup-export passphrase wrapper.
  - **M-14** — `finlynq-demo-reset.service` corrected (`WorkingDirectory=/home/projects/pf/pf-app`, `User=paperclip-agent`, per [DEPLOY.md](../DEPLOY.md)) and hardened with `ProtectSystem=strict`, `ProtectHome=read-only`, `PrivateTmp=true`, `NoNewPrivileges=true`, `ReadWritePaths=` pinned to WorkingDirectory + npm cache + /tmp.
  - **M-15** — `scripts/seed-demo.ts` `assertDemoDatabase()` guard at the top of `main()`: explicit opt-in (`PF_ALLOW_DEMO_SEED=1`) OR URL substring match for "demo" / "finlynq", AND a sanity check that refuses to run when `users` has more than one row.
  - **M-16** — `deploy.sh` `chmod 0700 /opt/finlynq-backups`, plus retention pruning of `*.sql{,.gz,.enc}` older than `${BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS:-14}` days. Encryption-at-rest for the dumps remains a follow-up.
  - **M-18** — `safeErrorMessage` ([src/lib/validate.ts](src/lib/validate.ts)) switched from a denylist heuristic to an allowlist — only `ZodError` and a new branded `AppError` class pass through. Postgres errors like "ECONNREFUSED" and `relation "..." does not exist` are now hidden behind the fallback. The cross-tenant FK rejection in `goals/route.ts` migrated to `AppError` so its message survives.
  - **M-22** — `/api/import` ([src/app/api/import/route.ts](src/app/api/import/route.ts)) gained a 20 MB `Content-Length` pre-check (413) and switched from `requireAuth` to `requireEncryption`.
  - **Tests added** for each finding: `tests/api-auth-constant-time.test.ts`, `tests/api/notifications-await.test.ts`, `tests/api/export-decrypt-failures.test.ts`, `tests/seed-demo-guard.test.ts`, `tests/api/import-content-length.test.ts`, plus rewritten `tests/validate.test.ts`.

### Added
- **MCP staging tools: list / get / update / link-pair / approve / reject (#156, 2026-05-06).** Seven new HTTP MCP tools so Claude can categorize an inbound statement before anything hits `transactions`. The staging tables (`staged_imports` + `staged_transactions`) become the natural surface for the "AI assistant manages your finances" workflow: an email/upload arrives → Claude lists what's pending → edits per-row metadata (category, payee, transfer pairing, holding, quantity) → approves what looks right. Tools registered in [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) at the bottom of `registerPgTools`. **Read tools:** `list_staged_imports` (statement-level metadata + a synthetic `reconciliation` block per the same shape the review page renders — statement balance vs current balance vs projected post-approval balance, NULL when un-bound), `get_staged_import` (full detail with decrypted display fields), `list_staged_transactions` (flat row list, filters by import / dedup-status / row-status / tx-type — useful for "show me every uncategorized pending row across all my pending statements"). **Write tools:** `update_staged_transaction` (per-row edits — same shape and validations as PATCH `/api/import/staged/[id]/rows/[rowId]`), `link_staged_transfer_pair` (sugar over update — sets `tx_type='R'` + cross-points `peer_staged_id` after validating same-import + opposite-amounts + different-account-names), `approve_staged_rows` (materialize into `transactions`), `reject_staged_import` (hard-delete + cascade). **Confirmation-token preview/execute pattern** on the destructive ones via [src/lib/mcp/confirmation-token.ts](src/lib/mcp/confirmation-token.ts): `approve_staged_rows` and `reject_staged_import` are single-tool, two-call — omit `confirmation_token` and you get a summary + signed token (5-min TTL); pass the token + same payload and the tool commits. `approve_staged_rows` ALSO accepts an optional caller-supplied `idempotencyKey` (UUID) — same 72h `mcp_idempotency_keys` pattern as `bulk_record_transactions`, scoped on `(user_id, key, tool_name='approve_staged_rows')`. Stored response body is metadata-only (`{ok, imported, errors, stagedImportId}`) so no plaintext redaction is required (CLAUDE.md "response_json MUST be redacted of plaintext payee / account name before persisting" — already metadata-only here, flagged in code). The preview branch deliberately does NOT consume the idempotency key — only the commit branch does — so a preview never blocks a future real submit. **Per-row encryption-tier branch** on every read (CLAUDE.md "Staged-transactions reads MUST branch on `encryption_tier` per row"): `'service'` rows decrypt with `decryptStaged()` (PF_STAGING_KEY, sv1: envelope); `'user'` rows decrypt with `tryDecryptField(dek, ...)` (user DEK, v1: envelope). Mixed tiers within the same staged_imports batch are expected mid-upgrade and handled correctly. `update_staged_transaction` re-encrypts edited fields under the row's EXISTING tier — never flips mid-edit. `import_hash` is NEVER recomputed by edits (load-bearing for cross-source dedup). The user-tier write path returns a clean error when the DEK is unavailable rather than half-applying. **Approve-time materialization** mirrors the REST `/api/import/staged/[id]/approve` route's classification: peer-paired transfers (both rows checked) mint a server-generated `link_id` (UUID v4 inside the handler) and INSERT both legs in one transaction with inverted amounts, `type='R'`, encrypted payee/note/tags under DEK, audit `source='import'`; target-account-paired transfers delegate to `createTransferPair()` which owns FX, transfer-category resolution, and the four-check link-id rule; everything else flows through `executeImport` with a per-row `portfolioHoldingId` hint that skips the holding-name resolver. Investment-account rows whose holding is unset get the account's Cash sleeve via `defaultHoldingForInvestmentAccount`. After commit, `invalidateUser(userId)` is called on the per-user MCP tx cache (CLAUDE.md "Every MCP tx-mutating write must call `invalidateUser(userId)`"). Staged rows are deleted on materialization; the parent `staged_imports` row is dropped only when nothing remains. **Cross-tenant isolation.** Every tool filters by `user_id` and returns "Not found" (the MCP equivalent of 404) when the id doesn't belong to the caller. `list_staged_transactions` verifies ownership before filtering when `stagedImportId` is supplied so cross-tenant attackers can't probe whether the id exists for someone else (vs. silently returning `[]`). `link_staged_transfer_pair` validates same-import + opposite-amounts + different-account-names. `update_staged_transaction` validates ownership of every FK pointer (`peerStagedId` → same staged_import + user; `targetAccountId` → user; `portfolioHoldingId` → user) and the mutual exclusion of `peerStagedId` vs `targetAccountId`. **Stdio MCP refuses all seven tools cleanly** with a single shared error message ("This tool requires the HTTP MCP transport because staging operations need to read or write encrypted data under your DEK. Connect via the HTTP MCP at /mcp instead, or use the web UI at /import/pending."). Read tools refuse too — even though service-tier rows would technically decrypt without a DEK, user-tier rows would silently surface raw `v1:` ciphertext to Claude. The refusal handler intentionally does NOT echo `PF_USER_ID` so stdio errors never leak the configured user id. Registered in [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts) so the tools appear in `tools/list` on stdio with a clear "use HTTP" pointer rather than vanishing. **MCP tool surface: 83 → 90 HTTP, 79 → 86 stdio** (+7 each). CLAUDE.md preamble + [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md) updated with the new count and per-tool descriptions. **Out of scope:** a "park this for later" tool that lets Claude push transactions INTO staging (separate ticket); a staging-aware `apply_rules_to_uncategorized` (the rule tools have a pre-existing `match_payee` bug — fix that first elsewhere); a bulk variant of `update_staged_transaction` (per-row updates over MCP are fine for the volumes we expect — single statement is ~50 rows max). PDF / connector ingest paths are unchanged. Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean.

- **Transfer / quantity / note / tags editors on the staging review page (#155, 2026-05-06).** Brings `/import/pending` to full-transaction parity. Each row in the review dialog now has an expand toggle that opens an inline editor exposing every editable field the live transactions table supports: **Type** (Expense / Income / Transfer), **Payee**, **Category**, **Note**, **Tags**, and — for investment-account rows — **Holding** + **Quantity**. Cross-currency rows can override **Entered amount** + **Entered currency**. Edits autosave on blur via a new `PATCH /api/import/staged/[id]/rows/[rowId]` endpoint. The dialog stays open across edits so the user can categorize a whole statement before approving. **Transfer pairing UI:** transfer rows (`tx_type='R'`) get a two-option pairing block — pick a **sibling staged row** (writes `peer_staged_id`) OR pick a **target Finlynq account** (writes `target_account_id`). The two are mutually exclusive, enforced both client-side (selecting one disables the other input and clears its value) and server-side (PATCH 400s when both are set). Peer dropdown only lists same-import siblings whose amount is the additive inverse of the editing row; target-account dropdown excludes the row's own source account. **Approve-time materialization extended:** [src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/approve/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/%5Bid%5D/approve/route.ts) classifies selected rows into three buckets before calling the pipeline. **Bucket A — peer-paired transfers:** mint a server-side `link_id` (UUID v4 inside the handler — NEVER from client) and INSERT both legs in one statement with inverted amounts, `type='R'`, `categoryId=<user's Transfer category>`, encrypted `payee`/`note`/`tags` under DEK, audit `source='import'`. Half-pairs (only one of two peer-linked rows checked in the user's selection) error out without materializing — committing one leg orphans it. **Bucket B — target-account-paired transfers:** call `createTransferPair()` (owns FX, transfer-category resolution, the four-check link-id rule). The staged row's amount sign decides which side is "from" / "to": negative = leaving the staged row's account, positive = arriving at the staged row's account. **Bucket C — everything else:** flows through `executeImport` as before. Investment-account rows pre-fill their `portfolio_holding_id` via a new `RawTransaction.portfolioHoldingId` hint that skips the holding-name resolver — no risk of auto-creating a phantom Cash sleeve when the user already picked one. Investment-account rows without a holding pick fall back to the per-account Cash sleeve via `defaultHoldingForInvestmentAccount` (mirrors the upload-pipeline's permissive behavior). **Encryption tier preserved on edits:** PATCH re-encrypts edited text fields under the **row's existing tier**: service-tier rows stay sv1: under `PF_STAGING_KEY`, user-tier rows stay v1: under the user's DEK. Never flips tiers mid-edit (login-time upgrade job is the only path that promotes service → user). **`import_hash` is NEVER recomputed** by the PATCH, even when payee was edited — load-bearing per CLAUDE.md ("`import_hash` always over plaintext payee", computed from bank-supplied plaintext at ingest, dedup-stable across the row's lifetime). Bank-side dedup runs against what the bank sent us, not what the user retitled it to. **Cross-tenant isolation:** every PATCH validates that the staged_import belongs to `userId`, the row belongs to `(userId, stagedImportId)`, the proposed `peer_staged_id` belongs to the same `(userId, stagedImportId)`, the proposed `target_account_id` belongs to `userId`, and the proposed `portfolio_holding_id` belongs to `userId`. All cross-tenant attacks return 404 (same shape as the rest of the staging API). **Per-row PATCH endpoint** added at [src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/rows/[rowId]/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/%5Bid%5D/rows/%5BrowId%5D/route.ts) — Zod-validated body, `requireEncryption()`. **MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio** — staging tools are tracked separately under #156. **Out of scope:** stdio MCP staging tools (#156); a `bulk_update_staged_transactions`; auto-detect transfer pairs at upload time (heuristic-heavy: same date, equal-and-opposite amounts, different accounts — defer; user can manually pair in the UI); backfilling existing email-staged rows with `tx_type='E'` (the schema migration's default already does this); fully-encrypted staging-row tags (still plaintext at staging today). Files touched: NEW [src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/rows/[rowId]/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/%5Bid%5D/rows/%5BrowId%5D/route.ts), NEW [src/components/staging/staged-row-editor.tsx](src/components/staging/staged-row-editor.tsx), [src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/%5Bid%5D/route.ts) (GET surfaces all editable fields), [src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/approve/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/%5Bid%5D/approve/route.ts) (transfer-pair branches + holding-id hint), [src/lib/import-pipeline.ts](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts) (`RawTransaction.portfolioHoldingId` hint), [src/app/(app)/import/pending/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/import/pending/page.tsx) (per-row expand + editor mount + accounts/holdings catalog fetch). Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean.

- **Statement-balance reconciliation callout on `/import/pending` (#154, 2026-05-06).** When a staged import carries a non-null `statement_balance` AND `bound_account_id`, the review dialog now renders a three-column "Statement says / Finlynq has now / After approval" callout above the row table. Match indicator is green when "After approval" lands within 0.01 of "Statement says", amber otherwise. Statement-balance-set-but-no-account renders a one-line "No account linked — statement balance not reconcilable." hint; CSV uploads without a balance render no callout at all (no clutter on the no-data path). **Display-only, never blocks** — the Approve button is unaffected by the match state. Statements lag, pending transactions clear later, the user is the judge. **Live-recompute** as checkboxes toggle: `currentBalance + Σ(amount of selected rows where dedup_status != 'existing')`. EXISTING-classified rows are filtered out of the sum even when selected, since they're already in the live `transactions` table and would double-count. The server's `projectedBalance` is the "approve-everything-eligible" baseline; the client owns the per-toggle live recompute. **Investment-account aware:** "Finlynq has now" routes through `getHoldingsValueByAccount(userId, dek)` for `is_investment=true` accounts (CLAUDE.md "Account balance for accounts with holdings = `holdings.value`, NOT `b.balance + holdings.value`") — same helper the dashboard already uses, no third aggregator added. Pure-cash accounts continue to use `SUM(transactions.amount)`. Statement balance is rendered in `staged_imports.statement_currency`; "Finlynq has now" is FX-converted via `getRate(account_currency → statement_currency, today, userId)` when they differ (same-currency hops at rate=1; FX-fetch failure falls back to displaying in account currency unconverted — accepted tradeoff for a display-only surface). **Manual-override input** for CSV/XLSX uploads was already plumbed under #153 (the upload card's "Statement balance" field) — no change to the upload route. OFX/QFX statements auto-prefill from `<LEDGERBAL>` at upload time. **MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio.** Files touched: [src/components/staging/reconciliation-callout.tsx](src/components/staging/reconciliation-callout.tsx) (new), [src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/route.ts) (GET handler returns a `reconciliation: { currentBalance, projectedBalance, pendingDelta, boundAccountCurrency }` block + surfaces `dedup_status`/`row_status` per row), [src/app/(app)/import/pending/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/import/pending/page.tsx) (imports + renders the callout above the row table inside the review dialog). **Out of scope:** CSV-footer balance auto-detect (heterogeneous formats — manual input handles it), multi-account reconciliation (bound to one account by design), per-row "this row brings the balance from X to Y" annotation (UI clutter, defer), share-count reconciliation against `holding_accounts.qty` (separate problem surface). Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean.

- **Route CSV/OFX/QFX uploads through staging; retire direct-commit (#153, 2026-05-06).** Replaces the old `/api/import/reconcile/{preview,commit}` pair with a unified single review surface — every upload now lands in `staged_imports` + `staged_transactions` and is reviewed at `/import/pending`, the same surface email-imported batches use. **New endpoint:** `POST /api/import/staging/upload` — multipart form (`file`, optional `accountId` / `templateId` / `tolerance`, optional CSV `statementBalance`) — parses, classifies NEW/EXISTING/PROBABLE_DUPLICATE against existing transactions, and persists everything in one transaction. Returns `{stagedImportId, redirectTo: "/import/pending?id=..."}`. CSV needs-mapping returns `422 type:"csv-needs-mapping"` so the same column-mapping dialog the reconcile UI used before still works. **Encryption tier:** uploads have a session DEK, so rows land at `encryption_tier='user'` (v1: under user DEK) — bypasses the service→user login-time upgrade job that email-import staging relies on. `import_hash` is computed from plaintext payee at ingest and never recomputed (load-bearing per CLAUDE.md). Read paths in `/api/import/staged/[id]` + `/approve` already branch on `encryption_tier` per row. **Approve-time format tag:** `/api/import/staged/[id]/approve` now selects `staged.source` + `staged.fileFormat` and tags `transactions.tags` with `source:csv|ofx|qfx|email|excel` accordingly — uploads no longer get `source:email` mis-stamped. Existing tags from staging are preserved (idempotent merge). **Schema fields populated** on `staged_imports`: `source='upload'`, `file_format` (`'csv'|'ofx'|'qfx'`), `original_filename`, `bound_account_id`, `statement_balance` + `statement_balance_date` + `statement_currency` (OFX `<LEDGERBAL>` / user-typed for CSV), `statement_period_start` + `statement_period_end` (derived from row dates). `staged_transactions.dedup_status` is persisted at ingest so the review page renders without re-classifying. **UI changes.** `/import/reconcile` is now an upload entry point only: the in-page edit-and-commit table is gone. Upload card adds an optional "Statement balance" input for CSVs. `/import/pending` reads `?id=…` from the URL and auto-opens the detail dialog (wrapped the page in a `<Suspense>` boundary for `useSearchParams`). Pending list now shows upload icon + filename for upload-source rows and the existing mail icon + subject for email rows. Pending list endpoint surfaces `originalFilename` + `fileFormat`. **Deletes.** Route files `src/app/api/import/reconcile/{preview,commit}/route.ts`, the old test `tests/api/import-reconcile-preview.test.ts`, and `commitReconcile` / `MAX_RECONCILE_ROWS` / `ApprovedReconcileRow` / `CommitResult` from `src/lib/reconcile.ts`. The classifier `classifyForReconcile` is kept for future review surfaces. Account ownership + investment-account holding constraints continue to be enforced inside `executeImport()` (which the approve endpoint calls). **Email-import staging path is unchanged** — the webhook still calls `stageEmailImport()` and rows still land at `encryption_tier='service'`; only the upload path is new. **Out of scope:** XLSX uploads still route through the existing `/import` flow (sheet selection + column mapping); folding them into staging needs a column-mapping dialog on the review page. Statement-balance reconciliation UI (#154), transfer/quantity/note/tags editors on the review page (#155), and MCP staging tools (#156) are separate tickets. MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio. Files touched: `src/app/api/import/staging/upload/route.ts` (new), `src/app/api/import/staged/route.ts`, `src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/approve/route.ts`, `src/app/(app)/import/reconcile/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/import/pending/page.tsx`, `src/components/reconcile/upload-card.tsx`, `src/lib/reconcile.ts`, `src/lib/external-import/parsers/csv-pipeline.ts`, `src/lib/external-import/duplicate-detect-pool.ts`. Build: `tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` clean.

- **Unified-ingest columns on staging tables (#152, 2026-05-06).** Pure additive schema migration that prepares `staged_imports` + `staged_transactions` to become the single landing zone for every import source — CSV/OFX/XLSX uploads, email attachments, future Plaid sync, and MCP "park for later" requests. **No behavior changes:** existing email-import flow continues to work unchanged because every new column either has a sensible default (`tx_type='E'`, `dedup_status='new'`, `row_status='pending'`) or is nullable. **`staged_imports` gains 8 columns** for per-statement metadata: `statement_balance` / `statement_balance_date` / `statement_currency` (OFX `LEDGERBAL.*` or CSV/XLSX footer), `statement_period_start` / `statement_period_end` (transactions window), `bound_account_id` (FK to `accounts(id)`, populated when the user picks an account on upload or OFX `ACCTID` matches), `file_format` (`'ofx' | 'qfx' | 'csv' | 'xlsx' | 'pdf' | 'plaid' | 'mcp'`), and `original_filename` (display only). **`staged_transactions` gains 11 columns** for full-transaction parity: `tx_type` (CHECK in `('E','I','R')`, default `'E'`), `quantity` (investment trades), `portfolio_holding_id` (FK with `ON DELETE SET NULL` — required at approve time for investment accounts per CLAUDE.md), `entered_amount` / `entered_currency` (cross-currency rows per #129), `tags` (matches live `transactions.tags`), `fit_id` (OFX `FITID` carried through staging so dedup at approve time uses the same key the import pipeline uses), `peer_staged_id` (self-FK, `ON DELETE SET NULL DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED` so both legs of an in-staging transfer pair can be inserted in the same transaction), `target_account_id` (FK; mutually exclusive with `peer_staged_id` — used when only one leg is in staging and the user picks the destination), `dedup_status` (CHECK in `('new','existing','probable_duplicate')`, default `'new'` — persisted so the review UI doesn't re-run dedup on every page load), and `row_status` (CHECK in `('pending','approved','rejected')`, default `'pending'` — lets the upcoming MCP "approve a subset" path mark intent before the actual delete). **Two new indexes:** `(staged_import_id, dedup_status)` for the review UI's group-by-status pages, `(user_id, row_status)` for the upcoming MCP `list_staged_transactions`. **Idempotent migration** uses `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` and the `pg_constraint` guard pattern from `20260506_staging_encryption_tier.sql` for CHECK / FK constraints (Postgres has no native `ADD CONSTRAINT IF NOT EXISTS`). New columns are NOT encrypted (structural / numeric / FK); plaintext encryption for `tags` / `original_filename` deferred per the issue spec. Drizzle `stagedImports` / `stagedTransactions` definitions in `src/db/schema-pg.ts` mirror the new columns. **Out of scope:** any code that reads the new columns (review UI, approve endpoint, MCP staging tools) lands in #153–#156. **Migration sort order:** `20260506_staging_unified_columns.sql` sorts after `20260506_staging_encryption_tier.sql` (`u` > `e`) so the encryption_tier column is in place before this migration's `(user_id, row_status)` index touches the same row-set. **MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio.** Files: [scripts/migrations/20260506_staging_unified_columns.sql](scripts/migrations/20260506_staging_unified_columns.sql), [src/db/schema-pg.ts](src/db/schema-pg.ts). Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean.

### Fixed
- **Goals page balance honors investment-account market value (#151, 2026-05-06).** `GET /api/goals` `currentAmount` previously ran a flat `SUM(transactions.amount)` across every linked account, regardless of whether the account was a cash account or an investment account. For investment accounts that produced "net cash contributed" (deposits − withdrawals + uninvested dividend cash) — under the issue #96 trade-pair convention, a stock buy posts a cash leg with `amount=-cost` and a paired stock leg with `amount=0`, so trades net to ≈0 and the holdings market value never enters the rollup. Concrete repro: a six-figure goal linked to a single investment account showed `currentAmount` ~13% of target while the accounts page showed the same account at ~55%; the two numbers should agree (within rounding) when goal currency = account currency. Same class of mistake CLAUDE.md already called out for the accounts page ("Account balance for accounts with holdings = `holdings.value`, NOT `b.balance + holdings.value`"). The goals page didn't get that branch. **Fix:** `/api/goals` now branches on `accounts.is_investment` per linked account. Investment accounts contribute `getHoldingsValueByAccount(userId, dek).get(accountId).value` (the same aggregator the dashboard / accounts page uses — full per-currency cost-basis bucketing per #129, cash-leg substitution per #96, paired-cash-leg sell-branch skip per #128, all preserved). Cash accounts continue to contribute `SUM(transactions.amount)` unchanged. **Per-account FX normalization:** each contribution is converted from the account's currency into the goal's currency via `getLatestFxRate(...)` before summing, so multi-currency goals (e.g. a CAD goal linked to a USD account) now report a meaningful `progress = currentAmount / targetAmount` instead of a 1:1 mis-add. **Batching:** the legacy code ran one SUM query per goal inside `Promise.all`. The new code resolves the union of linked account ids across all goals, fetches account metadata (currency, is_investment) in one round trip, pulls cash-flow basis in one batched query grouped by accountId, calls the holdings aggregator once (shared across goals), and shares an FX cache across goals. **Stdio MCP `get_goals` is unaffected** — it's a separate read path (already returns ciphertext-only rows post Phase 4) and is flagged out-of-scope by the issue. **Cached `holding_accounts.qty`/`cost_basis` columns untouched** per CLAUDE.md "the cache is reserved for future use; today it is a trap" — the aggregator reads live `SUM(transactions.quantity)` × current price like the other aggregators. Acceptance: a goal linked to a single investment account now reports a `currentAmount` that matches the accounts-page balance for the same account when goal currency = account currency; cash-account-only goals report identical numbers to before (no regression on the common path). MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio. Files: [src/app/api/goals/route.ts](src/app/api/goals/route.ts), [CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md). Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean.

- **MCP cross-currency cost basis mislabeling — per-currency bucketing in 3 portfolio aggregators (#129, 2026-05-04).** For a `portfolio_holdings` row whose `currency` differs from its parent `accounts.currency` (e.g. a USD ETF held inside a CAD brokerage account), MCP HTTP `get_portfolio_analysis` and `analyze_holding` returned the account-currency cost basis (`SUM(transactions.amount)`) but tagged it with the holding's currency. The downstream `*Reporting` field then re-FXed the already-account-currency value, producing inflated nonsense. Concrete repro: a single buy `entered_amount=2,819.64 USD`, `account_amount=3,839.38 CAD`, `quantity=10` returned `avgCostPerShare=$383.94 USD` (wrong, that's the CAD figure mislabeled) instead of the correct `$281.96 USD`. The REST `/api/portfolio/overview` path was already correct via `entered_amount` + `entered_currency` per-currency bucketing; this issue ports that pattern to the other read-side aggregators. **MCP HTTP `accumulate()` rewritten:** `aggregateHoldings()` SQL now SELECTs `t.entered_amount`, `t.entered_currency`, `a.currency AS account_currency`, `ph.currency AS holding_currency`, and the cash leg's `entered_amount`/`entered_currency` (issue #96 stays correct). A pre-pass populates an FX cache (`entered_currency → holding_currency`) so `accumulate()` stays synchronous. Each row's buy / sell / dividend amount is FX-converted from `entered_currency` into the holding currency before summation. `get_portfolio_analysis` already tagged `tagAmount(buyAmt, ph.currency, "account")` — that tag is now correct because `buyAmt` is finally in holding currency. `lifetimeCostBasisReporting = buyAmt × fx(holding→reporting)` is a single hop instead of double. **MCP HTTP `analyze_holding` two fixes in one tool:** (a) `holdingCurrency` is sourced from `ph.currency` (the JOINed `portfolio_holdings.currency`), not `txns[0]?.currency` (which was `a.currency` from the JOIN); (b) the per-row buy/sell/dividend loop applies the same FX normalization. Without both, the tool would have looked self-consistent (label and value both wrong but matching) but inconsistent with `get_portfolio_analysis` for the same holding. **`src/lib/holdings-value.ts` per-currency bucketing:** `fkAggRows` now GROUPs BY `(holding_id, entered_currency)`. The post-query loop sums each bucket's `buyAmountInEntered × fx(entered → holding)` into `totalBuyAmountInHoldingCcy`, computes `avgCost` in holding currency, then multiplies by `qty` and FX-converts the resulting holding-currency cost basis back into account currency for the `AccountHoldingsValue` contract. The legacy "approximation that transaction amounts == account currency" comment is removed — it was the bug for cross-currency holdings. **CAD/CAD holdings unaffected** (FX hop is 1.0). Issue #96 `trade_link_id` cash-leg substitution composes with the new normalization (cash leg's `entered_amount` + `entered_currency` are used). Issue #128 paired-cash-leg sell-branch skip preserved. **MCP stdio aggregators were already refusing on Stream D Phase 4** (`streamDRefuseRead`), so the bug only ever manifested on HTTP and `holdings-value.ts`. **MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio.** Files touched: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [src/lib/holdings-value.ts](src/lib/holdings-value.ts), [docs/architecture/encryption.md](docs/architecture/encryption.md). Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean.

- **One-off data fix: tx 29100 reassigned from cash holding 497 to TFSA cash holding 425 (#81, applied prod + dev 2026-05-04).** Transaction 29100 (a +$10,000 contribution) was booked by the bank-import pipeline against the wrong cash-sleeve holding (id 497) in a TFSA account. It belongs on the TFSA CAD cash holding (id 425) which covers account 614. Single-row UPDATE on `transactions` mutates `portfolio_holding_id` from 497 to 425 and bumps `updated_at`; `source` is intentionally **untouched** to honor the audit-trio invariant (`transactions.source` is INSERT-only). Statement is double-scoped (`user_id = ? AND portfolio_holding_id = 497`) so it is idempotent and impossible to widen. Both envs verified pre-state (tx on holding 497 / account 614, both holdings same user, `holding_accounts(425, 614, owner)` covering row present per the issue #95 backfill). UPDATE returned 1 row in each env, post-state confirmed `portfolio_holding_id=425` and zero remaining tx-29100 rows on holding 497. `source='manual'` preserved in both envs. `pf-dev.service` and `pf.service` restarted afterwards so the MCP per-user tx cache drops the affected user (raw psql bypasses `invalidateUser(userId)`). No code or schema change. Docs: [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md) — applied-dates added to the existing playbook entry, staging command line removed (deprecated 2026-05-03), personal account names sanitized for the public open-source repo. Out-of-scope: holding 497's $72,791 phantom `dividendsReceived` figure was tracked under #84 (now shipped); auditing other potentially mis-routed bank-import rows on 497 is a separate sweep.

### Added
- **MCP `get_portfolio_analysis` gains `account_id` / `account` filter (#123, 2026-05-04).** Two new optional parameters scope the response to a single account, mirroring the pattern shipped in `search_transactions`. `account_id: number` is the FK fast-path (cheap ownership check, no fuzzy resolution); `account: string` runs through the existing `resolveAccountStrict` waterfall and rejects low-confidence matches (length-≥3 token overlap gate, same as `record_transaction` et al). When both are passed, `account_id` wins — same precedence used everywhere else in the MCP write surface. Implementation rides the existing `holding_accounts` JOIN grain (issue #25): a single extra `AND ph.account_id = ${scopeAccountId}` predicate on the `phRaw` query plus a defensive `if (scopeAccountId != null && !info) continue` guard in the per-row metrics loop. No new joins, no helper-signature churn (followed Option B in the issue plan). The `holding_accounts.qty` / `cost_basis` cached columns stay untouched per CLAUDE.md "Portfolio aggregator" — aggregators still read live `SUM(transactions.quantity)`. Warnings array unchanged in shape: account-scope misses join the existing `symbols` warnings as plain strings (`account_id=N: not found`, `<input>: no matching account (did you mean <suggestion> id=N?)`, `<input>: no matching account`). Empty-holdings short-circuit on rejection instead of erroring — keeps callers monomorphic, same pattern as all-`symbols`-unmatched. Multi-currency cash-leg sibling (issue #96) is unaffected because the LEFT JOIN to `cash` runs inside `aggregateHoldings`, not against the new filter. **Stdio `get_portfolio_analysis` schema gets `account_id` / `account` for transport symmetry**, but the handler still refuses the call wholesale under Stream D Phase 4 (no DEK on stdio means no decrypted account/holding names). Documented in the schema description as accepted-but-unused on this transport. MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio. Cross-refs: #86 (warnings-array pattern + `holding_id`-keyed `phMap`), #25 (`holding_accounts` JOIN grain), #99 (account-move refusal — the new filter scopes the read, doesn't move anything). Out of scope: same filter on `get_portfolio_performance` and `analyze_holding` (separate follow-ups if a user asks). Files: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts), [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md). Build: `tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` clean.

- **Goals: Edit affordance + multi-account linking via `goal_accounts` (#130, 2026-05-04).** Two related enhancements to `/goals`. **(A) Edit dialog.** Each row now exposes a pencil-icon Edit button alongside Mark-complete and Delete. Editing opens a dialog prefilled with the goal's current values; Save submits via `PUT /api/goals` and the row refreshes inline. The Add and Edit dialogs share a new inline `<GoalEditForm>` (mirrors the `<HoldingEditForm>` pattern from #100) so the two surfaces can't drift on validation, payload shape, or which fields exist. **(B) Multi-account linking.** A goal can now span N accounts (emergency fund across Chequing + Savings; down-payment across HISA + TFSA + non-registered). New `goal_accounts` join table with `(goal_id, account_id, user_id)` grain — same shape as `holding_accounts`. ON DELETE CASCADE on both FKs so deleting either side cleans up the join automatically. `Current Progress` rollup now sums `transactions.amount` across every linked account via `IN (accountIds)` instead of the single `g.accountId`. UI renders one chip per linked account on the row. **REST `/api/goals`:** POST/PUT accept `accountIds: number[]`; legacy single `accountId` still honored as a one-element list. PUT with `accountIds: []` unlinks all; omitting leaves links untouched. Server verifies every supplied id belongs to the user before INSERT (no cross-tenant FK — same risk pattern as backup-restore FK remap). GET decrypts every linked account's `name_ct` and returns `accountIds` + `accounts: string[]` per goal. **MCP HTTP `add_goal` / `update_goal` / `get_goals`:** `account_ids: number[]` parameter on the writers; legacy single `account` (fuzzy-match) still resolves to a one-element list. `get_goals` returns `accountIds` + decrypted `accounts` per goal. **MCP stdio `add_goal` / `update_goal`** continue to refuse cleanly per Stream D Phase 4 (no DEK on stdio); `account_ids` plumbed through their schemas so the refusal covers it. **Stdio `get_goals` rewritten** to read ciphertext only — the previous query referenced the dropped `g.name` / `a.name` plaintext columns and would 500 on first call. Now returns `accountIds` per goal with no decrypted names. **Backup/restore:** export includes `goalAccounts` per user; import wipes `goal_accounts` BEFORE `goals` (FK-safe, even though CASCADE would cover it) and restores AFTER goals via a new `goalIdMap` in `strip()`, throwing on unmapped FK. The `wipeUserDataAndRewrap` (password-confirmed wipe) and `/api/data` clear path also delete `goal_accounts` before `goals` inside their single-transaction flows. **Schema migration:** `pf-app/scripts/migrate-goals-multi-account.sql` creates the table + indexes and backfills existing single-account links from `goals.account_id`. **DEPLOY ORDER MATTERS — SQL FIRST, then code** (the new code 500s on `relation "goal_accounts" does not exist` if the SQL hasn't run). `goals.account_id` stays as a one-release legacy fallback (writes mirror first id; reads prefer the join). MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio. Files: [scripts/migrate-goals-multi-account.sql](scripts/migrate-goals-multi-account.sql), [src/db/schema-pg.ts](src/db/schema-pg.ts), [src/app/api/goals/route.ts](src/app/api/goals/route.ts), [src/app/(app)/goals/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/goals/page.tsx), [src/app/api/data/import/route.ts](src/app/api/data/import/route.ts), [src/app/api/data/export/route.ts](src/app/api/data/export/route.ts), [src/app/api/data/route.ts](src/app/api/data/route.ts), [src/lib/auth/queries.ts](src/lib/auth/queries.ts), [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts), [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md). Build: `tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` clean.

### Fixed
- **MCP array-binding tuple-cast regression — `get_goals` 500 on >=2 goals + 6 sibling landmines (#142 follow-up, 2026-05-04).** PR #142's §B fix wrapped `ANY(${array}::int[])` thinking the cast applied to the bound parameter. Drizzle's `sql` tag expands a JS array as separate scalar params, so `ANY(${goalIds}::int[])` rendered as `ANY(($2, $3)::int[])` — Postgres parses `($2, $3)` as a ROW literal and rejects the row→array cast. `get_goals` 500'd on every user with ≥2 goals (live repro on dev after the deploy); the 5 sibling sites were latent landmines that fired the moment a multi-element array reached them. Single-element arrays coincidentally worked because `($2)` parses as just `$2`. **All 7 sites switched to `ANY(ARRAY[${sql.join(...)}]::int[])`** — a real array constructor, not a row literal. Sites fixed: `get_goals` goal_accounts JOIN (1295), `record_transaction` ownership check (2087), `bulk_record_transactions` duplicate-hint scan (2714, 2716), `update_goal` ownership check (3580), `record_trade` cross-source duplicate scan (7142), and its sibling-account index (7182, `text[]` variant — same bug shape, same function as 7142, was outside the spec's verified-6 list but fixed because it would 500 on the first multi-account `record_trade` call). **Smoke test extended.** [tests/api/mcp-http-smoke.test.ts](tests/api/mcp-http-smoke.test.ts) gains a multi-element regression gate that seeds 2 goals into a fake DB, invokes `get_goals`, and asserts the rendered SQL contains `ANY(ARRAY[...]` and NOT the broken `ANY((?, ?,...))` row-cast shape. The original Phase-4 smoke gate missed this because every handler ran against an empty rowset, so `goalIds` was always `[]` (skip path) — the broken tuple-cast was never exercised. **No tool surface change** (still 83 HTTP / 79 stdio). No response-shape change. Stdio (`register-core-tools.ts`) untouched per spec — its `get_goals` was rewritten to ciphertext-only in PR #131 with a different code path. Files: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [tests/api/mcp-http-smoke.test.ts](tests/api/mcp-http-smoke.test.ts). Cross-ref: PR #142 (`b6cd86b`), issue #141. Build: `tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` clean. Vitest: 507 passing (+1 from the new multi-element regression gate); 90 pre-existing failures unchanged.

- **Stream D Phase 4: HTTP MCP raw-SQL cleanup follow-up — ~25 sites (#131 + #93 follow-up, 2026-05-04).** PR #131 (commit `ef26551`) cleaned the stdio surface and `resolvePortfolioHoldingByName` HTTP helper but left ~25 raw-SQL sites in [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) still SELECTing/JOINing dropped plaintext columns (`a.name`, `a.alias`, `c.name`, `g.name`, `l.name`, `s.name`, `ph.name`, `ph.symbol`). Live MCP smoke on dev (2026-05-04) found every HTTP read tool 500'ing with `Failed query: ... a.name ...`. Phase 4 went to prod the same day so this was broken on prod too. **Sites fixed (mechanical: replace plaintext-column refs with `*_ct` ciphertext + `decryptNameish(rows, dek)` / `decryptField(dek, ct)` at the JS boundary):** `get_account_balances` (§A1), `search_transactions` SELECT + the dead-code plaintext-OR branch in the category WHERE clause (§A2-A3 — kept HMAC-only path), `get_budget_summary` (§A4), `get_spending_trends` + `get_income_statement` (§A5), `get_categories` + `get_loans` (§A6), `list_subscriptions` + `get_subscription_summary` (§A7), `get_spending_anomalies` (§A8), `get_spotlight_items` budget rollup + `get_weekly_recap` spending/notable (§A9), `bulk_record_transactions` category lookup map (§A11 + sibling file `03-93-bulk-record-resolved-category-name-empty.md` — closes the empty `resolvedCategory.name` symptom from #93's follow-up scope by inserting the missing `decryptNameish(allCats, dek)` pass), `get_portfolio_analysis` `phRaw` (§A13), `aggregateHoldings` `fkRows` (eliminates the orphan plaintext-fallback branch — only `holding_name_ct` survives), `update_portfolio_holding` rawHoldings + `add_portfolio_holding` dup pre-check (§A13 + #95 follow-up — pre-check now refuses gracefully when DEK is absent rather than 500'ing on the `name = ${name}` plaintext predicate), `analyze_holding` raw + decrypt loop (§A14), `trace_holding_quantity` candidates + legs SELECT (§A15-A16), `list_loans` + `get_loan_amortization` + `get_debt_payoff_plan` (§A17), `list_subscriptions` SELECT + `add_subscription` dup-check (§A18 — refuses cleanly without DEK rather than running a `name = ${name}` query against a dropped column), `delete_subscription` response message (decrypt `name_ct` instead of reading the dropped column), `update_rule` category resolver (added missing `decryptNameish` pass), `list_rules` (§A19), `test_rule` + `list_splits` + `previewBulk` + `preview_bulk_delete` joined transaction SELECTs (§A20), `execute_import` account-by-name dedup map (§A21 — added `decryptNameish` pass). **§B fix — `get_goals` `ANY(${goalIds})` array-binding bug:** Drizzle's `sql` tag was expanding the JS array as separate scalar params (`ANY(($2, $3))`), which Postgres parsed as `ANY(ROW(...))` and rejected. Fixed by adding `::int[]` cast to match the existing convention at lines 2709/7136/7176. Same fix applied to `update_goal` and `record_transaction` account-ownership checks for consistency. **§C fix — `transfer.ts` raw-SQL fallback in `getTransferPairBySql`:** the Drizzle path was already Phase-4-safe; the raw-SQL fallback (used by stdio's `loadTransferPairViaSql`) still SELECTed `a.name` / `c.name` and would 500. Now reads `a.name_ct` / `c.name_ct` and decrypts at the JS boundary; `account_name` / `category_name` render as `null` when no DEK is present (stdio transport — same degradation pattern as the rest of the stdio surface). **Stdio out of scope** — PR #131 already cleaned that up correctly via `streamDRefuseRead` / `streamDRefuse`; this PR touches HTTP only. **CI smoke gate added** — new [tests/api/mcp-http-smoke.test.ts](tests/api/mcp-http-smoke.test.ts) registers the HTTP MCP tool surface against a fake `DbLike` that captures Drizzle `sql` template texts, then asserts no captured SQL string contains a dropped Phase-4 column reference. 19 tools covered (`get_account_balances`, `get_categories`, `get_goals`, `get_loans`, `list_subscriptions`, `search_transactions`, `get_portfolio_analysis`, `get_portfolio_performance`, `get_spending_trends`, `get_spending_anomalies`, `get_recurring_transactions`, `get_budget_summary`, `get_debt_payoff_plan`, `list_rules`, `get_subscription_summary`, `get_income_statement`, `get_spotlight_items`, `get_weekly_recap`, plus an overall sanity check). Verified that running the same test against `dev` (without the fixes) FAILS on 13 of 19 tools — proving the regex pattern catches the regression class. **MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio.** No schema migration, no env vars, no cron change. **Out of scope:** stdio surface (PR #131), the residual stale-data cleanup for orphaned `holding_accounts` rows from past misuse (one-off psql per CLAUDE.md), and the long-standing `match_payee` non-existent-column gotcha in rule-management tools (pre-existing, separate). Files: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts), [tests/api/mcp-http-smoke.test.ts](tests/api/mcp-http-smoke.test.ts). Build: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean. Vitest: 506 passing (+19 from the new smoke gate); 90 pre-existing failures unchanged.

- **Stream D Phase 4: stdio MCP read tools and 1 HTTP helper still hit dropped plaintext columns (#131, 2026-05-04).** The Phase 4 cutover (2026-05-03) physically dropped 8 plaintext display-name columns on `accounts`/`categories`/`goals`/`loans`/`subscriptions`/`portfolio_holdings`. The HTTP MCP refactor caught the write surface, but three surfaces still SELECTed/JOINed/INSERTed against the dropped columns and 500'd on every call: (a) `mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts` — ~30 stdio read/write sites; (b) `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts:resolvePortfolioHoldingByName` — the lone HTTP holdout, called from `record_transaction`/`bulk_record_transactions`/`update_transaction` whenever a caller passes `portfolioHolding` as a string; (c) `mcp-server/tools-v2.ts` — 4 stdio tools touching the dropped columns. **HTTP fix:** `resolvePortfolioHoldingByName` drops the broken `LOWER(name)/LOWER(symbol)` plaintext OR-branch. Matching is now HMAC-only via `name_lookup`/`symbol_lookup`. Without a DEK the helper returns a clean error rather than running a query that would never match. The candidate-list fallback decrypts `name_ct`/`symbol_ct` only. **Stdio strategy — read tools that depend on names refuse cleanly:** new helper `streamDRefuseRead(tool, table)` mirrors the write-side `streamDRefuse` and refuses with an actionable error pointing the caller at HTTP MCP / the web UI. Refused stdio read tools: `get_account_balances`, `get_budget_summary`, `get_spending_trends`, `get_categories`, `get_loans`, `get_income_statement`, `get_spotlight_items`, `get_weekly_recap`, `list_loans`, `list_subscriptions`, `get_portfolio_analysis`, `get_portfolio_performance`, `analyze_holding`, `get_investment_insights`, `test_rule`, `suggest_transaction_details`, `preview_import`, `execute_import`, plus `tools-v2`'s `get_subscription_summary` / `get_financial_health_score` / `get_spending_anomalies` / `search_transactions`. **Stdio strategy — write tools that fuzzy-match a name argument refuse the name path, keep id-only paths working:** `record_transaction`, `update_transaction`, `record_transfer`, `update_transfer`, `delete_account`, `delete_goal`, `delete_budget`, `create_rule`, `update_rule` accept the numeric-id parameters (`account_id`/`category_id`/`goal_id`/`assign_category_id`/`from_account_id`/`to_account_id`) and refuse the corresponding name parameters with a message pointing to HTTP MCP / the web UI. `bulk_record_transactions` and `record_trade` are refused entirely on stdio (the latter's cash-sleeve auto-create path INSERTed plaintext `name`+`symbol` that Phase 4 dropped). Helper SELECTs in the surviving paths now read only ciphertext-free columns (`id`, `currency`, `is_investment`); read-back name fragments in success messages are replaced with `#<id>` references. `trace_holding_quantity` keeps working but now requires `holdingId` (numeric); `symbol` (name) is refused. `list_splits`/`list_rules`/`delete_loan`/`delete_subscription`/`get_loan_amortization`/`get_debt_payoff_plan` drop the joined `category_name`/`account_name`/`loanName` from the row shape and surface ids only on stdio — the tool stays callable; the field shape is degraded. **Acceptance:** `grep -nE "FROM (accounts|categories|goals|loans|subscriptions|portfolio_holdings)"` of the three mcp-server files returns zero SELECT-list references to `*.name`, `*.alias`, `*.symbol`. `INSERT INTO <6-table>` is gone from stdio. `LOWER\((name|alias|symbol)\)` returns zero matches (the only remaining hit is inside a description string explaining why `record_trade` is refused). MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio — no tools added or removed, only existing tool implementations modified to refuse cleanly or accept id-only parameters. Files touched: [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts), [mcp-server/tools-v2.ts](mcp-server/tools-v2.ts). Build: `tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` clean.

- **Portfolio aggregators: skip paired cash-leg rows in realized-gain sell branch (#128, 2026-05-04).** When a stock buy was recorded via the `tradeGroupKey` two-leg pattern (issue #96 — stock leg `qty>0` + cash leg `qty=0/<0, amount=0`, both sharing a `trade_link_id`), three of the four portfolio aggregators counted the cash leg as a `qty<0` "sell" against the cash sleeve. With `sellAmt=0` and `avgCost~=1` (cash sleeves track 1 unit per dollar), the FIFO formula `sellAmt - sellQty*avgCost` produced a phantom realized loss of roughly `-sellQty` even though no cash was sold and no proceeds received. The cash sleeve's `quantity` (running net) is unchanged by the fix — only the realized-gain branch. **Three aggregators fixed (Option A — narrow exclusion):** REST `/api/portfolio/overview` ([src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts) — added `AND NOT (trade_link_id IS NOT NULL AND amount = 0)` to the SELL `CASE WHEN COALESCE(quantity, 0) < 0` predicate for both `totalSellQty` and `totalSellAmountInEntered`), MCP HTTP `accumulate()` ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) — added `t.trade_link_id` to the FK SELECT and the matching JS skip in the `qty < 0` branch — also affects `get_portfolio_performance` since it consumes `aggregateHoldings`), and MCP HTTP `analyze_holding` ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) — same SELECT + branch skip). Predicate is conjunctive — legitimate cash withdrawals (`trade_link_id NULL`, `amount<0`) keep their existing realized-gain treatment, so no regression on the rare non-paired cash-sleeve sale. **Two stdio aggregators verified clean (no fix needed, documented inline):** stdio `get_portfolio_performance` already gates `cost_basis` on `amount<0` and `proceeds` on `amount>0`, so a paired cash leg with `amount=0` contributes zero to both columns and `realizedPnL = proceeds - costBasis = 0`. Stdio `analyze_holding` doesn't compute realized gain at all — its qty<0 branch only adjusts `totalShares`, never derives a P&L number. **`holdings-value.ts` is unchanged** per the issue spec — it computes quantity + cost-basis only, never `realizedGain`. **Aggregator alignment preserved** (CLAUDE.md "Portfolio aggregator: four implementations must stay aligned"). MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio. Source plan: `reviews/2026-05-02/04-new-issue-d2-phantom-realized-loss-cash-sleeves.md`. Documentation: [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md) "Cost basis rules" gained a new paragraph documenting the skip predicate. Build: `tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` clean.

- **MCP `delete_portfolio_holding` matcher hardened — wrong-row deletion fixed, stdio Phase 4 ported, tx-cache invalidated (#127, 2026-05-04).** Both transports' handlers used `fuzzyFind`'s reverse-includes branch to resolve the `holding` argument. When a tiny-named holding existed in the user's portfolio (e.g. decrypted name `"S"` from a malformed import or a Stream-D-degraded row), every longer input — including a destructive call against `"TESTV"` — silently bound to that wrong row because `"testv".includes("s")` is true. The SELECT/COUNT/DELETE all keyed off the wrong row's id, the response read e.g. `Holding "S" deleted; 6 transaction(s) unlinked`, and the user's intended row stayed alive while someone else's was destroyed. The "cosmetic" report from the daily review was actually a destructive-op safety bug; the response message just made it look cosmetic because both name and count derived from the same wrong id (a circular check). **Fix:** new `resolvePortfolioHoldingStrict` helper in both [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) and [mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts](mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts) — searches `name` + `symbol`, then gates the substring/reverse-substring tier on a length-≥3 whitespace-token overlap (same shape as `resolveAccountStrict` / `resolveCategoryStrict`). Substring matches that fail the gate now return a `did you mean "<suggestion>" (id=N)?` error instead of binding to a vaguely-similar row. Reads still use plain `fuzzyFind` per CLAUDE.md "wrong filters are recoverable, wrong writes aren't". **Stdio Phase 4 port:** the stdio handler's SELECT read plaintext `name`/`symbol` columns that Stream D Phase 4 dropped on dev — call would 500 on `column does not exist`. Now reads `name_ct`/`symbol_ct`; with no DEK on stdio the rows ship raw `v1:...` ciphertext as their effective name/symbol, so the strict matcher cleanly returns "not found" for plaintext user input rather than crashing. Documented stdio degradation: use HTTP MCP or web UI for destructive holding ops. **Cache invalidation:** added `invalidateUserTxCache(userId)` after the DELETE on both transports, per CLAUDE.md "Every MCP tx-mutating write must call `invalidateUser(userId)`" — the FK `ON DELETE SET NULL` mutates linked transactions' `portfolio_holding_id` so the per-user tx cache must be invalidated. This was a pre-existing miss on this handler. **Out of scope (flagged):** `holding_accounts` rows referencing the deleted holding aren't auto-cleaned (CLAUDE.md documents the one-off psql cleanup); audit-trio bump on transactions whose `portfolio_holding_id` is FK-nulled is also a project-wide pre-existing gap. **MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio.** Build: `tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` clean.

- **Reconcile preview routes investment OFX/QFX through the canonical parser (#126, 2026-05-04).** `/api/import/reconcile/preview` only handed OFX/QFX to the legacy `parseOfx()` (bank/CC only), so an `<INVSTMTRS>` upload returned zero rows and the route 400'd with `"No transactions found in OFX/QFX file"` — even though the SAME file imported fine via `/api/import/preview`. Mirrored the detect-and-dispatch from the regular preview route ([src/app/api/import/preview/route.ts](src/app/api/import/preview/route.ts) ~line 84): test `/<INVSTMTRS\b/i.test(text)` BEFORE calling `parseOfx`, then route investment files through `parseOfxToCanonical` (`.ofx`) or `parseQfxToCanonical` (`.qfx`) from [src/lib/external-import/parsers/ofx.ts](src/lib/external-import/parsers/ofx.ts) / [src/lib/external-import/parsers/qfx.ts](src/lib/external-import/parsers/qfx.ts). Bank/CC OFX still flows through the legacy path unchanged. **Single-account rewrite:** reconcile is single-account by design; the canonical emitter sets each row's `account` to a synthetic external id (`ofx:invacct:broker:acct`) per brokerage statement found in the file, so the route rewrites every emitted row's `account` to the user-bound `defaultAccountName` before classification — same pattern the legacy bank/CC branch already uses. The classifier ([src/lib/reconcile.ts](src/lib/reconcile.ts) `buildAccountLookup`) resolves accounts by lowercase-name lookup, so no downstream changes were required. **Per-row `portfolioHolding` is preserved unchanged** so the reconcile UI can bind to a `portfolioHoldingId` before commit; `commitReconcile()` line 495 already gates investment-account rows on a non-null holding id, so the investment-account holding-FK invariant continues to hold. **`source = 'import'` audit stamp** is set inside `commitReconcile()` (line 582) and unaffected — this fix is parser-routing only. **Tests:** new [tests/api/import-reconcile-preview.test.ts](tests/api/import-reconcile-preview.test.ts) covers `<INVSTMTRS>` `.ofx` + `.qfx` route through canonical with `rows.length > 0`, plain bank OFX still uses legacy `parseOfx` (regression guard), and `portfolioHolding` is preserved on canonical rows. Build: `tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` clean. **MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio** — REST-only fix. Files: [src/app/api/import/reconcile/preview/route.ts](src/app/api/import/reconcile/preview/route.ts), [tests/api/import-reconcile-preview.test.ts](tests/api/import-reconcile-preview.test.ts).

- **MCP `record_transfer` honors `receivedAmount` on same-account cash-sleeve forex (#125, 2026-05-04).** HTTP `record_transfer` silently ignored the `receivedAmount` parameter when both legs of an in-kind transfer landed on the SAME account between two cash sleeves of conceptually different currencies (e.g. `Cash - USD` → `Cash - CAD` inside one CAD-currency brokerage account). Both sleeves inherit the parent account's currency at the schema level (`portfolio_holdings.currency = accounts.currency`), so the `isCrossCurrency` short-circuit in `createTransferPair` ([src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts), keys on account currencies) treated the conversion as same-currency and rewrote `receivedAmount = sentAmount` plus `destQuantity = sourceQuantity` — write succeeded, the destination sleeve was understated/overstated by the FX rate, no warning surfaced. **Heuristic-driven fix (Option 1).** When `createTransferPair` resolves both source and destination to cash sleeves (`portfolio_holdings.symbol_ct IS NULL`) inside the SAME account, AND the holding names carry divergent ISO-4217 suffixes (regex `[\s\-_]([A-Z]{3})$`), AND the caller passed `receivedAmount` and omitted `destQuantity`, the destination leg's `amount`, `quantity`, and `enteredFxRate` derive from `receivedAmount`. Source leg unchanged. Plain `Cash` (no suffix) and same-suffix moves skip the heuristic — `receivedAmount` is ignored as before, preserving the safe path for true same-currency moves. Symbol-bearing holdings are excluded by the cash-sleeve gate so a user-named `My ETF - USD` cannot trip the heuristic. **Out of scope:** `updateTransferPair` and `createTransferPairViaSql` carry the same dual-write semantics (L1080-L1100/L1213-L1214 and L1761-L1768/L1864 in `transfer.ts`); flagged for follow-up. Stdio `record_transfer` already refuses investment-account writes per the no-DEK / no-`portfolioHolding`-surface carve-out, so the bug doesn't manifest there. **Load-bearing rules preserved:** four-check transfer-pair rule (relaxed for same-account in-kind already), `link_id` server-generated, `trade_link_id` not reused for transfer pairs, `invalidateUser(userId)` after commit, audit trio (`created_at`/`updated_at`/`source = 'mcp_http'`) all preserved on both legs. Issue #92's strict find-only on the destination cash sleeve is preserved — the `no-cash-holding` error fires before the divergence heuristic runs. **MCP tool surface unchanged at 83 HTTP / 79 stdio** — semantic refinement on existing `record_transfer`. Tool description and `finlynq_help` updated. Files: [src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts), [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), [docs/architecture/mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md). Build: `tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` clean.

- **MCP `get_portfolio_analysis` `symbols` filter — AND-tokenize within a single entry, mirrored on stdio (#124, 2026-05-04).** The `symbols` matcher introduced in #86 used OR-within-entry semantics: tokens from a filter like `["VCN.TO (TFSA)"]` matched if ANY token (`vcn.to` OR `tfsa`) appeared in `name + symbol`, so unrelated cash sleeves named `TFSA-CAD` / `ATFSA-CAD` got swept into a single-symbol query. **HTTP fix** ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts)): the matcher now requires every whitespace/paren-separated token in a single entry to appear in the row's `name + symbol + account` haystack (AND); across entries the union (OR) semantic is unchanged.
